simple:
According to @deva[शाकल्य], the particle @deva[उञ्] before @deva[इति] is optionally @deva[प्रगृह्य].

**Both correct:** @deva[उ इति] or @deva[विति]
standard:
The particle @deva[उञ्] (= @deva[उ]) before @deva[इति] is optionally @deva[प्रगृह्य], per @deva[शाकल्य].

**Both valid:** @deva[उ इति] or @deva[विति]
advanced:
@deva[उञः] — @deva[उञ्] before @deva[इति] is optionally @deva[प्रगृह्य], per @deva[शाकल्य].

**Option:** @deva[उ इति] ~ @deva[विति]

simple:
When a word consists of a single letter, that letter counts as both the initial and the final. Rules that apply to initials or finals therefore apply to it.

**Example:** The affix @deva[अण्] is just @deva[अ]. By @ref[3.1.3], affixes have @deva[उदात्त] on their initial vowel. Since @deva[अ] is both initial and final, it receives the accent: @deva[औपगव॑म्].
standard:
In a single-letter item, that letter is simultaneously initial and final. Operations on either apply to it.

**Example 1:** @ref[3.1.3] gives @deva[उदात्त] to an affix's initial. The affix @deva[अण्] (= @deva[अ]) receives accent on that @deva[अ]: @deva[औपगव॑म्].

**Example 2:** @ref[7.3.102] lengthens final short @deva[अ] before @deva[यञ्]-class consonant-initial case affixes. Even if the whole word is @deva[अ]: @deva[अ] + @deva[भ्याम्] = @deva[आभ्याम्] 'by those two'.

**Why 'in one'?** In multi-letter words, initial ≠ final. @deva[सभा] has initial @deva[अ] ≠ final @deva[आ], so it doesn't qualify as @deva[वृद्ध] by @ref[1.1.73].
advanced:
@deva[आदिरन्त्यवदेकस्मिन्] — in a single-letter item, operations on initial or final apply to that letter.

**@deva[उदात्त] on affix initial (@ref[3.1.3]):** @deva[अण्] (= @deva[अ]) → @deva[औपगव॑म्].

**Lengthening of final @deva[अ] (@ref[7.3.102]):** @deva[अ] + @deva[भ्याम्] → @deva[आभ्याम्].

**Why @deva[एकस्मिन्]?** Prevents misapplication to multi-letter words. @deva[सभा] (initial @deva[अ], final @deva[आ]) is not @deva[वृद्ध] (@ref[1.1.73]), so @deva[सभासन्नयन] takes @deva[अण्] (→ @deva[साभासन्नयनः]), not @deva[छ्] (@ref[4.2.114]).

simple:
The directional words @deva[पूर्व], @deva[पर], @deva[अवर], @deva[दक्षिण], @deva[उत्तर], @deva[अपर], @deva[अधर] are optionally @deva[सर्वनाम] before @deva[जस्]—but only when indicating relative position, not when used as proper names.

**Example:** @deva[दक्षिणे] or @deva[दक्षिणाः] 'southern ones' (relational); but @deva[उत्तराः कुरवः] 'the Northern Kurus' (proper name)—regular only.
standard:
These seven directional words (@deva[सर्वादि] members) are optionally @deva[सर्वनाम] before @deva[जस्], but only when expressing relative position (@deva[अवस्था]).

**Option (relational):** @deva[पूर्वे] ~ @deva[पूर्वाः], @deva[दक्षिणे] ~ @deva[दक्षिणाः].

**No option (names):** @deva[उत्तराः कुरवः] 'the Northern Kurus'—not pronominal.

**No option (other senses):** @deva[दक्षिणाः] @deva[इमे गायकाः] 'these clever minstrels' (@deva[दक्षिण] = 'clever')—regular.

This is @deva[प्राप्तविभाषा]: option only in nom. pl.; other cases remain pronominal.
advanced:
@deva[पूर्वपरावरदक्षिणोत्तरापराधराणि व्यवस्थायामसंज्ञायाम्] — optionally @deva[सर्वनाम] before @deva[जस्] when expressing @deva[अवस्था] (relative position), not @deva[संज्ञा] (proper name).

**Option:** @deva[पूर्वे] ~ @deva[पूर्वाः], @deva[उत्तरे] ~ @deva[उत्तराः].

**@deva[संज्ञा]:** @deva[उत्तराः कुरवः]—no option.

**@deva[अवस्था]:** Implies specification of a reference point (@deva[अवधि]).

This is @deva[प्राप्तविभाषा].

simple:
The word @deva[वषट्] (actually pronounced @deva[वौषट्]) may optionally be spoken with a raised tone (@deva[उदात्त]) or in @deva[एकश्रुति] (monotone), even during sacrificial rites.

**Both valid:** @deva[सोमस्याग्ने वीही३ वौषट्] (monotone) or with raised accent
standard:
The word @deva[वषट्] (pronounced @deva[वौषट्]) may optionally take @deva[उदात्त] accent or remain @deva[एकश्रुति], even in @deva[यज्ञकर्म] where monotone normally applies.

The @deva[सूत्र] uses @deva[वषट्] though the actual pronunciation is @deva[वौषट्]—an instance of @[pāṇini]'s cryptic style (@deva[विचित्राः हि सूत्रस्य कृतिः पाणिनेः]).

**Both valid:** @deva[सोमस्याग्ने वीही३ वौषट्] (monotone) or @deva[वौ॑षट्] (accented)
advanced:
The word @deva[वषट्] (= @deva[वौषट्]) optionally takes raised accent in @deva[यज्ञकर्म], despite @ref[1.2.34].

**Why @deva[वषट्] not @deva[वौषट्]?** @deva[विचित्राः हि सूत्रस्य कृतिः पाणिनेः] — @[pāṇini]'s formulations are cryptic.

**Both valid:** @deva[सोमस्याग्ने वीही३ वौषट्] or @deva[वौ॑षट्]

simple:
A @deva[तत्पुरुष] compound (determinative compound) in which both members would be in the same case when analyzed is called @deva[कर्मधारय] (appositional determinative). The first member describes the second.

**Examples:**
- @deva[नील] + @deva[उत्पल] → @deva[नीलोत्पलम्] 'blue lotus' (both nominative when separate)
- @deva[परम] + @deva[आत्मा] → @deva[परमात्मा] 'supreme soul'
standard:
A @deva[तत्पुरुष] where both members share the same case (@deva[समानाधिकरण]) is @deva[कर्मधारय].

In other @deva[तत्पुरुष] compounds, the first member is governed by the last (e.g., genitive). In @deva[कर्मधारय], both members refer to the same entity in the same case.

**Examples:**
- @deva[नीलोत्पलम्] 'blue lotus'
- @deva[परमात्मा] 'supreme soul'

**Accentual effect — @ref[6.2.130]:** @deva[राज्यम्] has first-syllable @deva[उदात्त] in @deva[तत्पुरुष] except @deva[कर्मधारय]: @deva[परमराज्यम्] (no special accent), but @deva[ब्रा॒ह्म॒णरा॑ज्यम्] (non-@deva[कर्मधारय]).

**Contrast with @deva[बहुव्रीहि]:** @deva[पाचिका] + @deva[भार्या] → @deva[पाचिकाभार्याः] 'one whose wife is a cook'—@deva[बहुव्रीहि], not @deva[कर्मधारय], so @deva[पाचिका] keeps feminine form.
advanced:
@deva[तत्पुरुष] with @deva[समानाधिकरण] members = @deva[कर्मधारय].

**Examples:** @deva[नीलोत्पलम्], @deva[परमात्मा]

**Effect — @ref[6.2.130] @deva[अकर्मधारये राज्यम्]:** @deva[राज्यम्] has first-syllable @deva[उदात्त] in non-@deva[कर्मधारय] @deva[तत्पुरुष]:
- @deva[ब्रा॒ह्म॒णरा॑ज्यम्] — non-@deva[कर्मधारय] (genitive relation)
- @deva[परमराज्यम्] — @deva[कर्मधारय] (no special accent)

**Effect — @ref[6.3.42] @deva[पुंवत्कर्मधारयजातीयदेशीयेषु]:** Feminine adjective → masculine in @deva[कर्मधारय]:
- @deva[पाचिका] + @deva[वृन्दारिका] → @deva[पाचकवृन्दारिका]

**Not @deva[कर्मधारय]:** @deva[बहुव्रीहि] even if same case: @deva[पाचिकाभार्याः] 'whose wife cooks'.

simple:
A meaningful word that is neither a @deva[धातु] (verbal root) nor a @deva[प्रत्यय] (affix) is called @deva[प्रातिपदिक] (nominal stem). This is the base form that receives case endings (@deva[सुप्]).

**Examples:** @deva[डित्थ] 'wooden elephant', @deva[कपित्थ] 'wood-apple tree', @deva[कुण्ड] 'bowl', @deva[पीठ] 'seat'

**Why 'meaningful'?** In @deva[वनम्] 'forest', we can't call @deva[वन्] alone a @deva[प्रातिपदिक]—it has no independent meaning, and final @deva[न्] would be dropped by @ref[8.2.7] if it were.
standard:
A significant form—not a @deva[धातु], not a @deva[प्रत्यय]—is @deva[प्रातिपदिक] (nominal stem).

**Examples:** @deva[डित्थ], @deva[कपित्थ], @deva[कुण्ड], @deva[पीठ]

**Why 'meaningful' (@deva[अर्थवत्])?** @deva[वन्] in @deva[वनम्] isn't @deva[प्रातिपदिक]—no independent meaning. If it were, @ref[8.2.7] (@deva[नलोपः प्रातिपदिकान्तस्य]) would drop the @deva[न्].

**Why 'not a root'?** @deva[अहन्] 'he killed' (imperfect of @deva[हन्])—@deva[न्] isn't dropped because it's a verb, not @deva[प्रातिपदिक].

**Why 'not an affix'?** @deva[कुण्डे] 'in the bowl'—ends in locative @deva[इ], so it's a @deva[पद], not @deva[प्रातिपदिक]. If it were @deva[प्रातिपदिक], @ref[1.2.47] would shorten the @deva[ए].

@deva[निपात]s (particles) also count as @deva[प्रातिपदिक] per the @deva[महाभाष्य].

simple:
Adjectives qualifying @deva[लुप्]-derived words also agree in gender and number—unless the @deva[लुप्]-word is a @deva[जाति] (class noun) used as a common noun.

**Agreeing:** @deva[पञ्चालाः रमणीयाः बह्वन्नाः] 'the pleasant, food-rich @[pañcālas]'

**Not agreeing (class noun):** @deva[पञ्चालाः जनपदः] 'the @[pañcāla] country'—@deva[जनपद] is singular masculine, not plural.
advanced: Adjectives of @deva[लुप्]-derived words agree in gender/number, except for @deva[जाति] (class nouns). **Agreement:** @deva[पञ्चालाः रमणीयाः] "the pleasant @[pañcālas]" — adjective matches **@deva[जाति] words — no agreement:** @deva[पञ्चालाः जनपदः] "the @[pañcāla] country" — @deva[जनपद] is a class noun, stays singular **Indirect qualification — no agreement:** @deva[पञ्चालाः जनपदो रमणीयः] — @deva[रमणीय] qualifies @deva[जनपद], not @deva[पञ्चालाः]

simple:
In the Vedas, the asterism @deva[पुनर्वसू] (a pair of stars) may optionally take singular form.

**Vedic options:**
- @deva[पुनर्वसुर्नक्षत्रम्] (singular)
- @deva[पुनर्वसू नक्षत्रम्] (dual)

**In classical Sanskrit:** Only dual @deva[पुनर्वसू].

**Not as asterism:** @deva[पुनर्वसू माणवकौ] 'two boys named Punarvasu'—dual only.
standard:
In Vedic, the dual @deva[पुनर्वसू] (asterism) optionally takes singular with dual meaning.

**Vedic:**
- @deva[पुनर्वसुर्नक्षत्रम्] (singular) or @deva[पुनर्वसू नक्षत्रमदितिर्देवता] (dual)

**Classical Sanskrit:** @deva[पुनर्वसू] (dual only).

**Not as asterism:** @deva[पुनर्वसू माणवकौ] 'two boys named Punarvasu'—no option.
advanced:
In Vedic, dual @deva[पुनर्वसू] optionally becomes singular: @deva[पुनर्वसुर्नक्षत्रम्] or @deva[पुनर्वसू नक्षत्रम्].

**Classical:** @deva[पुनर्वसू] only.

**Non-asterism:** @deva[पुनर्वसू माणवकौ]—dual only.

simple:
In the Vedas, the asterism @deva[विशाखा] (normally dual) may optionally take singular form.

**Vedic options:**
- @deva[विशाखा नक्षत्रम्] (singular)
- @deva[विशाखे नक्षत्रम्] (dual)

The presiding deities are @deva[इन्द्राग्नी].
standard:
In Vedic, the dual @deva[विशाखा] (asterism) optionally takes singular with dual meaning.

**Vedic:**
- @deva[विशाखा नक्षत्रम्] (singular)
- @deva[विशाखे नक्षत्रमिन्द्राग्नी देवता] (dual) 'the @[viśākhā] asterism, with Indra and Agni as deities'
advanced: In Vedic, dual @deva[विशाखा] optionally becomes singular: @deva[विशाखा नक्षत्रम्] or @deva[विशाखे नक्षत्रमिन्द्राग्नी देवता].

simple:
When @deva[तिष्य] (one star) and @deva[पुनर्वसू] (two stars) form a @deva[द्वन्द्व] compound, the result is always dual—not plural.

**Example:**
- @deva[उदितौ तिष्यपुनर्वसू दृश्येते] '@[tiṣya] and Punarvasu, having risen, are seen' (dual)

Logically there are three stars, so @deva[बहुवचन] (plural) would be expected. This rule overrides that.

**Synonyms included:** @deva[पुष्यपुनर्वसू], @deva[सिद्धपुनर्वसू].

**Not as star names:** @deva[तिष्यपुनर्वसवो माणवकाः] 'boys named @[tiṣya] and Punarvasu'—plural.
standard:
In @deva[द्वन्द्व] compounds of @deva[तिष्य] and @deva[पुनर्वसू] (asterisms), dual replaces the expected plural. Since @deva[तिष्य] is one star and @deva[पुनर्वसू] is two, the compound logically refers to three stars—but takes dual.

**Example:**
- @deva[उदितौ तिष्यपुनर्वसू दृश्येते] (dual)

**Synonyms:** @deva[पुष्यपुनर्वसू], @deva[सिद्धपुनर्वसू]—the word @deva[नक्षत्र] is repeated in the @[sūtra] to include these.

**Not as asterisms:** @deva[तिष्यपुनर्वसवो माणवकाः] 'boys named @[tiṣya] and @[punarvasū]'—plural.

**Not @[bahuvrīhi]:** @deva[तिष्यपुनर्वसवः] 'those who confuse @[tiṣya] with Punarvasu'—plural.

**Not singular dvandva:** @deva[तिष्यपुनर्वसू इदम्]—singular allowed.

The word @deva[नित्यम्] 'always' stops the @deva[विकल्प] (option) from @ref[1.2.58] extending here.
advanced:
In @deva[द्वन्द्व] of @deva[तिष्य] and @deva[पुनर्वसू], dual replaces plural. @deva[उदितौ तिष्यपुनर्वसू] (three stars → dual).

**Synonyms:** @deva[पुष्यपुनर्वसू], @deva[सिद्धपुनर्वसू].

**Other compounds:** @deva[विशाखानुराधाः]—plural. @deva[तिष्यपुनर्वसवो माणवकाः]—plural. @deva[तिष्यपुनर्वसवः] (@[bahuvrīhi])—plural.

@deva[नित्यम्] blocks @deva[विकल्प] from @ref[1.2.58]. The @[sūtra] implies every @deva[द्वन्द्व] optionally takes singular (@deva[ज्ञापक]).

simple:
When two or more words have identical form and the same @deva[विभक्ति] (case ending), only one is retained—this is @deva[एकशेष] (retention of one). The retained word takes the appropriate @deva[वचन] (number) for all.

**Examples:**
- @deva[वृक्षः] + @deva[वृक्षः] → @deva[वृक्षौ] 'two trees'
- @deva[वृक्षः] + @deva[वृक्षः] + @deva[वृक्षः] → @deva[वृक्षाः] 'trees'

**Different forms—no @[ekśeṣa]:** @deva[प्लक्षन्यग्रोधाः] 'the @[plakṣa] and nyagrodha trees'
standard:
When words of the same form and same @deva[विभक्ति] stand together, one is retained (@deva[एकशेष]) and the rest dropped. The retained word takes appropriate @deva[वचन].

**Examples:**
- @deva[वृक्षः] + @deva[वृक्षः] = @deva[वृक्षौ] (dual)
- @deva[वृक्षः] × 3 = @deva[वृक्षाः] (plural)

**Same form, different meaning:** @deva[अक्षः] (die) + @deva[अक्षः] (eye) + @deva[अक्षः] (axle) = @deva[अक्षाः]—still applies.

**Different forms:** @deva[प्लक्षन्यग्रोधाः]—no @[ekśeṣa].

**Different cases:** @deva[पयः] (nom.) and @deva[पयः] (acc.) standing together—no @[ekśeṣa].

The word @deva[शेष] indicates retention (not substitution).
advanced:
Identical words in the same @deva[विभक्ति] undergo @deva[एकशेष]—only the last is retained.

- @deva[वृक्षः] + @deva[वृक्षः] → @deva[वृक्षौ]
- @deva[अक्षः] (die) + @deva[अक्षः] (eye) + @deva[अक्षः] (axle) → @deva[अक्षाः]

**No @[ekśeṣa]:** @deva[प्लक्षन्यग्रोधाः] (different forms); @deva[पयः] (nom.) + @deva[पयः] (acc.) (different cases).

@deva[शेष] = retention, not @deva[आदेश] (substitution).

simple:
When a feminine @deva[गोत्र] word and a masculine @deva[युवन्] word stand together, the feminine is retained—but treated as masculine.

**Example:**
- @deva[गार्गी] (feminine gotra) + @deva[गार्ग्यायणः] (masculine yuvan) → @deva[गार्ग्यौ] (masculine dual)
- @deva[दाक्षी] + @deva[दाक्षायणः] → @deva[दाक्षौ]

The result is always masculine regardless of the feminine original.

When a masculine and feminine noun share the same base and differ only in gender suffix, the masculine is kept and the pair is expressed as a dual. For example, @deva[ब्राह्मणः] "@[brāhmaṇa]" + @deva[ब्राह्मणी] "female @[brāhmaṇa]" → @deva[ब्राह्मणौ] "a @[brāhmaṇa] couple." If the bases differ, or if the feminine suffix carries extra meaning, this rule does not apply.
standard: This @[ekaśeṣa] rule retains the masculine (@deva[पुंवद्भाव] outcome) when a masculine and feminine noun share the same @deva[प्रातिपदिक] and differ only in the @deva[स्त्रीप्रत्यय]. Conditioning environment: identical base, gender suffix the only difference. Examples: @deva[ब्राह्मणः] + @deva[ब्राह्मणी] → @deva[ब्राह्मणौ] "@[brāhmaṇa] couple"; @deva[कुक्कुटः] + @deva[कुक्कुटी] → @deva[कुक्कुटौ] "cock and hen." If bases differ (@deva[कुक्कुटः] + @deva[मयूरी]), the dual @deva[कुक्कुटमयूर्यौ] is formed instead. Similarly, if the feminine suffix conveys a distinct meaning — e.g., @deva[गणकी] meaning "wife of a @deva[गणक]" rather than a female @deva[गणक] — @[ekaśeṣa] is blocked and @deva[गणकगणक्यौ] results.
advanced: When masculine and feminine words differing only in gender-affix stand together, the masculine is retained. **Examples:** @deva[ब्राह्मणः] + @deva[ब्राह्मणी] → @deva[ब्राह्मणौ] "a @[brāhmaṇa] couple" @deva[कुक्कुटः] + @deva[कुक्कुटी] → @deva[कुक्कुटौ] "cock and hen" **Different bases — no @[ekaseṣa]:** @deva[कुक्कुटः] + @deva[मयूरी] → @deva[कुक्कुटमयूर्यौ] **Feminine affix with extra meaning — no @[ekaseṣa]:** @deva[गणकः] + @deva[गणकी] → @deva[गणकगणक्यौ] (@deva[गणकी] = "wife of," not female @deva[गणक])

simple: When "brother" (@deva[भ्रातृ]) is paired with "sister" (@deva[स्वसृ]), the masculine @deva[भ्रातृ] is retained, giving @deva[भ्रातरौ] "brother and sister." Likewise, "son" (@deva[पुत्र]) is retained over "daughter" (@deva[दुहितृ]), giving @deva[पुत्रौ] "son and daughter." This is a specific exception that names these pairs explicitly rather than leaving them to the general gender rule.
standard: This @[sūtra] is a named exception listing @deva[भ्रातृ–स्वसृ] and @deva[पुत्र–दुहितृ] as pairs where the masculine @deva[प्रातिपदिक] is retained via @[ekaśeṣa]. The conditioning environment: exactly these lexical pairs. @deva[भ्रातृ] + @deva[स्वसृ] → @deva[भ्रातरौ]; @deva[पुत्र] + @deva[दुहितृ] → @deva[पुत्रौ]. The legal importance is noted in the @[yājñavalkya] @[smṛti]: @deva[भ्रातरः] in inheritance contexts denotes brothers and sisters together. Without this specific rule the unlike bases would prevent @[ekaśeṣa] under the general rule.
advanced: @deva[भ्रातृ] and @deva[पुत्र] are retained when paired with @deva[स्वसृ] and @deva[दुहितृ]. **Examples:** @deva[भ्रातृ] + @deva[स्वसृ] → @deva[भ्रातरौ] "brother and sister" @deva[पुत्र] + @deva[दुहितृ] → @deva[पुत्रौ] "son and daughter" **Legal significance:** @[yājñavalkya] @[smṛti]: @deva[भ्रातरः] in inheritance rules means "brothers and sisters."

simple: When "father" (@deva[पितृ]) and "mother" (@deva[मातृ]) appear together, @deva[पितृ] may optionally be retained, giving @deva[पितरौ] "parents." The compound form @deva[मातापितरौ] is equally correct. The @[yājñavalkya] @[smṛti] uses @deva[पितरौ] specifically to mean "father and mother."
standard: An optional @[ekaśeṣa] (@deva[विकल्प]) is prescribed for the specific pair @deva[पितृ–मातृ]: the masculine @deva[पितृ] is retained, yielding the dual @deva[पितरौ]. The alternative is the @deva[द्वन्द्व] compound @deva[मातापितरौ], which is always available. Conditioning: exactly this lexical pair; the option does not extend to other kinship pairs covered by the general rule. Legal usage: the @[yājñavalkya] @[smṛti] employs @deva[पितरौ] in the sense of "both parents." Compare the mandatory retention for @deva[भ्रातृ–स्वसृ] at @ref[1.2.68].
advanced: When @deva[पितृ] and @deva[मातृ] stand together, @deva[पितृ] is optionally retained. **Both valid:** @deva[पिता] + @deva[माता] → @deva[पितरौ] or @deva[मातापितरौ] "parents" @[yājñavalkya] @[smṛti] uses @deva[पितरौ] for "father and mother."

simple: When "father-in-law" (@deva[श्वशुर]) and "mother-in-law" (@deva[श्वश्रू]) appear together, the masculine @deva[श्वशुर] may optionally be retained. This gives @deva[श्वशुरौ] "parents-in-law," alongside the alternative dual @deva[श्वश्रश्वशुरौ]. The rule is optional, so both outputs are grammatically correct.
standard: A @deva[विकल्प] (optional) @[ekaśeṣa] applies to the pair @deva[श्वशुर–श्वश्रू], retaining the masculine @deva[श्वशुर]. Conditioning environment: this specific kinship pair. When the option is taken: @deva[श्वशुरः] + @deva[श्वश्रूः] → @deva[श्वशुरौ]. When not taken: the @deva[द्वन्द्व] @deva[श्वश्रश्वशुरौ] is used. This parallels the optional retention for @deva[पितृ–मातृ] at @ref[1.2.70] and the mandatory retention for @deva[भ्रातृ–स्वसृ] at @ref[1.2.68].
advanced: @deva[श्वशुर] and @deva[श्वश्रू] optionally undergo @[ekaseṣa] to @deva[श्वशुर]. **Both valid:** @deva[श्वशुरः] + @deva[श्वश्रूः] → @deva[श्वशुरौ] or @deva[श्वश्रश्वशुरौ] "parents-in-law"

simple: When a @deva[त्यदादि] pronoun (the list beginning with @deva[त्यद्]) occurs alongside another word, the pronoun is always retained via @[ekaśeṣa]. For example, @deva[सः] + @deva[देवदत्तः] → @deva[तौ] "those two." When two @deva[त्यदादि] pronouns meet, the one that appears later in the @deva[त्यदादि] list wins.
standard: The @deva[त्यदादि] list (@ref[1.1.27]) — @deva[त्यद्], @deva[तद्], @deva[यद्], @deva[एतद्], @deva[इदम्], @deva[अदस्], @deva[एक], @deva[द्वि], @deva[युष्मद्], @deva[अस्मद्], @deva[भवत्], @deva[किम्] — always prevails in @[ekaśeṣa] over non-@deva[त्यदादि] words. Conditioning: one member must be from this class; the @deva[त्यदादि] member is invariably retained. Example: @deva[सः] + @deva[देवदत्तः] → @deva[तौ]. When two @deva[त्यदादि] pronouns co-occur, the later member in the list is retained: @deva[यः] + @deva[सः] → @deva[यौ] (@deva[यद्] follows @deva[तद्]); @deva[यः] + @deva[कः] → @deva[कौ] (@deva[किम्] follows @deva[यद्]). This is a non-optional (@deva[नित्य]) rule, unlike the optional rules at @ref[1.2.70] and @ref[1.2.71].
advanced: When @deva[त्यदादि] pronouns stand with other words, the pronoun is always retained. The @deva[त्यदादि] list (@ref[1.1.27]): @deva[त्यद्], @deva[तद्], @deva[यद्], @deva[एतद्], @deva[इदम्], @deva[अदस्], @deva[एक], @deva[द्वि], @deva[युष्मद्], @deva[अस्मद्], @deva[भवत्], @deva[किम्]. **Example:** @deva[सः] + @deva[देवदत्तः] → @deva[तौ] "those two" **Among themselves — later in list prevails:** @deva[यः] + @deva[सः] → @deva[यौ] (@deva[यद्] comes after @deva[तद्]) @deva[यः] + @deva[कः] → @deva[कौ] (@deva[किम्] comes after @deva[यद्])

simple:
Words that denote action, beginning with @deva[भू] 'to become,' are called @deva[धातु] (verbal root). The @deva[धातुपाठ] lists about two thousand roots organized into ten classes (@deva[गण]):

1. @deva[भ्वादि] 2. @deva[अदादि] 3. @deva[हुआदि] 4. @deva[दिवादि] 5. @deva[स्वादि] 6. @deva[तुदादि] 7. @deva[रुधादि] 8. @deva[तनादि] 9. @deva[क्र्यादि] 10. @deva[चुरादि]

Some roots take @deva[परस्मैपद] endings, some take @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings, and some take both. This chapter mostly determines which type each root takes.
standard:
Words denoting action, beginning with @deva[भू], are called @deva[धातु]. The term was coined by grammarians before @[pāṇini].

The @deva[धातुपाठ] lists ~2000 roots in ten @deva[गण]s (classes), each with characteristic modifications before certain endings. Some roots are @deva[परस्मैपदिन्], some @deva[आत्मनेपदिन्], some @deva[उभयपदिन्]. This @deva[अध्याय] largely determines those assignments.

The @deva[वा] in the @[sūtra] is for auspiciousness—the regular @deva[सन्धि] of @deva[भू] + @deva[आदि] would be @deva[भ्वादि], not @deva[भूवादि].

simple:
A nasalized vowel (@deva[अनुनासिक]) in a grammatical teaching (@deva[उपदेश]) is @deva[इत्]—an indicatory marker. It gets deleted and doesn't appear in the final form.

**What is @deva[उपदेश]?** The original statement of a @deva[धातु], @deva[प्रत्यय], or @deva[आगम] as listed in grammar texts—not a finished word ready for use.

**Examples:**
- @deva[क्विँप्]: the nasal @deva[इ] is @deva[इत्]
- @deva[एध] 'to increase': the final @deva[अ] is @deva[इत्]; the real root is @deva[एध्]
standard:
In @deva[उपदेश] (original enunciation of a @deva[धातु], @deva[प्रत्यय], or @deva[आगम]), a nasalized vowel is @deva[इत्].

@[sūtras] @ref[1.3.2]–@ref[1.3.9] define @deva[इत्] letters.

**Examples:**
- @deva[क्विँन्], @deva[विँद्], @deva[विँच्]: the nasal @deva[इ] is @deva[इत्]
- @deva[एधँ]: the nasal @deva[अ] is @deva[इत्]; the real root is @deva[एध्]

**Not in non-@deva[उपदेश]:** @deva[अभ्रँ आँ अपः]—the nasality is not @deva[इत्] because these are finished words.

**Only vowels:** In @deva[मनिन्] (@ref[3.2.74]), the nasal @deva[न्] is not @deva[इत्] by this rule—only nasal vowels qualify.
advanced:
In @deva[उपदेश], @deva[अनुनासिक] vowels are @deva[इत्]. @[sūtras] @ref[1.3.2]–@ref[1.3.9] define @deva[इत्].

- @deva[क्विँन्], @deva[विँद्], @deva[विँच्]: nasal @deva[इ] is @deva[इत्]
- @deva[एधँ]: nasal @deva[अ] is @deva[इत्]; real root = @deva[एध्]

**Not @deva[उपदेश]:** @deva[अभ्रँ आँ अपः]—not @deva[इत्].

**Only vowels:** @deva[मनिन्] (@ref[3.2.74])—nasal @deva[न्] not @deva[इत्] by this rule.

simple:
The initial @deva[ष्] of an affix is @deva[इत्]—a marker that gets deleted.

**Example:**
- @deva[ष्वुन्] (@ref[3.1.145]): @deva[ष्] and @deva[न्] are @deva[इत्]; @deva[वु] → @deva[अक] by @ref[7.1.1]
- @deva[नृत्] + @deva[ष्वुन्] → @deva[नर्तकः] 'dancer'

**Why @deva[ष्] matters:** Words formed by @deva[षित्] affixes take @deva[ङीष्] in the feminine (@ref[4.1.41]): @deva[नर्तकी], @deva[रजकी].

**Only in affixes:** @deva[षोडश्] 'sixteen' retains @deva[ष्]—it's not an affix.
standard:
Initial @deva[ष्] of a @deva[प्रत्यय] is @deva[इत्].

**Example:**
- @deva[ष्वुन्] (@ref[3.1.145]): @deva[ष्] is @deva[इत्], @deva[वु] → @deva[अक] (@ref[7.1.1])
- @deva[नृत्] + @deva[ष्वुन्] → @deva[नर्तकः] 'professional dancer'

**@deva[षित्] triggers @deva[ङीष्]:** @ref[4.1.41] @deva[षिद्गौरादिभ्यश्च]—feminine @deva[नर्तकी], @deva[रजकी].

**Only affixes:** @deva[षोडश्], @deva[षडिक]—not affixes, so @deva[ष्] retained.

**Must be initial:** @deva[टिषच्] (@deva[अविष], @deva[महिष])—@deva[ष्] not initial, not @deva[इत्].
advanced:
Initial @deva[ष्] of @deva[प्रत्यय] is @deva[इत्].

- @deva[ष्वुन्] (@ref[3.1.145]): @deva[नृत्] + @deva[ष्वुन्] → @deva[नर्तकः]; fem. @deva[नर्तकी] (@ref[4.1.41])

**Only affixes:** @deva[षोडश्], @deva[षडिक]—@deva[ष्] retained.

**Must be initial:** @deva[टिषच्] (@[uṇ]. I.45)—@deva[ष्] not initial: @deva[अविषः], @deva[महिषः].

simple:
In this grammar, a word with @deva[स्वरित] (circumflex) accent marks an @deva[अधिकार]—a governing rule that applies to subsequent @[sūtras].

**Major @deva[अधिकार] @[sūtras]:**
- @deva[प्रत्ययः] (@ref[3.1.1]) — 'affix' understood in all following rules
- @deva[धातोः] (@ref[3.1.91]) — 'after a root'
- @deva[अङ्गस्य] (@ref[6.4.1]) — 'of the stem'
- @deva[भस्य] (@ref[6.4.129]) — 'of the base'

@[sūtras] are normally unmarked; the accents are learned from commentaries.
standard:
A @deva[स्वरित] (circumflex) accent in this @deva[सूत्रपाठ] indicates @deva[अधिकार]—a governing rule whose terms continue into subsequent @[sūtras].

**Key @deva[अधिकार] @[sūtras]:**
- @deva[प्रत्ययः] (@ref[3.1.1])
- @deva[धातोः] (@ref[3.1.91])
- @deva[अङ्गस्य] (@ref[6.4.1])
- @deva[भस्य] (@ref[6.4.129])

@[sūtras] are not marked with accents in the text; accent information comes from commentaries. An @deva[अधिकार] may begin a topic (terms continue forward) or end one (separating what follows).
advanced:
@deva[स्वरित] accent indicates @deva[अधिकार].

**Major governing @[sūtras]:**
- @deva[प्रत्ययः] (@ref[3.1.1])
- @deva[धातोः] (@ref[3.1.91])
- @deva[अङ्गस्य] (@ref[6.4.1])
- @deva[भस्य] (@ref[6.4.129])

Accent information is preserved in commentaries, not in the @[sūtrapāṭha] itself.

simple:
Even in active voice (@deva[कर्तरि]), when the action is reciprocal (@deva[व्यतिहार]—one person doing another's duty), @deva[आत्मनेपद] is used.

**Examples:**
- @deva[व्यतिपचन्ते] 'they cook for each other'
- @deva[व्यतिलुनते] 'he cuts (doing another's work)'

**Without reciprocity—@deva[परस्मैपद]:**
- @deva[लुनन्ति] 'they cut'

The term @deva[व्यतिहार] continues into the next two @[sūtras].
standard:
When expressing @deva[व्यतिहार] (reciprocity—one person performing another's appropriate action), @deva[आत्मनेपद] is used even in @deva[कर्तरि].

**Examples:**
- @deva[व्यतिपचन्ते] 'they cook for each other'
- @deva[व्यतिलुनते] 'he performs cutting that was another's duty'

**No reciprocity:** @deva[लुनन्ति] 'they cut' (parasmaipada).

@deva[व्यतिहार] and @deva[कर्तरि] continue into @ref[1.3.15] and @ref[1.3.16]. The word @deva[कर्मन्] here means 'action,' not the technical @deva[कर्मन्] (object).

simple:
The verb @deva[जि] 'to conquer' takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] when preceded by @deva[वि] or @deva[परा].

**Examples:**
- @deva[विजयते] 'he conquers'
- @deva[पराजयते] 'he defeats'

**Must be @deva[उपसर्ग]:** When @deva[वि] or @deva[परा] are used as nouns, parasmaipada applies:
- @deva[बहुविजयति वनम्] (@deva[बहुवि] is a noun)
- @deva[पराजयति सेना] (@deva[परा] is a noun)
standard:
After @deva[वि]/@deva[परा] + @deva[जि] 'to conquer,' @deva[आत्मनेपद] is used.

**Examples:**
- @deva[विजयते] 'he conquers'
- @deva[पराजयते] 'he defeats'

**Must be @deva[उपसर्ग]:**
- @deva[बहुविजयति वनम्]—@deva[बहुवि] is noun, so parasmaipada
- @deva[पराजयति सेना]—@deva[परा] is noun, so parasmaipada
advanced:
After @deva[वि]/@deva[परा] + @deva[जि], @deva[आत्मनेपद]. @deva[विजयते], @deva[पराजयते].

**Must be @deva[उपसर्ग]:** @deva[बहुविजयति वनम्] (@deva[वि] part of noun). @deva[पराजयति सेना] (@deva[परा] noun).

simple:
The verb @deva[क्रीड्] 'to play' takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] when preceded by @deva[अनु], @deva[सम्], @deva[परि], or @deva[आ]. The @deva[च] in the @[sūtra] includes @deva[आङ्].

**Examples:**
- @deva[आक्रीडते] 'he plays'
- @deva[संक्रीडते], @deva[अनुक्रीडते], @deva[परिक्रीडते]

**Exception:** When @deva[सम्] + @deva[क्रीड्] means 'to make a creaking noise': @deva[संक्रीडन्ति शकटानि] 'the carts creak.'

The verb @deva[स्था] 'to stand' takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] when preceded by @deva[सम्], @deva[अव], @deva[प्र], or @deva[वि].

**Examples:**
- @deva[संतिष्ठते] 'he stays with'
- @deva[अवतिष्ठते] 'he waits patiently'
- @deva[प्रतिष्ठते] 'he sets forth'
- @deva[वितिष्ठते] 'he stands apart, perseveres'
standard:
After @deva[सम्]/@deva[अव]/@deva[प्र]/@deva[वि] + @deva[स्था] 'to stand,' @deva[आत्मनेपद].

**Examples:**
- @deva[संतिष्ठते] 'he stays with'
- @deva[अवतिष्ठते] 'he waits patiently'
- @deva[प्रतिष्ठते] 'he sets forth'
- @deva[वितिष्ठते] 'he perseveres'

@note[type=@[vārtika]]{@deva[आ] + @deva[स्था] meaning 'solemn declaration' is also @[ātmanepada]: @deva[जलं विषं वा तव कारणादास्थास्ये] 'for your sake I shall surely resort to water or poison.'}

simple:
The verb @deva[स्था] (without any prefix) takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] when meaning:

1. **@deva[प्रकाशन]** (disclosing one's intentions to another)
2. **@deva[स्थेयाख्य]** (acting as arbitrator, making an award)

**Examples:**
- @deva[तिष्ठते जायापत्ये] 'the wife expresses her wish to the husband'
- @deva[त्वयि तिष्ठते] 'he refers to you for settlement'
- @deva[संशय्य कर्णादिषु तिष्ठते यः] 'who, when in doubt, has recourse to @[karṇa] as judge'
advanced:
@deva[स्था] meaning @deva[प्रकाशन] (disclosure) or @deva[स्थेयाख्य] (arbitration) → @deva[आत्मनेपद].

- @deva[तिष्ठते जायापत्ये]
- @deva[त्वयि तिष्ठते]
- @deva[संशय्य कर्णादिषु तिष्ठते यः]

simple:
The verb @deva[स्था] preceded by @deva[उद्] takes @deva[आत्मनेपद]—EXCEPT when meaning 'to rise from a seat.'

**@deva[आत्मनेपद] (effort/striving):**
- @deva[गेहे उत्तिष्ठते] 'he strives for the house'
- @deva[कुटुम्बे उत्तिष्ठते] 'he exerts himself for the family'

**@deva[परस्मैपद] (rising from seat):**
- @deva[आसनादुत्तिष्ठति] 'he rises from the seat'

The word @deva[कर्मन्] here means 'action/activity,' not the grammatical @deva[कर्मन्] (object).

The verb @deva[स्था] preceded by @deva[उप] takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] when meaning 'to worship with hymns.'

**@deva[आत्मनेपद] (worship):**
- @deva[ऐन्द्र्या गार्हपत्यमुपतिष्ठते] 'he worships the @[gārhapatya] fire with the @[aindrī] hymn'

**@deva[परस्मैपद] (other meanings):**
- @deva[भर्तारमुपतिष्ठति यौवनेन] 'she approaches the husband through youth'

The verb @deva[स्था] preceded by @deva[उप] takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] when used intransitively (meaning 'to be present at,' especially at mealtimes).

**@deva[आत्मनेपद] (intransitive—attending):**
- @deva[भोजनकाले उपतिष्ठते] 'he stands ready at mealtime'
- @deva[यावदभुक्तमुपतिष्ठते] 'he is present whenever it is time to eat'
- @deva[यावदोदनमुपतिष्ठते] 'he is present whenever there is food'

**@deva[परस्मैपद] (transitive):**
- @deva[राजानमुपतिष्ठते] 'he approaches the king'

The word @deva[अकर्मक] governs the next three @[sūtras] (@ref[1.3.27]-@ref[1.3.29]) as well.
standard:
After @deva[उप] + @deva[स्था] used intransitively ('to attend'), @deva[आत्मनेपद].

**Intransitive:**
- @deva[भोजनकाले उपतिष्ठते] 'he stands ready at mealtime'
- @deva[यावदभुक्तमुपतिष्ठते] 'he is present at eating time'

**Transitive → parasmaipada:**
- @deva[राजानमुपतिष्ठति] 'he approaches the king'

@deva[अकर्मक] continues through @ref[1.3.29].

simple:
The roots @deva[यम्] 'to stop' and @deva[हन्] 'to strike' take @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings when preceded by @deva[आ] and used intransitively.

**Examples:**
- @deva[आयच्छते] 'it spreads'
- @deva[आहते] 'he strikes'

When transitive: @deva[आयच्छति कूपाद् रज्जुम्] 'he draws up the rope from the well.'
advanced:
After @deva[आङ्] + @deva[यम्/हन्] used intransitively, @deva[आत्मनेपद] is used.

@note[type=@[vārtika]]{When the object is the agent's own limb, @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies even transitively. But with another's limb: @deva[आहन्ति शिरः परकीयम्] (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).}

simple:
The root @deva[ह्वे] 'to call' takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings when preceded by @deva[नि], @deva[सम्], @deva[उप], or @deva[वि]—even when the fruit of the action does not accrue to the agent.

**Examples:**
- @deva[निह्वयते] 'he calls down'
- @deva[संह्वयते] 'he calls together'
- @deva[उपह्वयते] 'he invites'
- @deva[विह्वयते] 'he calls apart'
advanced:
After @deva[नि/सम्/उप/वि] + @deva[ह्वे], @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies regardless of whether the fruit accrues to the agent. From this @[sūtra] forward, general rules of @deva[आत्मनेपद] are treated (the intransitive condition of @ref[1.3.26] no longer applies).

@note[type=@[vārtika]]{@deva[अस्] and @deva[ऊह्] optionally take @deva[आत्मनेपद] with @deva[उपसर्ग]s.}

simple:
The root @deva[ह्वे] 'to call' takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings when preceded by @deva[आ] and meaning 'to challenge.'

**Examples:**
- @deva[मल्लो मल्लम् आह्वयते] 'a wrestler challenges a wrestler'
- @deva[छात्रश्छात्रम् आह्वयते] 'a student emulates another student'

When not meaning challenge: @deva[गाम् आह्वयति गोपालः] 'the cowherd calls the cows' (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).
standard:
After @deva[आङ्] + @deva[ह्वे] meaning 'to challenge' (@deva[स्पर्धा]—to emulate, vie, or desire to conquer), @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies even when the fruit does not accrue to the agent.

**Examples:** @deva[मल्लो मल्लम् आह्वयते], @deva[योद्धा योद्धारम् आह्वयते].

Without this meaning → @deva[परस्मैपद]: @deva[गाम् आह्वयति गोपालः].
advanced: After @deva[आङ्] + @deva[ह्वे] meaning @deva[स्पर्धा] (challenge, emulation), @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies regardless of whether the fruit accrues to the agent. Without this meaning → @deva[परस्मैपद].

simple:
The root @deva[कृ] takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings when preceded by @deva[वि] and the object is sound.

**Examples:**
- @deva[क्रोष्टा विकुरुते स्वरान्] 'the bird makes sounds'
- @deva[ध्वाङ्क्षो विकुरुते स्वरान्] 'the crow makes sounds'

With other meanings: @deva[चित्तं विकरोति कामः] 'love affects the mind' (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).
advanced: After @deva[वि] + @deva[कृ] when the object is @deva[शब्द], @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies. @deva[कर्मन्] = @deva[कारक] (objective case), not 'action' as in @ref[1.3.24]. @deva[वि] is @deva[अनुवृत्ति] in the next @[sūtra].

simple:
The root @deva[कृ] takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings when preceded by @deva[वि] and used intransitively.

**Examples:**
- @deva[विकुर्वन्ते सैन्धवाः] 'the horses move gracefully'
- @deva[ओदनस्य पूर्णाः छात्राः विकुर्वन्ते] 'the students, full of rice, wander aimlessly'
standard:
After @deva[वि] + @deva[कृ] used intransitively, @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies.

**Examples:**
- @deva[विकुर्वन्ते सैन्धवाः] 'the horses move gracefully'
- @deva[ओदनस्य पूर्णाः छात्राः विकुर्वन्ते] 'the students, full of rice, act as they will'
advanced: After @deva[वि] + @deva[कृ] used intransitively (@deva[अकर्मक]), @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies.

simple:
The root @deva[नी] 'to lead' takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings when used in seven special senses—even when the fruit does not accrue to the agent:

1. @deva[सम्मानन] (guide worthily)
2. @deva[उत्सञ्जन] (lift up)
3. @deva[आचार्यकरण] (make one a teacher)
4. @deva[ज्ञान] (determine truth)
5. @deva[भृति] (employ on wages)
6. @deva[विगणन] (pay debt)
7. @deva[व्यय] (give in charity)

**Examples:**
- @deva[नयते चर्वी लोकायते] 'the teacher instructs in @[lokāyata]'
- @deva[माणवकम् उदानयते] 'he lifts up the boy'
- @deva[शतं विनयते] 'he expends a hundred on merit'
standard:
After @deva[नी] meaning @deva[सम्मानन/उत्सञ्जन/आचार्यकरण/ज्ञान/भृति/विगणन/व्यय], @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies even when the fruit does not accrue to the agent.

**Examples:**
- @deva[नयते चर्वी लोकायते] 'the teacher expounds @[lokāyata]'
- @deva[माणवकम् उपनयते] 'he initiates the boy' (makes himself the @deva[आचार्य])
- @deva[कर्मकारान् उपनयते] 'he employs servants on wages'
- @deva[मद्राः करं विनयन्ते] 'the Madras pay their tax'
- @deva[शतं विनयते] 'he devotes a hundred to religious acts'

Without these senses → @deva[परस्मैपद]: @deva[अजां नयति ग्रामम्].
advanced:
After @deva[नी] with these seven senses, @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies regardless of whether the fruit accrues to the agent.

@deva[आचार्यकरण] = bringing a pupil near oneself such that he becomes an @deva[आचार्य]: @deva[माणवकम् उपनयते].

simple:
The root @deva[नी] takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings when governing an incorporeal object that resides in the agent.

**Examples:**
- @deva[क्रोधं विनयते] 'he subdues his anger'
- @deva[मन्युं विनयते] 'he suppresses his wrath'

When the object is corporeal: @deva[रथं नयति] 'he drives the chariot' (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).
standard:
After @deva[नी] governing an @deva[अशरीर] (incorporeal) object residing in the agent (@deva[कर्तृस्थ]), @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies.

**Examples:** @deva[क्रोधं विनयते], @deva[मन्युं विनयते].

@deva[शरीर] = body of living beings. The object must be abstract (no concrete existence) and reside in the agent.

If the object resides in another: @deva[देवदत्तो यज्ञदत्तस्य क्रोधं विनयति] (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).

If the object is corporeal: @deva[गडुं विनयति] 'he removes his wart' (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).
advanced: After @deva[नी] governing an @deva[अशरीर] @deva[कर्तृस्थ] object, @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies. With instrumental (not accusative): @deva[बुद्ध्या विनयति] 'he is humble through wisdom' (→ @deva[परस्मैपद], since @deva[बुद्धि] is not the @deva[कर्मन्]).

simple:
The root @deva[क्रम्] 'to step' takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings when used in three special senses:

1. @deva[वृत्ति] (continuity, facility)
2. @deva[सर्ग] (energy, determination)
3. @deva[उपचय] (development, growth)

**Examples:**
- @deva[ऋक्ष्वस्य क्रमते बुद्धिः] 'his intellect proceeds smoothly through the @[ṛgveda]'
- @deva[व्याकरणाध्ययनाय क्रमते] 'the student exerts to study grammar'
- @deva[अस्मिन् शास्त्राणि क्रमन्ते] 'the @[śāstras] develop in him'
standard:
After @deva[क्रम्] meaning @deva[वृत्ति] (continuity), @deva[सर्ग/प्रयत्न] (energy), or @deva[उपचय/तायन] (development), @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies.

@deva[क्रम्] governs @[sūtras] through @ref[1.3.43].

**Examples:**
- @deva[ऋक्ष्वस्य क्रमते बुद्धिः] 'his mind moves easily through the @[ṛgveda]'
- @deva[व्याकरणाध्ययनाय क्रमते] 'he exerts for grammar study'

Without these senses → @deva[परस्मैपद]: @deva[अपक्रामति] 'he runs away.'
advanced: After @deva[क्रम्] with @deva[वृत्ति/सर्ग/उपचय] senses, @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies. @deva[क्रम्] is @deva[अनुवृत्ति] through @ref[1.3.43].

simple:
The root @deva[क्रम्] takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings when preceded by @deva[उप] or @deva[पर] and used in the senses of continuity, energy, or development.

**Examples:**
- @deva[उपक्रमते] 'he commences'
- @deva[पराक्रमते] 'he exerts, marches to attack'

With other senses: @deva[उपक्रामति] 'he approaches' (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).
standard:
After @deva[उप/पर] + @deva[क्रम्] with @deva[वृत्ति/सर्ग/उपचय] senses, @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies.

A separate @[sūtra] was needed to restrict @deva[आत्मनेपद] to only @deva[उप] and @deva[पर] among the @deva[उपसर्ग]s. With other prefixes: @deva[संक्रामति] 'he progresses' (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).

Without these senses: @deva[उपक्रामति], @deva[पराक्रामति].
advanced: After @deva[उप/पर] + @deva[क्रम्] with these senses, @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies. Other @deva[उपसर्ग]s → @deva[परस्मैपद] even with these senses.

simple:
The root @deva[क्रम्] takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings when preceded by @deva[आ] and meaning 'rising of a heavenly body.'

**Examples:**
- @deva[आक्रमते सूर्यः] 'the sun rises'
- @deva[आक्रमते चन्द्रमाः] 'the moon rises'
- @deva[आक्रमन्त ज्योतींषि] 'the stars rise'

With other meanings: @deva[आक्रामति माणवकः कुतपम्] 'the boy attacks the ox' (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).
advanced:
After @deva[आङ्] + @deva[क्रम्] meaning rising of a luminary, @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies.

@note[type=@[vārtika]]{Only @deva[ज्योतिस्] (heavenly bodies). Non-luminaries → @deva[परस्मैपद].}

simple:
The root @deva[क्रम्] takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings when preceded by @deva[वि] and meaning 'pacing' or 'placing footsteps.'

**Example:** @deva[वाजी विक्रमते] 'the horse paces.'

The term @deva[विक्रमण] refers to the special stepping movements of horses.

With other meanings: @deva[विक्रामति अजिनसन्धिः] 'the fold of the antelope skin ruptures' (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).
standard:
After @deva[वि] + @deva[क्रम्] meaning @deva[पादविक्षेप] (placing footsteps), @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies.

**Example:** @deva[वाजी विक्रमते] 'the horse paces.'

Though the @deva[धातुपाठ] assigns @deva[पादविहरण/पादविक्षेप] meanings to @deva[क्रमि/क्रमु], this @[sūtra] is not redundant since roots have meanings beyond what the @deva[धातुपाठ] lists.

Without this meaning → @deva[परस्मैपद]: @deva[विक्रामति].
advanced: After @deva[वि] + @deva[क्रम्] meaning @deva[पादविक्षेप], @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies. Without this meaning → @deva[परस्मैपद].

simple:
The root @deva[क्रम्] takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings when preceded by both @deva[वि] and either @deva[प्र] or @deva[उप], with both prefixes meaning 'to begin.'

**Examples:**
- @deva[प्रक्रमते भोक्तुम्] 'he begins to eat'
- @deva[उपक्रमते भोक्तुम्] 'he begins to eat'

Here @deva[प्र] and @deva[उप] are synonyms, both conveying commencement.
standard:
After @deva[प्र/उप] + @deva[वि] + @deva[क्रम्] meaning 'to begin,' @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies when @deva[प्र] and @deva[उप] are synonymous (both meaning commencement).

**Examples:** @deva[प्रविक्रमते], @deva[उपविक्रमते].

When not synonymous: @deva[पूर्वेद्युः प्रक्रामति अपरेद्युः उपक्रामति] 'he goes in the morning, returns in the evening' (→ @deva[परस्मैपद], since @deva[प्र] = 'to go' and @deva[उप] = 'to return').

@ref[1.3.39] does not apply here because that rule requires the @deva[वृत्ति] sense.
advanced: After @deva[प्र/उप] + @deva[वि] + @deva[क्रम्] with @deva[प्र] and @deva[उप] as synonyms meaning commencement, @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies. When not synonymous → @deva[परस्मैपद].

simple:
The root @deva[क्रम्] optionally takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings when not preceded by any @deva[उपसर्ग].

**Both valid:**
- @deva[क्रमते] or @deva[क्रामति] 'he steps'

With an @deva[उपसर्ग], no option: @deva[संक्रामति] (always @deva[परस्मैपद]).
standard:
After @deva[क्रम्] without any @deva[उपसर्ग], @deva[आत्मनेपद] is optional (@deva[अप्राप्तविभाषा]—option not limiting a prior general rule).

**Examples:**
- @deva[क्रमते] or @deva[क्रामति] 'he goes over'
- @deva[अक्रमिष्ट] or @deva[अक्रामीत्] (@deva[लुङ्])

With @deva[उपसर्ग] → no option: @deva[संक्रामति].

@deva[उपसर्ग] is defined in @ref[1.4.59].
advanced: After @deva[क्रम्] without @deva[उपसर्ग], @deva[आत्मनेपद] is optional. This is @deva[अप्राप्तविभाषा]. With @deva[उपसर्ग] → no option applies.

simple:
The root @deva[ज्ञा] 'to know' takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings when meaning 'to deny.'

**Examples:**
- @deva[अपजानीते] 'he denies'
- @deva[शतम् अपजानीते] 'he denies a debt of a hundred'
- @deva[सहस्रम् अपजानीते] 'he denies a thousand'

@deva[ज्ञा] normally means 'to know' and is @deva[परस्मैपदिन्] (by @ref[1.3.78]). With a prefix conveying denial, it becomes @deva[आत्मनेपदिन्].
standard:
After @deva[ज्ञा] meaning 'to deny' (through a prefix), @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies.

**Examples:** @deva[अपजानीते] 'he deceives/denies', @deva[शतम् अपजानीते] 'he denies the hundred-rupee debt.'

Without this meaning: @deva[न त्वं किञ्चिदपि जानासि] 'you know nothing' (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).
advanced: After @deva[ज्ञा] meaning denial, @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies. @deva[ज्ञा] is ordinarily @deva[परस्मैपदिन्] by @ref[1.3.78].

simple:
The root @deva[ज्ञा] takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings when used intransitively—even when the fruit does not accrue to the agent.

**Examples:**
- @deva[सर्पिषो जानीते] 'he engages in sacrifice with ghee'
- @deva[मधुनो जानीते] 'he engages with honey'

Here @deva[सर्पिस्] is not the object of knowledge but the instrument of sacrifice. The @deva[षष्ठी] (genitive) is by @ref[2.3.51].
standard:
After @deva[ज्ञा] used intransitively, @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies even when the fruit does not accrue to the agent.

**Examples:** @deva[सर्पिषो जानीते], @deva[मधुनो जानीते].

The word @deva[सर्पिस्] is not the object but the @deva[करण] (instrument) of sacrifice—hence the genitive by @ref[2.3.51] (@deva[ज्ञोऽविदर्थस्य करणे]).

When transitive → @deva[परस्मैपद]: @deva[स्वरेण पुत्रं जानाति] 'he recognizes his son by his voice.'
advanced: After @deva[ज्ञा] used intransitively, @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies even when the fruit does not accrue to the agent. @deva[करण] takes @deva[षष्ठी] by @ref[2.3.51]. Transitive → @deva[परस्मैपद].

simple:
The root @deva[ज्ञा] takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings when preceded by @deva[सम्] or @deva[प्रति]—except when meaning 'remembering with regret.'

**Examples:**
- @deva[शतं संजानीते] 'he looks for a hundred'
- @deva[सहस्रं प्रतिजानीते] 'he promises a thousand'

When meaning 'remembering with regret': @deva[मातुः संजानाति] 'he remembers his mother with regret' (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).
standard:
After @deva[सम्/प्रति] + @deva[ज्ञा] (not meaning 'remembering with regret'), @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies.

**Examples:**
- @deva[शतं संजानीते] 'he looks for a hundred'
- @deva[सहस्रं प्रतिजानीते] 'he promises a thousand'

Meaning 'remembering with regret' → @deva[परस्मैपद]: @deva[मातुः संजानाति], @deva[पितुः संजानाति].
advanced: After @deva[सम्/प्रति] + @deva[ज्ञा] (not meaning @deva[अनुताप]-@deva[स्मरण]), @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies. With regretful remembrance → @deva[परस्मैपद].

simple:
The root @deva[वद्] 'to speak' takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings when used in six special senses:

1. @deva[भासन] (showing brilliance)
2. @deva[उपसंभाषा] (pacifying)
3. @deva[ज्ञान] (knowing completely)
4. @deva[यत्न] (striving)
5. @deva[विमति] (disagreeing)
6. @deva[उपमन्त्रण] (flattering/enticing)

**Examples:**
- @deva[वदते चर्वी लोकायते] 'the teacher illuminates the @[lokāyata]'
- @deva[कर्मकारान् उपवदते] 'he cajoles the servants'
- @deva[क्षेत्रे वदते] 'he toils in the field'
- @deva[कुलभार्याम् उपवदते] 'he entices a respectable wife'
standard:
After @deva[वद्] meaning @deva[भासन/उपसंभाषा/ज्ञान/यत्न/विमति/उपमन्त्रण], @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies.

**Examples:**
- @deva[वदते चर्वी लोकायते] 'the @[cārvī] illuminates/knows the @[lokāyata]'
- @deva[कर्मकारान् उपवदते] 'he conciliates the servants'
- @deva[क्षेत्रे वदते] 'he labors in the field'
- @deva[क्षेत्रे विवदन्ते] 'they disagree about the field'
- @deva[परदारान् उपवदते] 'he seduces another's wife'

Without these senses → @deva[परस्मैपद]: @deva[यत् किञ्चिद् वदति] 'he says something.'
advanced: After @deva[वद्] with these six senses, @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies. @deva[वद्] is ordinarily @deva[परस्मैपदिन्] by @ref[1.3.78].

simple:
The root @deva[वद्] takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings when meaning 'speaking articulately together.'

**Example:** @deva[संप्रवदन्ते ब्राह्मणाः] 'the @[brāhmaṇas] are speaking (in proper Sanskrit together).'

This applies only to humans capable of articulate speech speaking simultaneously. For animals: @deva[संप्रवदन्ति कुक्कुटाः] 'the cocks crow' (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).
standard:
After @deva[वद्] meaning @deva[व्यक्तवाच्] (articulate speech) with @deva[समुच्चारण] (speaking together), @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies.

**Example:** @deva[संप्रवदन्ते ब्राह्मणाः] 'the @[brāhmaṇas] are speaking together.'

Requires: (1) articulate human speech, (2) speaking in chorus. Animals making noise: @deva[संप्रवदन्ति कुक्कुटाः] (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]). Single speaker: @deva[ब्राह्मणो वदति] (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).
advanced: After @deva[वद्] with @deva[व्यक्तवाच्] and @deva[समुच्चारण], @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies. Without chorus speaking → @deva[परस्मैपद].

simple:
The root @deva[वद्] takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings when preceded by @deva[अनु], used intransitively, and meaning 'to echo' or 'imitate speech.'

**Example:** @deva[अनुवदते कठः कलापस्य] 'the @[kaṭha] echoes/imitates the @[kalāpa].'

When transitive: @deva[पूर्वमेव यजुः उदितम् अनुवदति] 'he repeats the Yajurveda he learned before' (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).
standard:
After @deva[अनु] + @deva[वद्] used intransitively meaning @deva[सदृशोच्चारण] (speaking similarly), @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies.

**Example:** @deva[अनुवदते कठः कलापस्य] 'the @[kaṭha] imitates the @[kalāpa].'

@deva[अनु] here means 'similarly.' The @deva[व्यक्तवाच्] condition continues—so: @deva[अनुवदति वीणा] 'the lute resounds' (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).

Transitive → @deva[परस्मैपद]: @deva[यजुः अनुवदति].
advanced: After @deva[अनु] + @deva[वद्] used intransitively with @deva[व्यक्तवाच्] sense, @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies. Transitive or non-human → @deva[परस्मैपद].

simple:
The root @deva[वद्] optionally takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings when meaning 'to contradict each other.'

**Both valid:** @deva[विप्रवदन्ते वैद्याः] or @deva[विप्रवदन्ति वैद्याः] 'the doctors disagree.'

Without the sense of contradiction, no option: @deva[संप्रवदन्ते ब्राह्मणाः] (always @deva[आत्मनेपद] by @ref[1.3.48]).
standard:
After @deva[वद्] meaning 'to contradict each other,' @deva[आत्मनेपद] is optional.

**Both valid:** @deva[विप्रवदन्ते] or @deva[विप्रवदन्ति वैद्याः].

The @deva[व्यक्तवाच्] and @deva[समुच्चारण] conditions apply here too. Birds quarreling: @deva[विप्रवदन्ति शकुनयः] (only @deva[परस्मैपद]). Sequential arguing: @deva[क्रमेण वैद्यो वैद्येन सह विप्रवदन्ति] (only @deva[परस्मैपद]).
advanced: After @deva[वद्] meaning contradiction, @deva[आत्मनेपद] is optional. @deva[व्यक्तवाच्] and @deva[समुच्चारण] required for option.

simple:
The root @deva[गॄ] 'to swallow' takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings when preceded by @deva[अव].

**Example:** @deva[अवगिरते] 'he swallows.'

Without @deva[अव]: @deva[गिरति] 'he swallows' (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).
standard:
After @deva[अव] + @deva[गॄ] (to swallow, @deva[तुदादि] class), @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies.

**Example:** @deva[अवगिरते] 'he swallows.'

This is not @deva[गॄ] 'to make sound' (@deva[क्र्यादि]), which doesn't combine with @deva[अव]. Without @deva[अव] → @deva[परस्मैपद] by @ref[1.3.78]: @deva[गिरति].
advanced: After @deva[अव] + @deva[गॄ] (@deva[तुदादि], 'to swallow'), @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies. This overrides @ref[1.3.78].

simple:
The root @deva[गॄ] takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings when preceded by @deva[सम्] and meaning 'to promise.'

**Example:** @deva[शतं संगिरते] 'he promises to pay a hundred.'

Without the sense of promising: @deva[संगिरति ग्रासम्] 'he swallows the mouthful' (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).
standard:
After @deva[सम्] + @deva[गॄ] meaning 'to promise/acknowledge,' @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies.

**Example:** @deva[शतं संगिरते] 'he promises a hundred rupees.'

Without this meaning → @deva[परस्मैपद]: @deva[संगिरति ग्रासम्] 'he swallows the morsel.'
advanced: After @deva[सम्] + @deva[गॄ] meaning 'to promise,' @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies. Other senses → @deva[परस्मैपद].

simple:
The root @deva[चर्] 'to walk' takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings when preceded by @deva[उद्] and used intransitively.

**Examples:**
- @deva[गेहम् उच्चरते] 'he strays from home'
- @deva[गुरुवचनम् उच्चरते] 'he transgresses his guru's words'

When transitive: @deva[बाष्पम् उच्चरति] 'the vapor rises' (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).
standard:
After @deva[उद्] + @deva[चर्] used intransitively, @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies.

**Examples:** @deva[गेहम् उच्चरते] 'he strays from home', @deva[गुरुवचनम् उच्चरते] 'he transgresses his guru's commands.'

Transitive → @deva[परस्मैपद]: @deva[बाष्पम् उच्चरति] 'the vapor rises.'
advanced: After @deva[उद्] + @deva[चर्] used intransitively, @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies. Transitive → @deva[परस्मैपद].

simple:
The root @deva[चर्] takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings when preceded by @deva[सम्] and connected with an instrumental (@deva[तृतीया]).

**Examples:**
- @deva[अश्वेन संचरते] 'he rides on horseback'
- @deva[रथेन संचरते] 'he travels by chariot'

Without explicit instrumental: @deva[उभौ लोकौ संचरति] 'he wanders through both worlds' (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).
standard:
After @deva[सम्] + @deva[चर्] with a noun in @deva[तृतीया] (instrumental), @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies.

**Examples:** @deva[अश्वेन संचरते], @deva[रथेन संचरते].

The instrumental must be explicit. If only implied: @deva[उभौ लोकौ संचरति इमं चामुं च देवल] 'O Devala, you wander through both worlds' (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).
advanced: After @deva[सम्] + @deva[चर्] with explicit @deva[तृतीया], @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies. Implied instrumental → @deva[परस्मैपद].

simple:
The root @deva[दा] 'to give' takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings when preceded by @deva[सम्] and connected with an instrumental that has the sense of a dative (recipient).

**Example:** @deva[दास्या संप्रयच्छते] 'he gives (in order to entice) the female servant.'

This applies when immoral conduct is implied. Without dative sense: @deva[पाणिना संप्रयच्छति] 'he gives with his hand' (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).
advanced: After @deva[सम्] + @deva[दा] with @deva[तृतीया] having @deva[चतुर्थी]-sense (immoral context), @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies. @deva[सम्] is @deva[षष्ठी] not @deva[पञ्चमी], allowing @deva[प्र] to intervene.

simple:
The root @deva[यम्] 'to give' takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings when preceded by @deva[उप] and meaning 'to espouse' or 'to accept as one's own.'

**Example:** @deva[भार्याम् उपयच्छते] 'he marries his wife.'

With someone else's wife: @deva[देवदत्तो यज्ञदत्तस्य भार्याम् उपयच्छति] 'Devadatta has illicit relations with @[yajñadatta]'s wife' (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).
standard:
After @deva[उप] + @deva[यम्] meaning @deva[स्वकरण] (to make one's own, to espouse), @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies.

**Example:** @deva[भार्याम् उपयच्छते] 'he marries his wife.'

@deva[यम्] is ordinarily @deva[परस्मैपदिन्] by @ref[1.3.78]. This @[sūtra] makes an exception when @deva[उप] + 'to marry/accept' applies.

Without this meaning → @deva[परस्मैपद]: @deva[देवदत्तो यज्ञदत्तस्य भार्याम् उपयच्छति] (illicit intercourse).
advanced: After @deva[उप] + @deva[यम्] meaning @deva[स्वकरण], @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies. @deva[स्वकरण] = making one's own what was not previously one's own (@[mahābhāṣya]).

simple:
Desideratives formed with @deva[सन्] from the roots @deva[ज्ञा] 'to know', @deva[श्रु] 'to hear', @deva[स्मृ] 'to remember', and @deva[दृश्] 'to see' take @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings.

**Examples:**
- @deva[धर्मं जिज्ञासते] 'he wishes to know dharma'
- @deva[गुरुं शुश्रूषते] 'he wishes to serve the teacher'
- @deva[नष्टं सुस्मूर्षते] 'he wishes to remember the lost'
- @deva[नृपं दिदृक्षते] 'he wishes to see the king'

The primitive verbs are @deva[परस्मैपदिन्]: @deva[जानाति], @deva[शृणोति], @deva[स्मरति], @deva[पश्यति].
advanced: After desideratives of @deva[ज्ञा/श्रु/स्मृ/दृश्], @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies. For @deva[ज्ञा/श्रु/दृश्], this generalizes prior limited rules. For @deva[स्मृ], this is a new ordainment.

simple:
The desiderative of @deva[ज्ञा] does NOT take @deva[आत्मनेपद] when preceded by @deva[अनु].

**Example:** @deva[पुत्रम् अनुजिज्ञासति] 'he wishes to inquire about the son.'

This is an exception to @ref[1.3.57]. Without @deva[अनु]: @deva[धर्मं जिज्ञासते] (→ @deva[आत्मनेपद]).
standard:
After @deva[अनु] + desiderative of @deva[ज्ञा], @deva[आत्मनेपद] does NOT apply. This excepts @ref[1.3.57].

**Example:** @deva[पुत्रम् अनुजिज्ञासति] 'he wishes to inquire about the son.'

This @[sūtra], read with @ref[1.3.45], limits the @deva[परस्मैपद] to the transitive desiderative with @deva[अनु].
advanced: After @deva[अनु] + desiderative of @deva[ज्ञा], @deva[आत्मनेपद] does NOT apply (exception to @ref[1.3.57]).

simple:
The desiderative of @deva[श्रु] does NOT take @deva[आत्मनेपद] when preceded by @deva[प्रति] or @deva[आ].

**Examples:**
- @deva[प्रतिशुश्रूषति] 'he wishes to answer'
- @deva[आशुश्रूषति] 'he wishes to listen'

This is an exception to @ref[1.3.57]. Without these prefixes: @deva[शुश्रूषते] (→ @deva[आत्मनेपद]).
advanced: After @deva[प्रति/आङ्] + desiderative of @deva[श्रु], @deva[आत्मनेपद] does NOT apply. @deva[प्रति/आङ्] must be @deva[उपसर्ग] not @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय].

simple:
The root @deva[शद्] 'to decay' takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings when followed by affixes marked with indicatory @deva[श्] (@deva[शित्]).

**Examples:**
- @deva[शीयते] 'it decays' (with @deva[शप्])
- @deva[शीयेते] 'they two decay'
- @deva[शीयन्ते] 'they decay'

Before other affixes: @deva[अशत्स्यत्] 'if he had decayed', @deva[शत्स्यति] 'he will decay' (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).
advanced: After @deva[शद्] with @deva[शित्] affixes, @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies. In other tenses (futures, perfect, conditional) → @deva[परस्मैपद]: @deva[मरिष्यति].

simple:
The root @deva[मृ] 'to die' takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings when followed by @deva[शित्] affixes, @deva[लुङ्] (aorist), or @deva[लिङ्] (benedictive).

**Examples:**
- @deva[म्रियते] 'he dies' (with @deva[शप्], a @deva[शित्] affix)
- @deva[अमृत] 'he died' (@deva[लुङ्])
- @deva[मृषीष्ट] 'may he die' (@deva[आशीर्लिङ्])

In futures, perfect, and conditional: @deva[मरिष्यति] 'he will die' (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).
standard:
After @deva[मृ] with @deva[शित्] affixes, @deva[लुङ्], or @deva[लिङ्], @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies. This is a restrictive rule (@deva[नियम]).

@deva[मृङ्] is marked with @deva[ङ्], so by @ref[1.3.12] it would always be @deva[आत्मनेपदिन्]. This @[sūtra] restricts that to only @deva[शित्]/@deva[आ]deva[@deva[लुङ्]]/@deva[लिङ्].

**Derivation:** @deva[मृ + श + ते] → @deva[म्रिय + अ + ते] (by @ref[7.4.28], @ref[6.4.77]) → @deva[म्रियते].

In futures/perfect/conditional → @deva[परस्मैपद]: @deva[मरिष्यति], @deva[अमरिष्यत्].
advanced: After @deva[मृ] with @deva[शित्]/@deva[लुङ्]/@deva[लिङ्], @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies. This restricts @ref[1.3.12] to these affixes only.

simple:
A desiderative (formed with @deva[सन्]) takes the same @deva[पद] as its primitive verb.

**Examples:**
- @deva[आस्ते] 'he sits' (@[ātm].) → @deva[आसिसिषते] 'he wishes to sit'
- @deva[शेते] 'he lies' (@[ātm].) → @deva[शिशयिषते] 'he wishes to lie'
- @deva[निविशते] 'he enters' (@[ātm].) → @deva[निविविक्षते] 'he wishes to enter'

If the primitive is @deva[परस्मैपदिन्], so is its desiderative.
standard:
A desiderative takes the same @deva[पद] as its primitive—the reason that caused @deva[आत्मनेपद] in the primitive causes it in the desiderative.

@deva[आस्] and @deva[शीङ्] have @deva[अनुदात्त]/@deva[ङ्]] markers (→ @deva[आत्मनेपद] by @ref[1.3.12]); their desideratives retain this.

**But:** @deva[शीयते] (@ref[1.3.60]) and @deva[म्रियते] (@ref[1.3.61]) are @deva[आत्मनेपदिन्] only under specific conditions. Their desideratives @deva[शिशत्सति] and @deva[मुमूर्षति] are @deva[परस्मैपदिन्] since those conditions don't apply.

For @deva[कृञ्]: @deva[अनुचिकीर्षति], @deva[पराचिकीर्षति] (→ @deva[परस्मैपद] by @ref[1.3.79]).
advanced: Desideratives follow the @deva[पद] of their primitives. Conditional @deva[आत्मनेपद] rules (like @ref[1.3.60], @ref[1.3.61]) don't transfer if conditions don't apply in the desiderative.

simple:
In the periphrastic perfect, the auxiliary @deva[कृ] takes the same @deva[पद] as the main verb (which ends in @deva[आम्]).

**Examples:**
- @deva[एध्] is @deva[आत्मनेपदिन्] → @deva[एधांचक्रे] 'he prospered'
- @deva[इन्द्] is @deva[परस्मैपदिन्] → @deva[इन्दांचकार] 'he appeared splendidly'

The other auxiliaries @deva[भू] and @deva[अस्] retain their own conjugation: @deva[एधाम्बभूव], @deva[एधामास].
standard:
When @deva[कृ] is used as auxiliary with an @deva[आम्]-ending verb (periphrastic perfect), @deva[कृ] takes the @deva[पद] of the main verb—even when the fruit does not accrue to the agent.

**Examples:** @deva[एधांचक्रे] (@deva[एध्] is @[ātm].), @deva[इन्दांचकार] (@deva[इन्द्] is para.).

The auxiliaries @deva[भू] and @deva[अस्] do NOT follow this rule—they retain their own conjugation: @deva[एधाम्बभूव], @deva[एधामास].

The word @deva[कृञ्] in @ref[3.1.40] is a @deva[प्रत्याहार] for @deva[कृञ्/भू/अस्], but in this @[sūtra] only @deva[कृञ्] is meant.
advanced: Auxiliary @deva[कृ] follows the @deva[पद] of the @deva[आम्]-ending main verb. @deva[भू] and @deva[अस्] retain their own conjugation.

simple:
The root @deva[युज्] 'to join' takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings when preceded by @deva[प्र] or @deva[उप]—except when referring to sacrificial vessels.

**Examples:**
- @deva[प्रयुङ्क्ते] 'he yokes/employs'
- @deva[उपयुङ्क्ते] 'he uses'

With sacrificial vessels: @deva[द्वन्द्वं न्यञ्चि पात्राणि प्रयुनक्ति] 'he prepares the vessels' (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).
advanced:
After @deva[प्र/उप] + @deva[युज्] (not with sacrificial vessels), @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies.

@note[type=@[vārtika]]{All @deva[उपसर्ग]s except @deva[सम्/निर्/दुर्] trigger @deva[आत्मनेपद].}

simple:
The root @deva[क्ष्णु] 'to sharpen' takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings when preceded by @deva[सम्].

**Examples:**
- @deva[संक्ष्णुते शस्त्रम्] 'he whets the weapon'
- @deva[संक्ष्णुवाते] 'they two whet'
- @deva[संक्ष्णुवते] 'they all whet'

Without @deva[सम्]: @deva[क्ष्णौति] (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).
standard:
After @deva[सम्] + @deva[क्ष्णु] 'to sharpen,' @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies.

**Example:** @deva[संक्ष्णुते शस्त्रम्] 'he whets the weapon.'

This could not be included in @ref[1.3.29] (which also uses @deva[सम्]) because that @[sūtra] treats intransitive verbs (@deva[अकर्मक] is understood there). This @[sūtra] treats transitive use.
advanced: After @deva[सम्] + @deva[क्ष्णु], @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies. Separate from @ref[1.3.29] because this is transitive.

simple:
The root @deva[भुज्] takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings—except when meaning 'to protect.'

**Examples:**
- @deva[भुङ्क्ते] 'he eats/enjoys'
- @deva[अन्नं भुङ्क्ते] 'he eats food'
- @deva[राज्यं भुङ्क्ते] 'he enjoys the kingdom'

Meaning 'to protect': @deva[पुत्रान् भुनक्ति पिता] 'the father protects his sons' (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).
standard:
After @deva[भुज्] (@deva[रुधादि] class) except meaning 'to protect,' @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies.

**Examples:** @deva[भुङ्क्ते], @deva[भुञ्जाते], @deva[भुञ्जते].

Meaning 'to protect' → @deva[परस्मैपद]: @deva[पुत्रान् भुनक्ति पिता], @deva[भुनक्त्येनम् अग्निः आहितः].

@deva[भुज्] of @deva[तुदादि] class (meaning 'to bend') is not meant here—it never means 'to protect': @deva[बिभुजति पाणिम्] 'he bends his hand' (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).
advanced: After @deva[भुज्] (@deva[रुधादि]) except meaning 'to protect,' @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies. The @deva[तुदादि] @deva[भुज्] 'to bend' is always @deva[परस्मैपदिन्].

simple: When a causative (@deva[णिच्]) verb is formed, and the original object of the simple verb effectively becomes the one doing the action in the causal frame, @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings are used. For example: "@[yajñadatta] has a mat made" — @deva[यज्ञदत्तः कटं कारयते] — because @deva[कट] (mat, originally the object) becomes the implied maker. The fruit of the action does not come back to the causer, so @deva[आत्मनेपद] signals this relationship.
standard: After @deva[णिच्] (causative suffix), @deva[आत्मनेपद] is prescribed when the @deva[अकारक] (non-causal object) of the simple verb becomes the @deva[कर्तृ] (agent) in the causative construction. Conditioning: the causer (@deva[हेतु]) is not the beneficiary of the action — the fruit accrues elsewhere, specifically to the entity that acts. Non-causal: @deva[देवदत्तः कटं करोति] "Devadatta makes a mat" (object: @deva[कट]). Causal: @deva[यज्ञदत्तः कटं कारयते] "@[yajñadatta] has a mat made" — @deva[कट] is the implied maker; @deva[यज्ञदत्त] is the causer who does not himself enjoy the making. The @deva[आत्मनेपद] here contrasts with @deva[परस्मैपद], which would apply when the causer is the beneficiary.
advanced: After a @deva[णिच्] (causative) where the non-causal object becomes the causal agent, @deva[आत्मनेपद] is used. When the object of the simple verb becomes the agent of the causative, and the fruit doesn't accrue to the causer: **Non-causal:** @deva[देवदत्तः कटं करोति] "D. makes a mat" **Causal:** @deva[यज्ञदत्तः कटं कारयते] "Y. has a mat made" @deva[कट] (object) becomes understood agent of making; @deva[यज्ञदत्त] is the causer.

simple:
Causatives of @deva[भी] 'to fear' and @deva[स्मि] 'to wonder' take @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings when the fear or wonder is directly caused by the agent (@deva[हेतुभय]).

**Examples:**
- @deva[जटिलो भीषयते] 'the ascetic (with matted hair) frightens' (his appearance itself causes fear)
- @deva[मुण्डो विस्मापयते] 'the shaven-headed one amazes'

When fear/wonder comes from an instrument, not directly from the agent: @deva[कुञ्चिकया एनं भाययति] 'he frightens him with the key' (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).
standard:
After causatives of @deva[भी/स्मि], @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies when the @deva[हेतु] (causative agent) directly produces fear/wonder—even when the fruit does not accrue to the agent.

@deva[हेतु] is defined in @ref[1.4.55] as @deva[स्वतन्त्रः कर्ता]. @deva[हेतुभय] = fear caused by the @deva[हेतु] itself.

**Examples:** @deva[जटिलो भीषयते], @deva[मुण्डो विस्मापयते].

When fear is from an instrument → @deva[परस्मैपद]: @deva[कुञ्चिकया एनं भाययति], @deva[रूपेण विस्माययति].
advanced: After causatives of @deva[भी/स्मि] with @deva[हेतुभय], @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies. Fear from an instrument → @deva[परस्मैपद].

simple:
Causatives of @deva[गृध्] 'to covet' and @deva[वञ्च्] 'to go' take @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings when meaning 'to deceive' (@deva[प्रलम्भन]).

**Examples:**
- @deva[माणवकं गर्धयते] 'he deceives the boy'
- @deva[माणवकं वञ्चयते] 'he cheats the boy'

Without this meaning: @deva[श्वानं गर्धयति] 'he makes the dog bark', @deva[अहिं वञ्चयति] 'he avoids the serpent' (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).
standard:
After causatives of @deva[गृध्/वञ्च्] meaning @deva[प्रलम्भन] (deceiving), @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies—even when the fruit does not accrue to the agent.

**Examples:** @deva[माणवकं गर्धयते], @deva[माणवकं वञ्चयते].

Without @deva[प्रलम्भन] sense → @deva[परस्मैपद]: @deva[श्वानं गर्धयति], @deva[अहिं वञ्चयति].
advanced: After causatives of @deva[गृध्/वञ्च्] meaning @deva[प्रलम्भन], @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies. Other senses → @deva[परस्मैपद].

simple:
Causatives of @deva[ली] 'to melt/stick' take @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings when meaning 'to show respect' (@deva[सम्मानन]), 'to subdue' (@deva[शालीनीकरण]), or 'to deceive' (@deva[प्रलम्भन]).

**Examples:**
- @deva[जटाभिः आलापयते] 'he gets respect through his matted hair'
- @deva[श्येनो वर्तिकाम् उल्लापयते] 'the hawk subdues the partridge'
- @deva[कस्त्वाम् उल्लापयते] 'who deceives you?'

Without these senses: @deva[बालकम् उल्लापयति] (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).
standard:
After causatives of @deva[ली] (@deva[दिवादि] 'to stick' or @deva[क्र्यादि] 'to melt') meaning @deva[सम्मानन/शालीनीकरण/प्रलम्भन], @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies—even when the fruit does not accrue to the agent.

@deva[च] in the @[sūtra] includes @deva[प्रलम्भन] from @ref[1.3.69].

**Examples:** @deva[जटाभिः आलापयते], @deva[श्येनो वर्तिकाम् उल्लापयते], @deva[कस्त्वाम् उल्लापयते].

Without these senses → @deva[परस्मैपद]: @deva[बालकम् उल्लापयति].

Note: @ref[6.1.51] allows optional @deva[ई] → @deva[आ] for @deva[ली], but with these three senses, the substitution is obligatory (@deva[व्यवस्थितविभाषा]).
advanced: After causatives of @deva[ली] meaning @deva[सम्मानन/शालीनीकरण/प्रलम्भन], @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies. Other senses → @deva[परस्मैपद].

simple:
The causative of @deva[कृ] takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings when @deva[मिथ्या] 'incorrectly' is the @deva[उपपद] and the meaning is 'habitual wrong utterance' (@deva[अभ्यास]).

**Example:** @deva[पदं मिथ्या कारयते] 'he habitually pronounces the word wrongly (with wrong accent, etc.).'

With a different @deva[उपपद]: @deva[पदं सुष्ठु कारयति] 'he pronounces the word correctly' (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).

With a different verb: @deva[पदं मिथ्या वाचयति] 'he utters the word wrongly' (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).

Without habitual sense: @deva[पदं मिथ्याकारयति] 'he pronounces wrongly (once)' (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).
standard:
After causative of @deva[कृ] with @deva[मिथ्या] as @deva[उपपद] meaning @deva[अभ्यास] (repeated wrong action), @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies—even when the fruit does not accrue to the agent.

**Example:** @deva[पदं मिथ्या कारयते] 'he habitually makes wrong pronunciation.'

Conditions: (1) @deva[मिथ्या] as @deva[उपपद], (2) @deva[कृञ्] specifically, (3) habitual (@deva[अभ्यास]). Without any → @deva[परस्मैपद].
advanced: After causative of @deva[कृ] with @deva[मिथ्या] as @deva[उपपद] and @deva[अभ्यास] sense, @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies. Without @deva[अभ्यास] or with different verb → @deva[परस्मैपद].

simple:
Roots marked with @deva[स्वरित] accent or indicatory @deva[ञ्] take @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings when the fruit of the action accrues to the agent.

**Examples (fruit to agent):**
- @deva[यजते] 'he sacrifices for himself'
- @deva[क्रीणीते] 'he buys for himself'

**Fruit to another (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]):**
- @deva[यजति] 'he sacrifices (as priest for another)'
- @deva[क्रीणाति] 'he buys for another'

@deva[स्वरितेत्] roots have @deva[स्वरित] (circumflex) accent marked on their vowel. @deva[ञित्] roots have indicatory @deva[ञ्] (like @deva[डुक्रीञ्]).
advanced: After @deva[स्वरितेत्/ञित्] roots, @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies when @deva[क्रियाफल] accrues to @deva[कर्ता]. When @deva[क्रियाफल] is for another → @deva[परस्मैपद].

simple:
The root @deva[वद्] takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings when preceded by @deva[अप] and the fruit of the action accrues to the agent.

**Example:** @deva[धनकामो न्यायम् अपवदते] 'the wealth-seeker forsakes justice' (he sacrifices justice to gain wealth for himself).

When fruit is for another: @deva[अपवदति] (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).
standard:
After @deva[अप] + @deva[वद्], @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies when the fruit accrues to the agent.

**Example:** @deva[धनकामो न्यायम् अपवदते] 'the wealth-seeker forsakes justice.'

When fruit does not accrue to agent → @deva[परस्मैपद]: @deva[अपवदति].
advanced: After @deva[अप] + @deva[वद्] when @deva[क्रियाफल] accrues to @deva[कर्ता], @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies.

simple:
Causatives (formed with @deva[णिच्]) take @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings when the fruit of the action accrues to the agent.

**Examples (fruit to agent):**
- @deva[कटं कारयते] 'he has a mat made for himself'
- @deva[ओदनं पाचयते] 'he has food cooked for himself'

**Fruit to another (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]):**
- @deva[कटं कारयति परस्य] 'he has another's mat made'
- @deva[ओदनं पाचयति] 'he has food cooked (for another)'
standard:
After @deva[णिच्] causatives, @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies when the fruit accrues to the agent.

**Examples:** @deva[कटं कारयते], @deva[ओदनं पाचयते].

When fruit does not accrue to agent → @deva[परस्मैपद]: @deva[कटं कारयति परस्य].
advanced: After @deva[णिच्] when @deva[क्रियाफल] accrues to @deva[कर्ता], @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies.

simple:
The root @deva[यम्] takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings when preceded by @deva[सम्], @deva[उद्], or @deva[आ] (not referring to a book) and the fruit accrues to the agent.

**Examples:**
- @deva[ब्रीहीन् संयच्छते] 'he gathers rice (for himself)'
- @deva[भारम् उद्यच्छते] 'he lifts the load (for himself)'
- @deva[वस्त्रम् आयच्छते] 'he draws out the cloth (for himself)'

Referring to a book: @deva[उद्यच्छति चिकित्सां वैद्यः] 'the physician studies medicine diligently' (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).

Fruit not to agent: @deva[संयच्छति], @deva[उद्यच्छति], @deva[आयच्छति] (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).
standard:
After @deva[सम्/उद्/आङ्] + @deva[यम्] (not referring to a @deva[ग्रन्थ]), @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies when the fruit accrues to the agent.

@deva[आङ्] + @deva[यम्] was already treated in @ref[1.3.28], but there it was intransitive. Here it's transitive—hence a separate @[sūtra].

**Examples:** @deva[ब्रीहीन् संयच्छते], @deva[भारम् उद्यच्छते], @deva[वस्त्रम् आयच्छते].

With @deva[ग्रन्थ] (book/treatise) → @deva[परस्मैपद]: @deva[उद्यच्छति चिकित्सां वैद्यः].

Fruit not to agent → @deva[परस्मैपद]: @deva[संयच्छति], @deva[उद्यच्छति], @deva[आयच्छति].
advanced: After @deva[सम्/उद्/आङ्] + @deva[यम्] (not with @deva[ग्रन्थ]) when @deva[क्रियाफल] accrues to @deva[कर्ता], @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies. Transitive counterpart to @ref[1.3.28].

simple:
The root @deva[ज्ञा] 'to know' takes @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings when not preceded by an @deva[उपसर्ग] and the fruit accrues to the agent.

**Examples:**
- @deva[गां जानीते] 'he recognizes his cow'
- @deva[अश्वं जानीते] 'he recognizes his horse'

With @deva[उपसर्ग]: @deva[स्वर्गं लोकं न प्रजानाति मूढः] 'the fool does not know the heavenly regions' (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).

Fruit not to agent: @deva[देवदत्तस्य गां जानाति] 'he recognizes Devadatta's cow' (→ @deva[परस्मैपद]).
standard:
After @deva[ज्ञा] without @deva[उपसर्ग], @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies when the @deva[क्रियाफल] accrues to the @deva[कर्ता].

**Examples:** @deva[गां जानीते] 'he recognizes his cow', @deva[अश्वं जानीते] 'he recognizes his horse'.

With @deva[उपसर्ग] → governed by other rules: @deva[स्वर्गं लोकं न प्रजानाति].

Fruit not to agent → @deva[परस्मैपद]: @deva[देवदत्तस्य गां जानाति].
advanced: After @deva[ज्ञा] without @deva[उपसर्ग], @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies when @deva[क्रियाफल] accrues to @deva[कर्ता]. With @deva[उपसर्ग] → other rules apply.

simple:
When an @deva[उपपद] (accompanying word) indicates that the fruit accrues to the agent, @deva[आत्मनेपद] becomes optional.

**Examples:**
- @deva[स्वं यज्ञं यजति] or @deva[यजते] 'he sacrifices for his own sacrifice'
- @deva[स्वं कटं करोति] or @deva[कुरुते] 'he makes his own mat'
- @deva[स्वं पुत्रम् अपवदति] or @deva[अपवदते] 'he speaks against his own son'

The @deva[उपपद] @deva[स्व] 'one's own' makes explicit what the verb would otherwise imply.
standard:
When an @deva[उपपद] indicates @deva[क्रियाफल] accruing to @deva[कर्ता], @deva[आत्मनेपद] is optional.

By @ref[1.3.72]–@ref[1.3.76], certain verbs take @deva[आत्मनेपद] when fruit accrues to agent. When an @deva[उपपद] explicitly conveys this, the need for @deva[आत्मनेपद] is removed—hence the option.

**Examples:** @deva[स्वं यज्ञं यजति/यजते], @deva[स्वं कटं करोति/कुरुते], @deva[स्वम् ओदनं पाचयति/पाचयते].
advanced: When an @deva[उपपद] indicates @deva[क्रियाफल] to @deva[कर्ता], @deva[पद] is optional. The @deva[उपपद] makes explicit what the verb would otherwise imply.

simple:
All other verbs—those not covered by the preceding rules (@ref[1.3.12]–@ref[1.3.77])—take @deva[परस्मैपद] endings when denoting the agent (active voice).

**Examples:**
- @deva[याति] 'he goes'
- @deva[वाति] 'it blows'
- @deva[पचति] 'he cooks'

In passive voice, verbs take @deva[आत्मनेपद]: @deva[पच्यते] 'it is cooked', @deva[गम्यते] 'it is gone'.

In reflexive usage (@deva[कर्मकर्तरि]), @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies: @deva[पच्यते ओदनः स्वयम् एव] 'the food cooks by itself'.
standard:
For all remaining verbs (@deva[शेष])—those not covered by @ref[1.3.12]–@ref[1.3.77]—@deva[परस्मैपद] is used when denoting the @deva[कर्ता] (active voice).

**Examples:** @deva[याति], @deva[वाति], @deva[पचति], @deva[गच्छति].

In passive (@deva[कर्मणि]) → @deva[आत्मनेपद]: @deva[पच्यते], @deva[गम्यते].

In reflexive (@deva[कर्मकर्तरि]) → @deva[आत्मनेपद]: @deva[पच्यते ओदनः स्वयम् एव].

The word @deva[कर्तरि] from @ref[1.3.14] continues—@deva[परस्मैपद] applies only when the agent is purely an agent.
advanced: For @deva[शेष] (remaining verbs), @deva[परस्मैपद] applies when denoting @deva[कर्ता]. In @deva[कर्मणि/कर्मकर्तरि] → @deva[आत्मनेपद].

simple:
The root @deva[कृ] takes @deva[परस्मैपद] endings when preceded by @deva[अनु] or @deva[परा]—even when the fruit accrues to the agent or in senses that would otherwise require @deva[आत्मनेपद].

**Examples:**
- @deva[अनुकरोति] 'he imitates'
- @deva[पराकरोति] 'he does well'

This overrides @ref[1.3.32] (special senses of @deva[कृ]) and @ref[1.3.72] (@deva[ञित्] roots with fruit to agent).
standard:
After @deva[अनु/परा] + @deva[कृ], @deva[परस्मैपद] applies—even when fruit accrues to agent or in senses of @ref[1.3.32] (divulging, reviling, etc.).

**Examples:** @deva[अनुकरोति] 'he imitates', @deva[पराकरोति] 'he does well'.

This is an exception to both @ref[1.3.32] and @ref[1.3.72].
advanced: After @deva[अनु/परा] + @deva[कृ], @deva[परस्मैपद] applies—overriding @ref[1.3.32] and @ref[1.3.72].

simple:
The root @deva[क्षिप्] 'to throw' takes @deva[परस्मैपद] endings when preceded by @deva[अभि], @deva[प्रति], or @deva[अति]—even when the fruit accrues to the agent.

**Examples:**
- @deva[अभिक्षिपति] 'he throws at'
- @deva[प्रतिक्षिपति] 'he rejects'
- @deva[अतिक्षिपति] 'he throws beyond'

With other @deva[उपसर्ग]s, @ref[1.3.72] applies: @deva[आक्षिपते] 'he throws down'.
standard:
After @deva[अभि/प्रति/अति] + @deva[क्षिप्], @deva[परस्मैपद] applies—even when fruit accrues to agent.

@deva[क्षिप्] is @deva[स्वरितेत्], so @ref[1.3.72] would ordinarily apply. This @[sūtra] overrides it for these three @deva[उपसर्ग]s.

**Examples:** @deva[अभिक्षिपति], @deva[प्रतिक्षिपति], @deva[अतिक्षिपति].

With other @deva[उपसर्ग]s → @ref[1.3.72]: @deva[आक्षिपते].
advanced: After @deva[अभि/प्रति/अति] + @deva[क्षिप्], @deva[परस्मैपद] applies—overriding @ref[1.3.72]. Other @deva[उपसर्ग]s → @ref[1.3.72].

simple:
The root @deva[वह्] 'to carry' takes @deva[परस्मैपद] endings when preceded by @deva[प्र]—even when the fruit accrues to the agent.

**Example:** @deva[प्रवहति] 'it flows' or 'he carries forth'

With other @deva[उपसर्ग]s, @ref[1.3.72] applies: @deva[आवहते] 'he brings'.
standard:
After @deva[प्र] + @deva[वह्], @deva[परस्मैपद] applies—even when fruit accrues to agent.

@deva[वह्] is @deva[स्वरितेत्], so @ref[1.3.72] would ordinarily apply. This @[sūtra] overrides it for @deva[प्र].

**Example:** @deva[प्रवहति] 'it flows'.

With other @deva[उपसर्ग]s → @ref[1.3.72]: @deva[आवहते] 'he brings'.
advanced: After @deva[प्र] + @deva[वह्], @deva[परस्मैपद] applies—overriding @ref[1.3.72]. Other @deva[उपसर्ग]s → @ref[1.3.72].

simple:
The root @deva[मृष्] 'to endure' takes @deva[परस्मैपद] endings when preceded by @deva[परि]—even when the fruit accrues to the agent.

**Example:** @deva[परिमृष्यति] 'he endures' or 'he becomes angry'

With other @deva[उपसर्ग]s, @ref[1.3.72] applies: @deva[आमृष्यते] 'he touches'.
advanced: After @deva[परि] + @deva[मृष्], @deva[परस्मैपद] applies—overriding @ref[1.3.72]. Other @deva[उपसर्ग]s → @ref[1.3.72].

simple:
The root @deva[रम्] 'to sport' takes @deva[परस्मैपद] endings when preceded by @deva[वि], @deva[आ], or @deva[परि].

**Examples:**
- @deva[विरमति] 'he takes rest' or 'he ceases'
- @deva[आरमति] 'he delights in'
- @deva[परिरमति] 'he sports'

With other @deva[उपसर्ग]s, @ref[1.3.12] applies: @deva[अभिरमते] 'he delights'.
standard:
After @deva[वि/आङ्/परि] + @deva[रम्], @deva[परस्मैपद] applies.

@deva[रम्] is @deva[अनुदात्तेत्], so @ref[1.3.12] would ordinarily apply. This @[sūtra] overrides it for these three @deva[उपसर्ग]s.

**Examples:** @deva[विरमति] 'he ceases', @deva[आरमति] 'he delights', @deva[परिरमति] 'he sports'.

With other @deva[उपसर्ग]s → @ref[1.3.12]: @deva[अभिरमते].
advanced: After @deva[वि/आङ्/परि] + @deva[रम्], @deva[परस्मैपद] applies—overriding @ref[1.3.12]. Other @deva[उपसर्ग]s → @ref[1.3.12].

simple:
The root @deva[रम्] also takes @deva[परस्मैपद] endings when preceded by @deva[उप].

**Example:** @deva[देवदत्तम् उपरमति] 'he causes Devadatta to refrain'

The sense here is causative—@deva[उपरमति] implies @deva[उपरमयति]. The verb itself carries causative force without @deva[णिच्].
standard:
After @deva[उप] + @deva[रम्], @deva[परस्मैपद] also applies.

This @[sūtra] is separate from @ref[1.3.83] because @ref[1.3.85] needs to apply only to @deva[उप] + @deva[रम्], not to the other compounds.

**Example:** @deva[देवदत्तम् उपरमति] 'he causes Devadatta to desist'.

The sense is causative—the verb implies @deva[उपरमयति] without the @deva[णिच्] affix.
advanced: After @deva[उप] + @deva[रम्], @deva[परस्मैपद] applies. Separate @[sūtra] needed for @ref[1.3.85] to apply only here.

simple:
When @deva[उप] + @deva[रम्] is used intransitively, @deva[परस्मैपद] is optional.

**Examples:**
- @deva[यावद् भुक्तम् उपरमति] or @deva[उपरमते] 'he stopped while it was being eaten'

The previous @[sūtra] (@ref[1.3.84]) requires @deva[परस्मैपद] absolutely. This makes it optional when the verb is intransitive.
standard:
After @deva[उप] + @deva[रम्] used intransitively (@deva[अकर्मक]), @deva[परस्मैपद] is optional.

This modifies @ref[1.3.84], which requires @deva[परस्मैपद] absolutely.

**Example:** @deva[यावद् भुक्तम् उपरमति] or @deva[उपरमते] 'he desisted while it was eaten'.
advanced: After @deva[उप] + @deva[रम्] used intransitively, @deva[पद] is optional—modifying @ref[1.3.84].

simple:
Causatives of @deva[बुध्], @deva[युध्], @deva[नश्], @deva[जन्], @deva[इङ्], @deva[प्रु], @deva[द्रु], and @deva[स्रु] take @deva[परस्मैपद] endings—even when the fruit accrues to the agent.

**Examples:**
- @deva[बोधयति] 'he awakens (someone)'
- @deva[योधयति] 'he makes fight'
- @deva[नाशयति] 'he destroys'
- @deva[जनयति] 'he begets'
- @deva[अध्यापयति] 'he teaches' (@deva[अधि] + @deva[इङ्])
- @deva[प्रावयति] 'he causes to leap'
- @deva[द्रावयति] 'he causes to run'
- @deva[स्रावयति] 'he causes to flow'
standard:
After causatives (@deva[णिच्]) of @deva[बुध्/युध्/नश्/जन्/इङ्/प्रु/द्रु/स्रु], @deva[परस्मैपद] applies—even when fruit accrues to agent.

This overrides @ref[1.3.74].

**Examples:** @deva[बोधयति], @deva[योधयति], @deva[नाशयति], @deva[जनयति], @deva[अध्यापयति], @deva[प्रावयति], @deva[द्रावयति], @deva[स्रावयति].

For intransitive roots, this also applies when the agent lacks will (@deva[अचेतन]): @deva[बोधयति पद्मम्] 'he makes the lotus expand'.
