For roots meaning 'motion', this also applies in non-motion senses: @deva[द्रु] = 'run' and 'melt', @deva[स्रु] = 'flow' and 'trickle'.
advanced: After causatives of @deva[बुध्/युध्/नश्/जन्/इङ्/प्रु/द्रु/स्रु], @deva[परस्मैपद] applies—overriding @ref[1.3.74].

simple:
Causatives of verbs that do not mean 'eating/swallowing' (@deva[निगरण]) or 'moving/shaking' (@deva[चलन]) take @deva[परस्मैपद]—even when the fruit accrues to the agent.

**Examples (no eating/moving sense → @deva[परस्मैपद]):**
- @deva[हारयति] 'he causes to take'

**Eating/moving senses (→ @deva[आत्मनेपद] by @ref[1.3.74]):**
- @deva[निगारयते] 'he causes to swallow'
- @deva[आशयते] 'he causes to eat'
- @deva[भोजयते] 'he feeds'
- @deva[चलयते] 'he causes to move'

@note[type=@[vārtika]]{The root @deva[अद्] 'to eat' is an exception—its causative takes @deva[आत्मनेपद]: @deva[आदयते].}
advanced: Causatives not meaning @deva[निगरण/चलन] take @deva[परस्मैपद]—overriding @ref[1.3.74]. Eating/moving verbs → @deva[आत्मनेपद].

simple:
Causatives of intransitive verbs whose non-causal agent was a sentient being (@deva[चेतन]) take @deva[परस्मैपद]—even when the fruit accrues to the agent.

**Examples:**
- @deva[शेते] (@[ātm].) 'he sleeps' → @deva[शाययति] (para.) 'he puts to sleep'
- @deva[आस्ते] (@[ātm].) 'he sits' → @deva[आसयति] (para.) 'he seats'

When the original agent is non-sentient (@deva[अचेतन]), @deva[आत्मनेपद] applies: @deva[शोषयते व्रीहीन् आतपः] 'the sunshine causes the paddy to dry'.
standard:
After causatives of intransitive verbs with sentient (@deva[चेतन]) original agents, @deva[परस्मैपद] applies—even when fruit accrues to agent.

This overrides @ref[1.3.74].

**Examples:** @deva[शेते] → @deva[शाययति], @deva[आस्ते] → @deva[आसयति].

**Conditions:**
- Original verb must be intransitive (@deva[अकर्मक])
- Original agent must be sentient

If the causative is formed from another causative (not from a simple root), this rule does not apply: @deva[आरोहयते] (not @deva[आरोहयति]).

If the original verb is transitive: @deva[कारयते] (not @deva[कारयति]).

If the original agent is non-sentient: @deva[शोषयते व्रीहीन् आतपः] (not @deva[शोषयति]).
advanced: Causatives of intransitive verbs with @deva[चेतन] original agents take @deva[परस्मैपद]—overriding @ref[1.3.74].

simple:
The causatives of @deva[पा] 'to drink', @deva[दम्] 'to tame', @deva[आयम्] 'to extend', @deva[आयस्] 'to exert', @deva[परिमुह्] 'to be bewildered', @deva[रुच्] 'to shine', @deva[नृत्] 'to dance', @deva[वद्] 'to speak', and @deva[वस्] 'to dwell' do NOT take @deva[परस्मैपद].

**Examples (→ @deva[आत्मनेपद]):**
- @deva[पाययते] 'he gives to drink'
- @deva[दमयते] 'he tames'
- @deva[आयामयते] 'he extends'
- @deva[आयासयते] 'he fatigues'
- @deva[परिमोहयते] 'he bewilders'
- @deva[रोचयते] 'he makes agreeable'
- @deva[नर्तयते] 'he causes to dance'
- @deva[वादयते] 'he makes to speak'
- @deva[वासयते] 'he causes to dwell'

@note[type=@[vārtika]]{@deva[धेट्] should also be included: @deva[धापयते] 'she suckles'.}
advanced: Causatives of @deva[पा/दम्/आयम्/आयस्/परिमुह्/रुच्/नृत्/वद्/वस्] take @deva[आत्मनेपद]—exceptions to @ref[1.3.87]–@ref[1.3.88].

simple:
Denominative verbs formed with the affix @deva[क्यष्] optionally take @deva[परस्मैपद] endings.

**Examples:**
- @deva[लोहितायति] or @deva[लोहितायते] 'it becomes red'
- @deva[पटपटायति] or @deva[पटपटायते] 'it makes a pattering sound'

The affix @deva[क्यष्] is added to words like @deva[लोहित] 'red' by @ref[3.1.12].
standard:
Denominatives formed with @deva[क्यष्] (@ref[3.1.12] @deva[भृशादिभ्यो भावे]) optionally take @deva[परस्मैपद].

**Examples:** @deva[लोहितायति/लोहितायते] 'it becomes red', @deva[पटपटायति/पटपटायते] 'it patters'.
advanced: After denominatives in @deva[क्यष्], @deva[परस्मैपद] is optional.

simple:
The @deva[द्युतादि] roots (22 in number) optionally take @deva[परस्मैपद] endings in the aorist (@deva[लुङ्]).

**Example:** @deva[अद्युतत्] or @deva[अद्योतिष्ट] 'it shone'

In other tenses, these roots are invariably @deva[आत्मनेपदी]: @deva[द्योतते] 'it shines'.

The @deva[द्युतादि] class is a subgroup within the @deva[भ्वादि] class.
standard:
After @deva[द्युतादि] roots (22 roots in the @deva[भ्वादि] class) with @deva[लुङ्] (aorist), @deva[परस्मैपद] is optional.

These roots are @deva[अनुदात्तेत्], so @ref[1.3.12] would normally apply. This @[sūtra] allows @deva[परस्मैपद] optionally in the aorist only.

**Example:** @deva[अद्युतत्] or @deva[अद्योतिष्ट] 'it shone'.

In other tenses → invariably @deva[आत्मनेपद]: @deva[द्योतते] 'it shines'.
advanced: After @deva[द्युतादि] roots with @deva[लुङ्], @deva[परस्मैपद] is optional. In other tenses → @deva[आत्मनेपद] only.

simple:
The @deva[वृतादि] roots (5 in number: @deva[वृत्], @deva[वृध्], @deva[शृध्], @deva[स्यन्द्], @deva[कृप्]) optionally take @deva[परस्मैपद] endings before @deva[स्य] (future/conditional) and @deva[सन्] (desiderative).

**Examples:**
- Future: @deva[वर्त्स्यति] or @deva[वर्तिष्यते] 'he will exist'
- Conditional: @deva[अवर्त्स्यत्] or @deva[अवर्तिष्यत] 'he would have existed'
- Desiderative: @deva[विवृत्सति] or @deva[विवर्तिषते] 'he wishes to exist'

In other tenses, these roots are invariably @deva[आत्मनेपदी]: @deva[वर्तते] 'he exists'.
standard:
After @deva[वृतादि] roots (5 roots within @deva[द्युतादि]) with @deva[स्य] (future/conditional) or @deva[सन्] (desiderative), @deva[परस्मैपद] is optional.

The 5 roots are: @deva[वृत्], @deva[वृध्], @deva[शृध्], @deva[स्यन्द्], @deva[कृप्].

**Examples:**
- Future: @deva[वर्त्स्यति/वर्तिष्यते]
- Conditional: @deva[अवर्त्स्यत्/अवर्तिष्यत]
- Desiderative: @deva[विवृत्सति/विवर्तिषते]

In other tenses → invariably @deva[आत्मनेपद]: @deva[वर्तते].
advanced: After @deva[वृतादि] roots with @deva[स्य/सन्], @deva[परस्मैपद] is optional. In other tenses → @deva[आत्मनेपद] only.

simple:
The root @deva[क्लिप्] 'to be fit' optionally takes @deva[परस्मैपद] endings with @deva[लुट्] (periphrastic future), @deva[स्य] (future/conditional), and @deva[सन्] (desiderative).

**Examples:**
- @deva[लुट्] (periphrastic future): @deva[कल्प्तासि] or @deva[कल्प्तासे] 'you will be fit'
- @deva[स्य] (future): @deva[कल्प्स्यति] or @deva[कल्प्स्यते] 'he will be fit'
- @deva[सन्] (desiderative): @deva[चिक्लिप्सति] or @deva[चिक्लिप्सते] 'he wishes to be fit'

@deva[क्लिप्] is one of the @deva[वृतादि] roots, so @ref[1.3.92] already covers @deva[स्य] and @deva[सन्]. This @[sūtra] adds @deva[लुट्].
standard:
After @deva[क्लिप्], @deva[परस्मैपद] is optional with @deva[लुट्] (periphrastic future), @deva[स्य], and @deva[सन्].

@deva[क्लिप्] is one of the 5 @deva[वृतादि] roots. The previous @[sūtra] covered @deva[स्य] and @deva[सन्]; this adds @deva[लुट्].

**Examples:**
- @deva[लुट्]: @deva[कल्प्तासि/कल्प्तासे]
- @deva[स्य]: @deva[कल्प्स्यति/कल्प्स्यते]
- @deva[सन्]: @deva[चिक्लिप्सति/चिक्लिप्सते]
- Conditional: @deva[अकल्प्स्यत्/अकल्प्स्यत]
advanced: After @deva[क्लिप्], @deva[परस्मैपद] is optional with @deva[लुट्/स्य/सन्]. Extends @ref[1.3.92] by adding @deva[लुट्].

simple:
From this @[sūtra] through @ref[2.2.38], each item receives only one technical name (@deva[संज्ञा]) at a time.

**Example:** A short vowel is called @deva[लघु] 'light' (@ref[1.4.10]). But before a conjunct consonant, it's called @deva[गुरु] 'heavy' (@ref[1.4.11]).

- In @deva[भिद्]: the @deva[इ] is @deva[लघु]
- In @deva[शिक्षा]: the @deva[इ] (before @deva[क्ष]) is @deva[गुरु]—not both names

The @deva[इ] in @deva[शिक्षा] has only one name: @deva[गुरु].
standard:
From here through @ref[2.2.38], each item receives only one @deva[संज्ञा]. When multiple designations could apply, @ref[1.4.2] determines which prevails.

**Example:** @deva[इ] is @deva[लघु] by @ref[1.4.10], but @deva[गुरु] before a conjunct by @ref[1.4.11].

- @deva[भिद्]: @deva[इ] is @deva[लघु]
- @deva[शिक्षा]: @deva[इ] is @deva[गुरु] (before @deva[क्ष])

The @deva[इ] in @deva[शिक्षा] has exactly one name: @deva[गुरु]. Similarly, in @deva[अततक्षत्] (aorist of @deva[तक्ष्]), the @deva[अ] is @deva[गुरु] (before @deva[क्ष]), so @ref[7.4.93] does not apply.
advanced: From here through @ref[2.2.38]: @deva[एकं नाम]—one @deva[संज्ञा] only. Conflicts resolved by @ref[1.4.2].

simple:
When two rules of equal force conflict, the later rule prevails.

@deva[विप्रतिषेध] means 'mutual prohibition'—when two equally strong rules both apply at the same step.

**Example:** @ref[7.3.102] says: long vowel replaces final @deva[अ] before @deva[यञ्]-initial affixes. @ref[7.3.103] says: @deva[ए] replaces final @deva[अ] before @deva[झल्]-initial plural affixes.

Before @deva[भ्यस्] (which is both @deva[यञ्] and @deva[झल्]): @deva[वृक्ष] + @deva[भ्यस्] → @deva[वृक्षेभ्यः] (later rule wins).
standard:
In @deva[विप्रतिषेध] (conflict between equally strong rules), the later rule prevails.

This does NOT apply when rules differ in strength:
- @deva[अपवाद] (exception) defeats @deva[उत्सर्ग] (general)
- @deva[नित्य] (invariable) defeats @deva[अनित्य] (optional)
- @deva[अन्तरङ्ग] (inner) defeats @deva[बहिरङ्ग] (outer)

**Example of equal conflict:** @ref[7.3.102] vs @ref[7.3.103] before @deva[भ्यस्]: @deva[वृक्ष] + @deva[भ्यस्] → @deva[वृक्षेभ्यः] (@ref[7.3.103] wins as later rule).
advanced: In @deva[विप्रतिषेध], the later rule prevails. Unequal conflicts (@deva[अपवाद/उत्सर्ग], @deva[नित्य/अनित्य], @deva[अन्तरङ्ग/बहिरङ्ग]) are resolved by strength, not position.

simple:
Feminine words ending in long @deva[ई] or @deva[ऊ] that denote females by their own form are called @deva[नदी].

**Examples:** @deva[कुमारी] 'maiden', @deva[यवागू] 'rice gruel'

**Effect:** @deva[नदी] words take special declension. By @ref[7.3.112], @deva[आट्] is added before @deva[ङित्] affixes:
- @deva[कुमारी] + @deva[ङे] → @deva[कुमार्यै] (dat. sg.)

Masculine @deva[ई/ऊ]-words are not @deva[नदी]: @deva[ग्रामणी] 'village head' (m.) + @deva[ङे] → @deva[ग्रामण्ये].
standard:
Feminine words ending in @deva[ई/ऊ] that are inherently feminine (not made feminine by an epithet) are called @deva[नदी].

**Examples:** @deva[कुमारी] 'maiden', @deva[यवागू] 'rice gruel'

**Effect:** @deva[नदी] words take @deva[आट्] augment before @deva[ङित्] affixes (@ref[7.3.112]): @deva[कुमारी] + @deva[ङे] → @deva[कुमार्यै]

**Why 'ending in @deva[ई/ऊ]'?** Feminines in other vowels decline differently: @deva[दुहितृ] + @deva[ङे] → @deva[दुहित्रे]

**Why 'denoting females'?** Masculine @deva[ई/ऊ]-words are not @deva[नदी]: @deva[ग्रामणी] (m.) + @deva[ङे] → @deva[ग्रामण्ये]
advanced: Feminine words ending in @deva[ई/ऊ] that are @deva[स्त्र्याख्या] (denote females by form) are @deva[नदी]. Effect: @deva[आट्] before @deva[ङित्] (@ref[7.3.112]).

simple:
Feminine @deva[ई/ऊ]-words that take @deva[इयङ्] or @deva[उवङ्] substitutes are not called @deva[नदी]—except @deva[स्त्री], which is always @deva[नदी].

**Not @deva[नदी]:**
- @deva[श्री] 'prosperity' → voc. sg. @deva[हे श्रीः]
- @deva[भ्रू] 'brow' → voc. sg. @deva[हे भ्रूः]

**Exception—always @deva[नदी]:**
- @deva[स्त्री] 'woman' → voc. sg. @deva[हे स्त्रि]

The @deva[इयङ्/उवङ्] substitutes are ordained by @ref[6.1.77].
standard:
Feminine @deva[ई/ऊ]-words that take @deva[इयङ्/उवङ्] substitutes (@ref[6.1.77]) are not @deva[नदी]—except @deva[स्त्री].

This limits @ref[1.4.3]. Words like @deva[श्री] and @deva[भ्रू] take semivowel substitutes before vowels, so they're excluded from @deva[नदी].

**Examples:**
- @deva[श्री] → voc. sg. @deva[हे श्रीः] (not @deva[नदी]-type)
- @deva[भ्रू] → voc. sg. @deva[हे भ्रूः]

**Exception:** @deva[स्त्री] takes @deva[इयङ्] but remains @deva[नदी]: voc. sg. @deva[हे स्त्रि]
advanced: @deva[इयङ्/उवङ्]-taking @deva[ई/ऊ]-words are not @deva[नदी]—except @deva[स्त्री]. Limits @ref[1.4.3].

simple:
Before the genitive plural @deva[आम्], @deva[ई/ऊ]-words that take @deva[इयङ्/उवङ्] are optionally @deva[नदी]—but @deva[स्त्री] is always @deva[नदी].

**Optional forms:**
- @deva[श्री] + @deva[आम्] → @deva[श्रीणाम्] (as @deva[नदी]) or @deva[श्रियाम्] (with @deva[इयङ्])
- @deva[भ्रू] + @deva[आम्] → @deva[भ्रूणाम्] or @deva[भ्रुवाम्]

**No option for @deva[स्त्री]:**
- @deva[स्त्री] + @deva[आम्] → @deva[स्त्रीणाम्] only
standard:
Before @deva[आम्] (gen. pl.), @deva[इयङ्/उवङ्]-taking @deva[ई/ऊ]-words are optionally @deva[नदी]—but @deva[स्त्री] is always @deva[नदी].

This creates an option for words excluded by @ref[1.4.4].

**Optional:** @deva[श्री] + @deva[आम्] → @deva[श्रीणाम्] or @deva[श्रियाम्]; @deva[भ्रू] + @deva[आम्] → @deva[भ्रूणाम्] or @deva[भ्रुवाम्]

**No option:** @deva[स्त्री] + @deva[आम्] → @deva[स्त्रीणाम्] only
advanced: Before @deva[आम्], @deva[इयङ्/उवङ्]-taking words are optionally @deva[नदी]. @deva[स्त्री] is always @deva[नदी].

simple:
Before @deva[ङित्] affixes, feminine words in short @deva[इ/उ] and @deva[ई/ऊ]-words taking @deva[इयङ्/उवङ्] are optionally @deva[नदी]—but @deva[स्त्री] is always @deva[नदी].

The @deva[ङित्] case-affixes are: dative, ablative, genitive, and @deva[सप्तमी] (locative) singular.

**Optional forms for short-vowel feminines:**
- @deva[मति] (f.) + @deva[ङे] → @deva[मत्यै] (as @deva[नदी]) or @deva[मतये] (as @deva[घि])
- @deva[धेनु] (f.) + @deva[ङे] → @deva[धेन्वै] or @deva[धेनवे]

**Optional for @deva[इयङ्/उवङ्]-words:**
- @deva[श्री] + @deva[ङे] → @deva[श्रियै] or @deva[श्रये]
standard:
Before @deva[ङित्] affixes (dat./abl./gen./loc. sg.), feminine words in short @deva[इ/उ] and @deva[ई/ऊ]-words taking @deva[इयङ्/उवङ्] are optionally @deva[नदी]—but @deva[स्त्री] is always @deva[नदी].

**Short-vowel feminines:**
- @deva[मति] + @deva[ङे] → @deva[मत्यै] or @deva[मतये]
- @deva[धेनु] + @deva[ङे] → @deva[धेन्वै] or @deva[धेनवे]

**@deva[इयङ्/उवङ्]-words:**
- @deva[श्री] + @deva[ङे] → @deva[श्रियै] or @deva[श्रये]
- @deva[भ्रू] + @deva[ङे] → @deva[भ्रुवै] or @deva[भ्रुवे]

**Always @deva[नदी]:** @deva[स्त्री] + @deva[ङे] → @deva[स्त्र्यै] only
advanced: Before @deva[ङित्] affixes, short @deva[इ/उ] feminines and @deva[इयङ्/उवङ्]-words are optionally @deva[नदी]. @deva[स्त्री] is always @deva[नदी].

simple:
Words ending in short @deva[इ] or @deva[उ] that are not @deva[नदी] are called @deva[घि]—except @deva[सखि] 'friend'.

**Effect:** @deva[घि] words take @deva[गुण] before @deva[ङित्] affixes (@ref[7.3.111]):
- @deva[अग्नि] + @deva[ङे] → @deva[अग्नये] 'to fire'
- @deva[वायु] + @deva[ङे] → @deva[वायवे] 'to wind'

**Exception—@deva[सखि]:**
- @deva[सखि] + @deva[ङे] → @deva[सख्ये] (no @deva[गुण])
- @deva[सखि] + @deva[टा] → @deva[सख्या]
standard:
Words ending in short @deva[इ/उ] that are not @deva[नदी] are called @deva[घि]—except @deva[सखि].

**Effect:** @deva[घि] words take @deva[गुण] before @deva[ङित्] affixes (@ref[7.3.111]):
- @deva[अग्नि] + @deva[ङे] → @deva[अग्नये]
- @deva[वायु] + @deva[ङे] → @deva[वायवे]

**Exception—@deva[सखि]:**
- @deva[सखि] + @deva[ङे] → @deva[सख्ये]
- @deva[सखि] + @deva[टा] → @deva[सख्या]
- @deva[सखि] + @deva[ङसि] → @deva[सख्युः]
- @deva[सखि] + @deva[ङि] → @deva[सख्यौ]
advanced: Short @deva[इ/उ]-words not @deva[नदी] are @deva[घि]—except @deva[सखि]. @deva[घि] takes @deva[गुण] before @deva[ङित्] (@ref[7.3.111]).

simple:
The word @deva[पति] 'lord' is @deva[घि] only when in a compound.

**In compound (as @deva[घि]):**
- @deva[प्रजापति] + @deva[ङे] → @deva[प्रजापतये]
- @deva[प्रजापति] + @deva[टा] → @deva[प्रजापतिना]

**Standalone (not @deva[घि]):**
- @deva[पति] + @deva[ङे] → @deva[पत्ये]
- @deva[पति] + @deva[टा] → @deva[पत्या]
standard:
@deva[पति] is @deva[घि] only in compounds. This restricts @ref[1.4.7].

Outside compounds, @deva[पति] declines irregularly:

| Case | In compound | Standalone |
|------|-------------|------------|
| Inst. sg. | @deva[प्रजापतिना] | @deva[पत्या] |
| Dat. sg. | @deva[प्रजापतये] | @deva[पत्ये] |
| Gen. sg. | @deva[प्रजापतेः] | @deva[पत्युः] |
| Loc. sg. | @deva[प्रजापतौ] | @deva[पत्यौ] |
advanced: @deva[पति] is @deva[घि] only in compounds. Restricts @ref[1.4.7].

simple:
In the Vedas, @deva[पति] governed by a genitive is optionally @deva[घि].

**Both forms valid in Vedic:**
- @deva[कुलुञ्चानां पतये] or @deva[पत्ये नमः] 'salutation to the lord of the @[kuluñcas]'

**In classical Sanskrit:**
- Only @deva[पत्ये] (not @deva[घि]): @deva[ग्रामस्य पत्ये]
standard:
In Vedic, @deva[पति] with a genitive dependent is optionally @deva[घि]. This excepts @ref[1.4.8] for Vedic usage.

**Vedic (both valid):** @deva[कुलुञ्चानां पतये] or @deva[पत्ये]

**Classical (no option):** @deva[ग्रामस्य पत्ये] only
advanced: In Vedic, @deva[पति] with genitive is optionally @deva[घि]. Excepts @ref[1.4.8] for Vedic.

simple:
A short vowel (@deva[ह्रस्व]) is called @deva[लघु] 'light'.

**Effect:** @deva[लघु] penultimate vowels take @deva[गुण] before @deva[सार्वधातुक/आर्धधातुक] affixes (@ref[7.3.86]):
- @deva[भिद्] + @deva[तृच्] → @deva[भेत्तृ] 'cutter' (not *@deva[भित्तृ])
- @deva[छिद्] + @deva[तृच्] → @deva[छेत्तृ]

The @deva[इ] in @deva[भिद्] is @deva[लघु], so it takes @deva[गुण].
standard:
A short vowel is called @deva[लघु] 'light'.

**Effect:** @deva[लघु] penultimate vowels take @deva[गुण] before @deva[सार्वधातुक/आर्धधातुक] affixes (@ref[7.3.86]):
- @deva[भिद्] + @deva[तृच्] → @deva[भेत्तृ]
- @deva[छिद्] + @deva[तृच्] → @deva[छेत्तृ]
- @deva[अचीकरत्], @deva[अजीहरत्] (reduplicated aorist)
advanced: A short vowel is @deva[लघु]. Effect: @deva[गुण] before @deva[सार्वधातुक/आर्धधातुक] (@ref[7.3.86]).

simple:
A short vowel followed by a conjunct consonant (@deva[संयोग]) is called @deva[गुरु] 'heavy'.

This overrides @ref[1.4.10]—the vowel is no longer @deva[लघु].

**Example:** In @deva[शिक्षा] 'learning', the @deva[इ] precedes @deva[क्ष] (a conjunct), so @deva[इ] is @deva[गुरु], not @deva[लघु].

**Effect on affix selection:** @ref[3.3.103] ordains @deva[अ] after roots with @deva[गुरु] penultimate:
- @deva[शिक्ष्] + @deva[अ] → @deva[शिक्षा]
- @deva[भिक्ष्] + @deva[अ] → @deva[भिक्षा]
standard:
A short vowel followed by a @deva[संयोग] (conjunct consonant) is @deva[गुरु] 'heavy'. This overrides @ref[1.4.10].

**Example:** In @deva[शिक्षा], the @deva[इ] precedes @deva[क्ष] (conjunct), so it's @deva[गुरु].

**Effect:** @ref[3.3.103] ordains @deva[अ] after roots with @deva[गुरु] penultimate: @deva[शिक्षा], @deva[भिक्षा], @deva[कुण्डा].
advanced: Short vowel + @deva[संयोग] = @deva[गुरु]. Overrides @ref[1.4.10].

simple:
A long vowel (@deva[दीर्घ]) is also called @deva[गुरु] 'heavy'.

**Summary of vowel weight:**
- Short vowel (no following conjunct) → @deva[लघु]
- Short vowel + conjunct → @deva[गुरु]
- Long vowel → @deva[गुरु]

**Example:** @deva[ईक्ष्] + @deva[लिट्] → @deva[ईक्षांचक्रे] 'he saw'. The @deva[ई] is @deva[गुरु], triggering @deva[आम्] augment in the perfect (@ref[3.1.36]).
standard:
A long vowel is @deva[गुरु] 'heavy'.

**Summary:**
- Short (no conjunct) → @deva[लघु]
- Short + conjunct → @deva[गुरु]
- Long → @deva[गुरु]

**Example:** @deva[ई] in @deva[ईक्ष्] is @deva[गुरु], so @ref[3.1.36] triggers @deva[आम्] in perfect: @deva[ईक्षांचक्रे], @deva[ईहांचक्रे].
advanced: Long vowel = @deva[गुरु]. Effect: @deva[आम्] augment in perfect for @deva[गुरु]-initial roots (@ref[3.1.36]).

simple:
Whatever stands before an affix is called @deva[अङ्ग] 'base'.

An @deva[अङ्ग] is defined relative to a following affix—whether verbal or nominal.

**Examples:**
- @deva[पच्] before @deva[ति] → @deva[पच्] is @deva[अङ्ग]
- @deva[राम] before @deva[सु] → @deva[राम] is @deva[अङ्ग]
- @deva[कारक] before @deva[अण्] → @deva[कारक] is @deva[अङ्ग]

Operations in the @deva[अङ्गाधिकार] (@ref[6.4.1] onward) affect the base before its affix.
standard:
Whatever stands before an affix (@deva[प्रत्यय]) is called @deva[अङ्ग] 'base'.

**Examples:**
- @deva[पच्] before @deva[ति] → @deva[पच्] is @deva[अङ्ग]
- @deva[राम] before @deva[सु] → @deva[राम] is @deva[अङ्ग]
- @deva[कारक] before @deva[अण्] → @deva[कारक] is @deva[अङ्ग]

The @deva[अङ्गाधिकार] (@ref[6.4.1]–@ref[7.4.97]) prescribes operations on @deva[अङ्ग]s before their affixes.
advanced: Whatever precedes an affix is @deva[अङ्ग]. @deva[अङ्गाधिकार] (@ref[6.4.1]–@ref[7.4.97]) governs @deva[अङ्ग]-operations.

simple:
A word ending in a @deva[सुप्] (case-affix) or @deva[तिङ्] (verb-affix) is called @deva[पद] 'complete word'.

@deva[सुप्] covers all 21 nominal endings (@deva[सु], @deva[औ], @deva[जस्], etc.). @deva[तिङ्] covers all 18 verbal endings (@deva[तिप्], @deva[तस्], @deva[झि], etc.).

**Examples:**
- @deva[राम] + @deva[सु] → @deva[रामः] (@deva[पद])
- @deva[गम्] + @deva[तिप्] → @deva[गच्छति] (@deva[पद])

A @deva[पद] is a complete, inflected unit ready for use in a sentence.
standard:
A word ending in @deva[सुप्] or @deva[तिङ्] is @deva[पद].

The explicit mention of @deva[अन्त] 'ending in' is significant. It implies (@deva[ज्ञापक]) that @ref[1.1.72] (@deva[तदन्त] principle) does not automatically extend @deva[संज्ञा] definitions to words containing those affixes anywhere.

**@deva[प्रत्याहार]s:**
- @deva[सुप्] = 21 case-affixes (@deva[सु], @deva[औ], @deva[जस्]...@deva[सुप्])
- @deva[तिङ्] = 18 verb-affixes (@deva[तिप्], @deva[तस्], @deva[झि]...@deva[महिङ्])

**Examples:** @deva[रामः], @deva[गच्छति], @deva[फलानि].
advanced: @deva[सुब्तिङन्तं पदम्]: what ends in @deva[सुप्] or @deva[तिङ्] is @deva[पद]. The @deva[ज्ञापक] from @deva[अन्त] indicates @ref[1.1.72] does not extend @deva[संज्ञा] definitions. @deva[पद]-operations governed by @ref[8.1.16]ff.

simple:
A word ending in @deva[न्] is called @deva[पद] when followed by @deva[क्यच्], @deva[क्यङ्], or @deva[क्यष्].

These affixes form denominative verbs (@deva[नामधातु]) from nouns.

**Examples:**
- @deva[राजन्] + @deva[क्यच्] → @deva[राजीयति] 'he acts like a king'
- @deva[राजन्] + @deva[क्यङ्] → @deva[राजायते] 'he wishes to be a king'

Because @deva[राजन्] is @deva[पद], the @deva[न्] undergoes @deva[पद]-final changes.
standard:
A @deva[न्]-final word is @deva[पद] before @deva[क्य]-initial affixes (@deva[क्यच्], @deva[क्यङ्], @deva[क्यष्]).

These denominative affixes form verbs from nouns.

**Examples:**
- @deva[राजन्] + @deva[क्यच्] → @deva[राजीयति]
- @deva[राजन्] + @deva[क्यङ्] → @deva[राजायते]
- @deva[चर्मन्] + @deva[क्यष्] → @deva[चर्मायते]

As @deva[पद], the @deva[न्] undergoes @deva[पद]-final operations (@ref[8.2.7], @ref[8.4.37]).
advanced: @deva[न्]-final = @deva[पद] before @deva[क्यच्], @deva[क्यङ्], @deva[क्यष्]. Effect: @deva[पद]-final operations apply. @deva[राजन्] + @deva[क्यच्] → @deva[राजीयति].

simple:
Before an affix marked with indicatory @deva[स्] (@deva[सित्]), what precedes is called @deva[पद].

**Example:**
- @deva[भवत्] + @deva[छस्] → @deva[भवदीयः] 'belonging to you'

The @deva[स्] in @deva[छस्] is indicatory (@ref[4.2.115]). Since @deva[भवत्] is @deva[पद], @deva[पद]-final rules apply.
standard:
Before a @deva[सित्] affix (one with indicatory @deva[स्]), the preceding word is @deva[पद].

This is an exception to @ref[1.4.18] (which would make it @deva[भ]).

**Example:** @deva[भवत्] + @deva[छस्] (@ref[4.2.115]) → @deva[भवदीयः] 'belonging to you'. The indicatory @deva[स्] triggers @deva[पद] status, so @ref[7.1.2] applies.
advanced: Before @deva[सित्] affixes, the preceding word is @deva[पद]. Exception to @ref[1.4.18]. @deva[भवत्] + @deva[छस्] → @deva[भवदीयः].

simple:
Before affixes ranging from @deva[सु] to @deva[कप्]—excluding @deva[सर्वनामस्थान] affixes—what precedes is called @deva[पद].

This covers most case-affixes and @deva[तद्धित] affixes from chapters 4–5.

**Examples:**
- @deva[राजन्] + @deva[भ्याम्] → @deva[राजभ्याम्] (@deva[न्] drops as @deva[पद]-final)
- @deva[राजन्] + @deva[भिस्] → @deva[राजभिः]

**Why exclude @deva[सर्वनामस्थान]?** Before those, @deva[न्] is retained: @deva[राजन्] + @deva[सु] → @deva[राजा] (not *@deva[राजः]).
standard:
Before affixes from @deva[सु] (@ref[4.1.2]) to @deva[कप्] (@ref[5.4.151]), excluding @deva[सर्वनामस्थान], what precedes is @deva[पद].

**Examples:**
- @deva[राजन्] + @deva[भ्याम्] → @deva[राजभ्याम्]
- @deva[राजन्] + @deva[त्व] → @deva[राजत्वम्]

**Exception for @deva[सर्वनामस्थान]:** @deva[राजन्] + @deva[सु] → @deva[राजा] (not *@deva[राजः])—@deva[न्] retained.
advanced: Before @deva[सु]–@deva[कप्] affixes (excluding @deva[सर्वनामस्थान]), what precedes is @deva[पद]. @deva[राजन्] + @deva[भ्याम्] → @deva[राजभ्याम्]. Exception: @deva[सर्वनामस्थान] affixes.

simple:
When such an affix (from @deva[सु] to @deva[कप्], excluding @deva[सर्वनामस्थान]) begins with @deva[य्] or a vowel, what precedes is called @deva[भ]—not @deva[पद].

**Examples:**
- @deva[गर्ग] + @deva[यञ्] → @deva[गार्ग्यः] 'descendant of Garga'
- @deva[दक्ष] + @deva[इञ्] → @deva[दाक्षिः]

As @deva[भ], the final @deva[अ] is elided by @ref[6.4.148].
standard:
Before @deva[य्]-initial or vowel-initial affixes (within @deva[सु]–@deva[कप्], excluding @deva[सर्वनामस्थान]), what precedes is @deva[भ]. This overrides @deva[पद] from @ref[1.4.17].

**Examples:**
- @deva[गर्ग] + @deva[यञ्] → @deva[गार्ग्यः] (final @deva[अ] elided by @ref[6.4.148])
- @deva[दक्ष] + @deva[इञ्] → @deva[दाक्षिः]
advanced: Before @deva[य्]/vowel-initial affixes (within @deva[सु]–@deva[कप्], non-@deva[सर्वनामस्थान]), what precedes is @deva[भ]. Overrides @ref[1.4.17]. @deva[गर्ग] + @deva[यञ्] → @deva[गार्ग्यः].

simple:
A word ending in @deva[त्] or @deva[स्] is called @deva[भ] when a @deva[मत्वर्थीय] affix (possessive suffix) follows.

**Examples:**
- @deva[विद्युत्] + @deva[वतुप्] → @deva[विद्युत्वान्] 'having lightning'
- @deva[यशस्] + @deva[विनि] → @deva[यशस्वी] 'famous'
- @deva[पयस्] + @deva[विनि] → @deva[पयस्वी] 'milky'

As @deva[भ], the @deva[त्/स्] is retained before the affix.
standard:
A @deva[त्]/@deva[स्]-final word is @deva[भ] before @deva[मत्वर्थीय] affixes (@ref[5.2.94]ff).

**Examples:**
- @deva[उदश्वित्] + @deva[वतुप्] → @deva[उदश्वित्वान्] 'having buttermilk'
- @deva[विद्युत्] + @deva[वतुप्] → @deva[विद्युत्वान्]
- @deva[यशस्] + @deva[विनि] → @deva[यशस्वी]

As @deva[भ], the final consonant is preserved.
advanced: @deva[त्]/@deva[स्]-final = @deva[भ] before @deva[मत्वर्थीय] affixes. @deva[विद्युत्] + @deva[वतुप्] → @deva[विद्युत्वान्]. @deva[यशस्] + @deva[विनि] → @deva[यशस्वी].

simple:
Forms like @deva[अयस्मय] are valid in Vedic usage.

Normally, @deva[अयस्] + @deva[मयट्] → @deva[अयोमयम्] 'made of iron' (the @deva[स्] becomes @deva[ओ] by @deva[पद]-rules). But in Vedic, @deva[अयस्] is treated as @deva[भ], preserving @deva[स्]:

- @deva[अयस्मयं वर्म] 'iron armor'
- @deva[अयस्मयानि पात्राणि] 'iron vessels'
standard:
In Vedic, @deva[अयस्मय] and similar forms are valid.

Normally @deva[अयस्] + @deva[मयट्] → @deva[अयोमयम्] (@deva[स्] → @deva[ओ] by @deva[पद]-final rules). But Vedic treats @deva[अयस्] as @deva[भ], preserving @deva[स्]:

- @deva[अयस्मयं वर्म] 'iron armor'
- @deva[अयस्मयानि पात्राणि] 'iron vessels'
advanced: Vedic: @deva[अयस्मय] valid. Normally @deva[अयस्] + @deva[मयट्] → @deva[अयोमयम्] (@deva[पद]-rules). Vedic treats @deva[अयस्] as @deva[भ], preserving @deva[स्].
@deva[बहुवचन] expresses multiplicity. @deva[ब्राह्मणाः पठन्ति]. @deva[अव्यय]s have no number distinction.

simple:
@deva[द्विवचन] (dual) affixes denote two. @deva[एकवचन] (singular) affixes denote one.

**Examples:**
- @deva[ब्राह्मणौ पचतः] 'the two Brahmins cook'
- @deva[ब्राह्मणः पचति] 'the Brahmin cooks'
standard:
@deva[द्विवचन] (Dual) affixes express duality; singular affixes express unity.

**Examples:**
- @deva[ब्राह्मणौ पचतः] 'the two Brahmins cook'
- @deva[ब्राह्मणः पचति] 'the Brahmin cooks'
advanced: @deva[द्विवचन] = duality; @deva[एकवचन] = unity. @deva[ब्राह्मणौ पचतः], @deva[ब्राह्मणः पचति].

simple:
The term @deva[कारक] 'case-relation' applies from here onward.

A @deva[कारक] is anything that contributes to accomplishing an action. There are six @deva[कारक]s:

| @deva[कारक] | Role | Example |
|--------|------|--------|
| @deva[कर्तृ] | Agent | @[rāma] |
| @deva[कर्मन्] | Object | food |
| @deva[करण] | Instrument | fire |
| @deva[सम्प्रदान] | Recipient | master |
| @deva[अपादान] | Source | furnace |
| @deva[अधिकरण] | Locus | vessel |
standard:
The @deva[कारकाधिकार] begins here. A @deva[कारक] is a participant that contributes to accomplishing an action.

**Six @deva[कारक]s:**
- @deva[कर्तृ] (agent)
- @deva[कर्मन्] (object)
- @deva[करण] (instrument)
- @deva[सम्प्रदान] (recipient)
- @deva[अपादान] (source)
- @deva[अधिकरण] (locus)

Each @deva[कारक] is defined in the following @[sūtras].
advanced: @deva[कारकाधिकार] begins. Six @deva[कारक]s: @deva[कर्तृ], @deva[कर्मन्], @deva[करण], @deva[सम्प्रदान], @deva[अपादान], @deva[अधिकरण]. Defined in @ref[1.4.24]–@ref[1.4.55].

simple:
The fixed point from which departure occurs is called @deva[अपादान] 'ablative source'.

**Examples:**
- @deva[ग्रामादागच्छति] 'he comes from the village'
- @deva[पर्वतादवरोहति] 'he descends from the mountain'
- @deva[रथात् पतितः] 'fallen from the chariot'

@deva[अपादान] takes the 5th case-affix (@deva[पञ्चमी]).
standard:
The fixed point from which departure occurs is @deva[अपादान].

**Examples:**
- @deva[ग्रामादागच्छति] 'he comes from the village'
- @deva[पर्वतादवरोहति] 'he descends from the mountain'

@deva[अपादान] takes @deva[पञ्चमी] (@ref[2.3.28]).
advanced: Fixed point of departure = @deva[अपादान]. Takes @deva[पञ्चमी] (@ref[2.3.28]). @deva[ग्रामादागच्छति], @deva[पर्वतादवरोहति].

simple:
With verbs of fear or protection, the source of danger is @deva[अपादान].

**Fear:**
- @deva[चौरेभ्यो बिभेति] 'he fears thieves'
- @deva[चौरेभ्य उद्विजते] 'he is agitated by thieves'

**Protection:**
- @deva[चौरेभ्यस्त्रायते] 'he protects from thieves'

Note: The place of fear uses locative: @deva[अरण्ये बिभेति] 'he fears in the forest'.
standard:
With verbs of fear (@deva[भी]) or protection (@deva[त्रै], @deva[रक्ष्]), the source of danger is @deva[अपादान].

**Examples:**
- @deva[चौरेभ्यो बिभेति] 'he fears thieves'
- @deva[चौरेभ्यस्त्रायते] 'he protects from thieves'

The place of fear takes locative: @deva[अरण्ये बिभेति].
advanced: With @deva[भी]/protection verbs, source of danger = @deva[अपादान]. @deva[चौरेभ्यो बिभेति]. Place of fear = locative.

simple:
With @deva[परा] + @deva[जि] meaning 'to find unbearable', what becomes unbearable is @deva[अपादान].

**Example:** @deva[अध्ययनात् पराजयते] 'he finds study unbearable'

Note: In the sense of 'defeat', the object is @deva[कर्म]: @deva[शत्रून् पराजयते] 'he defeats the enemies' (accusative).
standard:
With @deva[परा] + @deva[जि] 'to find unbearable', what becomes unbearable is @deva[अपादान].

**Example:** @deva[अध्ययनात् पराजयते] 'he finds study unbearable'

In the sense of 'defeat', the object is @deva[कर्म]: @deva[शत्रून् पराजयते] (accusative).
advanced: @deva[परा] + @deva[जि] 'find unbearable': what is unbearable = @deva[अपादान]. @deva[अध्ययनात् पराजयते]. 'Defeat' sense: @deva[कर्म].

simple:
With verbs of prevention, the desired object from which one is warded off is @deva[अपादान].

**Examples:**
- @deva[यवेभ्यो गां वारयति] 'he wards the cow off from the barley'
- @deva[यवेभ्यो गां निवर्तयति] 'he turns the cow back from the barley'

The place of prevention uses locative: @deva[गां वारयति क्षेत्रे] 'he wards off the cow in the field'.
standard:
With verbs of prevention (@deva[वृ], @deva[निवृत्]), the desired object from which one is blocked is @deva[अपादान].

**Examples:**
- @deva[यवेभ्यो गां वारयति] 'he wards the cow off from the barley'
- @deva[यवेभ्यो गां निवर्तयति]

Place of prevention = locative.
advanced: Prevention verbs: desired object = @deva[अपादान]. @deva[यवेभ्यो गां वारयति]. Place = locative.

simple:
With verbs of concealment, the person from whose sight one hides is @deva[अपादान].

**Examples:**
- @deva[उपाध्यायादन्तर्धत्ते] 'he hides from the teacher'
- @deva[उपाध्यायान्निलीयते] 'he conceals himself from the teacher'

Note: With verbs of not-wishing-to-see, the object is accusative: @deva[चौरान् न दिदृक्षते] 'he does not wish to see the thieves'.
standard:
With concealment verbs (@deva[अन्तर्धा], @deva[निली]), the person from whose sight one hides is @deva[अपादान].

**Examples:**
- @deva[उपाध्यायादन्तर्धत्ते] 'he hides from the teacher'
- @deva[उपाध्यायान्निलीयते]

Not-wishing-to-see takes accusative: @deva[चौरान् न दिदृक्षते].
advanced: Concealment verbs: person avoided = @deva[अपादान]. @deva[उपाध्यायादन्तर्धत्ते]. Not-wishing-to-see: accusative.

simple:
With verbs of formal learning, the teacher is @deva[अपादान].

**Example:** @deva[उपाध्यायादधीते] 'he learns from the teacher'

Note: With simple hearing, the source takes genitive: @deva[नटस्य शृणोति] 'he hears the actor'.
standard:
With formal learning verbs (@deva[अधि] + @deva[इ]), the teacher is @deva[अपादान].

**Examples:**
- @deva[उपाध्यायादधीते] 'he learns from the teacher'
- @deva[उपाध्यायादागमयति] 'he acquires knowledge from the teacher'

Simple hearing takes genitive: @deva[नटस्य शृणोति].
advanced: Formal learning: teacher = @deva[अपादान]. @deva[उपाध्यायादधीते]. Simple hearing: genitive.

simple:
With @deva[जन्] 'to be born', the material source is @deva[अपादान].

**Examples:**
- @deva[शृङ्गाच्छरो जायते] 'the arrow is produced from horn'
- @deva[गोमयाद् वृश्चिको जायते] 'the scorpion is produced from cowdung'
standard:
With @deva[जन्] 'to be born', the material cause of the product is @deva[अपादान].

**Examples:**
- @deva[शृङ्गाच्छरो जायते] 'the arrow is produced from horn'
- @deva[गोमयाद् वृश्चिको जायते] 'the scorpion is produced from cowdung'
advanced: @deva[जन्]: material source = @deva[अपादान]. @deva[शृङ्गाच्छरो जायते], @deva[गोमयाद् वृश्चिको जायते].

simple:
With @deva[भू] 'to become/originate', the source is @deva[अपादान].

**Examples:**
- @deva[हिमवतो गङ्गा प्रभवति] 'the Ganges originates from the Himalayas'
- @deva[काश्मीरेभ्यो वितस्ता प्रभवति] 'the @[vitastā] (Jhelum) originates from Kashmir'
standard:
With @deva[भू] 'to become/originate', the source is @deva[अपादान].

**Examples:**
- @deva[हिमवतो गङ्गा प्रभवति] 'the Ganges has its source in the Himalayas'
- @deva[काश्मीरेभ्यो वितस्ता प्रभवति]
advanced: @deva[भू]: source = @deva[अपादान]. @deva[हिमवतो गङ्गा प्रभवति].

simple:
The person one wishes to connect with the object of giving is @deva[सम्प्रदान] 'recipient'.

**Examples:**
- @deva[उपाध्यायाय गां ददाति] 'he gives the cow to the teacher'
- @deva[माणवकाय भिक्षां ददाति] 'he gives alms to the boy'

@deva[सम्प्रदान] takes the 4th case-affix (@deva[चतुर्थी]).
standard:
The person one wishes to connect with the object of giving is @deva[सम्प्रदान].

**Examples:**
- @deva[उपाध्यायाय गां ददाति] 'he gives the cow to the teacher'
- @deva[माणवकाय भिक्षां ददाति] 'he gives alms to the boy'

@deva[सम्प्रदान] takes @deva[चतुर्थी] (@ref[2.3.13]).
advanced: Person intended to receive gift = @deva[सम्प्रदान]. Takes @deva[चतुर्थी] (@ref[2.3.13]). @deva[उपाध्यायाय गां ददाति].

simple:
With verbs of liking (@deva[रुच्]), the one pleased is @deva[सम्प्रदान].

**Examples:**
- @deva[देवदत्ताय रोचते मोदकः] 'the sweet pleases Devadatta'
- @deva[यज्ञदत्ताय स्वदते अपूपः] '@[yajñadatta] likes the cake'
standard:
With @deva[रुच्]-sense verbs ('to please'), the one pleased is @deva[सम्प्रदान].

**Examples:**
- @deva[देवदत्ताय रोचते मोदकः] 'the sweet pleases Devadatta'
- @deva[यज्ञदत्ताय स्वदते अपूपः]
advanced: @deva[रुच्]-sense: one pleased = @deva[सम्प्रदान]. @deva[देवदत्ताय रोचते मोदकः].

simple:
With @deva[श्लाघ्] 'praise', @deva[ह्नु] 'conceal', @deva[स्था] 'resolve', @deva[शप्] 'curse'—the person one intends to inform is @deva[सम्प्रदान].

**Examples:**
- @deva[देवदत्ताय श्लाघते] 'he praises (intending Devadatta to know)'
- @deva[देवदत्ताय शपते] 'he curses (for Devadatta to hear)'
standard:
With @deva[श्लाघ्], @deva[ह्नु], @deva[स्था], @deva[शप्]—the person one intends to inform is @deva[सम्प्रदान].

**Examples:**
- @deva[देवदत्ताय श्लाघते] 'he praises (for Devadatta's knowledge)'
- @deva[देवदत्ताय ह्नुते] 'he conceals (from Devadatta)'
- @deva[देवदत्ताय शपते] 'he curses'
advanced: @deva[श्लाघ्], @deva[ह्नु], @deva[स्था], @deva[शप्]: person to be informed = @deva[सम्प्रदान]. @deva[देवदत्ताय श्लाघते].

simple:
With @deva[धारय्] 'to owe', the creditor is @deva[सम्प्रदान].

**Example:** @deva[देवदत्ताय शतं धारयति] 'he owes a hundred to Devadatta'

The place of owing takes locative: @deva[ग्रामे शतं धारयति] 'he owes a hundred in the village'.
standard:
With @deva[धारय्] 'to owe', the creditor (@deva[उत्तमर्ण]) is @deva[सम्प्रदान].

**Example:** @deva[देवदत्ताय शतं धारयति] 'he owes a hundred to Devadatta'

Place of owing = @deva[सप्तमी] (locative).
advanced: @deva[धारय्] 'to owe': creditor = @deva[सम्प्रदान]. @deva[देवदत्ताय शतं धारयति]. Place = locative.

simple:
With @deva[स्पृह्] 'to desire', the thing desired is @deva[सम्प्रदान].

**Examples:**
- @deva[पुष्पेभ्यः स्पृहयति] 'he desires flowers'
- @deva[फलेभ्यः स्पृहयति] 'he desires fruits'

The place of desiring takes locative: @deva[वने पुष्पेभ्यः स्पृहयति] 'he desires flowers in the forest'.
standard:
With @deva[स्पृह्] 'to desire', the thing desired is @deva[सम्प्रदान].

**Examples:**
- @deva[पुष्पेभ्यः स्पृहयति] 'he desires flowers'
- @deva[फलेभ्यः स्पृहयति] 'he desires fruits'

Place of desiring = locative.
advanced: @deva[स्पृह्] 'to desire': thing desired = @deva[सम्प्रदान]. @deva[पुष्पेभ्यः स्पृहयति]. Place = locative.

simple:
With verbs of anger (@deva[क्रुध्]), malice (@deva[द्रुह्]), envy (@deva[ईर्ष्य]), fault-finding (@deva[असूय])—the target is @deva[सम्प्रदान].

**Examples:**
- @deva[देवदत्ताय क्रुध्यति] 'he is angry at Devadatta'
- @deva[देवदत्ताय द्रुह्यति] 'he bears malice toward Devadatta'
- @deva[देवदत्ताय ईर्ष्यति] 'he envies Devadatta'
- @deva[देवदत्ताय असूयति] 'he finds fault with Devadatta'
standard:
With @deva[क्रुध्], @deva[द्रुह्], @deva[ईर्ष्य], @deva[असूय]—the target of the feeling is @deva[सम्प्रदान].

**Examples:**
- @deva[देवदत्ताय क्रुध्यति] 'he is angry at Devadatta'
- @deva[देवदत्ताय द्रुह्यति] 'he bears malice'
- @deva[देवदत्ताय ईर्ष्यति] 'he envies'
- @deva[देवदत्ताय असूयति] 'he finds fault'
advanced: @deva[क्रुध्], @deva[द्रुह्], @deva[ईर्ष्य], @deva[असूय]: target = @deva[सम्प्रदान]. @deva[देवदत्ताय क्रुध्यति].

simple:
But @deva[क्रुध्] and @deva[द्रुह्] with @deva[उपसर्ग]s take the target as @deva[कर्म] (accusative).

**Examples:**
- @deva[देवदत्तमभिक्रुध्यति] 'he is angry at Devadatta'
- @deva[देवदत्तमभिद्रुह्यति] 'he bears malice toward Devadatta'

Without @deva[उपसर्ग], dative applies by @ref[1.4.37].
standard:
@deva[क्रुध्] and @deva[द्रुह्] with @deva[उपसर्ग]s take the target as @deva[कर्म] (accusative). Exception to @ref[1.4.37].

**Examples:**
- @deva[देवदत्तमभिक्रुध्यति] 'he is angry at Devadatta'
- @deva[देवदत्तमभिद्रुह्यति]

Without @deva[उपसर्ग]: dative (@ref[1.4.37]).
advanced: @deva[क्रुध्]/@deva[द्रुह्] + @deva[उपसर्ग]: target = @deva[कर्म]. Exception to @ref[1.4.37]. @deva[देवदत्तमभिक्रुध्यति].

simple:
With @deva[राध्] 'propitiate' and @deva[ईक्ष्] 'examine' in fortune-telling, the person whose fortune is told is @deva[सम्प्रदान].

**Examples:**
- @deva[देवदत्ताय राध्यति गर्गः] 'Garga is favorable to Devadatta'
- @deva[देवदत्ताय ईक्षते] 'he examines Devadatta's fortune'
standard:
With @deva[राध्] and @deva[ईक्ष्] in fortune-telling sense, the person whose fortune is examined is @deva[सम्प्रदान].

**Examples:**
- @deva[देवदत्ताय राध्यति गर्गः] 'Garga is favorable to Devadatta'
- @deva[देवदत्ताय ईक्षते]
advanced: @deva[राध्]/@deva[ईक्ष्] (fortune-telling): person = @deva[सम्प्रदान]. @deva[देवदत्ताय राध्यति गर्गः].

simple:
With @deva[प्रति] + @deva[श्रु] or @deva[आ] + @deva[श्रु] meaning 'to promise', the promisee is @deva[सम्प्रदान].

**Examples:**
- @deva[देवदत्ताय प्रतिशृणोति] 'he promises Devadatta'
- @deva[देवदत्ताय आशृणोति] 'he promises Devadatta'

The recipient of the promise is the one who initiated the request.
standard:
With @deva[प्रति]/@ + @deva[श्रु] 'to promise', the person who first requested (prior agent) is @deva[सम्प्रदान].

**Examples:**
- @deva[देवदत्ताय प्रतिशृणोति] 'he promises Devadatta'
- @deva[देवदत्ताय आशृणोति]
advanced: @deva[प्रति]/@deva[आङ्] + @deva[श्रु] 'to promise': promisee = @deva[सम्प्रदान]. @deva[देवदत्ताय प्रतिशृणोति].

simple:
With @deva[अनु] + @deva[गॄ] or @deva[प्रति] + @deva[गॄ] meaning 'to encourage by repeating', the prior agent is @deva[सम्प्रदान].

**Examples:**
- @deva[होत्रे अनुगृणाति] 'they encourage the @[hotṛ] (by repeating his invocation)'
- @deva[होत्रे प्रतिगृणाति] 'they respond to the @[hotṛ]'
standard:
With @deva[अनु]/@deva[प्रति] + @deva[गॄ] 'to encourage by repeating', the prior agent is @deva[सम्प्रदान].

**Examples:**
- @deva[होत्रे अनुगृणाति] 'they encourage the @[hotṛ]'
- @deva[होत्रे प्रतिगृणाति] 'they respond to the @[hotṛ]'
advanced: @deva[अनु]/@deva[प्रति] + @deva[गॄ]: prior agent = @deva[सम्प्रदान]. @deva[होत्रे अनुगृणाति].

simple:
The most effective auxiliary in accomplishing an action is @deva[करण] 'instrument'.

**Examples:**
- @deva[दात्रेण लुनाति] 'he cuts with the sickle'
- @deva[परशुना छिनत्ति] 'he splits with the axe'

@deva[करण] takes the 3rd case-affix (@deva[तृतीया]).
standard:
That which is most instrumental in accomplishing the action is @deva[करण].

**Examples:**
- @deva[दात्रेण लुनाति] 'he cuts with the sickle'
- @deva[परशुना छिनत्ति] 'he splits with the axe'

@deva[करण] takes @deva[तृतीया] (@ref[2.3.18]).
advanced: Most effective auxiliary = @deva[करण]. Takes @deva[तृतीया] (@ref[2.3.18]). @deva[दात्रेण लुनाति], @deva[परशुना छिनत्ति].

simple:
With @deva[दिव्] 'to play', the instrument is optionally @deva[कर्म] (accusative).

**Examples:**
- @deva[अक्षान् दीव्यति] 'he plays dice' (accusative)
- @deva[अक्षैर् दीव्यति] 'he plays with dice' (instrumental)

Both forms are valid.
standard:
With @deva[दिव्] 'to play', the instrument is optionally @deva[कर्म].

**Examples:**
- @deva[अक्षान् दीव्यति] (accusative)
- @deva[अक्षैर् दीव्यति] (instrumental)
advanced: @deva[दिव्] 'to play': instrument optionally = @deva[कर्म]. @deva[अक्षान् दीव्यति] or @deva[अक्षैर् दीव्यति].

simple:
With @deva[परि] + @deva[क्री] 'to hire', the wages are optionally @deva[सम्प्रदान].

**Examples:**
- @deva[शताय परिक्रीतः] 'hired for a hundred' (dative)
- @deva[शतेन परिक्रीतः] 'hired for a hundred' (instrumental)

This applies to hiring for wages, not outright purchase.
standard:
With @deva[परि] + @deva[क्री] 'to hire', the wages are optionally @deva[सम्प्रदान].

**Examples:**
- @deva[शताय परिक्रीतः] (dative)
- @deva[शतेन परिक्रीतः] (instrumental)

Applies to hiring, not purchase.
advanced: @deva[परि] + @deva[क्री] 'to hire': wages optionally = @deva[सम्प्रदान]. @deva[शताय परिक्रीतः] or @deva[शतेन परिक्रीतः].

simple:
The location where the action occurs (via agent or object being there) is @deva[अधिकरण] 'locus'.

**Examples:**
- @deva[कटे आस्ते] 'he sits on the mat'
- @deva[कटे शेते] 'he lies on the mat'
- @deva[स्थाल्यां पचति] 'he cooks in the pot'

@deva[अधिकरण] takes the 7th case-affix (@deva[सप्तमी]).
standard:
The site where an action is supported—by virtue of the agent or object being there—is @deva[अधिकरण].

**Examples:**
- @deva[कटे आस्ते] 'he sits on the mat'
- @deva[स्थाल्यां पचति] 'he cooks in the pot'

@deva[अधिकरण] takes @deva[सप्तमी] (@ref[2.3.36]).
advanced: Site supporting action = @deva[अधिकरण]. Takes @deva[सप्तमी] (@ref[2.3.36]). @deva[कटे आस्ते], @deva[स्थाल्यां पचति].

simple:
With @deva[अधि] + @deva[शी], @deva[स्था], or @deva[आस्] ('to lie/stand/sit upon'), the location is @deva[कर्म] (accusative).

**Examples:**
- @deva[ग्राममधिशेते] 'he rules the village'
- @deva[ग्राममधितिष्ठति] 'he presides over the village'
- @deva[ग्राममध्यास्ते] 'he occupies the village'

This is an exception to @ref[1.4.45].
standard:
With @deva[अधि] + @deva[शी]/@deva[स्था]/@deva[आस्], the location is @deva[कर्म]. Exception to @ref[1.4.45].

**Examples:**
- @deva[ग्राममधिशेते] 'he rules the village'
- @deva[ग्राममधितिष्ठति] 'he presides over the village'
- @deva[ग्राममध्यास्ते] 'he occupies the village'
advanced: @deva[अधि] + @deva[शी]/@deva[स्था]/@deva[आस्]: location = @deva[कर्म]. Exception to @ref[1.4.45]. @deva[ग्राममधिशेते].

simple:
With @deva[अभि] + @deva[नि] + @deva[विश्] 'to enter/resort to', the location is optionally @deva[कर्म].

**Examples:**
- @deva[ग्राममभिनिविशते] 'he resorts to the village' (accusative)
- @deva[पापे अभिनिवेशः] 'attachment to sin' (locative)
standard:
With @deva[अभि] + @deva[नि] + @deva[विश्] 'to resort to', the location is optionally @deva[कर्म].

**Examples:**
- @deva[ग्राममभिनिविशते] (accusative)
- @deva[पापे अभिनिवेशः] (locative)
advanced: @deva[अभि] + @deva[नि] + @deva[विश्]: location optionally = @deva[कर्म]. @deva[ग्राममभिनिविशते] or @deva[पापे अभिनिवेशः].

simple:
With @deva[उप], @deva[अनु], @deva[आ], or @deva[अधि] + @deva[वस्] 'to dwell', the location is @deva[कर्म].

**Examples:**
- @deva[ग्राममुपवसति सेना] 'the army dwells in the village'
- @deva[पर्वतमनुवसति] 'he dwells near the mountain'
- @deva[पर्वतमधिवसति] 'he dwells on the mountain'

**Exception:** When @deva[उपवस्] means 'to fast': @deva[ग्रामे उपवसति] 'he fasts in the village' (locative).
standard:
With @deva[उप]/@deva[अनु]/@deva[आङ्]/@deva[अधि] + @deva[वस्] 'to dwell', the location is @deva[कर्म].

**Examples:**
- @deva[ग्राममुपवसति सेना]
- @deva[पर्वतमनुवसति]

**Exception:** @deva[उपवस्] 'to fast' takes locative: @deva[ग्रामे उपवसति].
advanced: @deva[उप]/@deva[अनु]/@deva[आङ्]/@deva[अधि] + @deva[वस्]: location = @deva[कर्म]. Exception: @deva[उपवस्] 'to fast' takes locative.

simple:
What the agent most desires to accomplish is @deva[कर्म] 'object'.

**Examples:**
- @deva[कटं करोति] 'he makes a mat'
- @deva[ग्रामं गच्छति] 'he goes to the village'

**Note:** In @deva[माषेषु अश्वं बध्नाति] 'he ties the horse in the gram field', the horse desires the gram, but the agent intends the horse—so @deva[अश्वम्] is @deva[कर्म].
standard:
That which the agent most intends to affect is @deva[कर्म].

**Examples:**
- @deva[कटं करोति] 'he makes a mat'
- @deva[ग्रामं गच्छति] 'he goes to the village'

The agent's intention determines @deva[कर्म], not the object's desire.
advanced: What agent most intends to affect = @deva[कर्म]. @deva[कटं करोति], @deva[ग्रामं गच्छति]. Agent's intention, not object's desire.

simple:
What is not desired but similarly connected with the action is also @deva[कर्म].

This extends @ref[1.4.49] to unintended objects.

**Examples:**
- @deva[विषं भक्षयति] 'he eats poison' (unintended consumption)
- @deva[चोरान् पश्यति] 'he sees thieves' (unintended sight)
standard:
What is not intended but similarly connected with the action is also @deva[कर्म]. Extension of @ref[1.4.49].

**Examples:**
- @deva[विषं भक्षयति] 'he eats poison'
- @deva[चोरान् पश्यति] 'he sees thieves'
advanced: Unintended but similarly connected = also @deva[कर्म]. Extension of @ref[1.4.49]. @deva[विषं भक्षयति], @deva[चोरान् पश्यति].

simple:
A @deva[कारक] that is not mentioned under any other special relation (@deva[अकथित]) is also @deva[कर्म].

Some verbs take two accusatives—a primary and a secondary (@deva[अकथित]) object.

**Examples:**
- @deva[गां दोग्धि पयः] 'he milks the cow (for) milk'
- @deva[बलिं याचते वसुधाम्] 'he begs Bali for the earth'

Roots with double accusatives: @deva[दुह्], @deva[याच्], @deva[पच्], @deva[दण्ड्], @deva[रुध्], @deva[प्रच्छ्], @deva[चि], @deva[ब्रू], @deva[शास्], @deva[जि], @deva[मन्थ्], @deva[मुष्], @deva[नी], @deva[हृ], @deva[कृष्], @deva[वह्].
standard:
A @deva[कारक] not covered by other relations (@deva[अकथित]) is also @deva[कर्म]. This explains double accusatives.

**Examples:**
- @deva[गां दोग्धि पयः] 'he milks the cow (for) milk'
- @deva[बलिं याचते वसुधाम्] 'he begs Bali for the earth'
- @deva[माणवकं धर्मं शास्ति] 'he instructs the boy in dharma'

Roots: @deva[दुह्], @deva[याच्], @deva[पच्], @deva[दण्ड्], @deva[रुध्], @deva[प्रच्छ्], @deva[चि], @deva[ब्रू], @deva[शास्], @deva[जि], @deva[मन्थ्], @deva[मुष्], @deva[नी], @deva[हृ], @deva[कृष्], @deva[वह्].
advanced: @deva[अकथित] (unstated @deva[कारक]) = @deva[कर्म]. Double accusatives: @deva[गां दोग्धि पयः]. Roots: @deva[दुह्], @deva[याच्], @deva[पच्], @deva[ब्रू], @deva[शास्], etc.

simple:
For causatives of verbs of motion (@deva[गति]), knowledge (@deva[बुद्धि]), eating (@deva[भक्षण]), literary-object (@deva[शब्दकर्म]), and intransitive verbs (@deva[अकर्मक])—the primitive agent becomes @deva[कर्म].

**Examples:**
- Primitive: @deva[शत्रवः स्वर्गमगच्छन्] 'the enemies went to heaven'
- Causative: @deva[शत्रून् स्वर्गमगमयत्] 'he made the enemies go to heaven'

- Primitive: @deva[देवा अमृतमाश्नन्] 'the gods ate nectar'
- Causative: @deva[देवानमृतमाशयत्] 'he made the gods eat nectar'
advanced: Causatives of @deva[गति]/@deva[बुद्धि]/@deva[भक्षण]/@deva[शब्दकर्म]/@deva[अकर्मक]: primitive agent = @deva[कर्म]. @deva[शत्रून् स्वर्गमगमयत्]. @note[type=@[vārtika]]{@deva[नी]/@deva[वह्] causatives take instrumental.}

simple:
With @deva[हृ] and @deva[कृ] causatives, the primitive agent is optionally @deva[कर्म].

**Examples:**
- Primitive: @deva[करोति कटं देवदत्तः] 'Devadatta makes a mat'
- Causative: @deva[कारयति कटं देवदत्तम्] or @deva[देवदत्तेन] 'he makes Devadatta make a mat'

Both accusative and @deva[तृतीया] (instrumental) are valid for the primitive agent.
advanced: @deva[हृ]/@deva[कृ] causatives: primitive agent optionally = @deva[कर्म]. @deva[कारयति कटं देवदत्तम्] or @deva[देवदत्तेन].

simple:
The independent source of action that the speaker treats as primary is @deva[कर्तृ] 'agent'.

**Examples:**
- @deva[देवदत्तः पचति] 'Devadatta cooks'
- @deva[स्थाली पचति] 'the pot cooks'

The agent is what the speaker chooses to foreground—even a pot can be agent if treated as the primary actor.
standard:
Whatever the speaker treats as the independent, principal source of action is @deva[कर्तृ].

**Examples:**
- @deva[देवदत्तः पचति] 'Devadatta cooks'
- @deva[स्थाली पचति] 'the pot cooks'

The agent is absolute and unconditioned—it depends on the speaker's perspective.
advanced: Independent source of action (per speaker's choice) = @deva[कर्तृ]. @deva[देवदत्तः पचति], @deva[स्थाली पचति].

simple:
The mover of an independent agent is called @deva[हेतु] 'causer'—and is also @deva[कर्तृ].

**Examples:**
- @deva[कारयति] 'he causes to be made'
- @deva[हारयति] 'he causes to be taken'

When @deva[हेतु] is employed as agent, the verb takes the causative form (@ref[3.1.26]).
standard:
The mover of an independent agent is @deva[हेतु] (causer) and also @deva[कर्तृ].

**Examples:**
- @deva[कारयति] 'he causes to be made'
- @deva[हारयति] 'he causes to be taken'

When @deva[हेतु] is agent, the verb takes @deva[णिच्] (@ref[3.1.26]). The word @deva[च] gives both names (@deva[हेतु] and @deva[कर्तृ]) to the mover.
advanced: Mover of independent agent = @deva[हेतु] and @deva[कर्तृ]. Verb takes @deva[णिच्] (@ref[3.1.26]). @deva[कारयति], @deva[हारयति].

simple:
From here through @ref[1.4.97], the terms described are called @deva[निपात] 'particle'.

The @deva[निपाताधिकार] begins. Words called @deva[गति], @deva[उपसर्ग], and @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] also receive the name @deva[निपात].
standard:
From here through @ref[1.4.97], the terms described are @deva[निपात].

This @deva[अधिकार] gives @deva[गति], @deva[उपसर्ग], and @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] a second name: @deva[निपात].
advanced: @deva[निपाताधिकार]: through @ref[1.4.97]. @deva[गति], @deva[उपसर्ग], @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] also called @deva[निपात].

simple:
Words in the @deva[प्रादिगण] are called @deva[उपसर्ग] 'verbal prefix' when compounded with a verb.

**Examples:**
- @deva[प्र] + @deva[नयति] → @deva[प्रणयति] 'leads forth'
- @deva[परि] + @deva[नयति] → @deva[परिणयति] 'marries'

The @deva[न] becomes @deva[ण] because @deva[प्र] is an @deva[उपसर्ग] (@ref[8.4.14]).

When not with a verb, these words are just @deva[निपात], not @deva[उपसर्ग].
advanced: @deva[प्रादयः] + verb = @deva[उपसर्ग]. @deva[प्रणयति] (@deva[न] → @deva[ण] by @ref[8.4.14]). With nouns = @deva[निपात] only: @deva[प्रनायकः] 'leaderless' (no @deva[ण]). @note[type=@[vārtika]]{@deva[मरुत्] = @deva[उपसर्ग]: @deva[मरुत्तः] (@ref[7.4.47], vowel-ending requirement waived). @deva[श्रत्] = @deva[उपसर्ग]: @deva[श्रद्धा] (@ref[3.3.106]).}

simple:
An onomatopoeic word (sound-imitation) is @deva[गति] when not followed by @deva[इति].

**Examples:**
- @deva[खाट्कृत्य] 'having made the sound @[khāṭ]'
- @deva[खाट्कृतम्] 'the @[khāṭ] sound was made'

**Contrast:** @deva[खाडिति कृत्वा] (with @deva[इति])—here @deva[खाट्] is not @deva[गति], so no compounding.
standard:
Onomatopoeic words = @deva[गति] when @deva[इति] does not follow (@deva[अनितिपर]).

**Examples:**
- @deva[खाट्कृत्य] (compound, @deva[ल्यप्])
- @deva[खाट्कृतम्] (@ref[6.2.49] accent)
- @deva[यत्खाट्करोति] (@ref[8.2.71] accent)

**Contrast:** @deva[खाडिति कृत्वा निरष्ठीवत्] 'having made the sound @[khāṭ], he spat'—no @deva[गति] status when @deva[इति] follows.
advanced: Onomatopoeia = @deva[गति] when @deva[अनितिपर] (not followed by @deva[इति]). @deva[खाट्कृत्य], @deva[खाट्कृतम्] (@ref[6.2.49]), @deva[यत्खाट्करोति] (@ref[8.2.71]). With @deva[इति]: @deva[खाडिति कृत्वा]—no @deva[गति], hence @deva[त्वा] (not @deva[ल्यप्]).

simple:
@deva[सत्] and @deva[असत्] are @deva[गति] when meaning 'respect' or 'disrespect' respectively.

**Examples:**
- @deva[सत्कृत्य] 'having honored'
- @deva[असत्कृत्य] 'having dishonored'

**Contrast:** @deva[सत् कृत्वा काण्डं गतः] 'having made something real, he went'—here @deva[सत्] means 'real', not 'respect', so not @deva[गति].
standard:
@deva[सत्] and @deva[असत्] + verb = @deva[गति] when meaning 'respect/love' or 'disrespect/indifference'.

**Examples:**
- @deva[सत्कृत्य] 'having honored' (@ref[2.2.18], @ref[7.1.37])
- @deva[सत्कृतम्], @deva[असत्कृतम्] (@ref[6.2.49])
- @deva[यत्सत्करोति], @deva[यदसत्करोति] (@ref[8.2.71])

**Contrast:** @deva[सत् कृत्वा काण्डं गतः]—@deva[सत्] = 'real', not @deva[गति].
advanced: @deva[सत्]/@deva[असत्] + verb = @deva[गति] when @deva[सत्कार]/@ (respect/disrespect). @deva[सत्कृत्य] (@ref[2.2.18], @ref[7.1.37]); @deva[सत्कृतम्] (@ref[6.2.49]); @deva[यत्सत्करोति] (@ref[8.2.71]). Not @deva[गति] otherwise: @deva[सत् कृत्वा काण्डं गतः] (@deva[सत्] = 'real').

simple:
@deva[अन्तर्] is @deva[गति] when meaning 'non-acceptance' or 'abandoning'.

**Examples:**
- @deva[अन्तर्हत्य गृहं गतः] 'having abandoned home, he went'
- @deva[अन्तर्हतम् अनृतम्] 'falsehood being abandoned'

**Contrast:** @deva[अन्तर्हत्वा मूषिकां श्येनो गतः] 'the hawk went having seized the mouse'—here @deva[अन्तर्] means 'within', not @deva[गति].
advanced: @deva[अन्तर्] + verb = @deva[गति] in @deva[अपरिग्रह] sense. @deva[अन्तर्हत्य], @deva[अन्तर्हतम्], @deva[यदन्तर्हन्ति]. Contrast: @deva[अन्तर्हत्वा मूषिकां] (= 'grasping inside'). @note[type=@[vārtika]]{@deva[अन्तर्] = @deva[उपसर्ग] for @ref[3.3.106] (@deva[अन्तर्धा]); @ref[3.3.92] (@deva[अन्तर्धिः]); @ref[8.4.14] (@deva[अन्तर्णयति]).}

simple:
@deva[कणे] and @deva[मनस्] are @deva[गति] when meaning 'satiation' (satisfying desire to the point of revulsion).

**Examples:**
- @deva[कणेहत्य पयः पिबति] 'he drinks milk to complete satisfaction'
- @deva[मनोहत्य पयः पिबति] 'he drinks milk until sated'

**Contrast:** @deva[कणे हत्वा गतः] or @deva[मनो हत्वा गतः] (not meaning satiation)—no @deva[गति].
standard:
@deva[कणे] and @deva[मनस्] + verb = @deva[गति] when meaning @deva[श्रद्धाप्रतीघात] (satiation, satisfying desire to revulsion).

**Examples:**
- @deva[कणेहत्य पयः पिबति]
- @deva[मनोहत्य पयः पिबति]

The compound expresses drinking until desire is fully quenched.

**Contrast:** @deva[कणे हत्वा गतः]—not @deva[गति] when satiation not meant.
advanced: @deva[कणे]/@deva[मनस्] + verb = @deva[गति] in @deva[श्रद्धाप्रतीघात] sense (desire-satiation). @deva[कणेहत्य पयः पिबति], @deva[मनोहत्य पयः पिबति]. Contrast: @deva[कणे/मनो हत्वा गतः]—no @deva[गति].

simple:
The indeclinable @deva[अस्तम्] 'setting, disappearing' is @deva[गति].

**Examples:**
- @deva[अस्तंगत्य सविता पुनरुदेति] 'the sun, having set, rises again'
- @deva[अस्तंगतानि धनानि] 'riches that have vanished'

@deva[अस्तम्] is indeclinable by @ref[1.1.39] (ending in @deva[म्]).
standard:
Indeclinable @deva[अस्तम्] + verb = @deva[गति].

**Examples:**
- @deva[अस्तंगत्य] 'having set/disappeared'
- @deva[अस्तंगतानि धनानि] 'vanished wealth'
- @deva[यदस्तं गच्छति] 'when it sets'

@deva[अस्तम्] = @deva[अव्यय] by @ref[1.1.39] (@deva[म्]-final).

**Contrast:** @deva[अस्तं काण्डम्] 'the arrow is thrown'—different word, not @deva[गति].
advanced: Indeclinable @deva[अस्तम्] + verb = @deva[गति]. @deva[अस्तंगत्य], @deva[अस्तंगतानि], @deva[यदस्तं गच्छति]. @deva[अस्तम्] = @deva[अव्यय] by @ref[1.1.39] (@deva[म्]-final). Contrast: @deva[अस्तं काण्डम्] (= 'thrown')—different word.

simple:
The word @deva[अदस्] 'that' is @deva[गति] when not used demonstratively (not pointing to something external).

**Examples:**
- @deva[अदःकृत्य] 'having done that (reflexively)'
- @deva[अदःकृतम्] 'that which was done'

**Contrast:** @deva[अदः कृत्वा काण्डं गतः] 'having done that, he went to the arrow'—here @deva[अदस्] is demonstrative, not @deva[गति].
standard:
@deva[अदस्] + verb = @deva[गति] when @deva[अनुपदेश] (not addressing/pointing to another; reflexive use).

**Examples:**
- @deva[अदःकृत्य], @deva[अदःकृतम्], @deva[यददःकरोति]

**Contrast:** @deva[अदः कृत्वा काण्डं गतः]—demonstrative @deva[अदस्], not @deva[गति].
advanced: @deva[अदस्] + verb = @deva[गति] when @deva[अनुपदेश] (non-demonstrative, reflexive). @deva[अदःकृत्य], @deva[अदःकृतम्], @deva[यददःकरोति]. Contrast: demonstrative @deva[अदस्] (@deva[उपदेश]): @deva[अदः कृत्वा]—no @deva[गति].

simple:
@deva[तिरस्] is @deva[गति] when meaning 'disappearance'.

**Examples:**
- @deva[तिरोभूय] 'having disappeared'
- @deva[तिरोभूतम्] 'disappeared'

**Contrast:** @deva[तिरो भूत्वा अस्थितः] 'having stood apart'—here @deva[तिरस्] means 'aside', not 'disappearance', so not @deva[गति].
standard:
@deva[तिरस्] + verb = @deva[गति] when meaning @deva[तिरोधान] (disappearance).

**Examples:**
- @deva[तिरोभूय], @deva[तिरोभूतम्], @deva[यत्तिरोभवति]

**Contrast:** @deva[तिरो भूत्वा अस्थितः] (@deva[तिरस्] = 'apart')—not @deva[गति].
advanced: @deva[तिरस्] + verb = @deva[गति] in @deva[तिरोधान] sense. @deva[तिरोभूय], @deva[तिरोभूतम्], @deva[यत्तिरोभवति]. Contrast: @deva[तिरो भूत्वा अस्थितः] (= 'apart')—no @deva[गति].

simple:
@deva[तिरस्] with @deva[कृ] is optionally @deva[गति] (when meaning 'disappearance').

**Examples:**
- @deva[तिरःकृत्य] or @deva[तिरस्कृत्य] 'having made disappear'
- @deva[तिरःकृत्वा] or @deva[तिरस्कृत्वा] (without @deva[गति])

The @deva[विसर्ग] → @deva[स्] change is optional (@ref[8.3.42]).

**Contrast:** @deva[तिरः कृत्वा काष्ठं तिष्ठति] 'having laid aside the stick'—not 'disappearance', not @deva[गति].
standard:
@deva[तिरस्] + @deva[कृ] = optionally @deva[गति] (@deva[प्राप्तविभाषा]).

**Examples:**
- @deva[तिरःकृत्य] or @deva[तिरस्कृत्य] (@ref[8.3.42] optional @deva[विसर्ग] → @deva[स्])
- @deva[तिरःकृत्वा] or @deva[तिरस्कृत्वा] (non-@deva[गति] option)

**Contrast:** @deva[तिरः कृत्वा काष्ठं तिष्ठति] (= 'laying aside')—no @deva[गति].
advanced: @deva[तिरस्] + @deva[कृ] = optional @deva[गति] (@deva[प्राप्तविभाषा]). @deva[तिरःकृत्य]/@deva[तिरस्कृत्य] (@ref[8.3.42]). Non-@deva[गति]: @deva[तिरःकृत्वा]/@deva[तिरस्कृत्वा]. Contrast: @deva[तिरः कृत्वा काष्ठम्] (= 'laying aside').

simple:
@deva[उपाजे] and @deva[अन्वाजे] ('supporting the weak') with @deva[कृ] are optionally @deva[गति].

**Examples:**
- @deva[उपाजेकृत्य] or @deva[उपाजे कृत्वा] 'having given support'
- @deva[अन्वाजेकृत्य] or @deva[अन्वाजे कृत्वा]
standard:
@deva[उपाजे]/@deva[अन्वाजे] + @deva[कृ] = optionally @deva[गति].

Both words mean 'supporting/assisting the weak'.

**Examples:**
- @deva[उपाजेकृत्य] or @deva[उपाजे कृत्वा]
- @deva[अन्वाजेकृत्य] or @deva[अन्वाजे कृत्वा]
advanced: @deva[उपाजे]/@deva[अन्वाजे] (= assisting weak) + @deva[कृ] = optional @deva[गति]. @deva[उपाजेकृत्य]/@deva[उपाजे कृत्वा]; @deva[अन्वाजेकृत्य]/@deva[अन्वाजे कृत्वा].

simple:
@deva[साक्षात्] etc. with @deva[कृ] are optionally @deva[गति].

**Examples:**
- @deva[साक्षात्कृत्य] or @deva[साक्षात् कृत्वा] 'having made visible/evident'
- @deva[मिथ्याकृत्य] or @deva[मिथ्या कृत्वा] 'having made false'

These express making something what it was not before (like @deva[च्वि] sense).
advanced: @deva[साक्षात्] etc. + @deva[कृ] = optional @deva[गति]. @deva[साक्षात्कृत्य]/@deva[साक्षात् कृत्वा]; @deva[मिथ्याकृत्य]/@deva[मिथ्या कृत्वा]. @note[type=@[vārtika]]{@deva[च्वि] force implied—transformation to opposite state.}

simple:
@deva[उरसि] 'in the heart' and @deva[मनसि] 'in the mind' with @deva[कृ] are optionally @deva[गति]—but not when meaning literal 'placing'.

**Examples:**
- @deva[उरसिकृत्य] or @deva[उरसि कृत्वा] 'having taken to heart'
- @deva[मनसिकृत्य] or @deva[मनसि कृत्वा] 'having kept in mind'

**Contrast:** @deva[उरसि कृत्वा पाणिं शेते] 'he lies having placed his hand on his chest'—literal placing, not @deva[गति].
standard:
@deva[उरसि]/@deva[मनसि] + @deva[कृ] = optionally @deva[गति] when not meaning literal placement.

**Examples:**
- @deva[उरसिकृत्य]/@deva[उरसि कृत्वा]
- @deva[मनसिकृत्य]/@deva[मनसि कृत्वा]

**Contrast:** @deva[उरसि कृत्वा पाणिं शेते] (physical placing); @deva[मनसि कृत्वा वचनं ध्यायति] (= 'placing word in mind')—no @deva[गति].
advanced: @deva[उरसि]/@deva[मनसि] + @deva[कृ] = optional @deva[गति] when @deva[अनिधान] (not placing). @deva[उरसिकृत्य], @deva[मनसिकृत्य]. Contrast @deva[निधान]: @deva[उरसि कृत्वा पाणिं शेते]; @deva[मनसि कृत्वा वचनं ध्यायति]—no @deva[गति].

simple:
@deva[मध्ये] 'in the middle', @deva[पदे] 'at the step/place', and @deva[निवचने] 'speechless' with @deva[कृ] are optionally @deva[गति]—but not when meaning literal 'placing'.

**Examples:**
- @deva[मध्येकृत्य] or @deva[मध्ये कृत्वा]
- @deva[पदेकृत्य] or @deva[पदे कृत्वा]
- @deva[निवचनेकृत्य] or @deva[निवचने कृत्वा]

**Contrast:** @deva[हस्तिनः पदे कृत्वा शिरः शेते] 'he lies having placed his head under the elephant's foot'—literal, not @deva[गति].
advanced: @deva[मध्ये]/@deva[पदे]/@deva[निवचने] + @deva[कृ] = optional @deva[गति] when @deva[अनिधान]. @deva[मध्येकृत्य], @deva[पदेकृत्य], @deva[निवचनेकृत्य]. Contrast @deva[निधान]: @deva[हस्तिनः पदे कृत्वा शिरः शेते].

simple:
@deva[हस्ते] and @deva[पाणौ] 'in the hand' with @deva[कृ] are always @deva[गति] when meaning 'marriage' (taking the hand in marriage).

**Examples:**
- @deva[पाणौकृत्य] 'having married'
- @deva[हस्तेकृत्य] 'having married'

**Contrast:** @deva[हस्ते कृत्वा कार्षापणं गतः] 'having taken a coin in his hand, he went'—literal placing, not @deva[गति].
advanced: @deva[हस्ते]/@deva[पाणौ] + @deva[कृ] = @deva[नित्यगति] in @deva[पाणिग्रहण] (marriage) sense. @deva[पाणौकृत्य], @deva[हस्तेकृत्य]. Contrast: @deva[हस्ते कृत्वा कार्षापणम्]—no @deva[गति].

simple:
The indeclinable @deva[प्राध्वम्] with @deva[कृ] is always @deva[गति] when meaning 'binding'.

**Example:** @deva[प्राध्वंकृत्य] 'having bound'

@deva[प्राध्वम्] normally means 'suitably, favorably'. Only in the sense of 'binding' is it obligatory @deva[गति].

**Contrast:** @deva[प्राध्वं कृत्वा शकटं गतः] 'having made the cart suitable, he went'—not 'binding', not @deva[गति].
standard:
Indeclinable @deva[प्राध्वम्] + @deva[कृ] = obligatory @deva[गति] when meaning @deva[बन्धन] (binding).

**Example:** @deva[प्राध्वंकृत्य] 'having bound'

@deva[प्राध्वम्] = @deva[अव्यय] (@deva[म्]-final). Usual meaning: 'suitably'.

**Contrast:** @deva[प्राध्वं कृत्वा शकटं गतः] (= 'making suitable')—no @deva[गति].
advanced: Indeclinable @deva[प्राध्वम्] + @deva[कृ] = @deva[नित्यगति] in @deva[बन्धन] sense. @deva[प्राध्वंकृत्य]. Contrast: @deva[प्राध्वं कृत्वा शकटम्] (= 'suitably')—no @deva[गति].

simple:
@deva[जीविका] and @deva[उपनिषद्] with @deva[कृ] are @deva[गति] when meaning 'resemblance' (treating something as if it were that).

**Examples:**
- @deva[जीविकाकृत्य] 'having treated it like a livelihood'
- @deva[उपनिषत्कृत्य] 'having treated it like an @[upaniṣad]'

**Contrast:** @deva[जीविकां कृत्वा गतः] 'having made his livelihood, he went'—not resemblance, not @deva[गति].
advanced: @deva[जीविका]/@deva[उपनिषद्] + @deva[कृ] = @deva[गति] in @deva[सादृश्य] sense. @deva[जीविकाकृत्य], @deva[उपनिषत्कृत्य]. Contrast: @deva[जीविकां कृत्वा] (= actual livelihood)—no @deva[गति].

simple:
Words called @deva[गति] and @deva[उपसर्ग] are placed before the verb (as prefixes).

**Examples:**
- @deva[प्र] + @deva[गच्छति] → @deva[प्रगच्छति]
- @deva[सत्] + @deva[करोति] → @deva[सत्करोति]

All the preceding examples illustrate this prefix placement.
advanced: @deva[गति]/@deva[उपसर्ग] = @deva[प्राग्धातोः] (before the root). @deva[ते] includes @deva[उपसर्ग]. All prior examples illustrate: @deva[प्रणयति], @deva[सत्कृत्य], @deva[अलंकृतम्], etc.

simple:
The word @deva[अनु] is @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] when meaning 'inferior to' or 'subordinate to.'

**Example:** @deva[अनुशाकटायनं वैयाकरणाः] 'all grammarians are inferior to @[śākaṭāyana]'

Here @deva[अनु] governs the superior person (@[śākaṭāyana]) in the @deva[द्वितीया] (accusative) case.
advanced: @deva[अनु] = @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] when expressing @deva[हीन] (inferiority). @deva[अनुशाकटायनं वैयाकरणाः]—'grammarians are inferior to @[śākaṭāyana].' The superior term takes accusative, governed by @deva[अनु].

simple:
The word @deva[उप] is @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] when meaning 'superior to' or 'inferior to.'

**Examples:**
- @deva[उपखार्यां द्रोणः] 'a @deva[द्रोण] exceeds a @deva[खारी]' (superior)
- @deva[उप शाकटायनं वैयाकरणाः] 'grammarians are inferior to @[śākaṭāyana]'

@deva[उप] governs the locative for the inferior thing, or @deva[द्वितीया] (accusative) for the superior person.
standard:
@deva[उप] is @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] when expressing superiority or inferiority.

**Examples:**
- @deva[उपखार्यां द्रोणः] 'a @deva[द्रोण] is above a @deva[खारी]'—locative for inferior (@ref[2.3.9])
- @deva[उप निष्के कार्षापणः] 'a @deva[कार्षापण] exceeds a @deva[निष्क]'
- @deva[उप शाकटायनं वैयाकरणाः] 'grammarians are inferior to @[śākaṭāyana]'—accusative for superior
advanced: @deva[उप] = @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] for superiority/inferiority. Governs locative for the inferior (@ref[2.3.9]): @deva[उपखार्यां द्रोणः]. Governs accusative for the superior: @deva[उप शाकटायनं वैयाकरणाः].

simple:
The words @deva[अप] and @deva[परि] are @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] when meaning 'exclusion' or 'except.'

**Examples:**
- @deva[अपत्रिगर्तेभ्यो वृष्टो देवः] 'it rained except for Trigarta'
- @deva[परि त्रिगर्तेभ्यो वृष्टो देवः] 'it rained except for Trigarta'

They govern the excluded noun in the ablative case.

**Contrast:** @deva[ओदनं परिषिञ्चति] 'he waters the rice'—here @deva[परि] is an @deva[उपसर्ग], so @deva[स] → @deva[ष].
standard:
@deva[अप] and @deva[परि] are @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] when meaning exclusion ('except').

**Examples:**
- @deva[अपत्रिगर्तेभ्यो वृष्टो देवः] 'it rained except for Trigarta'
- @deva[परि त्रिगर्तेभ्यो वृष्टो देवः] 'same meaning'

They govern the ablative (@ref[2.3.10]).

**Contrast:** @deva[ओदनं परिषिञ्चति]—@deva[परि] is @deva[उपसर्ग], causing @deva[स] → @deva[ष] (@ref[8.3.65]).
advanced: @deva[अप] and @deva[परि] = @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] for exclusion. They govern ablative (@ref[2.3.10]): @deva[अपत्रिगर्तेभ्यो वृष्टो देवः]. When not exclusion, they're @deva[उपसर्ग]: @deva[ओदनं परिषिञ्चति] shows @deva[स] → @deva[ष] by @ref[8.3.65].

simple:
The word @deva[आङ्] is @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] when expressing a limit—either inclusive ('as far as including') or exclusive ('as far as but not including').

**Examples:**
- @deva[आपाटलिपुत्राद् वृष्टो देवः] 'it rained up to (excluding) @[pāṭaliputra]' (ablative)
- @deva[आपाटलिपुत्रं वृष्टो देवः] 'it rained up to (including) @[pāṭaliputra]' (accusative)
- @deva[आकुमारं यशः पाणिनेः] '@[pāṇini]'s fame extends even to children'
standard:
@deva[आङ्] is @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] when expressing @deva[मर्यादा] (limit)—inclusive or exclusive.

**Examples:**
- @deva[आपाटलिपुत्राद् वृष्टो देवः] 'rained up to (excluding) @[pāṭaliputra]'—ablative for exclusive limit
- @deva[आपाटलिपुत्रं वृष्टो देवः] 'rained up to (including) @[pāṭaliputra]'—accusative for inclusive

When meaning 'little' or as a verbal prefix, @deva[आङ्] is not @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय].
advanced: @deva[आङ्] = @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] for @deva[मर्यादावचन] (limit expression). Ablative for exclusive: @deva[आपाटलिपुत्राद् वृष्टो देवः]. Accusative for inclusive: @deva[आपाटलिपुत्रं वृष्टो देवः]. Not @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] when meaning 'little' or as @deva[उपसर्ग].

simple:
The words @deva[प्रति], @deva[परि], and @deva[अनु] are @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] when expressing:

1. **Direction (@deva[लक्षण]):** @deva[वृक्षं प्रति विद्योतते विद्युत्] 'lightning flashes toward the tree'
2. **Circumstance:** @deva[साधुर्देवदत्तो मातरं प्रति] 'Devadatta is good as regards his mother'
3. **Share (@deva[भाग]):** @deva[हरं प्रति हलाहलम्] 'the poison fell to Hara's share'
4. **Distribution (@deva[ईप्सा]):** @deva[वृक्षं वृक्षं प्रति सिञ्चति] 'he waters tree after tree'
standard:
@deva[प्रति], @deva[परि], and @deva[अनु] are @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] when expressing:

1. **@deva[लक्षण]** (direction): @deva[वृक्षं प्रति विद्योतते विद्युत्] 'lightning toward the tree'
2. **Circumstance**: @deva[साधुर्देवदत्तो मातरं प्रति] 'Devadatta is good as regards his mother'
3. **@deva[भाग]** (share): @deva[हरं प्रति हलाहलम्] 'poison fell to Hara's share'
4. **@deva[ईप्सा]** (pervasion/distribution): @deva[वृक्षं वृक्षं प्रति सिञ्चति] 'he waters tree by tree'

All three particles work interchangeably in these senses.
advanced: @deva[प्रति], @deva[परि], @deva[अनु] = @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] for @deva[लक्षण] (direction), circumstance, @deva[भाग] (share), and @deva[ईप्सा] (distribution). @deva[वृक्षं प्रति विद्योतते]—direction. @deva[मातरं प्रति साधुः]—circumstance. @deva[हरं प्रति हलाहलम्]—share. @deva[वृक्षं वृक्षं प्रति सिञ्चति]—distribution.

simple:
The word @deva[अभि] is @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] in the senses of direction, circumstance, and distribution—but **not** for 'share.'

**Examples:**
- @deva[वृक्षमभि विद्योतते] 'it lightens toward the tree' (direction)
- @deva[मातरमभि साधुः] 'good as regards his mother' (circumstance)

**Not @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय]:** @deva[यदत्र ममाभिष्यात् तद् दीयताम्] 'give what falls to my share'—here @deva[अभि] is an @deva[उपसर्ग], so @deva[स] → @deva[ष].
advanced: @deva[अभि] = @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] for @deva[लक्षण], circumstance, @deva[ईप्सा]—not for @deva[भाग]. For share: @deva[यदत्र ममाभिष्यात्]—@deva[अभि] is @deva[उपसर्ग], triggering @deva[स] → @deva[ष].

simple:
The word @deva[प्रति] is @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] when meaning 'representative of' (@deva[प्रतिनिधि]) or 'in exchange for' (@deva[प्रतिदान]).

**Examples:**
- @deva[अभिमन्युरर्जुनतः प्रति] 'Abhimanyu represents Arjuna'
- @deva[माषानस्मै तिलेभ्यः प्रतियच्छति] 'he exchanges @[māṣas] for these sesame seeds'

In these senses, @deva[प्रति] governs the ablative case.
standard:
@deva[प्रति] is @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] for @deva[प्रतिनिधि] (representative) and @deva[प्रतिदान] (exchange).

**Examples:**
- @deva[अभिमन्युरर्जुनतः प्रति] 'Abhimanyu is Arjuna's representative'
- @deva[माषानस्मै तिलेभ्यः प्रतियच्छति] 'exchanges @[māṣas] for sesame'

Governs ablative (@ref[2.3.11]).
advanced: @deva[प्रति] = @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] for @deva[प्रतिनिधि] and @deva[प्रतिदान]. @deva[अभिमन्युरर्जुनतः प्रति]—representative. @deva[माषानस्मै तिलेभ्यः प्रतियच्छति]—exchange. Governs ablative by @ref[2.3.11].

simple:
The words @deva[अधि] and @deva[परि] are @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] when used as mere expletives (adding no meaning).

**Examples:**
- @deva[कुतोऽभ्यागच्छति] 'whence has he come?'
- @deva[कुतः पर्यागच्छति] 'whence has he come?' (same meaning)

They are called @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] so they don't get @deva[उपसर्ग] or @deva[गति] status and trigger the effects of those categories.
advanced: @deva[अधि] and @deva[परि] = @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] as expletives. @deva[कुतोऽभ्यागच्छति] = @deva[कुतः पर्यागच्छति]. Designating them @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] prevents @deva[उपसर्ग]/@deva[गति] classification and their consequent operations.

simple:
The word @deva[सु] is @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] when expressing respect or excellence ('excellently').

**Examples:**
- @deva[सुस्तुतं भवता] 'well-praised by your honor'
- @deva[सुसिक्तं भवता] 'excellently sprinkled by your honor'

Because @deva[सु] is @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] (not @deva[उपसर्ग]), @deva[स] does not become @deva[ष].

**Contrast:** @deva[सुषिक्तं किं तवात्र] 'has your place been well sprinkled?'—here @deva[सु] is not respectful, so it's @deva[उपसर्ग], and @deva[स] → @deva[ष].
standard:
@deva[सु] is @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] when expressing @deva[पूजा] (respect).

**Examples:** @deva[सुस्तुतं भवता] 'excellently praised by your honor'; @deva[सुसिक्तं भवता].

As @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय], @deva[सु] doesn't trigger @deva[स] → @deva[ष] (@ref[8.3.65]).

**Contrast:** @deva[सुषिक्तं किं तवात्र]—no respect intended, so @deva[सु] is @deva[उपसर्ग], causing @deva[स] → @deva[ष].
advanced: @deva[सु] = @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] for @deva[पूजा]. @deva[सुस्तुतं भवता]—no @deva[ष] since not @deva[उपसर्ग]. Without respect: @deva[सुषिक्तम्]—@deva[सु] is @deva[उपसर्ग], @ref[8.3.65] applies.

simple:
The word @deva[अपि] is @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] in five senses:

1. **Understood word (@deva[पदार्थ]):** @deva[सर्पिषोऽपि स्यात्] 'there may be a bit of ghee' (a word like 'drop' is understood)
2. **Possibility (@deva[संभावन]):** @deva[अपि सिञ्चेत् मूलकसहस्रम्] 'he might water a thousand radishes' (potential mood)
3. **Permission (@deva[अन्वसर्ग]):** @deva[अपि सिञ्च] 'sprinkle if you like' (imperative mood)
4. **Censure (@deva[गर्हा]):** expressing disgrace
5. **Collection (@deva[समुच्चय]):** @deva[अपि स्तुहि अपि सिञ्च] 'both praise and sprinkle'

Being @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय], @deva[अपि] doesn't cause @deva[स] → @deva[ष].
standard:
@deva[अपि] is @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] for:

1. **@deva[पदार्थ]** (understood word): @deva[सर्पिषोऽपि स्यात्]—'a bit of ghee' (word like 'drop' implied)
2. **@deva[संभावन]** (possibility): @deva[अपि सिञ्चेत्]—potential mood
3. **@deva[अन्वसर्ग]** (permission): @deva[अपि सिञ्च]—imperative mood
4. **@deva[गर्हा]** (censure)
5. **@deva[समुच्चय]** (collection): @deva[अपि स्तुहि अपि सिञ्च] 'praise as well as sprinkle'

Not being @deva[उपसर्ग], @deva[स] → @deva[ष] doesn't apply.
advanced: @deva[अपि] = @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] for @deva[पदार्थ], @deva[संभावन], @deva[अन्वसर्ग], @deva[गर्हा], @deva[समुच्चय]. @deva[सर्पिषोऽपि स्यात्]—understood word. @deva[अपि सिञ्चेत्]—possibility (potential). @deva[अपि सिञ्च]—permission (imperative). Not @deva[उपसर्ग], so no @deva[स] → @deva[ष].

simple:
The word @deva[अधि] is @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] when meaning 'lordship' (@deva[ईश्वर])—either 'being a lord of' or 'having as a lord.'

**Examples:**
- @deva[अधि ब्रह्मदत्ते पञ्चालाः] 'the @[pañcālas] have Brahmadatta as lord' (locative of lord)
- @deva[अधि पञ्चालेषु ब्रह्मदत्तः] 'Brahmadatta is lord over the @[pañcālas]' (locative of domain)

@deva[अधि] governs the locative case.
standard:
@deva[अधि] is @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] for @deva[ईश्वर] (lordship).

**Examples:**
- @deva[अधि ब्रह्मदत्ते पञ्चालाः] 'the @[pañcālas] have Brahmadatta as lord'—locative of possessor
- @deva[अधि पञ्चालेषु ब्रह्मदत्तः] 'Brahmadatta rules over the @[pañcālas]'—locative of property

Governs @deva[सप्तमी] (locative) case.
advanced: @deva[अधि] = @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] for @deva[ईश्वर]. Governs locative—either of possessor (@deva[अधि ब्रह्मदत्ते पञ्चालाः]) or of property (@deva[अधि पञ्चालेषु ब्रह्मदत्तः]).

simple:
The word @deva[अधि] is **optionally** @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] when the verb @deva[कृ] follows.

**Example:** @deva[यदत्र मामधि करिष्यति] 'what he will do regarding me'

Here @deva[अधि] may be treated as either @deva[गति] or @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय]. The accent differs depending on which classification applies.
advanced: @deva[अधि] = optionally @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] with @deva[कृ]. @deva[यदत्र मामधि करिष्यति]—@deva[अधि] may be @deva[गति] (accent by @ref[8.1.71]) or @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय].

simple:
The substitutes for @deva[ल] (the ten tense-mood markers) are called @deva[परस्मैपद] 'active voice endings.'

The nine @deva[परस्मैपद] endings are:

| Person | Singular | Dual | Plural |
|--------|----------|------|--------|
| 3rd | @deva[तिप्] | @deva[तस्] | @deva[झि] |
| 2nd | @deva[सिप्] | @deva[थस्] | @deva[थ] |
| 1st | @deva[मिप्] | @deva[वस्] | @deva[मस्] |

The participle affixes @deva[शतृ] and @deva[क्वसु] are also @deva[परस्मैपद].
standard:
Substitutes of @deva[ल] are called @deva[परस्मैपद]. @deva[ल] is the generic term for the ten tense-moods (@deva[लट्], @deva[लिट्], @deva[लुट्], @deva[लृट्], @deva[लेट्], @deva[लोट्], @deva[लङ्], @deva[लिङ्], @deva[लुङ्], @deva[लृङ्]).

**@deva[परस्मैपद] endings:**
- 3rd: @deva[तिप्], @deva[तस्], @deva[झि]
- 2nd: @deva[सिप्], @deva[थस्], @deva[थ]
- 1st: @deva[मिप्], @deva[वस्], @deva[मस्]

Also: @deva[शतृ] (@ref[3.2.124]) and @deva[क्वसु] (@ref[3.2.107]).
advanced: @deva[ल]-substitutes = @deva[परस्मैपद]. The nine endings: @deva[तिप्], @deva[तस्], @deva[झि]; @deva[सिप्], @deva[थस्], @deva[थ]; @deva[मिप्], @deva[वस्], @deva[मस्]. Also @deva[शतृ] (@ref[3.2.124]), @deva[क्वसु] (@ref[3.2.107]), and @ref[3.4.78].

simple:
The nine endings in the @deva[तङ्] @deva[प्रत्याहार], plus the @deva[आन]-ending affixes (@deva[शानच्] and @deva[कानच्]), are called @deva[आत्मनेपद] 'middle voice endings.'

The nine @deva[आत्मनेपद] endings are:

| Person | Singular | Dual | Plural |
|--------|----------|------|--------|
| 3rd | @deva[त] | @deva[आताम्] | @deva[झ] |
| 2nd | @deva[थास्] | @deva[आथाम्] | @deva[ध्वम्] |
| 1st | @deva[इट्] | @deva[वहि] | @deva[महिङ्] |

The participle affixes @deva[शानच्] and @deva[कानच्] are also @deva[आत्मनेपद].
standard:
The @deva[तङ्] affixes and @deva[आन]-ending affixes (@deva[शानच्], @deva[कानच्]) are called @deva[आत्मनेपद].

**@deva[आत्मनेपद] endings:**
- 3rd: @deva[त], @deva[आताम्], @deva[झ]
- 2nd: @deva[थास्], @deva[आथाम्], @deva[ध्वम्]
- 1st: @deva[इट्], @deva[वहि], @deva[महिङ्]

Also: @deva[शानच्] and @deva[कानच्] (@ref[3.2.106]).
advanced: @deva[तङ्] affixes + @deva[आन]-ending affixes = @deva[आत्मनेपद]. The nine endings: @deva[त], @deva[आताम्], @deva[झ]; @deva[थास्], @deva[आथाम्], @deva[ध्वम्]; @deva[इट्], @deva[वहि], @deva[महिङ्]. Also @deva[शानच्], @deva[कानच्] (@ref[3.2.106]).

simple:
The @deva[तिङ्] endings (all 18 personal endings) are grouped into triads, named in order:

1. **@deva[प्रथम]** 'first' = 3rd person (in Western grammar)
2. **@deva[मध्यम]** 'middle' = 2nd person
3. **@deva[उत्तम]** 'highest' = 1st person

Each of the three persons has three numbers (@deva[एकवचन] (singular), dual, plural).
standard:
The @deva[तिङ्] endings (the @deva[प्रत्याहार] from @deva[तिप्] to @deva[महिङ्]) are grouped into three triads called:

- @deva[प्रथम] (3rd person)
- @deva[मध्यम] (2nd person)
- @deva[उत्तम] (1st person)

Both @deva[परस्मैपद] and @deva[आत्मनेपद] have these three persons, each with three numbers.
advanced: @deva[तिङ्] (@deva[तिप्] through @deva[महिङ्]) comprises 18 endings: 9 @deva[परस्मैपद], 9 @deva[आत्मनेपद]. Each set has three triads: @deva[प्रथम] (3rd), @deva[मध्यम] (2nd), @deva[उत्तम] (1st).

simple:
Within each person triad, the three endings are called:

1. **@deva[एकवचन]** 'singular' — for one
2. **@deva[द्विवचन]** 'dual' — for two
3. **@deva[बहुवचन]** 'plural' — for many

So @deva[पचति] (3rd sg.), @deva[पचतः] (3rd du.), @deva[पचन्ति] (3rd pl.).
standard:
Within each person triad (@deva[प्रथम], @deva[मध्यम], @deva[उत्तम]), the three endings are:

- @deva[एकवचन] (singular)
- @deva[द्विवचन] (dual)
- @deva[बहुवचन] (plural)

This gives 9 forms per voice (3 persons × 3 numbers).
advanced: Each person triad subdivides by number: @deva[एकवचन], @deva[द्विवचन], @deva[बहुवचन]. Combined with two voices = 18 @deva[तिङ्] endings total.

simple:
The @deva[सुप्] (case endings for nouns) also have the same number terminology:

1. **@deva[एकवचन]** (singular)
2. **@deva[द्विवचन]** (dual)
3. **@deva[बहुवचन]** (plural)

There are 21 case endings total (7 cases × 3 numbers).
standard:
The @deva[सुप्] endings (the @deva[प्रत्याहार] from @deva[सु] to @deva[प्]) also use @deva[एकवचन], @deva[द्विवचन], @deva[बहुवचन].

There are 21 @deva[सुप्] endings: 7 cases × 3 numbers (see @ref[4.1.2]).
advanced: @deva[सुप्] (21 case endings: 7 cases × 3 numbers) also uses @deva[एकवचन], @deva[द्विवचन], @deva[बहुवचन]. See @ref[4.1.2].

simple:
Each triad of @deva[तिङ्] (verbal endings) and @deva[सुप्] (case endings) is called @deva[विभक्ति] 'inflection.'

**Examples:**
- @deva[सप्तमी विभक्ति] = the three locative endings (sg., du., pl.)
- @deva[प्रथमा विभक्ति] = the three 3rd-person endings (sg., du., pl.)

So @deva[विभक्ति] refers to a complete set of three endings.
standard:
Triads of @deva[तिङ्] and @deva[सुप्] affixes are called @deva[विभक्ति] (inflection).

@deva[विभक्ति] means a complete triad:
- @deva[सप्तमी विभक्ति] = locative sg., du., pl.
- @deva[प्रथमा विभक्ति] = 3rd person sg., du., pl.
advanced: Triads of @deva[तिङ्] and @deva[सुप्] = @deva[विभक्ति]. @deva[सप्तमी विभक्ति] = locative triad. @deva[प्रथमा विभक्ति] = 3rd person triad.

simple:
