When @deva[युष्मद्] 'you' (expressed or understood) is the subject, use @deva[मध्यम] (2nd person) endings.

**Examples:**
- @deva[त्वं पचसि] or just @deva[पचसि] 'you cook'
- @deva[युवां पचथः] or @deva[पचथः] 'you two cook'
- @deva[यूयं पचथ] or @deva[पचथ] 'you (all) cook'

The pronoun can be omitted—the ending indicates 2nd person.
standard:
When @deva[युष्मद्] (expressed or understood) agrees with the verb, use @deva[मध्यम] (2nd person) endings.

**Examples:**
- @deva[त्वं पचसि] / @deva[पचसि] 'you cook'
- @deva[युवां पचथः] / @deva[पचथः] 'you two cook'
- @deva[यूयं पचथ] / @deva[पचथ] 'you (pl.) cook'
advanced: @deva[युष्मद्] (expressed or understood) as subject → @deva[मध्यम पुरुष]. @deva[त्वं पचसि], @deva[युवां पचथः], @deva[यूयं पचथ].

simple:
In joking or ironic contexts, a special person-shift occurs: @deva[मन्ये] 'I think' takes 1st person, while the dependent verb takes 2nd person.

**Example:** @deva[एहि मन्ये ओदनं भोक्ष्यसे] 'Come, I suppose you'll eat the rice' (ironic—implying you won't)

Literally: the speaker mockingly attributes a thought to the addressee.

**Without irony:** @deva[एहि मन्यसे ओदनं भोक्ष्ये] 'Come, you think "I will eat rice"' (normal person agreement)
standard:
For @deva[प्रहास] (joking/irony), @deva[मन्] takes @deva[उत्तम एकवचन] (@deva[मन्ये]), while the dependent verb takes @deva[मध्यम].

**Example:** @deva[एहि मन्ये ओदनं भोक्ष्यसे] 'I suppose you'll eat the rice' (ironic)

**Normal usage:** @deva[एहि मन्यसे ओदनं भोक्ष्ये] 'you think "I will eat"' (proper person agreement)
advanced: For @deva[प्रहास]: @deva[मन्] → @deva[उत्तम एकवचन]; dependent verb → @deva[मध्यम]. @deva[एहि मन्ये ओदनं भोक्ष्यसे]—ironic. Without @deva[प्रहास]: @deva[एहि मन्यसे ओदनं भोक्ष्ये]—regular agreement.

simple:
When @deva[अस्मद्] 'I/we' (expressed or understood) is the subject, use @deva[उत्तम] (1st person) endings.

**Examples:**
- @deva[अहं पचामि] or just @deva[पचामि] 'I cook'
- @deva[आवां पचावः] or @deva[पचावः] 'we two cook'
- @deva[वयं पचामः] or @deva[पचामः] 'we cook'

The pronoun can be omitted—the ending indicates 1st person.
standard:
When @deva[अस्मद्] (expressed or understood) agrees with the verb, use @deva[उत्तम] (1st person) endings.

**Examples:**
- @deva[अहं पचामि] / @deva[पचामि] 'I cook'
- @deva[आवां पचावः] / @deva[पचावः] 'we two cook'
advanced: @deva[अस्मद्] (expressed or understood) as subject → @deva[उत्तम पुरुष]. @deva[अहं पचामि], @deva[आवां पचावः].

simple:
When the subject is neither @deva[युष्मद्] (you) nor @deva[अस्मद्] (I/we), use @deva[प्रथम] (3rd person) endings.

**Examples:**
- @deva[पचति] 'he/she cooks'
- @deva[पचतः] 'they two cook'
- @deva[पचन्ति] 'they cook'

This is the default when the subject is a third party.
standard:
When the subject is other than @deva[युष्मद्] or @deva[अस्मद्], use @deva[प्रथम] (3rd person) endings.

**Examples:** @deva[पचति], @deva[पचतः], @deva[पचन्ति].
advanced: Subject ≠ @deva[युष्मद्]/@deva[अस्मद्] → @deva[प्रथम पुरुष]. @deva[पचति], @deva[पचतः], @deva[पचन्ति].

simple:
When sounds are in the closest possible proximity—with no more than half a @deva[मात्रा] of pause between them—this is called @deva[संहिता] 'junction.' When sounds are in @deva[संहिता], sandhi rules apply.

**Example:** @deva[दधि] + @deva[अत्र] → @deva[दध्यत्र] 'curd here'

The @deva[इ] and @deva[अ] are in @deva[संहिता], so @deva[इ] → @deva[य्].
advanced: @deva[संहिता] = @deva[परः सन्निकर्षः] (utmost proximity). @deva[दधि] + @deva[अत्र] → @deva[दध्यत्र]. @deva[संहिता] operates within words (always), between words (continuous speech), and across verse boundaries. Sixth @deva[अध्याय] covers @deva[संहिता] operations.

simple:
The absence of a following sound is called @deva[अवसान] 'pause' or 'cessation.'

This term is used when a word ends with no following sound—for example, at the end of a sentence or before a pause in speech.

The term appears in rules like @ref[8.3.15].
standard:
@deva[अवसान] = absence of succeeding letters (pause, cessation).

This defines the condition when a word ends without a following sound. The term occurs in @ref[8.3.15] and related @[sūtras] dealing with final sounds.
advanced: @deva[अवसान] = cessation, absence of succeeding letters. Occurs in @ref[8.3.15] etc. for rules applying at word-final position before pause.

simple:
From here through @ref[2.1.21], compounds described are called @deva[अव्ययीभाव] (adverbial compound). In an @deva[अव्ययीभाव], the first member's meaning is principal.

The name means 'becoming an indeclinable'—the entire compound functions as an @deva[अव्यय] (indeclinable), regardless of what its parts originally were.
standard:
This @deva[अधिकार] extends through @ref[2.1.21]. Compounds described here are @deva[अव्ययीभाव].

The term @deva[अव्ययीभाव] is self-descriptive (like @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय]), not arbitrary (like @deva[डि], @deva[घि]). It means 'becoming an @deva[अव्यय]'—the compound becomes indeclinable.

In @deva[अव्ययीभाव], the first member's sense is principal (@deva[पूर्वपदार्थप्रधान]).
advanced:
@deva[अधिकार] through @ref[2.1.21]. @deva[अव्ययीभाव] = @deva[पूर्वपदार्थप्रधान] @deva[समास].

The term is self-descriptive: @deva[अव्ययी] + @deva[भाव] = 'becoming an @deva[अव्यय].' Compare @deva[कर्मप्रवचनीय] (@ref[1.4.83])—not arbitrary like @deva[डि], @deva[घि].

simple:
An @deva[अव्यय] (indeclinable) invariably compounds with a related @deva[सुबन्त] when expressing these sixteen meanings:

1. @deva[विभक्ति] (case-relation): @deva[अधिस्त्रि] 'concerning women'
2. @deva[समीप] (nearness): @deva[उपकुम्भम्] 'near the jar'
3. @deva[समृद्धि] (prosperity): @deva[सुमद्रम्] 'prosperous with the Madras'
4. @deva[व्यृद्धि] (adversity): @deva[दुर्यवनम्] 'ill with the Yavanas'
5. @deva[अभाव] (absence): @deva[निर्मक्षिकम्] 'free from flies'
6. @deva[अत्यय] (departure): @deva[निर्हिमम्] 'after the cold season'
7. @deva[असम्प्रति] ('no longer'): @deva[अतितैलृकम्] 'past quilt-season'
8. @deva[शब्दप्रादुर्भाव] (sound): @deva[इतिहरि] 'the cry Hari!'
9. @deva[पश्चात्] (after): @deva[अनुरथम्] 'after the chariots'
10. @deva[यथा] (accordance): @deva[यथाशक्ति] 'according to ability'
11. @deva[आनुपूर्व्य] (sequence): @deva[अनुज्येष्ठम्] 'in order of seniority'
12. @deva[यौगपद्य] (simultaneity): @deva[सचक्रम्] 'along with the wheel'
13. @deva[सादृश्य] (resemblance): @deva[ससखि] 'like a friend'
14. @deva[सम्पत्ति] (fitness): @deva[सक्षत्रम्] 'as warriors should'
15. @deva[साकल्य] (totality): @deva[सतृणम्] 'down to the grass'
16. @deva[अन्त] (limit): @deva[साग्नि] 'up to the fire chapter'
standard:
An @deva[अव्यय] invariably (@deva[नित्यम्]) compounds with a @deva[समर्थ] @deva[सुबन्त] when expressing sixteen senses. The word @deva[वचन] is read with each:

| Sense | Example |
|-------|--------|
| @deva[विभक्तिवचन] | @deva[अधिस्त्रि] 'about women' |
| @deva[समीपवचन] | @deva[उपकुम्भम्] 'near the jar' |
| @deva[समृद्धिवचन] | @deva[सुमद्रम्] 'prosperous Madras' |
| @deva[व्यृद्धिवचन] | @deva[दुर्यवनम्] 'ill Yavanas' |
| @deva[अभाववचन] | @deva[निर्मक्षिकम्] 'fly-free' |
| @deva[अत्ययवचन] | @deva[निर्हिमम्] 'winter passed' |
| @deva[असम्प्रतिवचन] | @deva[अतितैलृकम्] 'past quilt-time' |
| @deva[शब्दप्रादुर्भाववचन] | @deva[इतिहरि] 'cry of Hari' |
| @deva[पश्चाद्वचन] | @deva[अनुरथम्] 'after chariots' |
| @deva[यथार्थवचन] | @deva[यथाशक्ति] 'per ability' |
| @deva[आनुपूर्व्यवचन] | @deva[अनुज्येष्ठम्] 'by seniority' |
| @deva[यौगपद्यवचन] | @deva[सचक्रम्] 'with wheel' |
| @deva[सादृश्यवचन] | @deva[ससखि] 'like friend' |
| @deva[सम्पत्तिवचन] | @deva[सक्षत्रम्] 'as warriors' |
| @deva[साकल्यवचन] | @deva[सतृणम्] 'to the grass' |
| @deva[अन्तवचन] | @deva[साग्नि] 'up to fire' |
advanced:
@deva[अव्यय] + @deva[समर्थ] @deva[सुबन्त] → @deva[नित्य] @deva[अव्ययीभाव] in sixteen senses. @deva[वचन] is read with each term.

1. @deva[विभक्तिवचन]: @deva[अधिस्त्रि], @deva[अधिकुमारि]
2. @deva[समीपवचन]: @deva[उपकुम्भम्], @deva[उपमणिकम्]
3. @deva[समृद्धिवचन]: @deva[सुमद्रम्], @deva[सुमगधम्]
4. @deva[व्यृद्धिवचन]: @deva[दुर्गवादिकम्], @deva[दुर्यवनम्]
5. @deva[अभाववचन]: @deva[निर्मक्षिकम्], @deva[निर्मशकम्]
6. @deva[अत्ययवचन]: @deva[निर्हिमम्], @deva[निःशीतम्]
7. @deva[असम्प्रतिवचन]: @deva[अतितैलृकम्]
8. @deva[शब्दप्रादुर्भाववचन]: @deva[इतिहरि]
9. @deva[पश्चाद्वचन]: @deva[अनुरथं पादातम्]
10. @deva[यथार्थवचन]: @deva[अनुरूपम्], @deva[प्रत्यर्थम्], @deva[यथाशक्ति] (4 senses of @deva[यथा]: correspondence, severalty, limit, likeness)
11. @deva[आनुपूर्व्यवचन]: @deva[अनुज्येष्ठम्]
12. @deva[यौगपद्यवचन]: @deva[सचक्रम्]
13. @deva[सादृश्यवचन]: @deva[ससखि]
14. @deva[सम्पत्तिवचन]: @deva[सक्षत्रम्]
15. @deva[साकल्यवचन]: @deva[सतृणम्]
16. @deva[अन्तवचन]: @deva[साग्नि]

simple:
The words @deva[अक्ष] (die), @deva[शलाका] (ivory gambling piece), and numerals (@deva[सङ्ख्या]) compound with @deva[परि] to form @deva[अव्ययीभाव]. These compounds denote unlucky throws in gambling.

Examples:
- @deva[अक्षपरि] 'unlucky throw of dice'
- @deva[शलाकापरि] 'unlucky throw of ivory pieces'
- @deva[एकपरि], @deva[द्विपरि], @deva[त्रिपरि], @deva[चतुष्परि] 'unlucky by one/two/three/four'
standard:
@deva[अक्ष], @deva[शलाका], or @deva[सङ्ख्या] + @deva[परि] → @deva[अव्ययीभाव] (gambling terms).

In the game @deva[पञ्चिका], five dice/pieces are thrown. If all five land same-side up (all faces up or all down), the thrower wins. Otherwise, an unlucky throw:
- @deva[अक्षपरि] 'bad dice throw'
- @deva[एकपरि] through @deva[चतुष्परि] 'off by 1–4'
advanced:
@deva[अक्ष], @deva[शलाका], @deva[सङ्ख्या] + @deva[परि] → @deva[अव्ययीभाव]. Gambling terminology.

@deva[पञ्चिका] game: 5 dice/ivory pieces. Win = all 5 same orientation. Losing throws: @deva[अक्षपरि], @deva[शलाकापरि], @deva[एकपरि], @deva[द्विपरि], @deva[त्रिपरि], @deva[चतुष्परि] (max = 4 off).

simple:
The words @deva[अप] 'away from,' @deva[परि] 'around,' @deva[बहिस्] 'outside,' and direction words from @deva[अञ्चु] (@deva[प्राञ्च्] 'east,' @deva[उदञ्च्] 'north,' etc.) optionally compound with a @deva[पञ्चमी] word to form @deva[अव्ययीभाव].

Examples:
- @deva[अपत्रिगर्तम्] or @deva[अप त्रिगर्तेभ्यः] 'away from Trigarta'
- @deva[बहिर्ग्रामम्] or @deva[बहिर् ग्रामात्] 'outside the village'
- @deva[प्राग्ग्रामम्] or @deva[प्राक् ग्रामात्] 'east of the village'
standard:
@deva[अप], @deva[परि], @deva[बहिस्], @deva[अञ्चु]-derivatives + @deva[पञ्चमी] → optional @deva[अव्ययीभाव].

Examples:
- @deva[अपत्रिगर्तं वृष्टो देवः] or @deva[अप त्रिगर्तेभ्यो वृष्टो देवः] 'it rained away from Trigarta'
- @deva[परित्रिगर्तम्] / @deva[परि त्रिगर्तेभ्यः] 'around Trigarta'
- @deva[बहिर्ग्रामम्] / @deva[बहिर् ग्रामात्] 'outside the village'

@deva[अञ्चु]-derivatives: @deva[प्राञ्च्] (east), @deva[उदञ्च्] (north), etc.

This @deva[सूत्र] implies (@deva[ज्ञापक]) that @deva[बहिस्] governs @deva[पञ्चमी] (see @ref[2.3.29]).

simple:
The word @deva[आङ्] meaning 'limit' (@deva[मर्यादा] = exclusive limit, @deva[अभिविधि] = inclusive limit) optionally compounds with a @deva[पञ्चमी] word to form @deva[अव्ययीभाव].

Examples:
- @deva[आपाटलिपुत्रम्] or @deva[आ पाटलिपुत्रात्] 'up to @[pāṭaliputra]' (exclusive—not including it)
- @deva[आकुमारम्] or @deva[आ कुमारेभ्यः] 'even to the boys' (inclusive—including them)

A numeral (@deva[सङ्ख्या]) may compound with a word meaning 'family member' (@deva[वंश्य]) to form @deva[अव्ययीभाव].

@deva[वंश] = lineage through birth or knowledge (teacher-student succession).

Examples:
- @deva[द्विमुनि व्याकरणस्य] 'grammar with two sages' (@[pāṇini] and @[patañjali])
- @deva[त्रिमुनि व्याकरणस्य] 'grammar with three sages' (adding @[kātyāyana])
- @deva[एकविंशति भारद्वाजम्] 'twenty-one @[bhāradvājas]'
standard:
@deva[सङ्ख्या] + @deva[वंश्य] → optional @deva[अव्ययीभाव].

@deva[वंश] = uninterrupted descent through birth or knowledge (@[guru-śiṣya] lineage).

Examples:
- @deva[द्विमुनि व्याकरणस्य]—two sages (@[pāṇini], @[patañjali])
- @deva[त्रिमुनि व्याकरणस्य]—three (+ @[kātyāyana]/@[śākaṭāyana])

When expressing equality: @deva[द्विमुनि व्याकरणम्] (same case). Birth lineage: @deva[एकविंशति भारद्वाजम्] 'twenty-one @[bhāradvājas].'

These resemble @deva[द्विगु].

simple:
A numeral (@deva[सङ्ख्या]) may compound with river names to form @deva[अव्ययीभाव], denoting where multiple streams of that river meet (@deva[समाहार]).

Examples:
- @deva[सप्तगङ्गम्] 'where seven Ganges streams meet'
- @deva[द्वियमुनम्] 'where two Yamunas meet'
- @deva[पञ्चनदम्] 'where the five rivers meet'
- @deva[सप्तगोदावरम्] 'where seven @[godāvarīs] meet'
standard:
@deva[सङ्ख्या] + river name → optional @deva[अव्ययीभाव] (@deva[समाहार] sense).

Per @[patañjali], these denote confluence:
- @deva[सप्तगङ्गम्] 'meeting of seven Ganges'
- @deva[द्वियमुनम्] 'meeting of two Yamunas'
- @deva[पञ्चनदम्] 'five-river confluence'
- @deva[सप्तगोदावरम्] 'seven @[godāvarīs]'
advanced:
@deva[सङ्ख्या] + @deva[नदीनाम्] → @deva[विभाषा] @deva[अव्ययीभाव] (@deva[समाहार]).

Per @[patañjali]: @deva[सप्तगङ्गम्], @deva[द्वियमुनम्], @deva[पञ्चनदम्], @deva[सप्तगोदावरम्]—confluence of multiple streams.

simple:
A @deva[सुबन्त] invariably compounds with a river name when the compound is a proper name (@deva[संज्ञा]) denoting something other than its parts. The compound is @deva[अव्ययीभाव].

Examples:
- @deva[उन्मत्तगङ्गम्] 'the @[unmatta-gaṅga] region'
- @deva[लोहितगङ्गम्], @deva[कृष्णगङ्गम्], @deva[शनैर्गङ्गम्]

No compound when not a proper name: @deva[शीघ्रो गङ्गो देशः] 'the region where the Ganges flows swiftly.'

From this @deva[सूत्र] through @ref[2.2.23], the word @deva[तत्पुरुष] is understood in each rule. In a @deva[तत्पुरुष] compound, the last member's sense is principal (@deva[उत्तरपदार्थप्रधान]).

@deva[कर्मधारय] and @deva[द्विगु] are sub-types of @deva[तत्पुरुष].
standard:
This @deva[अधिकार] extends through @ref[2.2.23] (the @deva[बहुव्रीहि] section). @deva[तत्पुरुष] is @deva[अनुवृत्त] in all intervening @deva[सूत्र]s.

In @deva[तत्पुरुष], the last member's sense governs: @deva[उत्तरपदार्थप्रधान].

@deva[कर्मधारय] (appositional) and @deva[द्विगु] (numeral) are @deva[तत्पुरुष] sub-types.

The term @deva[तत्पुरुष] predates @[pāṇini]—coined by earlier grammarians.

simple:
The word @deva[खट्वा] 'bedstead' in @deva[द्वितीया] compounds with a @deva[क्त]-ending word when censure (@deva[क्षेप]) is implied. The compound is @deva[तत्पुरुष].

Examples:
- @deva[खट्वारूढः] or @deva[खट्वाप्लुतः] = 'silly, stupid' (lit. 'who ascended the bed')

No compound without censure: @deva[खट्वाम् आरूढः] 'who is lying on a bed' (literal meaning).

Origin: @deva[ब्रह्मचारी]s must sleep on the ground, not beds. One who abandoned studentship prematurely was called @deva[खट्वारूढः]—extended to mean any fool.

A word in @deva[तृतीया] compounds with @deva[पूर्व] 'prior', @deva[सदृश] 'like', @deva[सम] 'similar', @deva[ऊन]-type words ('less'), @deva[कलह] 'quarrel', @deva[निपुण] 'skilled', @deva[मिश्र] 'mixed', and @deva[श्लक्ष्ण] 'polished'.

**Examples:**
- @deva[मासपूर्वः] 'prior by a month'
- @deva[मातृसदृशः] 'like the mother'
- @deva[माषोनम्] 'less by a @deva[माष]'
- @deva[वाक्कलहः] 'word-quarrel'
standard:
@deva[तृतीयान्त] compounds with @deva[पूर्व], @deva[सदृश], @deva[सम], @deva[ऊनार्थक] (words meaning 'less'), @deva[कलह], @deva[निपुण], @deva[मिश्र], @deva[श्लक्ष्ण] → @deva[तत्पुरुष].

This rule incidentally teaches that these words govern @deva[तृतीया].

**Examples:**
- @deva[मासपूर्वः] / @deva[संवत्सरपूर्वः] 'prior by a month/year'
- @deva[मातृसदृशः] / @deva[पितृसदृशः] 'like mother/father'
- @deva[मातृसमः] 'similar to mother'
- @deva[माषोनम्] / @deva[माषविकलम्] 'less by a @deva[माष]'
- @deva[असिकलहः] 'sword-fight', @deva[वाक्कलहः] 'word-fight'
- @deva[वाङ्निपुणः] 'skilled in speech'
- @deva[तिलमिश्रः] 'mixed with sesame'
- @deva[आचारश्लक्ष्णः] 'polished in conduct'
advanced:
@deva[तृतीयान्त] + @deva[पूर्व]/@deva[सदृश]/@deva[सम]/@deva[ऊनार्थक]/@deva[कलह]/@deva[निपुण]/@deva[मिश्र]/@deva[श्लक्ष्ण] → @deva[तत्पुरुष].

**Examples:**
- @deva[मासपूर्वः], @deva[संवत्सरपूर्वः]
- @deva[मातृसदृशः], @deva[पितृसदृशः], @deva[मातृसमः], @deva[पितृसमः]
- @deva[माषोनम्], @deva[माषविकलम्]
- @deva[असिकलहः], @deva[वाक्कलहः]
- @deva[वाङ्निपुणः], @deva[आचारनिपुणः]
- @deva[तिलमिश्रः], @deva[गुडमिश्रः]
- @deva[आचारश्लक्ष्णः]

@note[type=@[vārtika]]: @deva[अवर] should also be listed: @deva[मासावरः] 'later than a month'.

simple:
A word in @deva[तृतीया] denoting @deva[मिश्रीकरण] (a flavoring/mixing ingredient) compounds with @deva[भक्ष्य] words (any edible, hard or soft) to form @deva[तत्पुरुष].

**Examples:**
- @deva[गुडधानाः] 'jaggery-barley' (barley mixed with jaggery)
- @deva[गुडपृथकाः] 'jaggery-flattened rice'

A word in @deva[पञ्चमी] compounds with @deva[अपेत] 'gone', @deva[अपोढ] 'carried away', @deva[मुक्त] 'freed', @deva[पतित] 'fallen', @deva[अपत्रस्त] 'frightened'—but only when the separation is gradual (@deva[अल्पशः]), not sudden or violent.

**Examples:**
- @deva[सुखापेतः] 'departed from happiness'
- @deva[स्वर्गपतितः] 'fallen from heaven' (gradual decline)

**Counter-example:** @deva[प्रासादात् पतितः] 'fallen from a mansion'—no compound; the fall is sudden.
standard:
@deva[पञ्चम्यन्त] + @deva[अपेत]/@deva[अपोढ]/@deva[मुक्त]/@deva[पतित]/@deva[अपत्रस्त] → @deva[तत्पुरुष], when the separation is @deva[अल्पशः] (gradual, not sudden).

**Examples:**
- @deva[सुखापेतः] 'gone from pleasure'
- @deva[कल्पनापोढः] 'carried away by imagination'
- @deva[चक्रमुक्तः] 'freed from the wheel'
- @deva[स्वर्गपतितः] 'fallen from heaven'
- @deva[तरङ्गापत्रस्तः] 'frightened of the waves'

**Counter-examples (sudden, not gradual):**
- @deva[प्रासादात् पतितः] 'fallen from a mansion'
- @deva[भोजनादपत्रस्तः] 'afraid of eating'

This expands @ref[2.1.32].
advanced:
@deva[पञ्चम्यन्त] + @deva[अपेत]/@deva[अपोढ]/@deva[मुक्त]/@deva[पतित]/@deva[अपत्रस्त] → @deva[तत्पुरुष] when @deva[अल्पशः] (gradual separation).

**Examples:** @deva[सुखापेतः], @deva[कल्पनापोढः], @deva[चक्रमुक्तः], @deva[स्वर्गपतितः], @deva[तरङ्गापत्रस्तः].

**@deva[अल्पशः] restricts scope:** Sudden/violent events don't compound: @deva[प्रासादात् पतितः] 'fallen from mansion', @deva[भोजनादपत्रस्तः] 'afraid of eating'.

Expands @ref[2.1.32].

simple:
A word in the genitive case forms a @deva[तत्पुरुष] compound with @deva[याजक] 'sacrificer' and similar words, even though @ref[2.2.16] would otherwise prohibit it.

**Examples:**
- @deva[ब्राह्मणयाजकः] 'one who sacrifices for @deva[ब्राह्मण]s'
- @deva[क्षत्रिययाजकः] 'one who sacrifices for @deva[क्षत्रिय]s'

A word in the @deva[षष्ठी] (genitive) case does not form a compound with:
1. A word having ordinal sense (@deva[पूरणार्थ]): @deva[छात्राणां पञ्चमः] 'fifth among students'
2. An adjective/attribute (@deva[गुणवचन]): @deva[काकस्य कार्ष्ण्यम्] 'the blackness of the crow'
3. A word meaning 'satisfied' (@deva[तृप्त्यर्थ]): @deva[फलानां सुहितः] 'satisfied with fruits'
4. A @deva[सत्]-participle (@ref[3.2.127]): @deva[ब्राह्मणस्य कुर्वन्] 'the @deva[ब्राह्मण]'s doing'
5. An indeclinable: @deva[ब्राह्मणस्य कृत्वा] 'having been done by a @deva[ब्राह्मण]'
6. A @deva[तव्य]-word: @deva[ब्राह्मणस्य कर्तव्यम्] (but @deva[तव्यत्]-affixed words compound: @deva[ब्राह्मणकर्तव्यम्])
7. A word denoting the same referent (@deva[समानाधिकरण]): @deva[राज्ञः पाटलिपुत्रकस्य] 'of the king of @deva[पाटलिपुत्र]'
standard:
A @deva[षष्ठी]-ending word does not compound with these seven categories:

1. **@deva[पूरणार्थ]:** @deva[छात्राणां पञ्चमः], @deva[छात्राणां दशमः]
2. **@deva[गुणवचन]:** @deva[काकस्य कार्ष्ण्यम्], @deva[वलाकायाः शौक्ल्यम्]
3. **@deva[तृप्त्यर्थ]:** @deva[फलानां सुहितः], @deva[फलानां तृप्तः]
4. **@deva[सत्]-participle:** @deva[ब्राह्मणस्य कुर्वन्], @deva[ब्राह्मणस्य कुर्वाणः]
5. **@deva[अव्यय]:** @deva[ब्राह्मणस्य कृत्वा], @deva[ब्राह्मणस्य हुत्वा]
6. **@deva[तव्य]:** @deva[ब्राह्मणस्य कर्तव्यम्] (but @deva[तव्यत्] with @deva[इत्]-@deva[त्] compounds: @deva[ब्राह्मणकर्तव्यम्]—see @ref[3.1.96])
7. **@deva[समानाधिकरण]:** @deva[राज्ञः पाटलिपुत्रकस्य], @deva[पाणिनेः सूत्रकारस्य]

But words in apposition can compound by @ref[2.1.57]: @deva[सूत्रकारपाणिनिः]—there the position differs.
advanced:
@deva[षष्ठी] does not compound with:

1. **@deva[पूरणार्थ]:** @deva[छात्राणां पञ्चमः]
2. **@deva[गुणवचन]:** @deva[काकस्य कार्ष्ण्यम्]
3. **@deva[तृप्त्यर्थ]:** @deva[फलानां सुहितः]
4. **@deva[सदन्त]:** @deva[ब्राह्मणस्य कुर्वन्]
5. **@deva[अव्यय]:** @deva[ब्राह्मणस्य कृत्वा]
6. **@deva[तव्य]:** @deva[ब्राह्मणस्य कर्तव्यम्] (but @deva[तव्यत्] compounds: @deva[ब्राह्मणकर्तव्यम्])
7. **@deva[समानाधिकरण]:** @deva[राज्ञः पाटलिपुत्रकस्य]

By @ref[2.1.57], @deva[समानाधिकरण] words compound with reversed order: @deva[सूत्रकारपाणिनिः].

simple:
A word in the genitive case does not form a compound with a @deva[क्त]-ending word when @deva[क्त] expresses 'location' (where something happened). This applies to @deva[क्त] from roots meaning 'sitting', 'motion', or 'eating' (@ref[3.4.76]).

**Examples (no compound):**
- @deva[इदमेषां यातम्] 'here they went'
- @deva[इदमेषां भुक्तम्] 'here they ate'
- @deva[इदमेषामासितम्] 'here they sat'
standard:
A @deva[षष्ठी]-ending word does not compound with @deva[क्त] when @deva[क्त] denotes the location of an action. By @ref[3.4.76], @deva[क्त] after roots of @deva[स्थान] (fixedness), @deva[गति] (motion), or @deva[आसन] (eating) gives both agent and location sense.

**Examples:** @deva[इदमेषां यातम्] 'this is where they went', @deva[इदमेषां भुक्तम्] 'this is where they ate'.
advanced:
@deva[षष्ठी] does not compound with @deva[क्त] when @deva[क्त] has @deva[आधारार्थ] (locative sense). By @ref[3.4.76], @deva[क्त] after @deva[स्थानास्तिगतिभुज्]-roots gives @deva[कर्तृ] and @deva[आधार] sense.

**Examples:** @deva[इदमेषां यातम्], @deva[इदमेषां भुक्तम्], @deva[इदमेषामासितम्].

simple:
A word in the genitive case invariably (@deva[नित्य]) forms a @deva[तत्पुरुष] compound with an @deva[अक]-ending word when the @deva[अक] affix denotes sport or livelihood. Such compounds cannot be analyzed.

**Examples (sport):**
- @deva[उद्दालकपुष्पभञ्जिका] 'the @deva[उद्दालक]-flower-breaking game'
- @deva[वारणपुष्पप्रचायिका] 'the flower-gathering game'

**Examples (livelihood):**
- @deva[दन्तलेखकः] 'tooth-painter (by profession)'
- @deva[नखलेखकः] 'nail-painter (by profession)'
standard:
A @deva[षष्ठी]-ending word obligatorily compounds with @deva[अकन्त] words when @deva[अक] denotes @deva[क्रीडा] (sport) or @deva[जीविका] (livelihood). This is @deva[नित्यसमास]—incapable of dissolution.

The @deva[अनुवृत्ति] of @deva[न] (prohibition) ends here.

@deva[तृच्] never has @deva[क्रीडा]/@deva[जीविका] sense—only @deva[अक] examples exist.

**@deva[क्रीडा]:** @deva[उद्दालकपुष्पभञ्जिका] (eastern districts' game), @deva[वारणपुष्पप्रचायिका].

**@deva[जीविका]:** @deva[दन्तलेखकः], @deva[नखलेखकः].

**Why @deva[क्रीडा]/@deva[जीविका]?** Otherwise @deva[ओदनस्य भोजकः]—no compound.
advanced:
@deva[षष्ठी] obligatorily compounds with @deva[अकन्त] in @deva[क्रीडार्थ] or @deva[जीविकार्थ]. This is @deva[नित्यसमास].

The @deva[प्रतिषेध] (@deva[न]) from @ref[2.2.10] ends.

**@deva[क्रीडा]:** @deva[उद्दालकपुष्पभञ्जिका], @deva[वारणपुष्पप्रचायिका].

**@deva[जीविका]:** @deva[दन्तलेखकः], @deva[नखलेखकः].

**Counter-example:** @deva[ओदनस्य भोजकः]—neither @deva[क्रीडा] nor @deva[जीविका].

simple:
The indeclinable @deva[कु] 'bad', particles called @deva[गति] (@ref[1.4.60]), and the prefixes @deva[प्रादि] invariably form a @deva[तत्पुरुष] compound with words they govern.

**Examples:**
- @deva[कु]: @deva[कुपुरुषः] 'bad man'
- @deva[गति]: @deva[उररीकृतम्] 'asserted'
- @deva[प्रादि] as particles (not @deva[उपसर्ग]): @deva[दुष्पुरुषः] 'bad man', @deva[सुपुरुषः] 'good man', @deva[अतिपुरुषः] 'excellent man', @deva[आपिङ्गलः] 'brownish'
standard:
The @deva[अव्यय] @deva[कु] 'bad', @deva[गति] words (@ref[1.4.60]), and @deva[प्रादि] (when mere particles, not @deva[उपसर्ग]) obligatorily compound. This is @deva[नित्यसमास].

**@deva[कु]:** @deva[कुपुरुषः] 'sinful man'.

**@deva[गति]:** @deva[उररीकृतम्] (@ref[1.4.61]).

**@deva[प्रादि] as particles:** @deva[दुर्] = 'bad': @deva[दुष्पुरुषः]; @deva[सु]/@deva[अति] = 'respect': @deva[सुपुरुषः], @deva[अतिपुरुषः]; @deva[आ] = 'slightly': @deva[आपिङ्गलः].

These are typically attributive but found elsewhere: @deva[कदुष्णम्]/@deva[कवोष्णम्] 'tepid', @deva[दुष्कृतम्], @deva[अतिस्तुतम्], @deva[आबद्धम्].
advanced:
@deva[कु], @deva[गति], and @deva[प्रादि] (as @deva[निपात], not @deva[उपसर्ग]) obligatorily compound. @deva[नित्यसमास].

**@deva[कु]:** @deva[कुपुरुषः].

**@deva[गति]:** @deva[उररीकृतम्] (@ref[1.4.60]-@ref[1.4.61]).

**@deva[प्रादि] as @deva[निपात]:** @deva[दुष्पुरुषः], @deva[सुपुरुषः], @deva[अतिपुरुषः], @deva[आपिङ्गलः].

Generally @deva[विशेषण], but also: @deva[कदुष्णम्], @deva[दुष्कृतम्], @deva[अतिस्तुतम्], @deva[आबद्धम्].

simple:
An @deva[उपपद] compounds with an @deva[अव्यय] (indeclinable) only when the @deva[अव्यय] ends in the affix @deva[अम्] (such as @deva[ण्मुल्]—@ref[3.4.26]).

**Examples:**
- @deva[स्वादुकारं भुङ्क्ते] 'he eats having made it sweet'
- @deva[लवणकारम्] 'having seasoned'

**Not with other @deva[अव्यय]s:** @deva[कालो भोक्तुम्] 'time to eat'—the @deva[अव्यय] ends in @deva[उम्] (@deva[तुमुन्]), not @deva[अम्].
standard:
@deva[उपपद] compounds with @deva[अव्यय]s only when they end in @deva[अम्] (@deva[ण्मुल्] etc.). This restricts @ref[2.2.19].

**Examples:** @deva[स्वादुकारम्], @deva[लवणकारम्].

**Not @deva[तुमुन्]:** @deva[कालो भोक्तुम्]—@deva[तुमुन्] ends in @deva[उम्], not @deva[अम्].

**Why 'only'?** This composition occurs only when @deva[अम्] is the sole affix ordained for that @deva[उपपद]-@deva[धातु] combination. When @deva[अम्] is co-ordained with @deva[क्त्वा] (@ref[3.4.24]), no compound: @deva[अग्रेभोजम्] 'having first eaten'—no compound.
advanced:
@deva[उपपद] compounds with @deva[अमन्त]-@deva[अव्यय]s only. Restricts @ref[2.2.19].

**Examples:** @deva[स्वादुकारम्], @deva[लवणकारम्].

**@deva[तुमुन्] excluded:** @deva[कालो भोक्तुम्]—@deva[तुमुन्] = @deva[उम्], not @deva[अम्].

**@deva[एवकार]:** Compounding requires @deva[अम्] to be the sole affix ordained (@deva[तुल्यविधान]). Where @deva[क्त्वा] is co-ordained (@ref[3.4.24]): @deva[अग्रेभोजम्]—no compound.

simple:
An @deva[उपपद] ending in the third case-affix (or remaining cases) optionally compounds with a word ending in the affix @deva[क्त्वा]. When compounded, @deva[क्त्वा] is replaced by @deva[ल्यप्] (@ref[7.1.37]).

**Examples:**
- @deva[उच्चैःकृत्य] or @deva[उच्चैः कृत्वा] 'having made loud'

The @deva[उपपद] must be in 3rd case etc.—so no compound in @deva[अलं कृत्वा] or @deva[खलु कृत्वा].
standard:
@deva[तृतीयादि]-@deva[उपपद] optionally compounds with @deva[क्त्वान्त] words. This handles @deva[क्त्वा]-ending @deva[अव्यय]s not covered by @ref[2.2.20]-@ref[2.2.21].

When compounded, @deva[क्त्वा] → @deva[ल्यप्] (@ref[7.1.37]); otherwise not.

**Example:** @deva[उच्चैःकृत्य] ~ @deva[उच्चैः कृत्वा].

**Why @deva[तृतीयादि]?** @deva[अलं कृत्वा], @deva[खलु कृत्वा]—no compound.

simple:
The remaining compound (one not covered by previous rules) is called @deva[बहुव्रीहि]. A @deva[बहुव्रीहि] compound denotes something beyond what its individual members denote.

**Example:** @deva[चित्रगुः] 'one who has a spotted cow' (@deva[चित्रा गौः यस्य सः])
standard:
Any compound not falling under the preceding rules is @deva[बहुव्रीहि]. This is an @deva[अधिकार] extending through @ref[2.2.28].

**Example:** @deva[चित्रगुः] 'possessing a spotted cow'.
advanced:
@deva[शेष] compound = @deva[बहुव्रीहि]. This @deva[अधिकार] extends to @ref[2.2.28].

**Example:** @deva[चित्रगुः].

simple:
Two or more words ending in case-affixes form a @deva[बहुव्रीहि] compound when together they denote something new—not denoted by the individual words. The compound takes the meaning of all case-affixes except the first.

**Examples:**
- @deva[प्राप्तोदको ग्रामः] 'a village that has received water' (@deva[प्राप्तमुदकं यं ग्रामम्])
- @deva[ऊढरथोऽनड्वान्] 'a bull by whom a cart is drawn'
- @deva[उपहृतपशूरुद्रः] '@deva[रुद्र] to whom cattle is offered'
- @deva[उद्धृतौदना स्थाली] 'a pot in which rice is placed'
- @deva[चित्रगुर्देवदत्तः] '@deva[देवदत्त] who has a spotted cow'
- @deva[वीरपुरुषको ग्रामः] 'a village possessing heroic men'
standard:
Two or more @deva[सुबन्त] words form @deva[बहुव्रीहि] when denoting @deva[अन्यपदार्थ] (something other than what the members individually mean). The compound acquires the sense of non-first @deva[विभक्ति]s.

**Examples:** @deva[प्राप्तोदको ग्रामः], @deva[ऊढरथोऽनड्वान्], @deva[उपहृतपशूरुद्रः], @deva[उद्धृतौदना स्थाली], @deva[चित्रगुर्देवदत्तः], @deva[वीरपुरुषको ग्रामः].

**Not @deva[प्रथमार्थ]:** @deva[वृष्टे देवे गतः] 'gone when it had rained'—no @deva[बहुव्रीहि] with @deva[प्रथमा] sense.

**Why @deva[बहूनि]?** Many words may compound:

@deva[सुसूक्ष्मजटकेशेन सुभाजिनवाससा] | @deva[पुत्री पर्वतराजस्य कुतो हेतोर्विवाहिता] ||
advanced:
@deva[बहूनि सुबन्तानि] form @deva[बहुव्रीहि] when denoting @deva[अन्यपदार्थ]. The compound acquires @deva[अप्रथमार्थ]-@deva[विभक्ति] senses.

**Examples:** @deva[प्राप्तोदको ग्रामः], @deva[ऊढरथोऽनड्वान्], @deva[उपहृतपशूरुद्रः], @deva[उद्धृतौदना स्थाली], @deva[चित्रगुः], @deva[वीरपुरुषको ग्रामः].

**@deva[प्रथमार्थ] excluded:** @deva[वृष्टे देवे गतः]—no @deva[बहुव्रीहि].

**@deva[बहूनि]:** @deva[सुसूक्ष्मजटकेशेन सुभाजिनवाससा पुत्री पर्वतराजस्य...]—multi-member @deva[बहुव्रीहि].

simple:
Indeclinables, @deva[आसन्न] 'near', @deva[अदूर] 'near', @deva[अधिक] 'more', and @deva[संख्या] (numeral) words form a @deva[बहुव्रीहि] compound with another @deva[संख्या] word when the meaning is numerical.

**Examples:**
- @deva[उपदशाः] 'about ten' (nine or eleven)—see @ref[5.4.73]
- @deva[उपविंशः] 'about twenty' (nineteen or twenty-one)—see @ref[6.4.142]
- @deva[आसन्नदशाः] 'near ten'
- @deva[अधिकदशाः] 'more than ten' (eleven)
- @deva[द्वित्राः] 'two or three'
- @deva[द्विदशाः] 'twenty'
standard:
@deva[अव्यय], @deva[आसन्न], @deva[अदूर], @deva[अधिक], and @deva[संख्या] words compound with @deva[संख्या] when the sense is numerical. This is @deva[बहुव्रीहि].

**Examples:** @deva[उपदशाः] (@ref[5.4.73]), @deva[उपविंशः] (@ref[6.4.142]), @deva[आसन्नदशाः], @deva[अदूरदशाः], @deva[अधिकदशाः], @deva[द्वित्राः], @deva[द्विदशाः].

**Why 'with @deva[संख्या]'?** @deva[पञ्च ब्राह्मणाः] 'five @deva[ब्राह्मण]s'—no compound; @deva[ब्राह्मण] ≠ @deva[संख्या].

**Why '@deva[अव्यय] etc.'?** @deva[ब्राह्मणाः पञ्च]—no compound.

**Why 'numerical sense'?** @deva[अधिका विंशतिर्गवाम्] 'more than twenty of cows'—not numerical sense, no compound.
advanced:
@deva[अव्यय], @deva[आसन्न], @deva[अदूर], @deva[अधिक], @deva[संख्या] compound with @deva[संख्या] when @deva[संख्यार्थ]. @deva[बहुव्रीहि].

**Examples:** @deva[उपदशाः], @deva[उपविंशः], @deva[आसन्नदशाः], @deva[अधिकदशाः], @deva[द्वित्राः], @deva[द्विदशाः].

**Restrictions:**
- @deva[संख्यया]: @deva[पञ्च ब्राह्मणाः]—no compound
- @deva[अव्ययादि]: @deva[ब्राह्मणाः पञ्च]—no compound
- @deva[संख्यार्थ]: @deva[अधिका विंशतिर्गवाम्]—no compound

simple:
Words that are names of cardinal directions form a @deva[बहुव्रीहि] compound when the compound signifies an intermediate direction.

**Examples:**
- @deva[दक्षिणपूर्वा] 'south-east' (midway between south and east)
- @deva[पूर्वोत्तरा] 'north-east'

Derivative direction-words do not compound: @deva[ऐन्द्र्याश्च कौबेर्याश्च दिशोर्यदन्तरालम्] 'the point between east and north'—@deva[ऐन्द्री] 'east' and @deva[कौबेरी] 'north' are derivatives, so no compound.
standard:
Direction-names (@deva[दिङ्नामानि]) compound when signifying @deva[अन्तराल] (intermediate point). @deva[बहुव्रीहि].

**Examples:** @deva[दक्षिणपूर्वा] (@deva[दक्षिणपूर्वा दिक्]), @deva[पूर्वोत्तरा].

**Why @deva[नाम]?** Derivative words (@deva[ऐन्द्री], @deva[कौबेरी]) whose primary sense is not direction do not compound: @deva[ऐन्द्र्याश्च कौबेर्याश्च दिशोर्यदन्तरालम्].

simple:
Words in a relation expressible by 'and' (@deva[च]) form a compound called @deva[द्वन्द्व]. The compound expresses either mutual conjunction or an aggregate.

**Example:** @deva[राम] and @deva[लक्ष्मण] → @deva[रामलक्ष्मणौ]
standard: Several @deva[सुबन्त] words in @deva[च]-relation (conjunctive) form @deva[द्वन्द्व]. The meanings are @deva[इतरेतरयोग] (mutual conjunction) or @deva[समाहार] (aggregate).
advanced: @deva[चार्थ]-related @deva[सुबन्त]s form @deva[द्वन्द्व]. Meanings: @deva[इतरेतरयोग] or @deva[समाहार].

simple:
The @deva[उपसर्जन] (subordinate member, per @ref[1.2.43]) is placed first in a compound.

**Example:** @deva[राजपुरुषः] 'king's man' (not *@deva[पुरुषराजः])

The @deva[उपसर्जन] is the word shown in the nominative case in compounding rules—it is subordinate to the principal member.
advanced:
@deva[उपसर्जन] (@ref[1.2.43]) is placed first (@deva[पूर्वनिपात]). @deva[समासे] understood.

**Example:** @deva[राजपुरुषः].

simple:
In a @deva[द्वन्द्व] compound, a @deva[घि]-ending word (@ref[1.4.7]—ending in short @deva[इ] or @deva[उ]) is placed first.

**Examples:**
- @deva[हरिहरौ] '@deva[हरि] and @deva[हर]'
- @deva[पटुगुप्तौ]
- @deva[मृदुगुप्तौ]
standard:
In @deva[द्वन्द्व], a @deva[घि]-word (@ref[1.4.7]) is placed first.

**Examples:** @deva[हरिहरौ], @deva[पटुगुप्तौ], @deva[मृदुगुप्तौ].

When multiple @deva[घि]-words: @deva[मृदुपटुगुप्ताः]—place the longer-voweled first, then @deva[गुप्त] (non-@deva[घि]) last.
advanced:
In @deva[द्वन्द्व], @deva[घि] (@ref[1.4.7]) is placed first.

**Examples:** @deva[हरिहरौ], @deva[पटुगुप्तौ], @deva[मृदुगुप्तौ].

Multiple @deva[घि]: @deva[मृदुपटुगुप्ताः].

simple:
In a @deva[द्वन्द्व] compound, a word that begins with a vowel and ends in short @deva[अ] is placed first.

**Examples:**
- @deva[उष्ट्रखरम्] 'camel and donkey'
- @deva[उष्ट्रशशकम्] 'camel and hare'
standard:
In @deva[द्वन्द्व], a word beginning with a vowel and ending in short @deva[अ] (@deva[अजाद्यतन्त]) is placed first.

**Examples:** @deva[उष्ट्रखरम्], @deva[उष्ट्रशशकम्].
advanced:
In @deva[द्वन्द्व], @deva[अजाद्यतन्त] (vowel-initial, short-@deva[अ]-final) is placed first.

**Examples:** @deva[उष्ट्रखरम्], @deva[उष्ट्रशशकम्].

simple:
In a @deva[द्वन्द्व] compound, the word with fewer vowels is placed first.

**Examples:**
- @deva[प्लक्षन्यग्रोधौ] (@deva[प्लक्ष] has 2 vowels, @deva[न्यग्रोध] has 3)
- @deva[धवखदिरपलाशाः]
standard:
In @deva[द्वन्द्व], the word with fewer vowels (@deva[अल्पाच्]) is placed first.

**Examples:** @deva[प्लक्षन्यग्रोधौ] (2 vs 3), @deva[धवखदिरपलाशाः].

With many words, the least-voweled first, then increasing.
advanced:
In @deva[द्वन्द्व], @deva[अल्पाच्] (fewer vowels) is placed first.

**Examples:** @deva[प्लक्षन्यग्रोधौ], @deva[धवखदिरपलाशाः].

simple:
In a @deva[बहुव्रीहि] compound, a word ending in @deva[निष्ठा] (@deva[क्त] or @deva[क्तवतु]—@ref[1.1.26]) is placed first.

**Examples:**
- @deva[युक्तयोगः] 'one devoted to devotion'
- @deva[कृतकठः] 'one who has made a mat'
- @deva[भिन्नघटः] 'one whose pot is broken'
standard:
@deva[निष्ठान्त] (@deva[क्त]/@deva[क्तवतु]) is placed first in @deva[बहुव्रीहि].

**Examples:** @deva[युक्तयोगः], @deva[कृतकठः], @deva[भिन्नघटः].
advanced:
@deva[निष्ठान्त] (@ref[1.1.26]) is placed first in @deva[बहुव्रीहि].

**Examples:** @deva[युक्तयोगः], @deva[कृतकठः], @deva[भिन्नघटः].

simple:
The @deva[कर्तृ] (agent) or @deva[करण] (instrument) takes @deva[तृतीया] (instrumental).

**Examples:**
- @deva[देवदत्तेन कृतम्] 'done by Devadatta' (agent)
- @deva[दात्रेण लुनाति] 'he cuts with a sickle' (instrument)
- @deva[यज्ञदत्तेन भुक्तम्] 'eaten by @[yajñadatta]'
- @deva[परशुना छिनत्ति] 'he splits with an axe'
standard:
The @deva[कर्तृ] ('independent agent' per @ref[1.4.54]) or @deva[करण] ('most effective means' per @ref[1.4.42]) takes @deva[तृतीया] (instrumental).

**Examples:**
- @deva[देवदत्तेन कृतम्] 'done by Devadatta' (agent in passive)
- @deva[दात्रेण लुनाति] 'he cuts with a sickle' (instrument)
- @deva[यज्ञदत्तेन भुक्तम्] 'eaten by @[yajñadatta]'
- @deva[परशुना छिनत्ति] 'he splits with an axe'

@note[type=@[vārtika]]: The following words also take @deva[तृतीया]: @deva[प्रकृति] 'original', @deva[प्राय] 'almost', @deva[गोत्र] 'lineage', @deva[सम] 'equal', @deva[विषम] 'unequal', @deva[द्विद्रोण] '@[two-droṇa] measure', @deva[पञ्चक] 'five', @deva[साहस्र] 'thousand'. Examples: @deva[प्रायेण याज्ञिकः] 'mostly a ritualist', @deva[गार्ग्योऽस्मि गोत्रेण] 'I am @[gārgya] by lineage', @deva[समेन धावति] 'runs equally'.

simple:
With @deva[सह] 'together with', the accompaniment takes @deva[तृतीया]—when it is not the principal.

**Example:**
- @deva[पुत्रेण सहागतः पिता] 'the father came with the son'
- @deva[पुत्रेण सार्धम्] 'together with the son'
standard:
When @deva[सह] 'with' is joined to a word, that word takes @deva[तृतीया]—when it denotes the accompaniment, not the principal.

**Examples:**
- @deva[पुत्रेण सहागतः पिता] 'the father came with the son'
- @deva[पुत्रेण सार्धम्] 'together with the son' (synonym of @deva[सह])

This also applies when @deva[सह] is understood but not expressed, as @[pāṇini] himself uses in @ref[1.2.65]: @deva[वृद्धो युना] 'an elder with a youth'.

**Why 'not the principal'?** In @deva[शिष्येण सहोपाध्यायस्य गौः] 'the cow of the teacher [who is] with the student', the student is the principal—the teacher accompanies.
advanced:
With @deva[सह] (and synonyms like @deva[सार्धम्], @deva[समम्]), the accompaniment takes @deva[तृतीया]—when not the principal.

**Examples:** @deva[पुत्रेण सहागतः पिता], @deva[पुत्रेण सार्धम्].

The condition 'not principal' (@deva[अप्रधान]) distinguishes accompaniment from joint agency. Counterexample: @deva[शिष्येण सहोपाध्यायस्य गौः] 'the teacher's cow [who is] with the student'—here the student is principal.

@[pāṇini] uses implicit @deva[सह] in @ref[1.2.65]: @deva[वृद्धो युना].

simple:
The body part (@deva[अङ्ग]) by which a person's defect is indicated takes @deva[तृतीया].

**Examples:**
- @deva[अक्ष्णा काणः] 'blind in one eye'
- @deva[पादेन खञ्जः] 'lame in the foot'
- @deva[पाणिना कुण्ठः] 'maimed in the hand'
standard:
The body part (@deva[अङ्ग]) by which a person's physical defect is indicated takes @deva[तृतीया].

**Examples:**
- @deva[अक्ष्णा काणः] 'blind in one eye'
- @deva[पादेन खञ्जः] 'lame in the foot'
- @deva[पाणिना कुण्ठः] 'maimed in the hand'

The word @deva[अङ्ग] here means any part of the body. The defect is predicated of the whole person, but the limb specifying the defect takes @deva[तृतीया].
advanced:
The @deva[अङ्ग] (body part) by which a person's defect (@deva[विकार]) is indicated takes @deva[तृतीया].

**Examples:** @deva[अक्ष्णा काणः], @deva[पादेन खञ्जः], @deva[पाणिना कुण्ठः].

The term @deva[अङ्ग] covers any bodily member. The defect is predicated of the whole person, but the specific limb takes @deva[तृतीया].

simple:
The mark or sign by which a state (@deva[इत्थम्भूत]) is indicated takes @deva[तृतीया].

**Examples:**
- @deva[जटाभिस्तापसः] 'he is an ascetic by his matted hair'
- @deva[कमण्डलुना छात्रम्] 'the student [recognized] by his water-pot'
- @deva[शिखया परिव्राजकम्] 'a wandering mendicant by his topknot'
standard:
The mark or attribute by which a state or condition (@deva[इत्थम्भूत]) is indicated takes @deva[तृतीया].

**Examples:**
- @deva[जटाभिस्तापसः] 'he is an ascetic by his matted hair'
- @deva[अपि भवान् कमण्डलुना छात्रमद्राक्षीत्] 'did you see the student by his water-pot?'
- @deva[छात्रेणोपाध्यायम्] 'a teacher [recognized] by having students'
- @deva[शिखया परिव्राजकम्] 'a mendicant by his topknot'

**Counterexample:** @deva[कमण्डलुपाणिछात्रः] 'a student with water-pot in hand'—here the mark is hidden in the compound, so @deva[तृतीया] doesn't apply.

**Why @deva[इत्थम्भूत]?** This specifies 'being in such a state'. In @deva[वृक्षं प्रति विद्योतनम्] 'lightning towards the tree', no state is indicated, so @deva[तृतीया] doesn't apply.
advanced:
The mark (@deva[लक्षण]) indicating @deva[इत्थम्भूत] (a particular state) takes @deva[तृतीया].

**Examples:** @deva[जटाभिस्तापसः], @deva[कमण्डलुना छात्रम्], @deva[छात्रेणोपाध्यायम्], @deva[शिखया परिव्राजकम्].

**Why @deva[इत्थम्भूत]?** The term means 'being in such a state'. Counterexample: @deva[वृक्षं प्रति विद्योतनम्]—no state indicated.

**Compound exception:** @deva[कमण्डलुपाणिछात्रः]—the mark is hidden (@deva[गुप्त]) in the compound @deva[कमण्डलुपाणि], so @deva[तृतीया] doesn't apply externally.

simple:
With @deva[सम् + ज्ञा] 'to recognize', the object optionally takes @deva[तृतीया].

**Examples:**
- @deva[पित्रा संजानीते] or @deva[पितरं संजानीते] 'he recognizes his father'
- @deva[मात्रा संजानीते] or @deva[मातरं संजानीते] 'he recognizes his mother'
standard:
With the verb @deva[सम् + ज्ञा] 'to recognize', the object optionally takes @deva[तृतीया] (instead of the regular @deva[द्वितीया]).

**Examples:**
- @deva[पित्रा संजानीते] or @deva[पितरं संजानीते] 'he recognizes his father'
- @deva[मात्रा संजानीते] or @deva[मातरं संजानीते] 'he recognizes his mother'
advanced:
With @deva[सम् + ज्ञा], the @deva[कर्मन्] optionally takes @deva[तृतीया].

**Examples:** @deva[पित्रा/पितरं संजानीते], @deva[मात्रा/मातरं संजानीते].

simple:
A word denoting cause (@deva[हेतु]) takes @deva[तृतीया].

**Examples:**
- @deva[विद्यया यशः] 'fame through learning'
- @deva[धनेन कुलम्] 'family through wealth'
- @deva[कन्यया शोकः] 'grief through a daughter'
standard:
A word denoting cause (@deva[हेतु]) takes @deva[तृतीया].

**Examples:**
- @deva[विद्यया यशः] 'fame is produced by learning'
- @deva[धनेन कुलम्] 'family [status comes] by wealth'
- @deva[कन्यया शोकः] 'grief [comes] by a daughter'

The word @deva[हेतु] here is used in its popular sense (anything capable of accomplishing a desired result), not the technical grammatical sense of @ref[1.4.55].
advanced:
A @deva[हेतु] (cause) takes @deva[तृतीया].

**Examples:** @deva[विद्यया यशः], @deva[धनेन कुलम्], @deva[कन्यया शोकः].

The term @deva[हेतु] here is @deva[लौकिक] (popular usage)—anything capable of producing a result—not the technical @deva[हेतु] of @ref[1.4.55] (@deva[तत्प्रयोजको हेतुश्च]).

simple:
A word denoting debt, considered as cause but not as agent, takes @deva[पञ्चमी] (ablative).

**Example:**
- @deva[शताद्बद्धः] 'he was imprisoned for a debt of one hundred'
standard:
A word denoting debt (@deva[ऋण]), considered as cause (@deva[हेतु]) but not as agent (@deva[कर्तृ]), takes @deva[पञ्चमी] (ablative).

**Example:**
- @deva[शताद्बद्धः] 'he was imprisoned on account of a debt of one hundred'

**Why 'not denoting agent'?** When the debt is personified as an agent (instigator), @deva[तृतीया] applies: @deva[शतेन बन्धितः] 'a debt of one hundred caused him to be imprisoned'—here @deva[शत] is a @deva[प्रयोजक हेतु] (instigating cause), hence an agent.
advanced:
A @deva[ऋण]-denoting word, when @deva[हेतु] but not @deva[कर्तृ], takes @deva[पञ्चमी].

**Example:** @deva[शताद्बद्धः] 'imprisoned for a debt of 100'.

**Counterexample:** @deva[शतेन बन्धितः]—here @deva[शत] is @deva[प्रयोजक हेतु] (instigating cause = agent), so @deva[तृतीया] applies per @ref[2.3.18].

simple:
A quality-word (@deva[गुणवचन]) denoting cause, when not feminine, optionally takes @deva[पञ्चमी].

**Examples:**
- @deva[जाड्याद् बद्धः] or @deva[जाड्येन बद्धः] 'imprisoned due to stupidity'
- @deva[पाण्डित्याद् मुक्तः] or @deva[पाण्डित्येन मुक्तः] 'released through learning'
standard:
A quality-word (@deva[गुणवचन], i.e., an abstract noun) expressing cause, when not feminine, optionally takes @deva[पञ्चमी] (instead of @deva[तृतीया]).

**Examples:**
- @deva[जाड्याद् बद्धः] or @deva[जाड्येन बद्धः] 'imprisoned by reason of stupidity'
- @deva[पाण्डित्याद् मुक्तः] or @deva[पाण्डित्येन मुक्तः] 'released through learning'

**Why 'not feminine'?** Feminine abstract nouns take only @deva[तृतीया]: @deva[बुद्ध्या मुक्तः] or @deva[प्रज्ञया मुक्तः] 'released by wisdom'—no @deva[पञ्चमी] option.

**Why 'quality-word'?** Concrete nouns don't apply: @deva[धनेन कुलम्] 'family through wealth'—@deva[धन] is not abstract.
advanced:
A @deva[गुणवचन] (quality-word) expressing @deva[हेतु], when @deva[अस्त्री] (not feminine), optionally takes @deva[पञ्चमी].

**Examples:** @deva[जाड्याद्/जाड्येन बद्धः], @deva[पाण्डित्याद्/पाण्डित्येन मुक्तः].

**Feminine exception:** @deva[बुद्ध्या मुक्तः], @deva[प्रज्ञया मुक्तः]—only @deva[तृतीया], no option.

**@deva[गुणवचन] = abstract nouns.** Concrete nouns excluded: @deva[धनेन कुलम्].

simple:
With a pronoun (@deva[सर्वनामन्]) plus @deva[हेतु], both @deva[षष्ठी] and @deva[तृतीया] are possible.

**Examples:**
- @deva[कस्य हेतुना वसति] or @deva[केन हेतुना वसति] 'for what reason does he dwell?'
- @deva[यस्य हेतुना] or @deva[येन हेतुना] 'for which reason'
standard:
With a pronoun (@deva[सर्वनामन्] per @ref[1.1.27]) plus @deva[हेतु], both @deva[षष्ठी] and @deva[तृतीया] are possible.

**Examples:**
- @deva[कस्य हेतुना वसति] or @deva[केन हेतुना वसति] 'for what reason does he dwell?'
- @deva[यस्य हेतुना] or @deva[येन हेतुना] 'for which reason'

@note[type=@[vārtika]]: With @deva[निमित्त] or @deva[कारण], nearly all cases are possible: @deva[किं निमित्तम्], @deva[केन निमित्तेन], @deva[कस्मै निमित्ताय], @deva[कस्मान्निमित्तात्], @deva[कस्मिन् निमित्ते वसति]. The same applies to synonyms like @deva[प्रयोजन]: @deva[किं प्रयोजनम्], @deva[केन प्रयोजनेन], @deva[कस्मै प्रयोजनाय], etc.

simple:
The @deva[अपादान] (source of separation) takes @deva[पञ्चमी] (ablative).

**Examples:**
- @deva[ग्रामादागच्छति] 'he comes from the village' (@ref[1.4.24])
- @deva[वृकेभ्यो बिभेति] 'he fears wolves' (@ref[1.4.25])
- @deva[अध्ययनात् पराजयते] 'he is defeated in study' (@ref[1.4.26])

With words meaning 'other', @deva[आरात्] 'near/far', @deva[इतर] 'different', @deva[ऋते] 'without', direction-words, @deva[अञ्चु]-compounds, or @deva[आच्]/@deva[आहि]-endings, @deva[पञ्चमी] is used.

**Examples:**
- @deva[अन्यो देवदत्तात्] 'other than Devadatta'
- @deva[आराद् देवदत्तात्] 'near/far from Devadatta'
- @deva[ऋते देवदत्तात्] 'except Devadatta'
- @deva[पूर्वो ग्रामात्] 'east of the village'
standard:
With words meaning 'other than' (@deva[अन्य], @deva[भिन्न], @deva[इतर], @deva[विलक्षण]), @deva[आरात्] 'near/far', @deva[ऋते] 'without', direction-words, @deva[अञ्चु]-ending directional compounds, or @deva[आच्]/@deva[आहि]-endings (@ref[5.3.36]–37), @deva[पञ्चमी] is used.

**Examples:**
- @deva[अन्यो/भिन्नो/इतरो देवदत्तात्] 'other/different from Devadatta'
- @deva[आराद् देवदत्तात्] 'near or far from Devadatta'
- @deva[ऋते देवदत्तात्] 'except Devadatta'
- @deva[पूर्वो ग्रामात्] 'east of the village'
- @deva[उत्तरो ग्रामात्] 'north of the village'
- @deva[पूर्वो ग्रीष्माद् वसन्तः] 'spring is prior to summer'
- @deva[प्राक्/प्रत्यग् ग्रामात्] 'to the east/west of the village'
- @deva[दक्षिणा/दक्षिणाहि ग्रामात्] 'to the south of the village'

The separate mention of @deva[अञ्चु]-words shows that @ref[2.3.30]'s @deva[षष्ठी] does not apply to them.

simple:
With words ending in @deva[अतसुच्] affixes (@deva[दक्षिणतः], @deva[उत्तरतः], etc.), @deva[षष्ठी] is used.

**Examples:**
- @deva[ग्रामस्य दक्षिणतः] 'to the south of the village'
- @deva[ग्रामस्य उत्तरतः] 'to the north of the village'
standard:
With words ending in affixes having the sense of @deva[अतसुच्] (@ref[5.3.28]), @deva[षष्ठी] is used.

**Examples:**
- @deva[ग्रामस्य दक्षिणतः] 'to the south of the village'
- @deva[ग्रामस्य उत्तरतः] 'to the north of the village'
- @deva[ग्रामस्य पुरस्तात्] 'in front of the village'
- @deva[ग्रामस्य उपरि] 'above the village'
advanced:
With @deva[अतसुच्]-ending words (@ref[5.3.28]: @deva[दक्षिणोत्तराभ्यामतसुच्]), @deva[षष्ठी] applies.

**Examples:** @deva[ग्रामस्य दक्षिणतः], @deva[ग्रामस्य उत्तरतः], @deva[ग्रामस्य पुरस्तात्], @deva[ग्रामस्य उपरि].

simple:
With words ending in @deva[एनप्], both @deva[द्वितीया] and @deva[षष्ठी] are used.

**Example:**
- @deva[दक्षिणेन ग्रामम्] or @deva[दक्षिणेन ग्रामस्य] 'south of the village'
standard:
With words ending in @deva[एनप्] (@ref[5.3.35]), both @deva[द्वितीया] (accusative) and @deva[षष्ठी] (genitive) are used.

**Example:**
- @deva[दक्षिणेन ग्रामम्] or @deva[दक्षिणेन ग्रामस्य] 'south of the village'
advanced:
With @deva[एनप्]-ending words (@ref[5.3.35]: @deva[एनबन्यतरस्यामदूरेऽपञ्चम्याः]), both @deva[द्वितीया] and @deva[षष्ठी] apply.

**Example:** @deva[दक्षिणेन ग्रामम्/ग्रामस्य].

simple:
With @deva[पृथक्], @deva[विना], or @deva[नाना] ('without/separately'), @deva[तृतीया] is optionally used (along with @deva[पञ्चमी] and @deva[द्वितीया]).

**Examples:**
- @deva[रामेण विना] or @deva[रामाद् विना] or @deva[रामं विना] 'without @[rāma]'
- @deva[रामेण पृथक्] or @deva[रामात् पृथक्] 'separately from @[rāma]'
standard:
With @deva[पृथक्] 'separately', @deva[विना] 'without', or @deva[नाना] 'variously/without', @deva[तृतीया] is optionally used. @deva[पञ्चमी] and @deva[द्वितीया] are also possible.

**Examples:**
- @deva[रामेण/रामाद्/रामं विना] 'without @[rāma]'
- @deva[रामेण/रामात्/रामं पृथक्] 'separately from @[rāma]'

**Verse:** @deva[विना वातं विना वर्षं विद्युत्प्रपतनं विना। विना हस्तिकृतान्दोषान् केनेमौ पातितौ द्रुमौ॥] 'Without wind, without rain, without lightning falling—without elephant-caused faults—by whom were these two trees felled?'
advanced:
With @deva[पृथक्], @deva[विना], @deva[नाना], @deva[तृतीया] is optional (along with @deva[पञ्चमी] and @deva[द्वितीया]).

**Examples:** @deva[रामेण/रामाद्/रामं विना/पृथक्/नाना].

The @[sūtra] can be parsed as @deva[पृथग्विनानानाभिः] + @deva[तृतीयान्यतरस्याम्], allowing @deva[द्वितीया] by implication.

simple:
When @deva[स्तोक], @deva[अल्प], @deva[कृच्छ्र], or @deva[कतिपय] express instrument (not material substance), @deva[पञ्चमी] is optionally used.

**Examples:**
- @deva[स्तोकात् मुक्तः] or @deva[स्तोकेन मुक्तः] 'released with little [effort]'
- @deva[अल्पात् मुक्तः] or @deva[अल्पेन मुक्तः] 'released easily'
standard:
When @deva[स्तोक] 'little', @deva[अल्प] 'little', @deva[कृच्छ्र] 'difficulty', or @deva[कतिपय] 'some' express @deva[करण] (instrument) and do not qualify material objects, @deva[पञ्चमी] is optionally used (instead of @deva[तृतीया]).

**Examples:**
- @deva[स्तोकात्/स्तोकेन मुक्तः] 'released with little [effort]'
- @deva[अल्पात्/अल्पेन मुक्तः] 'released easily'

**Counterexamples (material substance—no option):**
- @deva[स्तोकेन विषेण हतः] 'killed by a little poison'
- @deva[अल्पेन मधुना मत्तः] 'intoxicated by a little honey'

Also not when adverbial: @deva[स्तोकं मुञ्चति] 'he releases a little'—here @deva[स्तोक] is not @deva[करण].
advanced:
When @deva[स्तोक/अल्प/कृच्छ्र/कतिपय] express @deva[करण] (not qualifying @deva[द्रव्य]), @deva[पञ्चमी] is optional.

**Examples:** @deva[स्तोकात्/स्तोकेन मुक्तः], @deva[अल्पात्/अल्पेन मुक्तः].

**Counterexamples:** @deva[स्तोकेन विषेण हतः] (qualifies substance), @deva[स्तोकं मुञ्चति] (adverbial, not @deva[करण]).

simple:
With words meaning @deva[दूर] 'far' or @deva[अन्तिक] 'near', @deva[षष्ठी] is optionally used.

**Examples:**
- @deva[ग्रामाद् दूरम्] or @deva[ग्रामस्य दूरम्] 'far from the village'
- @deva[ग्रामादन्तिकम्] or @deva[ग्रामस्यान्तिकम्] 'near the village'
standard:
With words meaning @deva[दूर] 'distant' or @deva[अन्तिक] 'near' (and synonyms like @deva[विप्रकृष्ट], @deva[अभ्याश], @deva[निकट], @deva[समीप]), @deva[षष्ठी] is optionally used (instead of @deva[पञ्चमी]).

**Examples:**
- @deva[ग्रामाद् वनं दूरम्] or @deva[ग्रामस्य वनं दूरम्] 'the forest is far from the village'
- @deva[ग्रामादन्तिकम्] or @deva[ग्रामस्यान्तिकम्] 'near the village'

The word @deva[अन्यतरस्याम्] 'optionally' prevents @deva[पञ्चमी] from being the only option.
advanced:
With @deva[दूर/अन्तिक]-meaning words (@deva[विप्रकृष्ट], @deva[अभ्याश], @deva[निकट], @deva[समीप]), @deva[षष्ठी] is optional.

**Examples:** @deva[ग्रामाद्/ग्रामस्य वनं दूरम्/अन्तिकम्].

The @deva[अन्यतरस्याम्] prevents @deva[पञ्चमी] from extending here as the sole option.

simple:
The words @deva[दूर] and @deva[अन्तिक] themselves take @deva[द्वितीया], @deva[पञ्चमी], or @deva[तृतीया].

**Examples:**
- @deva[दूरं ग्रामस्य] or @deva[दूराद् ग्रामस्य] or @deva[दूरेण ग्रामस्य] 'far from the village'
- @deva[अन्तिकं ग्रामस्य] or @deva[अन्तिकात् ग्रामस्य] or @deva[अन्तिकेन ग्रामस्य] 'near the village'
standard:
The words @deva[दूर] 'distant' and @deva[अन्तिक] 'near' themselves take @deva[द्वितीया], @deva[पञ्चमी], or @deva[तृतीया].

**Examples:**
- @deva[दूरं/दूराद्/दूरेण ग्रामस्य] 'far from the village'
- @deva[अन्तिकं/अन्तिकात्/अन्तिकेन ग्रामस्य] 'near the village'

This applies only when these words retain their original meaning of distance/proximity. When used as substantives, regular case usage applies: @deva[दूरः पन्थाः] 'a long road' (nom.), @deva[दूराय पथे देहि] 'give for the long road' (dat.).

simple:
The @deva[अधिकरण] (location) takes @deva[सप्तमी] (locative). Also with @deva[दूर]/@deva[अन्तिक].

**Examples:**
- @deva[कटे आस्ते] 'he sits on the mat'
- @deva[स्थाल्यां पचति] 'he cooks in the pot'
- @deva[अन्तिके ग्रामस्य] 'near the village'
- @deva[दूरे ग्रामस्य] 'far from the village'

When the action (@deva[भाव]) of something marks the time of another action, @deva[सप्तमी] is used. This is the **locative absolute**.

**Examples:**
- @deva[गोषु दुह्यमानासु गतः] 'he went while the cows were being milked'
- @deva[दुग्धासु आगतः] 'he returned when they were milked'
standard:
When the action (@deva[भाव]) of something indicates the time of another action, @deva[सप्तमी] is used. This is the **locative absolute** construction.

**Examples:**
- @deva[गोषु दुह्यमानासु गतः] 'the cows being milked, he went away'
- @deva[दुग्धासु आगतः] 'when they were milked, he returned'
- @deva[अग्निषु हूयमानेषु गतः] 'he went while oblations were being offered'
- @deva[हुतेषु आगतः] 'he returned when they were offered'

**Why 'action of whatsoever'?** In @deva[यो जटाभिः स भुक्ते] 'he who has matted hair, he eats'—@deva[जटा] is not an action, so no locative absolute.

**Why 'action' twice?** In @deva[यो भुक्ते स देवदत्तः] 'he who eats is Devadatta'—the eating doesn't indicate the time of another action.
advanced:
When the @deva[भाव] (action) of X indicates the time of another action, @deva[सप्तमी] applies—the **locative absolute**.

**Examples:** @deva[गोषु दुह्यमानासु गतः], @deva[दुग्धासु आगतः], @deva[अग्निषु हूयमानेषु गतः], @deva[हुतेषु आगतः].

**Counterexamples:** @deva[यो जटाभिः स भुक्ते] (no action), @deva[यो भुक्ते स देवदत्तः] (no temporal indication).

simple:
When disregard (@deva[अनादर]) is shown, @deva[षष्ठी] is used (as well as @deva[सप्तमी]). This is the **genitive absolute**.

**Examples:**
- @deva[रुदतः प्राव्राजीत्] or @deva[रुदति प्राव्राजीत्] 'he left despite her weeping'
- @deva[पश्यतो राक्षसस्य नन्दाः हताः] 'the Nandas were killed while @[rākṣasa] looked on'
standard:
When disregard (@deva[अनादर]) is expressed, @deva[षष्ठी] is used (as well as @deva[सप्तमी]) for the action marking time. This is the **genitive absolute**.

**Examples:**
- @deva[रुदतः/रुदति प्राव्राजीत्] 'in spite of her weeping, he departed'
- @deva[नन्दाः पशव इव हताः पश्यतो राक्षसस्य] 'the Nandas were slaughtered like beasts—@[rākṣasa] looking on [helplessly]'

The @deva[षष्ठी] (genitive) expresses 'notwithstanding', 'in spite of', 'despite'.
advanced:
When @deva[अनादर] (disregard) is expressed, @deva[षष्ठी] applies (alongside @deva[सप्तमी]) for the action marking time—the **genitive absolute**.

**Examples:** @deva[रुदतः/रुदति प्राव्राजीत्], @deva[पश्यतो राक्षसस्य नन्दाः हताः].

The genitive conveys 'notwithstanding', 'in spite of'.

simple:
With @deva[स्वामिन्], @deva[ईश्वर], @deva[अधिपति], @deva[दायाद], @deva[साक्षिन्], @deva[प्रतिभू], @deva[प्रसूत], both @deva[षष्ठी] and @deva[सप्तमी] are used.

**Examples:**
- @deva[गवां स्वामी] or @deva[गोषु स्वामी] 'master of cows'
- @deva[गवामधिपतिः] or @deva[गोष्वधिपतिः] 'ruler of cows'
standard:
With @deva[स्वामिन्] 'master', @deva[ईश्वर] 'lord', @deva[अधिपति] 'ruler', @deva[दायाद] 'heir', @deva[साक्षिन्] 'witness', @deva[प्रतिभू] 'surety', @deva[प्रसूत] 'begotten', both @deva[षष्ठी] and @deva[सप्तमी] are used.

**Examples:**
- @deva[गवां/गोषु स्वामी] 'master of cows'
- @deva[गवां/गोषु ईश्वरः] 'lord of cows'
- @deva[गवाम्/गोष्वधिपतिः] 'ruler of cows'
- @deva[गवां/गोषु दायादः] 'heir to cows'
- @deva[गवां/गोषु साक्षी] 'witness regarding cows'
- @deva[गवां/गोषु प्रतिभूः] 'surety for cows'

These words naturally govern @deva[षष्ठी] (genitive); this @[sūtra] adds locative as an option.
advanced:
With @deva[स्वामिन्], @deva[ईश्वर], @deva[अधिपति], @deva[दायाद], @deva[साक्षिन्], @deva[प्रतिभू], @deva[प्रसूत], both @deva[षष्ठी] and @deva[सप्तमी] apply.

**Examples:** @deva[गवां/गोषु स्वामी/ईश्वरः/अधिपतिः/दायादः/साक्षी/प्रतिभूः].

These words would naturally govern @deva[षष्ठी]; this @[sūtra] adds @deva[सप्तमी].

simple:
With @deva[आयुक्त] or @deva[कुशल] meaning 'deeply absorbed', both @deva[षष्ठी] and @deva[सप्तमी] are used.

**Examples:**
- @deva[कटकरणे आयुक्तः] or @deva[कटकरणस्य आयुक्तः] 'deeply absorbed in mat-making'
- @deva[कटकरणे कुशलः] or @deva[कटकरणस्य कुशलः] 'skilled in mat-making'
standard:
With @deva[आयुक्त] 'engaged' or @deva[कुशल] 'skilled', when meaning entire absorption in an activity, both @deva[षष्ठी] and @deva[सप्तमी] are used.

**Examples:**
- @deva[कटकरणे/कटकरणस्य आयुक्तः] 'deeply absorbed in mat-making'
- @deva[कटकरणे/कटकरणस्य कुशलः] 'skilled in mat-making'

**When not meaning deep absorption:** Only @deva[सप्तमी] applies: @deva[आयुक्तो गौः शकटे] 'the cow is slightly yoked to the cart'—here there's no deep engagement.
advanced:
With @deva[आयुक्त/कुशल] meaning @deva[तत्परता] (entire absorption), both @deva[षष्ठी] and @deva[सप्तमी] apply.

**Examples:** @deva[कटकरणे/कटकरणस्य आयुक्तः/कुशलः].

**Non-absorption sense:** Only @deva[सप्तमी]: @deva[आयुक्तो गौः शकटे] 'the cow is [casually] yoked to the cart'.

simple:
In @deva[निर्धारण] (specification—singling out one from a group), both @deva[षष्ठी] and @deva[सप्तमी] are used.

**Examples:**
- @deva[गवां कृष्णा बहुक्षीरा] or @deva[गोषु कृष्णा बहुक्षीरा] 'among cows, the black one gives most milk'
- @deva[मनुष्याणां क्षत्रियः शूरतमः] or @deva[मनुष्येषु क्षत्रियः शूरतमः] 'among men, the @[kṣatriya] is bravest'
standard:
In @deva[निर्धारण] (specification—singling out an individual from a class), both @deva[षष्ठी] and @deva[सप्तमी] are used for the group from which specification is made.

**Examples:**
- @deva[गवां/गोषु कृष्णा बहुक्षीरा] 'among cows, the black one gives most milk'
- @deva[मनुष्याणां/मनुष्येषु क्षत्रियः शूरतमः] 'among men, the @[kṣatriya] is bravest'

@deva[निर्धारण] is made by separating one from many by genus, quality, or action (see @ref[2.2.10]).

simple:
When the specified is different from (not included in) the comparison group, @deva[पञ्चमी] is used.

**Example:**
- @deva[माथुराः पाटलिपुत्रेभ्यः सुकुमारतराः] 'Mathura people are more delicate than those of @[pāṭaliputra]'
standard:
When the item specified is different from (not included in) the comparison group, @deva[पञ्चमी] is used. This debars @deva[षष्ठी] and @deva[सप्तमी].

**Example:**
- @deva[माथुराः पाटलिपुत्रेभ्यः सुकुमारतराः] 'people of Mathura are more delicate than those of @[pāṭaliputra]'

Here the Mathura people are being compared to a different group (@[pāṭaliputra] people), not singled out from within them.
advanced:
When @deva[निर्धारण] involves comparison with a different (@deva[भिन्न]) group (not specification within the same group), @deva[पञ्चमी] applies—debars @deva[षष्ठी/सप्तमी].

**Example:** @deva[माथुराः पाटलिपुत्रेभ्यः सुकुमारतराः] 'Mathura people are more delicate than @[pāṭaliputra] people'.

simple:
With @deva[साधु] or @deva[निपुण] meaning 'respectfully disposed toward', @deva[सप्तमी] is used—unless @deva[प्रति] is present.

**Examples:**
- @deva[मातरि साधुः] 'good toward mother'
- @deva[मातरि निपुणः] 'skillfully attentive toward mother'

**With @deva[प्रति]:** @deva[साधुर्देवदत्तो मातरं प्रति] 'Devadatta is good toward his mother'—@deva[द्वितीया] used.
standard:
With @deva[साधु] 'good' or @deva[निपुण] 'skilled', when expressing respect/deference, @deva[सप्तमी] is used—unless @deva[प्रति] (or similar prepositions) is present.

**Examples:**
- @deva[मातरि साधुः/निपुणः] 'well-behaved/attentive toward mother'

**With @deva[प्रति]/@deva[परि]/@deva[अनु]:** @deva[साधुर्देवदत्तो मातरं प्रति] or @deva[मातरं परि साधुर्देवदत्तः]—@deva[द्वितीया] used instead.

**Why 'respect'?** In @deva[साधुर्भृत्यो राज्ञः] 'the servant is good toward the king'—this is a bare statement, not deferential; so no @deva[सप्तमी].
advanced:
With @deva[साधु/निपुण] expressing @deva[गौरव] (respect), @deva[सप्तमी] applies—except when @deva[प्रति/परि/अनु] is used.

**Examples:** @deva[मातरि साधुः/निपुणः].

**With preposition:** @deva[साधुर्देवदत्तो मातरं प्रति]—@deva[द्वितीया].

**Non-respect sense:** @deva[साधुर्भृत्यो राज्ञः]—no @deva[सप्तमी] (bare statement).

simple:
With @deva[प्रसित] or @deva[उत्सुक] 'greatly desirous', both @deva[तृतीया] and @deva[सप्तमी] are used.

**Examples:**
- @deva[निद्रायाम् उत्सुकः] or @deva[निद्रया उत्सुकः] 'longing for sleep'
- @deva[केशेषु प्रसितः] or @deva[केशैः प्रसितः] 'absorbed in [grooming] hair'
standard:
With @deva[प्रसित] 'absorbed/intent' or @deva[उत्सुक] 'longing/eager', both @deva[तृतीया] and @deva[सप्तमी] are used.

**Examples:**
- @deva[निद्रायाम्/निद्रया उत्सुकः] 'longing for sleep'
- @deva[केशेषु/केशैः प्रसितः] 'absorbed in hair'
advanced:
With @deva[प्रसित/उत्सुक], both @deva[तृतीया] and @deva[सप्तमी] apply.

**Examples:** @deva[निद्रायाम्/निद्रया उत्सुकः], @deva[केशेषु/केशैः प्रसितः].

simple:
When a @deva[नक्षत्र]-denoting @deva[तद्धित] affix is @deva[लुप्]-elided, both @deva[सप्तमी] and @deva[तृतीया] are used.

**Examples:**
- @deva[पुष्येण पायसमश्नीयात्] or @deva[पुष्ये पायसमश्नीयात्] 'when the moon is in @[puṣya], let him drink milk'
- @deva[मघाभिः पललौदनम्] or @deva[मघासु पललौदनम्] 'meat-rice when the moon is in @[maghā]'
standard:
When a @deva[तद्धित] affix denoting asterism-time is @deva[लुप्]-elided (@ref[4.2.4]), both @deva[सप्तमी] and @deva[तृतीया] are used.

**Examples:**
- @deva[पुष्येण/पुष्ये पायसमश्नीयात्] 'when the moon is in @[puṣya], let him drink milk'
- @deva[मघाभिः/मघासु पललौदनम्] 'meat-rice when in @[maghā]'

**Not applicable when:**
- No @deva[लुप्] elision: @deva[मघासु ग्रहः] 'the planet in @[maghā]'—only @deva[सप्तमी]
- Country-meaning: @deva[पञ्चालेषु वसति] 'he lives in @[pañcāla]'—@deva[सप्तमी] only
- No location-sense: @deva[अद्यपुष्यः] 'today is @[puṣya]'—no option
advanced:
When @deva[नक्षत्रकाल]-denoting @deva[तद्धित] is @deva[लुप्]-elided (@ref[4.2.4]), both @deva[सप्तमी] and @deva[तृतीया] apply.

**Examples:** @deva[पुष्येण/पुष्ये पायसमश्नीयात्], @deva[मघाभिः/मघासु पललौदनम्].

**Restrictions:** No @deva[लुप्] → only @deva[सप्तमी] (@deva[मघासु ग्रहः]). Country-meaning → @deva[सप्तमी] only (@deva[पञ्चालेषु वसति]). No @deva[अधिकरण] sense → no option (@deva[अद्यपुष्यः]).

simple:
@deva[प्रथमा] (nominative) is used when the sense is: @deva[प्रातिपदिकार्थ] (stem-meaning), @deva[लिङ्ग] (gender), @deva[परिमाण] (measure), or @deva[संख्या] (number).

**Examples:**
- @deva[वृक्षः] 'tree' (stem-meaning)
- @deva[कुमारी] 'girl' (gender)
- @deva[द्रोणः] 'a @[droṇa] measure' (measure)
- @deva[एकः] 'one', @deva[द्वौ] 'two', @deva[बहवः] 'many' (number)
standard:
@deva[प्रथमा] (nominative) is used when the sense is: @deva[प्रातिपदिकार्थ] (stem-meaning), @deva[लिङ्ग] (gender), @deva[परिमाण] (measure), or @deva[संख्या] (grammatical number).

**Examples:**
- @deva[वृक्षः] 'tree'—@deva[प्रातिपदिकार्थ] (mere existence of tree)
- @deva[कुमारी] 'girl'—@deva[लिङ्ग] (feminine gender)
- @deva[द्रोणः], @deva[खारी], @deva[आढकः]—@deva[परिमाण] (measures)
- @deva[एकः], @deva[द्वौ], @deva[बहवः]—@deva[संख्या] (singular, dual, plural)

@deva[प्रातिपदिक] = meaningful non-root, non-affix element (@ref[1.2.45]).

simple:
@deva[प्रथमा] is also used when the sense is @deva[सम्बोधन] (addressing).

**Examples:**
- @deva[हे राम] 'O @[rāma]'
- @deva[हे रामौ] 'O you two @[rāmas]'
- @deva[हे रामाः] 'O @[rāmas]'
standard:
@deva[प्रथमा] is also used when the sense is @deva[सम्बोधन] (addressing/calling).

**Examples:**
- @deva[हे राम] 'O @[rāma]' (singular)
- @deva[हे रामौ] 'O you two @[rāmas]' (dual)
- @deva[हे रामाः] 'O @[rāmas]' (plural)

This extends the scope of @deva[प्रथमा] beyond @deva[प्रातिपदिकार्थ] etc. to include vocative function.

simple:
A @deva[प्रथमा]-ending word used in @deva[सम्बोधन] (address) is called @deva[आमन्त्रित] (vocative).

**Example:**
- @deva[हे राम]—@deva[राम] is @deva[आमन्त्रित]
standard:
A @deva[प्रथमा]-ending word used in @deva[सम्बोधन] (address) receives the technical name @deva[आमन्त्रित] (vocative).

**Example:**
- @deva[हे राम]—the word @deva[राम] is called @deva[आमन्त्रित]

This @deva[संज्ञा] (technical term) is used in subsequent rules.
advanced:
A @deva[प्रथमान्त] (nominative-ending word) in @deva[सम्बोधन] sense receives the @deva[संज्ञा] '@deva[आमन्त्रित]'.

**Example:** @deva[हे राम]—@deva[राम] is @deva[आमन्त्रित].

This @deva[संज्ञा] triggers subsequent operations.

simple:
The @deva[एकवचन] (singular) of @deva[आमन्त्रित] is called @deva[सम्बुद्धि].

**Example:**
- @deva[हे राम]—@deva[राम] is @deva[सम्बुद्धि] (vocative singular)
standard:
The @deva[एकवचन] (singular) of @deva[आमन्त्रित] receives the technical name @deva[सम्बुद्धि].

**Example:**
- @deva[हे राम]—@deva[राम] is @deva[सम्बुद्धि]

This @deva[संज्ञा] triggers rules like @ref[6.1.69]: after stems ending in @deva[एङ्] or short vowel, the @deva[सम्बुद्धि] consonant is elided (@deva[राम] from @deva[रामस्]).
advanced:
The @deva[एकवचन] of @deva[आमन्त्रित] is called @deva[सम्बुद्धि].

**Example:** @deva[हे राम]—@deva[राम] is @deva[सम्बुद्धि].

This @deva[संज्ञा] conditions @ref[6.1.69]: @deva[एङ्ह्रस्वात्सम्बुद्धेः]—consonant elision after @deva[एङ्]/short vowel in @deva[सम्बुद्धि].

simple:
In @deva[शेष] (remaining relations)—where neither @deva[कारक] nor @deva[प्रातिपदिकार्थ] applies—@deva[षष्ठी] (genitive) is used.

**Examples:**
- @deva[राज्ञः पुरुषः] 'the king's man'
- @deva[पशोः पादः] 'the animal's foot'
- @deva[पितुः पुत्रः] 'the father's son'
standard:
In @deva[शेष] (remaining relations)—where neither @deva[कारक] relations nor @deva[प्रातिपदिकार्थ] applies—@deva[षष्ठी] (genitive) is used.

**Examples:**
- @deva[राज्ञः पुरुषः] 'the king's man' (ownership)
- @deva[पशोः पादः] 'the animal's foot' (part-whole)
- @deva[पितुः पुत्रः] 'the father's son' (kinship)

@deva[शेष] covers relations like possession, kinship, part-whole—not expressible by verb-related @deva[कारक].

simple:
With @deva[ज्ञा] (not meaning 'to know'), the @deva[करण] (instrument) takes @deva[षष्ठी].

**Examples:**
- @deva[मधुनो जानीते] 'he engages (in sacrifice) with honey'
- @deva[सर्पिषो जानीते] 'he engages with ghee'
standard:
With @deva[ज्ञा] (not meaning 'to know'), the @deva[करण] (instrument) takes @deva[षष्ठी] instead of @deva[तृतीया].

**Examples:**
- @deva[मधुनो जानीते] 'he engages in sacrifice with honey'
- @deva[सर्पिषो जानीते] 'he engages with ghee'

**When meaning 'to know':** @deva[स्वरेण पुत्रं जानाति] 'he recognizes the son by the voice'—@deva[तृतीया] used.

@deva[ज्ञा] when not meaning 'to know' = 'to act/engage' or 'false knowledge' (@ref[1.3.45]).
advanced:
With @deva[ज्ञा] (not in @deva[अवबोधन] sense), @deva[करण] takes @deva[षष्ठी].

**Examples:** @deva[मधुनो जानीते], @deva[सर्पिषो जानीते].

**Counterexample:** @deva[स्वरेण पुत्रं जानाति] ('recognize')—@deva[तृतीया].

@deva[ज्ञा] = @deva[प्रतिपत्ति/मिथ्याज्ञान] (cf. @ref[1.3.45]).

simple:
With verbs meaning @deva[स्मृ] (remember), @deva[दय्] (give/pity), and @deva[ईश्] (rule), the @deva[कर्मन्] (object) takes @deva[षष्ठी].

**Examples:**
- @deva[मातुः अध्येति] 'he remembers the mother'
- @deva[सर्पिषो दयते] 'he gives ghee'
- @deva[गात्राणामीष्टे] 'he rules his limbs'
standard:
With verbs meaning @deva[स्मृ] (remember), @deva[दय्] (give/pity/protect), and @deva[ईश्] (rule/master), the @deva[कर्मन्] takes @deva[षष्ठी].

**Examples:**
- @deva[मातुः अध्येति] 'he remembers the mother'
- @deva[सर्पिषो दयते] 'he gives ghee'
- @deva[नायं गात्राणामीष्टे] 'he cannot rule his limbs'

**Counterexample:** @deva[मातुर्गुणैः स्मरति]—@deva[गुण] is not the object.

@deva[शेष] is also understood—so @deva[मातरं स्मरति] (@deva[द्वितीया]) is allowed.
advanced:
With @deva[स्मृ]-meaning verbs, @deva[दय्], and @deva[ईश्], @deva[कर्मन्] takes @deva[षष्ठी].

**Examples:** @deva[मातुः अध्येति], @deva[सर्पिषो दयते], @deva[गात्राणामीष्टे].

**Counterexample:** @deva[मातुर्गुणैः स्मरति]—@deva[गुण] not @deva[कर्मन्].

@deva[शेष] understood → @deva[द्वितीया] also valid: @deva[मातरं स्मरति].

simple:
With @deva[कृ] meaning 'to transform/prepare', the @deva[कर्मन्] takes @deva[षष्ठी].

**Example:**
- @deva[एधोदकस्योपस्कुरुते] 'he prepares the wood and water (for sacrifice)'
standard:
With @deva[कृ] meaning 'to impart new quality' (@deva[प्रतियत्न]), the @deva[कर्मन्] takes @deva[षष्ठी].

**Example:**
- @deva[एधोदकस्योपस्कुरुते] 'he prepares the wood and water'

**When not @deva[प्रतियत्न]:** @deva[कटं करोति] 'he makes a mat'—@deva[द्वितीया] used.

@deva[शेष] understood—so @deva[एधोदकमुपस्कुरुते] (@deva[द्वितीया]) is also valid. See @ref[1.3.32].
advanced:
With @deva[कृ] in @deva[प्रतियत्न] sense (@ref[1.3.32]), @deva[कर्मन्] takes @deva[षष्ठी].

**Example:** @deva[एधोदकस्योपस्कुरुते].

**Non-@deva[प्रतियत्न]:** @deva[कटं करोति]—@deva[द्वितीया].

@deva[शेष] understood → @deva[एधोदकमुपस्कुरुते] also valid. @deva[स]-insertion by @ref[6.1.139].

simple:
With @deva[रुज्]-class verbs (except causative @deva[ज्वरय्]), when the subject is a @deva[भाव] (abstract noun), the @deva[कर्मन्] takes @deva[षष्ठी].

**Examples:**
- @deva[चौरस्य रुजति रोगः] 'disease afflicts the thief'
- @deva[चौरस्यामयति आमयः] 'illness afflicts the thief'
standard:
With @deva[रुज्]-meaning verbs (except causative @deva[ज्वरय्]), when the subject is a @deva[भाव] (abstract/condition noun), @deva[कर्मन्] takes @deva[षष्ठी].

**Examples:**
- @deva[चौरस्य रुजति रोगः] 'disease afflicts the thief'
- @deva[चौरस्यामयति आमयः] 'illness afflicts the thief'

**Counterexamples:**
- @deva[नदी कूलानि रुजति] 'the river breaks the banks'—subject not abstract
- @deva[चौरं ज्वरयति ज्वरः] 'fever burns the thief'—causative @deva[ज्वरय्] excluded

@deva[शेष] understood—so @deva[चौरं रुजति रोगः] (@deva[द्वितीया]) also valid.
advanced:
With @deva[रुज्]-meaning verbs (except causative @deva[ज्वरय्]), when subject is @deva[भाव], @deva[कर्मन्] takes @deva[षष्ठी].

**Examples:** @deva[चौरस्य रुजति रोगः], @deva[चौरस्यामयति आमयः].

**Counterexamples:** @deva[नदी कूलानि रुजति] (non-abstract subject), @deva[चौरं ज्वरयति ज्वरः] (@deva[णिच्] excluded).

@deva[शेष] → @deva[चौरं रुजति रोगः] also valid.

simple:
With @deva[नाथ्] meaning 'to bless', the @deva[कर्मन्] takes @deva[षष्ठी].

**Example:**
- @deva[मधुनो नाथते] 'he blesses the honey'
standard:
With @deva[नाथ्] meaning 'to bless' (@deva[आशिषि]), the @deva[कर्मन्] takes @deva[षष्ठी].

**Example:**
- @deva[मधुनो नाथते] 'he blesses the honey'

**Counterexample:** @deva[माणवकमुपनाथति] 'he solicits the student'—@deva[नाथ्] not meaning 'bless', so @deva[द्वितीया] used.
advanced:
With @deva[नाथ्] in @deva[आशिषि] sense, @deva[कर्मन्] takes @deva[षष्ठी].

**Example:** @deva[मधुनो नाथते].

**Counterexample:** @deva[माणवकमुपनाथति] ('solicit')—@deva[द्वितीया].

simple:
With @deva[व्यवहृ] and @deva[पण्] meaning 'deal/stake', the @deva[कर्मन्] takes @deva[षष्ठी].

**Examples:**
- @deva[शतस्य व्यवहरति] 'he deals in a hundred'
- @deva[शतस्य पणते] 'he stakes a hundred'
standard:
With @deva[व्यवहृ] and @deva[पण्] when meaning 'dealing/staking' (sale, purchase, gambling), @deva[कर्मन्] takes @deva[षष्ठी].

**Examples:**
- @deva[शतस्य व्यवहरति/पणते] 'he deals in/stakes a hundred'

**Counterexamples:**
- @deva[शलाकां व्यवहरति] 'he throws dice'—not dealing sense
- @deva[ब्राह्मणान् पणायते] 'he praises @[brāhmaṇas]'—@deva[पण्] = 'praise' takes @deva[आय्]

@deva[शेष] understood—so @deva[शतं पणते] (@deva[द्वितीया]) also valid.
advanced:
With @deva[व्यवहृ/पण्] in @deva[क्रय-विक्रय/द्यूत] sense, @deva[कर्मन्] takes @deva[षष्ठी].

**Examples:** @deva[शतस्य व्यवहरति/पणते].

**Counterexamples:** @deva[शलाकां व्यवहरति], @deva[ब्राह्मणान् पणायते] (@deva[पण्] + @deva[आय्] = 'praise').

@deva[शेष] → @deva[शतं पणते] also valid.

simple:
With @deva[दिव्] meaning 'stake/deal', the @deva[कर्मन्] takes @deva[षष्ठी].

**Example:**
- @deva[शतस्य दीव्यति] 'he stakes a hundred'
standard:
With @deva[दिव्] meaning 'staking/dealing', the @deva[कर्मन्] takes @deva[षष्ठी].

**Example:**
- @deva[शतस्य दीव्यति] 'he stakes/deals in a hundred'

**Counterexample:** @deva[ब्राह्मणं दीव्यति] 'he jokes with the @[brāhmaṇa]'—not staking sense, so @deva[द्वितीया].

This @deva[योगविभाग] (separate @[sūtra]) allows @deva[दिव्] to carry forward (@deva[अनुवृत्ति]) into subsequent @[sūtras].
advanced:
With @deva[दिव्] in @deva[द्यूत/क्रय-विक्रय] sense, @deva[कर्मन्] takes @deva[षष्ठी].

**Example:** @deva[शतस्य दीव्यति].

**Counterexample:** @deva[ब्राह्मणं दीव्यति] ('joke').

@deva[योगविभाग] for @deva[अनुवृत्ति] of @deva[दिव्] into subsequent @[sūtras].

simple:
With @deva[दिव्] preceded by an @deva[उपसर्ग], the @deva[कर्मन्] optionally takes @deva[षष्ठी].

**Examples:**
- @deva[शतस्य प्रतिदीव्यति] or @deva[शतं प्रतिदीव्यति] 'he stakes a hundred'
standard:
With @deva[दिव्] preceded by an @deva[उपसर्ग] (preverb), the @deva[कर्मन्] optionally takes @deva[षष्ठी] or @deva[द्वितीया].

**Examples:**
- @deva[शतस्य/शतं प्रतिदीव्यति] 'he stakes a hundred'

**Note:** Without @deva[उपसर्ग], @deva[षष्ठी] is obligatory (@ref[2.3.58]).

**Counterexample:** @deva[शलाकां प्रतिदीव्यति] 'he throws the missile'—not staking sense.
advanced:
With @deva[उपसर्ग] + @deva[दिव्] in @deva[द्यूत] sense, @deva[कर्मन्] optionally takes @deva[षष्ठी].

**Example:** @deva[शतस्य/शतं प्रतिदीव्यति].

Without @deva[उपसर्ग] → @deva[षष्ठी] obligatory (@ref[2.3.58]). Non-@deva[द्यूत] sense → @deva[द्वितीया]: @deva[शलाकां प्रतिदीव्यति].

simple:
In @deva[छन्दस्], @deva[षष्ठी] is diversely used for the @deva[करण] (instrument) of @deva[यज्].

**Examples:**
- @deva[घृतस्य यजते] = @deva[घृतेन यजते] 'he sacrifices with ghee'
- @deva[सोमस्य यजते] = @deva[सोमेन यजते] 'he sacrifices with soma'
standard:
In @deva[छन्दस्], @deva[षष्ठी] is diversely used for the @deva[करण] of @deva[यज्] 'to sacrifice' (instead of @deva[तृतीया]).

**Examples:**
- @deva[घृतस्य/घृतेन यजते] 'he sacrifices with ghee'
- @deva[सोमस्य/सोमेन यजते] 'he sacrifices with soma'

@deva[बहुलम्] = not universal.

simple:
With @deva[कृत्वसुच्]-ending words ('so many times'), the @deva[काल]-word takes @deva[षष्ठी] (for @deva[अधिकरण] sense).

**Examples:**
- @deva[पञ्चकृत्वोऽह्नो भुङ्क्ते] 'he eats five times a day'
- @deva[द्विरह्नोऽधीते] 'he studies twice a day'
standard:
With @deva[कृत्वसुच्]-ending words (@ref[5.4.17]: 'so many times'), the @deva[काल] (time) word takes @deva[षष्ठी] instead of @deva[सप्तमी] for @deva[अधिकरण] sense.

**Examples:**
- @deva[पञ्चकृत्वोऽह्नो भुङ्क्ते] 'he eats five times a day'
- @deva[द्विरह्नोऽधीते] 'he studies twice a day'

**Counterexamples:**
- @deva[अह्नि शेते] 'he sleeps in the day'—no @deva[कृत्वसुच्] word
- @deva[द्विः कांस्यपात्र्यां भुङ्क्ते] 'he eats in two brass vessels'—not @deva[काल]

@deva[शेष] understood → @deva[द्विरहन्यधीते] (@deva[सप्तमी]) also valid.
advanced:
With @deva[कृत्वसुच्]-ending words (@ref[5.4.17]), @deva[काल]-word takes @deva[षष्ठी] for @deva[अधिकरण].

**Examples:** @deva[पञ्चकृत्वोऽह्नो भुङ्क्ते], @deva[द्विरह्नोऽधीते].

**Counterexamples:** @deva[अह्नि शेते] (no @deva[कृत्वसुच्]), @deva[द्विः कांस्यपात्र्यां भुङ्क्ते] (not @deva[काल]).

@deva[शेष] → @deva[सप्तमी] also valid: @deva[द्विरहन्यधीते].

simple:
With @deva[कृत्]-ending words, the @deva[कर्तृ] (agent) and @deva[कर्मन्] (object) take @deva[षष्ठी].

**Examples:**
- @deva[कालिदासस्य क्रिया] 'composition of @[kālidāsa]' (@deva[कर्तृ])
- @deva[पुरां भेत्ता] 'destroyer of cities' (@deva[कर्मन्])
standard:
With @deva[कृत्]-ending words (@ref[3.1.93]), both @deva[कर्तृ] and @deva[कर्मन्] take @deva[षष्ठी].

**Examples:**
- @deva[कालिदासस्य क्रिया] 'composition of @[kālidāsa]' (subjective genitive)
- @deva[पुरां भेत्ता] 'destroyer of cities' (objective genitive)
- @deva[अपां स्रष्टा] 'creator of waters'
- @deva[वज्रस्य भर्त्ता] 'wielder of the thunderbolt'

**Counterexample:** @deva[शस्त्रेण भेत्ता] 'cutting with weapon'—@deva[करण] takes @deva[तृतीया], not @deva[षष्ठी].

**Not with @deva[तद्धित]:** @deva[कृतपूर्वीकटम्]—@deva[षष्ठी] doesn't apply.
advanced:
With @deva[कृत्]-ending words (@ref[3.1.93]), @deva[कर्तृ/कर्मन्] takes @deva[षष्ठी].

**Examples:** @deva[कालिदासस्य क्रिया] (subjective), @deva[पुरां भेत्ता] (objective), @deva[अपां स्रष्टा], @deva[वज्रस्य भर्त्ता].

**Counterexample:** @deva[शस्त्रेण भेत्ता]—@deva[करण] → @deva[तृतीया].

Not with @deva[तद्धित]: @deva[कृतपूर्वीकटम्].

simple:
When both @deva[कर्तृ] and @deva[कर्मन्] of a @deva[कृत्]-word are expressed, only @deva[कर्मन्] takes @deva[षष्ठी]; @deva[कर्तृ] takes @deva[तृतीया].

**Example:**
- @deva[गवां दोहोऽगोपालकेन] 'the milking of cows by a non-cowherd'
standard:
When both @deva[कर्तृ] and @deva[कर्मन्] of a @deva[कृत्]-word are expressed, only @deva[कर्मन्] takes @deva[षष्ठी]; @deva[कर्तृ] takes @deva[तृतीया].

**Examples:**
- @deva[आश्चर्यो गवां दोहोऽगोपालकेन] 'the milking of cows by a non-cowherd is a wonder'
- @deva[ओदनस्य भोजनं देवदत्तेन] 'the eating of rice by Devadatta'
- @deva[पयसः पानं यज्ञदत्तेन] 'the drinking of milk by @[yajñadatta]'

simple:
@deva[क्त] denoting @deva[अधिकरण] (from roots of fixedness, motion, eating) takes @deva[षष्ठी].

**Examples:**
- @deva[इदमेषामासितम्] 'this is their seat'
- @deva[इदमेषां शयितम्] 'this is their sleeping place'
standard:
@deva[क्त] denoting @deva[अधिकरण] (@ref[3.4.76]: from @deva[ध्रौव्य/गति/प्रत्यवसान]-meaning roots) takes @deva[षष्ठी].

**Examples:**
- @deva[इदमेषामासितम्] 'this is their seat'
- @deva[इदमेषां शयितम्] 'this is their sleeping place'

This is an exception to @ref[2.3.69].

**With @deva[द्विकर्मक] verbs:** Both objects take @deva[षष्ठी]: @deva[नेताऽश्वस्य ग्रामस्य चैत्रः] 'Caitra leads the horse to the village'. If one is principal: @deva[नेताऽश्वस्य ग्रामं चैत्रः].

simple:
@deva[षष्ठी] is NOT used for @deva[कर्तृ/कर्मन्] with words ending in: @deva[ल/उ/उक], @deva[अव्यय], @deva[निष्ठा], @deva[खलर्थ], or @deva[तृन्].

**Examples:**
- @deva[ओदनं पचन्] 'cooking rice' (not *@deva[ओदनस्य])
- @deva[कटं कृत्वा] 'having made a mat' (not *@deva[कटस्य])
standard:
@deva[षष्ठी] is NOT used for @deva[कर्तृ/कर्मन्] with words ending in:

1. **@deva[ल]** (@deva[शतृ/शानच्/कानच्/क्वसु/कि/किन्]): @deva[ओदनं पचन्/पचमानः]
2. **@deva[उ]** (@ref[3.2.168]): @deva[कटं चिकीर्षुः], @deva[ओदनं बुभुक्षुः]
3. **@deva[उक]** (@ref[3.2.154]): @deva[आगामुकं वाराणसीं रक्ष आहुः]
4. **@deva[कृत्]-@deva[अव्यय]**: @deva[कटं कृत्वा], @deva[ओदनं भुक्त्वा]
5. **@deva[निष्ठा]** (@deva[क्त/क्तवतु]): @deva[ओदनं भुक्तवान्], @deva[देवदत्तेन कृतम्]
6. **@deva[खलर्थ]** (@ref[3.3.126]): @deva[ईषत्करो भवता कटः]
7. **@deva[तृन्]** (@[pratyāhāra]): @deva[कर्ता कटान्], @deva[वदिता जनापवादान्]

Instead: @deva[कर्तृ] → @deva[तृतीया], @deva[कर्मन्] → @deva[द्वितीया].

simple:
@deva[षष्ठी] is NOT used with @deva[अक] denoting futurity or @deva[इन्] denoting futurity/debt.

**Examples:**
- @deva[कटं कारको व्रजति] 'he goes to make a mat' (not *@deva[कटस्य])
- @deva[ग्रामं गमी] 'one who must go to the village'
- @deva[शतं दायी] 'he owes a hundred'
standard:
@deva[षष्ठी] is NOT used with:

1. **@deva[अक]** (@deva[ण्वुल/वुञ्] etc.) denoting **futurity**: @deva[कटं कारको व्रजति] 'he goes to make a mat'
2. **@deva[इन्]** (@deva[णिनि] @ref[3.3.3], @ref[3.3.170]) denoting **futurity or debt**: @deva[ग्रामं गमी] 'must go', @deva[शतं दायी] 'owes a hundred'

**Counterexamples (not futurity/debt sense):**
- @deva[यवानां लावकः] 'cutter of barley'—@deva[षष्ठी] valid
- @deva[वर्षशतस्य पूरकः], @deva[पुत्रपौत्राणां दर्शकः]—different @deva[अक] (not @deva[भविष्यत्])

simple:
With @deva[कृत्य] (future passive participle) words, @deva[कर्तृ] optionally takes @deva[षष्ठी]; @deva[कर्मन्] always does.

**Example:**
- @deva[भवतः/भवता कटः कर्तव्यः] 'the mat is to be made by you'
standard:
With @deva[कृत्य] (future passive participle, @ref[3.1.95]) words, @deva[कर्तृ] optionally takes @deva[षष्ठी] or @deva[तृतीया]; @deva[कर्मन्] obligatorily takes @deva[षष्ठी].

**Examples:**
- @deva[भवतः/भवता कटः कर्तव्यः] 'the mat is to be made by you' (@deva[कर्तृ] optional)
- @deva[गेयो माणवकः साम्नाम्] 'the student is to be taught @[sāmans]'—@deva[कर्मन्] (@deva[साम्नाम्]) obligatory @deva[षष्ठी]

simple:
With @deva[सादृश्य] (resemblance) words, both @deva[तृतीया] and @deva[षष्ठी] work—except @deva[तुला/उपमा] which require @deva[षष्ठी] only.

**Examples:**
- @deva[तुल्यो देवदत्तेन/देवदत्तस्य] 'like Devadatta'
- @deva[सदृशो देवदत्तेन/देवदत्तस्य] 'resembling Devadatta'
standard:
With words meaning @deva[सादृश्य] (resemblance), both @deva[तृतीया] and @deva[षष्ठी] are used—except @deva[तुला] and @deva[उपमा] which require only @deva[षष्ठी].

**Examples:**
