standard: After @deva[साल्वादि] words, the affix @deva[वुञ्] comes in 'born etc.' senses when the meaning is 'a cow' or 'barley gruel' (debarring @deva[अण्] @ref[4.2.133]): @deva[साल्वको गौः] (cows born in @[sālva]); @deva[साल्विका यवागूः] (barley gruel of @[sālva]). In other cases: @deva[साल्वम्] (by @deva[अण्]).
advanced: This @[sūtra] provides @deva[वुञ्] (debarring @deva[अण्] @ref[4.2.133]) for @deva[साल्वादि] when @deva[गौ]/@deva[यवागू]: @deva[साल्वकः गौः], @deva[साल्विका यवागूः]. Otherwise @deva[साल्वम्].

simple: After locality-names ending in @deva[गर्त] (as second member), @deva[छ] is used. Examples: @deva[वृकगर्तीयम्], @deva[शृगालगर्तीयम्], @deva[श्वाविद्गर्तीयम्] (born in wolf-hole, jackal-hole, porcupine-hole).
standard: After @deva[प्रातिपदिक] (nominal stems) denoting a place and having @deva[गर्त] (hole/pit) as the second member (@deva[उत्तरपद]), the affix @deva[छ] comes in remaining senses (debarring @deva[अण्]). @deva[देशे] is understood. This subsequent rule also debars @deva[ठञ्]/@deva[ञिठ्] of @ref[4.2.117]. Examples: @deva[वृकगर्तीयम्], @deva[शृगालगर्तीयम्], @deva[श्वाविद्गर्तीयम्] (born in a porcupine's hole etc.). The word @deva[उत्तरपद] prevents applying to words that END in @deva[गर्त] but have an affix after it.
advanced: This @[sūtra] provides @deva[छ] (debarring @deva[अण्], @deva[ठञ्]/@deva[ञिठ्] @ref[4.2.117]) for @deva[देश गर्तोत्तरपद]: @deva[वृकगर्तीयम्], @deva[शृगालगर्तीयम्]. @deva[उत्तरपद] excludes @deva[गर्तान्त] with following affix.

simple: After @deva[गह] etc., @deva[छ] is used. Examples: @deva[गहीयः] (belonging to a cave), @deva[अन्तःस्थीयः], @deva[मध्यमीयाः].
standard: After @deva[गहादि] words, the affix @deva[छ] comes in remaining senses (debarring @deva[अण्] etc.): @deva[गहीयः] (belonging to a cave), @deva[अन्तःस्थीयः]. Special rule in the list: '@deva[मध्यमध्यमं चाण् चरणे]'—@deva[मध्य] becomes @deva[मध्यम] when @deva[छ] is added: @deva[मध्यमीयाः]. But when the sense is a Vedic school (@deva[चरण]), @deva[अण्] is used: @deva[माध्यमाः]. @deva[मध्यम] here means 'middle of the body' specifically.
advanced: This @[sūtra] provides @deva[छ] (debarring @deva[अण्]) for @deva[गहादि]: @deva[गहीयः], @deva[अन्तःस्थीयः]. Special: @deva[मध्य] → @deva[मध्यम] + @deva[छ] = @deva[मध्यमीयाः]; for @deva[चरण]: @deva[अण्] = @deva[माध्यमाः].

simple: After @deva[कद्]-initial words denoting Eastern places, @deva[छ] is used. Examples: @deva[कटनगरीयम्], @deva[कटघोषीयम्], @deva[कटपल्वलीयम्].
standard: After words beginning with @deva[कद्] (which become @deva[कट्]-initial) denoting places of the Eastern people (@deva[प्राच्य]), the affix @deva[छ] comes in remaining senses (debarring @deva[अण्]). @deva[देशे] is understood; @deva[प्राक्] qualifies @deva[देशे]. Examples: @deva[कटनगरीयम्], @deva[कटघोषीयम्], @deva[कटपल्वलीयम्].
advanced: This @[sūtra] provides @deva[छ] (debarring @deva[अण्]) for @deva[कदादि प्राग्देश]: @deva[कटनगरीयम्], @deva[कटघोषीयम्], @deva[कटपल्वलीयम्].

simple: Before @deva[छ], the final of @deva[राजन्] is replaced by @deva[क]. Example: @deva[राजकीयम्].
standard: When @deva[छ] is added to @deva[राजन्], the final is replaced by @deva[क]: @deva[राजन्] + @deva[छ] → @deva[राजकीयम्]. This @[sūtra] only teaches substitution, as @deva[राजन्] would already take @deva[छ] by @ref[4.2.114]. The word @deva[देशे] doesn't govern this @[sūtra] (not applicable here).
advanced: This @[sūtra] provides @deva[क]-substitution for @deva[राजन्] + @deva[छ]: @deva[राजकीयम्]. @deva[छ] by @ref[4.2.114]; this @[sūtra] adds substitution. @deva[देशे] doesn't apply.

simple: After @deva[वृद्ध] place-names ending in @deva[अक्]/@deva[इक्]] or with penultimate @deva[ख्], @deva[छ] is used. Examples: @deva[आरोहणकीयम्], @deva[आश्वपथिकीयम्], @deva[कौटिशिखीयम्].
standard: After @deva[वृद्ध] words denoting a place and ending in @deva[अक्] or @deva[इक्], or having @deva[ख्] as penultimate (@deva[खोपध]), the affix @deva[छ] comes in remaining senses. @deva[देशे] must be read in. This debars @deva[अण्] of @ref[4.2.132] and the affixes of @ref[4.2.117] and @ref[4.2.123]. @deva[अकन्त] examples: @deva[आरोहणकीयम्], @deva[द्रौघणकीयम्]. @deva[इकन्त] examples: @deva[आश्वपथिकीयम्], @deva[शाल्मलिकीयम्]. @deva[खोपध] examples: @deva[कौटिशिखीयम्], @deva[आयोमुखीयम्]. @[vārtika]: @deva[सौसुकादि] words also take this rule though ending in @deva[उ].
advanced: This @[sūtra] provides @deva[छ] (debarring @deva[अण्] @ref[4.2.132], @ref[4.2.117], @ref[4.2.123]) for @deva[वृद्ध देश अकन्त]/@deva[इकन्त]/@deva[खोपध]: @deva[आरोहणकीयम्], @deva[आश्वपथिकीयम्], @deva[कौटिशिखीयम्]. @[vārtika]: @deva[सौसुकादि] (@deva[उकारान्त]) too.

simple: After @deva[वृद्ध] words with @deva[कन्था]/@deva[पलद]/@deva[नगर]/@deva[ग्राम]/@deva[हृद] as second member, @deva[छ] is used. Examples: @deva[दाक्षिकन्थीयम्], @deva[दाक्षिनगरीयम्], @deva[दाक्षिग्रामीयम्].
standard: After @deva[वृद्ध] words having @deva[कन्था], @deva[पलद], @deva[नगर], @deva[ग्राम], or @deva[हृद] as the second member (@deva[उत्तरपद]), the affix @deva[छ] comes in remaining senses (debarring @deva[ठञ्]/@deva[ञिठ्] of @ref[4.2.117]). Examples: @deva[दाक्षिकन्थीयम्], @deva[माहिकिकन्थीयम्]; @deva[दाक्षिपलदीयम्], @deva[माहिकिपलदीयम्]; @deva[दाक्षिनगरीयम्], @deva[माहिकिनगरीयम्]; @deva[दाक्षिग्रामीयम्], @deva[माहिकिग्रामीयम्]; @deva[दाक्षिह्रदीयम्], @deva[माहिकिह्रदीयम्].
advanced: This @[sūtra] provides @deva[छ] (debarring @deva[ठञ्]/@deva[ञिठ्] @ref[4.2.117]) for @deva[वृद्ध] with @deva[उत्तरपद] = @deva[कन्था]/@deva[पलद]/@deva[नगर]/@deva[ग्राम]/@deva[हृद]: @deva[दाक्षिकन्थीयम्], @deva[दाक्षिनगरीयम्], @deva[दाक्षिग्रामीयम्], @deva[दाक्षिह्रदीयम्].

simple: After @deva[पर्वत] (mountain), @deva[छ] is used. Examples: @deva[पर्वतीयो राजा] (hill-king), @deva[पर्वतीयः पुरुषः] (hill man).
standard: After @deva[पर्वत] (mountain), the affix @deva[छ] comes in remaining senses (debarring @deva[अण्]): @deva[पर्वतीयो राजा] (the hill-king); @deva[पर्वतीयः पुरुषः] (the hill man).
advanced: This @[sūtra] provides @deva[छ] (debarring @deva[अण्]) for @deva[पर्वत]: @deva[पर्वतीयो राजा], @deva[पर्वतीयः पुरुषः].

simple: After @deva[पर्वत], @deva[छ] is optional when NOT meaning 'a man'. Examples: @deva[पर्वतीयानि फलानि] or @deva[पार्वतानि फलानि] (hill fruits).
standard: After @deva[पर्वत], the affix @deva[छ] comes optionally in remaining senses when the meaning is NOT 'a man' (@deva[अमनुष्य]): @deva[पर्वतीयानि फलानि] (@deva[छ]) or @deva[पार्वतानि फलानि] (@deva[अण्]) (hill fruits); @deva[पर्वतीयमुदकम्] or @deva[पार्वतमुदकम्] (hill water). Why 'not human'? For humans, no option: @deva[पर्वतीयो मनुष्यः] (only @deva[छ]).
advanced: This @[sūtra] provides optional @deva[छ] for @deva[पर्वत] when @deva[अमनुष्य]: @deva[पर्वतीयानि]/@deva[पार्वतानि] @deva[फलानि]. For @deva[मनुष्य]: only @deva[छ] (@deva[पर्वतीयः]).

simple: After @deva[कृकण] and @deva[पर्ण] (when meaning @deva[भारद्वाज] country), @deva[छ] is used. Examples: @deva[कृकणीयम्], @deva[पर्णीयम्].
standard: After @deva[कृकण] and @deva[पर्ण], when denoting the country of @deva[भारद्वाज] (not the @deva[गोत्र]), the affix @deva[छ] comes in remaining senses: @deva[कृकणीयम्], @deva[पर्णीयम्]. @deva[देशे] is understood. @deva[भारद्वाज] here means country, not lineage. Why 'denoting @[bhāradvāja] country'? Otherwise: @deva[कार्कणम्], @deva[पार्णम्] (by @deva[अण्]).
advanced: This @[sūtra] provides @deva[छ] for @deva[कृकण]/@deva[पर्ण] when @deva[भारद्वाज-देश]: @deva[कृकणीयम्], @deva[पर्णीयम्]. @deva[भारद्वाज] = @deva[देश] (not @deva[गोत्र]). Otherwise: @deva[कार्कणम्], @deva[पार्णम्] (@deva[अण्]).

simple:
The affixes @deva[अञ्] and @deva[ठञ्] come after @deva[अर्ध] (half) when preceded by a direction word, and when the meaning refers to a particular portion of a village (@deva[ग्राम]) or inhabited country (@deva[जनपद]).

This replaces @deva[यत्] from the previous rules.

Example: @deva[पौर्वार्धाः] or @deva[पौर्वार्धिकाः] means 'those belonging to the eastern half of our village or country'. Similarly @deva[दाक्षिणार्धाः] or @deva[दाक्षिणार्धिकाः].

The word @deva[पौर्वार्धाः] = @deva[ग्रामस्य पूर्वस्मिन् अर्धे भवाः] (those dwelling in the eastern half of the village).
standard:
When @deva[पूर्वपद] denotes direction and the sense is a portion of @deva[ग्राम] or @deva[जनपद], @deva[अञ्] and @deva[ठञ्] apply after @deva[अर्ध], debarring @deva[यत्].

The phrase @deva[दिक्पूर्वपदात्] continues from @ref[4.3.6].

Examples: @deva[इमे खल्वस्माकं ग्रामस्य जनपदस्य वा पौर्वार्धाः] or @deva[पौर्वार्धिकाः] (these belong to the eastern half of our village/country). Similarly @deva[दाक्षिणार्धाः] / @deva[दाक्षिणार्धिकाः].

@deva[पौर्वार्धाः] is a @deva[तद्धितार्थ] compound: @deva[ग्रामस्य पूर्वस्मिन् अर्धे भवाः].

simple:
The affix @deva[अ] comes after @deva[मध्य] (middle) when the meaning is 'fit' or 'proper' (@deva[साम्प्रतिक]).

Examples:
• @deva[मध्यं काष्ठम्] — the proper piece of wood (neither too long nor too short).
• @deva[मध्ये वैयाकरणः] — the proper grammarian (neither too refined nor too dull).
• @deva[मध्या स्त्री] — a proper wife (of moderate qualities).

Here @deva[साम्प्रतिक] means 'equity, propriety, or appropriateness'.
standard:
The affix @deva[अ] applies after @deva[मध्य] when expressing @deva[साम्प्रतिक] (fitness, propriety).

Examples: @deva[नातिदीर्घं नाति ह्रस्वं मध्यं काष्ठम्] (the proper wood—neither too long nor too short). @deva[मध्ये वैयाकरणः] (a moderate grammarian). @deva[मध्या स्त्री] (a proper wife).

The sense @deva[साम्प्रतिक] (equity, rightness) distinguishes this from the locational @deva[मध्य].

simple:
The affix @deva[यञ्] comes after @deva[द्वीप] (island) when it means 'near the sea' (@deva[अनुसमुद्र]).

@deva[द्वीप] is listed in the @deva[कच्छादि] class and would normally take @deva[अण्] or @deva[वुञ्]. This rule overrides both when the island is coastal.

Example: @deva[द्वैप्यम्] means 'living on or relating to an island', as in @deva[द्वैप्यं भवन्तोऽनुचरन्ति चक्रम्] (the islanders follow the discus).

But for inland islands: @deva[द्वैपकम्] or @deva[द्वैपम्].
standard:
The affix @deva[यञ्] applies after @deva[द्वीप] when meaning @deva[अनुसमुद्र] (near the sea), debarring @deva[अण्] and @deva[वुञ्] from @ref[4.2.133]–@ref[4.2.134].

Example: @deva[द्वैप्यम्] (pertaining to a coastal island). As in @deva[द्वैप्यं भवन्तोऽनुचरन्ति चक्रम्].

When not @deva[अनुसमुद्र]: @deva[द्वैपकम्] or @deva[द्वैपम्] (@ref[4.2.133], @ref[4.2.134]).

@deva[अनुसमुद्र] is an @deva[अव्ययीभाव] compound by @ref[2.1.15].

simple:
The affix @deva[ठञ्] comes after words denoting time (@deva[काल]) in the remaining senses. This replaces both @deva[अण्] and @deva[छ] (which would apply to @deva[वृद्ध] words).

Examples: @deva[मासिकः] (monthly), @deva[आर्धमासिकः] (fortnightly), @deva[सांवत्सरिकः] (annual).

Even words that indirectly denote time take this affix: @deva[कादम्बपुष्पिकम्] (related to the time of Kadamba flowers), @deva[व्रैहिपलालिकम्] (related to rice-straw season).

The word @deva[काल] governs the subsequent rules up through 4.3.25.
standard:
The affix @deva[ठञ्] applies after @deva[कालवाचिन्] (time-denoting) words in @deva[शेष] senses, debarring @deva[अण्] and @deva[छ] (for @deva[वृद्ध] words, @ref[4.2.114]).

Examples: @deva[मासिकः] (monthly), @deva[आर्धमासिकः] (bi-monthly), @deva[सांवत्सरिकः] (annual).

Words indirectly indicating time also qualify: @deva[कादम्बपुष्पिकम्], @deva[व्रैहिपलालिकम्].

The @deva[अधिकार] of @deva[काल] extends through @ref[4.3.25] (@deva[तत्र जातः]).

simple:
The affix @deva[ठञ्] comes after @deva[शरद्] (autumn) when the meaning involves @deva[श्राद्ध] (funeral oblation).

This replaces @deva[अण्] which would apply by the general rule for seasons.

Example: @deva[शारदिकं श्राद्धम्] means 'the autumnal funeral rite'.

When not referring to funeral rites: @deva[शारदम्] (autumnal), with @deva[अण्].

The affix @deva[ठञ्] optionally comes after @deva[शरद्] (autumn) when the meaning involves illness (@deva[रोग]) or heat (@deva[आतप]).

Examples: @deva[शारदिको रोगः] (the autumnal disease), @deva[शारदिक आतपः] (autumnal sunshine/heat).

When not referring to illness or heat: @deva[शारदं दधि] (autumnal curd, with @deva[अण्]).

This @deva[ठञ्] replaces the seasonal @deva[अण्] from 4.3.16.
standard:
The affix @deva[ठञ्] optionally applies after @deva[शरद्] when denoting @deva[रोग] or @deva[आतप], debarring @deva[ऋतु]-governed @deva[अण्] (@ref[4.3.16]).

Examples: @deva[शारदिको रोगः] (autumnal disease), @deva[शारदिक आतपः] (autumnal heat).

Non-illness/heat context: @deva[शारदं दधि] (autumnal curd).
advanced:
@deva[ठञ्] optionally applies after @deva[शरद्] in @deva[रोग]/@deva[आतप]] senses, superseding @deva[ऋतु]-@deva[अण्] (@ref[4.3.16]).

Examples: @deva[शारदिको रोगः], @deva[शारदिक आतपः]. Counter-example: @deva[शारदं दधि].

simple:
The affix @deva[एण्य] comes after @deva[प्रावृष्] (the rainy season) in the remaining senses.

This replaces @deva[अण्] from @ref[4.3.16], even though @deva[प्रावृष्] is a season-denoting word.

Example: @deva[प्रावृषेण्यो बलाहकः] (the cloud belonging to the rainy season).
standard:
The affix @deva[एण्य] applies after @deva[प्रावृष्] in @deva[शेष] senses, debarring @deva[ऋतु]-governed @deva[अण्] (@ref[4.3.16]).

Example: @deva[प्रावृषेण्यो बलाहकः] (the rainy-season cloud).
advanced:
@deva[एण्य] is ordained after @deva[प्रावृष्] in @deva[शेष] senses, superseding @deva[ऋतु]-@deva[अण्] (@ref[4.3.16]).

Example: @deva[प्रावृषेण्यो बलाहकः].

simple:
In Vedic literature (@deva[छन्दस्]), the affix @deva[ठञ्] comes after @deva[वसन्त] (spring) in the remaining senses.

This replaces @deva[अण्] from 4.3.16.

Example: @deva[मधुश्च माधवश्च वासन्तिकावृतू] (Madhu and @[mādhava] are the two spring months).
standard:
In @deva[छन्दस्], @deva[ठञ्] applies after @deva[वसन्त] in @deva[शेष] senses, debarring @deva[ऋतु]-@deva[अण्] (@ref[4.3.16]).

Example: @deva[मधुश्च माधवश्च वासन्तिकावृतू].
advanced:
In @deva[छन्दस्], @deva[ठञ्] supersedes @deva[ऋतु]-@deva[अण्] (@ref[4.3.16]) after @deva[वसन्त].

Example: @deva[मधुश्च माधवश्च वासन्तिकावृतू].

simple:
After the words @deva[सायम्] (at evening), @deva[चिरम्] (for a long time), @deva[प्राह्णे] (in the forenoon), @deva[प्रगे] (at dawn), and after other indeclinables expressing time, the affixes @deva[ट्यु] and @deva[ट्युल्] apply, with the augment @deva[तुट्].

Examples:
• @deva[सायम्] + @deva[तुट्] + @deva[ट्यु] = @deva[सायंतनम्] (belonging to evening)
• @deva[चिरंतनम्] (lasting, enduring)
• @deva[प्राह्णेतनम्] (of the forenoon)
• @deva[प्रगेतनम्] (of early dawn)

For other time-indeclinables: @deva[दोषातनम्] (of the night), @deva[दिवातनम्] (of the day).

Supplementary rules (@deva[वार्त्तिक]):
• @deva[त्न] comes after @deva[चिर], @deva[परुत्], @deva[परारि]: @deva[चिरत्नम्], @deva[परुत्नम्], @deva[परारित्नम्].
• In Vedas, @deva[ग] of @deva[प्रग] is elided before @deva[त्न]: @deva[प्रत्नम्].
• @deva[डिमच्] comes after @deva[अग्र], @deva[आदि], @deva[पश्चाद्]: @deva[अग्रिमम्], @deva[आदिमम्], @deva[पश्चिमम्]. Also after @deva[अन्त]: @deva[अन्तिमम्].

The difference between @deva[ट्यु] and @deva[ट्युल्] is accent.

After @deva[प्रावृट्] (rainy season) in the locative case, the affix @deva[ठप्] comes in the sense of 'produced therein'.

This replaces @deva[एण्य] from 4.3.17. The @deva[प्] in @deva[ठप्] is for accent purposes.

Example: @deva[प्रावृषि जातः] = @deva[प्रावृषिकः] (produced in the rainy season).
standard:
The affix @deva[ठप्] applies after @deva[प्रावृट्] (locative) in @deva[तत्र जातः] sense, debarring @deva[एण्य] (@ref[4.3.17]). The @deva[प्] is @deva[इत्] for @deva[स्वर].

Example: @deva[प्रावृषि जातः] → @deva[प्रावृषिकः].
advanced:
@deva[ठप्] supersedes @deva[एण्य] (@ref[4.3.17]) after @deva[प्रावृट्] (@deva[सप्तम्यन्त]) in @deva[जात]-sense. @deva[प्] = @deva[इत्] (@deva[स्वरार्थ]).

Example: @deva[प्रावृषि जातः] → @deva[प्रावृषिकः].

simple:
The affix @deva[वुञ्] comes after @deva[पथ्] (path, way) in the sense of 'produced therein', and @deva[पथ्] is replaced by @deva[पन्थ].

This replaces @deva[अण्].

Example: @deva[पथि जातः] = @deva[पन्थकः] (produced on the road).
standard:
The affix @deva[वुञ्] applies after @deva[पथ्] in @deva[जात]-sense with the substitute @deva[पन्थ] for @deva[पथ्], debarring @deva[अण्].

Example: @deva[पथि जातः] → @deva[पन्थकः].
advanced:
@deva[वुञ्] applies after @deva[पथ्] in @deva[जात]-sense; @deva[पथादेशः पन्थ], superseding @deva[अण्].

Example: @deva[पथि जातः] → @deva[पन्थकः].

simple:
The affix @deva[अ] is also added to @deva[अमावास्या] (new moon day) in the sense of 'born therein'.

This adds a third affix alongside @deva[वुञ्] and @deva[अण्] from the previous rules.

Three forms exist: @deva[अमावास्यः] (with @deva[अ]), @deva[अमावास्यकः] (with @deva[वुञ्]), @deva[आमावास्यः] (with @deva[अण्]).

The variant @deva[अमावस्य] also takes these: @deva[अमावस्यः], @deva[अमावस्यकः], @deva[आमावस्यः].
advanced:
@deva[अ] is additionally ordained after @deva[अमावास्या] in @deva[जात]-sense, supplementing @deva[वुञ्] (@ref[4.3.30]) and @deva[अण्].

Forms: @deva[अमावास्यः], @deva[अमावास्यकः], @deva[आमावास्यः]; @deva[अमावस्य]: @deva[अमावस्यः], @deva[अमावस्यकः], @deva[आमावस्यः].

simple:
The affixes @deva[अण्] and @deva[अञ्] come after @deva[सिन्धु] and @deva[अपकर] respectively (in addition to @deva[कन्] from the previous rule).

Examples: @deva[सैन्धवः] (from Sindhu, with @deva[अण्]), @deva[आपकरः] (from Apakara, with @deva[अञ्]).
standard:
The affixes @deva[अण्] and @deva[अञ्] apply after @deva[सिन्धु] and @deva[अपकर] respectively, in addition to @deva[कन्] (@ref[4.3.32]).

Examples: @deva[सैन्धवः], @deva[आपकरः].
advanced:
@deva[अण्] after @deva[सिन्धु], @deva[अञ्] after @deva[अपकर]—supplementing @deva[कन्] (@ref[4.3.32]).

Examples: @deva[सैन्धवः], @deva[आपकरः].

simple:
The affix denoting 'born therein' is elided by @deva[लुक्] after the asterism names: @deva[श्रविष्ठा], @deva[फाल्गुनी], @deva[अनुराधा], @deva[स्वाति], @deva[तिष्य], @deva[पुनर्वसु], @deva[हस्त], @deva[विशाखा], @deva[आषाढा], and @deva[बहुला].

The feminine affixes are also elided by 1.2.49.

Examples: @deva[श्रविष्ठासु जातः] = @deva[श्रविष्ठः] (born under @[śraviṣṭhā]). Similarly: @deva[फल्गुनः], @deva[अनुराधः], @deva[स्वातिः], @deva[तिष्यः], @deva[पुनर्वसुः], @deva[हस्तः], @deva[विशाखः], @deva[आषाढः], @deva[बहुलः]. @deva[बहुला] is another name for @deva[कृत्तिका].

Supplementary rules:
• After @deva[चित्रा], @deva[रेवती], @deva[रोहिणी], the affix is elided when forming feminine derivatives: @deva[चित्रायां जाता] = @deva[चित्रा].
• @deva[ट] and @deva[अन्] come after @deva[फल्गुनी] and @deva[आषाढा] for feminine: @deva[फल्गुनी], @deva[आषाढा].
• @deva[छण्] also comes after @deva[श्रविष्ठा] and @deva[आषाढा]: @deva[श्राविष्ठीयः], @deva[आषाढीयः].

The affix denoting 'born therein' is elided by @deva[लुक्] after words ending in @deva[स्थान] (place), and after @deva[गोशाला] (cow-shed) and @deva[खरशाला] (donkey-shed).

Examples: @deva[गोस्थाने जातः] = @deva[गोस्थानः] (born in the cow-place), @deva[अश्वस्थानः] (born in the horse-place), @deva[गोशालः] (born in the cow-shed), @deva[खरशालः] (born in the donkey-shed).
standard:
The @deva[जात]-affix undergoes @deva[लुक्] after @deva[स्थानान्त] words and after @deva[गोशाला], @deva[खरशाला].

Examples: @deva[गोस्थानः], @deva[अश्वस्थानः], @deva[गोशालः], @deva[खरशालः].
advanced:
@deva[लुक्] applies to @deva[जात]-@deva[प्रत्यय] after @deva[स्थानान्त] and @deva[गोशाला]/@deva[खरशाला]].

Examples: @deva[गोस्थानः], @deva[अश्वस्थानः], @deva[गोशालः], @deva[खरशालः].

simple:
The affix denoting 'born therein' is optionally elided by @deva[लुक्] after @deva[वत्सशाला] (calf-shed), @deva[अभिजित्] (an asterism), @deva[अश्वयुज्] (an asterism), and @deva[शतभिषज्] (an asterism).

Examples:
• @deva[वत्सशालायां जातः] = @deva[वत्सशालः] or @deva[वात्सशालः]
• @deva[अभिजित्] or @deva[आभिजितः]
• @deva[अश्वयुक्] or @deva[आश्वयुजः]
• @deva[शतभिक्] or @deva[शातभिषजः]

Both @deva[वत्सशाल] and @deva[वत्सशाला] are valid forms. This exemplifies the @deva[बहुल] (diverse) application mentioned in the next rule.
standard:
The @deva[जात]-affix optionally undergoes @deva[लुक्] after @deva[वत्सशाला], @deva[अभिजित्], @deva[अश्वयुज्], @deva[शतभिषज्].

Examples: @deva[वत्सशालः] / @deva[वात्सशालः], @deva[अभिजित्] / @deva[आभिजितः], @deva[अश्वयुक्] / @deva[आश्वयुजः], @deva[शतभिक्] / @deva[शातभिषजः].

Both @deva[वत्सशाल]/@deva[वत्सशाला]] are valid. These exemplify @deva[बहुल] from @ref[4.3.37].
advanced:
@deva[लुक्] optionally applies after @deva[वत्सशाला], @deva[अभिजित्], @deva[अश्वयुज्], @deva[शतभिषज्] in @deva[जात]-sense.

Forms: @deva[वत्सशालः]/@deva[वात्सशालः]], @deva[अभिजित्]/@deva[आभिजितः]], @deva[अश्वयुक्]/@deva[आश्वयुजः]], @deva[शतभिक्]/@deva[शातभिषजः]]. Both @deva[वत्सशाल]/@deva[वत्सशाला]] valid. These instantiate @deva[बहुल] (@ref[4.3.37]).

simple:
The affix denoting 'born therein' is diversely (@deva[बहुलम्]) elided by @deva[लुक्] after asterism names.

This means the elision is optional and varies.

Examples: @deva[रोहिणः] (with @deva[लुक्]) or @deva[रौहिणः] (with @deva[अण्] from @ref[4.3.16]), @deva[मृगशिरा] (with @deva[लुक्]) or @deva[मार्गशीर्षः] (with @deva[अण्]).
standard:
@deva[लुक्] applies @deva[बहुलम्] (diversely) to the @deva[जात]-affix after @deva[नक्षत्र] names.

Examples: @deva[रोहिणः] / @deva[रौहिणः] (@ref[4.3.16]), @deva[मृगशिरा] / @deva[मार्गशीर्षः] (@ref[4.3.16]).
advanced:
@deva[लुक्] applies @deva[बहुलम्] after @deva[नक्षत्र] in @deva[जात]-sense.

Examples: @deva[रोहिणः]/@deva[रौहिणः]], @deva[मृगशिरा]/@deva[मार्गशीर्षः]] (@ref[4.3.16]).

simple:
An affix comes after a word in the locative case in the sense of 'done there' (@deva[कृत]), 'obtained there' (@deva[लब्ध]), 'bought there' (@deva[क्रीत]), or 'dexterous therein' (@deva[कुशल]).

These are four additional @deva[तद्धित] senses.

Examples: @deva[स्रौघ्नः] may mean 'done in Srughna', 'obtained in Srughna', 'bought in Srughna', or 'skillful in Srughna'. Similarly @deva[माथुरः], @deva[राष्ट्रियः].
standard:
An affix applies after @deva[सप्तम्यन्त] in the senses @deva[कृत] (done there), @deva[लब्ध] (obtained there), @deva[क्रीत] (bought there), @deva[कुशल] (skilled therein).

Examples: @deva[स्रौघ्नः] (done/obtained/bought in, or skilled in Srughna); @deva[माथुरः], @deva[राष्ट्रियः].
advanced:
Affixes apply after @deva[सप्तम्यन्त] in @deva[कृत]/@deva[लब्ध]]/@deva[क्रीत]]/@deva[कुशल]] senses.

Examples: @deva[स्रौघ्नः], @deva[माथुरः], @deva[राष्ट्रियः].

simple:
An affix comes after a word in the @deva[सप्तमी] (locative) case in the sense of 'generally found therein' (@deva[प्रायभव]).

@deva[प्राय] means 'abundant' or 'mostly'. This refers to something frequently but not always present.

Example: @deva[स्रौघ्नः] (what is mostly found in Srughna) = @deva[स्रुघ्ने प्रायेण बाहुल्येन भवति]. Similarly @deva[माथुरः], @deva[राष्ट्रियः].

This rule might seem superfluous since @deva[तत्र भवः] (@ref[4.3.53]) is more comprehensive. But if @deva[प्रायभव] means @deva[अनित्यभव] (sometimes present, sometimes not), there is a subtle distinction.
standard:
An affix applies after @deva[सप्तम्यन्त] in @deva[प्रायभव] sense (mostly found therein). @deva[प्राय] = abundant, less than all.

Examples: @deva[स्रौघ्नः] = @deva[स्रुघ्ने प्रायेण भवति]; @deva[माथुरः], @deva[राष्ट्रियः].

This may seem covered by @ref[4.3.53] (@deva[तत्र भवः]). If @deva[प्रायभव] = @deva[अनित्यभव], a distinction exists.
advanced:
Affixes apply after @deva[सप्तम्यन्त] in @deva[प्रायभव] sense.

Examples: @deva[स्रौघ्नः], @deva[माथुरः], @deva[राष्ट्रियः].

Apparent redundancy with @ref[4.3.53] (@deva[तत्र भवः]); justified if @deva[प्रायभव] ≠ @deva[नित्यभव] but = @deva[अनित्यभव].

simple:
The affix @deva[ठक्] comes in the sense of 'mostly found there' after @deva[उपजानु] (near the knee), @deva[उपकर्ण] (near the ear), and @deva[उपनीवि] (near the waist-band), in the locative case.

This replaces @deva[अण्] etc.

Examples: @deva[औपजानुकः], @deva[औपकर्णिकः], @deva[औपनीविकः].
standard:
The affix @deva[ठक्] applies after @deva[उपजानु], @deva[उपकर्ण], @deva[उपनीवि] (@deva[सप्तम्यन्त]) in @deva[प्रायभव] sense, debarring @deva[अण्] etc.

Examples: @deva[औपजानुकः], @deva[औपकर्णिकः], @deva[औपनीविकः].
advanced:
@deva[ठक्] applies after @deva[उपजानु], @deva[उपकर्ण], @deva[उपनीवि] in @deva[प्रायभव] sense, superseding @deva[अण्].

Examples: @deva[औपजानुकः], @deva[औपकर्णिकः], @deva[औपनीविकः].

standard:
After @deva[प्रातिपदिक] or @deva[ङीबन्त]/@deva[आबन्त]] (@deva[सप्तम्यन्त]), an affix applies in @deva[संभूत] sense (adapted to, suited for). @deva[संभूत] ≠ origin/existence; rather = @deva[अवक्लृप्ति], @deva[प्रमाणातिरेक].

Example: @deva[स्रुघ्ने संभवति] → @deva[स्रौघ्नः] (suited to Srughna); @deva[माथुरः], @deva[राष्ट्रियः].
advanced:
After @deva[प्रातिपदिक]/@deva[ङीबन्त]]/@deva[आबन्त]] (@deva[सप्तम्यन्त]), affixes apply in @deva[संभूत] sense. @deva[संभूत] = @deva[अवक्लृप्ति]/@deva[प्रमाणातिरेक]], not @deva[जात]/@deva[भव]].

Examples: @deva[स्रौघ्नः], @deva[माथुरः], @deva[राष्ट्रियः].

simple:
The affix @deva[ढञ्] comes after @deva[कोश] (cocoon, sheath) and similar words in the sense of 'adapted to that'.

This replaces @deva[अण्].

Example: @deva[कोशे संभूतम्] = @deva[कौशेयं वस्त्रम्] (silken clothes). @deva[कोश] means 'cocoon', so @deva[कौशेय] literally means 'suited to the cocoon', but conventionally it means 'silken'.

Note: This rule might fit better under @deva[विकार] (@ref[4.3.134]) rather than @deva[संभूत], or after @ref[4.3.159] (@deva[एण्याढञ्]) with @deva[कोशाच्च] added.
standard:
The affix @deva[ढञ्] applies after @deva[कोशादि] in @deva[संभूत] sense, debarring @deva[अण्].

Example: @deva[कोशे संभूतम्] → @deva[कौशेयं वस्त्रम्] (silken). @deva[कोश] = cocoon; @deva[कौशेय] is @deva[रूढि] (conventional) for 'silken', not 'suited to the sheath'.

Placement note: This rule would fit better under @deva[विकार] (@ref[4.3.134]) or after @ref[4.3.159] with @deva[कोशाच्च].
advanced:
@deva[ढञ्] applies after @deva[कोशादि] in @deva[संभूत] sense, superseding @deva[अण्].

Example: @deva[कौशेयं वस्त्रम्]. @deva[कौशेय] = @deva[रूढि] for 'silken'. Placement should arguably follow @ref[4.3.134] (@deva[विकार]) or @ref[4.3.159] with @deva[कोशाच्च].

simple:
An affix comes after a word denoting time in the @deva[सप्तमी] (locative) case, in the sense of 'being good therein' (@deva[साधु]), 'flowering therein' (@deva[पुष्प्यति]), or 'ripening therein' (@deva[पच्यते]).

Examples:
• @deva[हेमन्ते साधुः] = @deva[हैमन्तः] (good in winter), as in @deva[हैमन्तः प्राकारः] (a wall suited for winter).
• @deva[वसन्ते पुष्प्यन्ति] = @deva[वासन्त्यः] (vernal creepers that flower in spring), @deva[ग्रैष्म्यः पाटलाः] (summer-flowering @[pāṭala]).
• @deva[शरदि पच्यन्ते] = @deva[शारदाः शालयः] (rice ripening in autumn), @deva[ग्रैष्मा यवाः] (barley ripening in summer).

This rule teaches the meaning; the specific affix (@deva[अण्] etc.) comes from earlier rules.
standard:
An affix applies after @deva[कालवाचिन्] (@deva[सप्तम्यन्त]) in senses @deva[साधु] (good therein), @deva[पुष्प्यति] (flowering), @deva[पच्यते] (ripening).

Examples: @deva[हैमन्तः प्राकारः] (winter-suited wall); @deva[वासन्त्यः] (spring-flowering creepers); @deva[शारदाः शालयः] (autumn-ripening rice); @deva[ग्रैष्मा यवाः] (summer-ripening barley).

This is an @deva[अर्थसूत्र] (meaning-rule); the affix (@deva[अण्] etc.) is supplied from prior rules.
advanced:
Affixes apply after @deva[कालवाचिन्] (@deva[सप्तम्यन्त]) in @deva[साधु]/@deva[पुष्प्यति]]/@deva[पच्यते]] senses.

Examples: @deva[हैमन्तः], @deva[वासन्त्यः], @deva[शारदाः शालयः], @deva[ग्रैष्मा यवाः].

This @deva[अर्थसूत्र] specifies @deva[प्रातिपदिक] and @deva[प्रत्ययार्थ]; @deva[अण्]-@deva[आदि] affixes are supplied.

simple:
The affix @deva[वुञ्] comes after @deva[आश्वयुजी] (the full-moon day in the asterism @[aśvinī]) in the sense of 'sown therein'.

This replaces @deva[ठञ्].

Example: @deva[आश्वयुज्यां उप्ताः] = @deva[आश्वयुजका माषाः] (pulse sown in @[āśvayujī]).

@deva[आश्वयुजी] is the name of the full-moon in the asterism @[aśvinī]. @deva[अश्वयुज्] and @deva[अश्विनी] are the same; some texts read @deva[अश्वनी] instead.
advanced:
@deva[वुञ्] applies after @deva[आश्वयुजी] in @deva[उप्त] sense, superseding @deva[ठञ्].

Example: @deva[आश्वयुजका माषाः]. @deva[आश्वयुजी] = @deva[अश्विनी]-@deva[पूर्णिमा]. Variant: @deva[अश्वनी].

simple:
The affix @deva[वुञ्] optionally comes after @deva[ग्रीष्म] (summer) and @deva[वसन्त] (spring) in the sense of 'sown therein'.

This replaces @deva[अण्] from 4.3.16.

Examples: @deva[ग्रैष्मं सस्यम्] or @deva[ग्रैष्मकं सस्यम्] (crop sown in summer), @deva[वासन्तम्] or @deva[वासन्तकम्] (sown in spring).
standard:
The affix @deva[वुञ्] optionally applies after @deva[ग्रीष्म] and @deva[वसन्त] in @deva[उप्त] sense, debarring @deva[अण्] (@ref[4.3.16]).

Examples: @deva[ग्रैष्मम्] / @deva[ग्रैष्मकं सस्यम्]; @deva[वासन्तम्] / @deva[वासन्तकम्].
advanced:
@deva[वुञ्] optionally applies after @deva[ग्रीष्म]/@deva[वसन्त]] in @deva[उप्त] sense, superseding @deva[अण्] (@ref[4.3.16]).

Examples: @deva[ग्रैष्मम्]/@deva[ग्रैष्मकम्]], @deva[वासन्तम्]/@deva[वासन्तकम्]].

simple:
After a @deva[प्रातिपदिक] (nominal stem) denoting time in the locative case, an affix comes in the sense of 'being then due', provided that the thing due is a debt (@deva[ऋण]).

Examples: @deva[मासे देयम् ऋणम्] = @deva[मासिकम्] (a debt due in a month), @deva[आर्धमासिकम्] (due in a fortnight), @deva[सांवत्सरिकम्] (annual debt).

Why @deva[ऋणे] (debt)? Observe @deva[मासे देया भिक्षा] (alms due in a month)—here no affix is added because it's not a debt.
standard:
After @deva[कालवाचिन्] (@deva[सप्तम्यन्त]), an affix applies in @deva[देय] (due) sense when the object is @deva[ऋण] (debt).

Examples: @deva[मासिकम्] (monthly debt), @deva[आर्धमासिकम्], @deva[सांवत्सरिकम्].

Condition @deva[ऋणे]: @deva[मासे देया भिक्षा] takes no affix since it's not debt.
advanced:
After @deva[कालवाचिन्] (@deva[सप्तम्यन्त]), affixes apply in @deva[देय]-sense when @deva[ऋण]-restricted.

Examples: @deva[मासिकम्], @deva[आर्धमासिकम्], @deva[सांवत्सरिकम्]. Counter-example: @deva[मासे देया भिक्षा]—no affix since non-@deva[ऋण].

@deva[वुञ्] applies after @deva[कलापिन्]/@deva[अश्वत्थ]]/@deva[यवबुस्]] in @deva[ऋण]-@deva[देय] sense. These are @deva[अप्रत्यक्षकालवाचिन्].

Examples: @deva[कलापकम्], @deva[अश्वत्थकम्], @deva[यवबुसकम्].

simple:
The affix @deva[वुञ्] comes in the sense of 'debt due' after @deva[ग्रीष्म] (summer) and @deva[अवरसम] (the past year or next year).

This replaces @deva[अण्] and @deva[ठञ्].

Examples: @deva[ग्रीष्मे देयम् ऋणम्] = @deva[ग्रैष्मकम्] (summer debt), @deva[आवरसमकम्] (debt due in the past/coming year).

This @deva[वुञ्] causes @deva[वृद्धि], whereas @deva[वुन्] would not—hence the separate rule. @deva[समा] = @deva[वर्ष] (year). @deva[आवरसमकम्] means 'due in the first year of the coming years' or 'due in the past year though unpaid'.
advanced:
@deva[वुञ्] applies after @deva[ग्रीष्म]/@deva[अवरसम]] in @deva[ऋण]-@deva[देय] sense, superseding @deva[अण्]/@deva[ठञ्]]. @deva[वुञ्] ≠ @deva[वुन्] due to @deva[वृद्धि]-effect.

Examples: @deva[ग्रैष्मकम्], @deva[आवरसमकम्]. @deva[समा] = @deva[वर्ष].

@deva[ठञ्] (plus @deva[वुञ्] via @deva[च]) applies after @deva[संवत्सर]/@deva[आग्रहायणी]] in @deva[ऋण]-@deva[देय] sense.

Examples: @deva[सांवत्सरिकम्]/@deva[सांवत्सरकम्]], @deva[आग्रहायणिकम्]/@deva[आग्रहायणकम्]]. @deva[संधिवेलादि] (@ref[4.3.16]) assigns @deva[अण्] for @deva[फल]/@deva[पर्व]].

simple:
An affix comes after a word denoting time in the @deva[सप्तमी] (locative) case, in the sense of 'a wild animal that wanders then' (@deva[व्याहरति मृगः]).

Examples: @deva[निशायां व्याहरति मृगः] = @deva[नैशः] (an animal that wanders at night), @deva[प्रादोषः] or @deva[प्रादोषिकः] (a beast that roams at dusk).

Why @deva[मृग] (wild beast)? Observe @deva[निशायां व्याहरति उलूकः] (the owl hoots at night)—no affix, since @deva[उलूक] is a bird, not a @deva[मृग].

@deva[व्याहरति] also means 'to make noise', so the rule can mean: 'an affix for a wild beast that makes noise at that time'.
standard:
An affix applies after @deva[कालवाचिन्] (@deva[सप्तम्यन्त]) in @deva[व्याहरति मृगः] sense (wild beast that wanders/makes noise then).

Examples: @deva[नैशः मृगः]; @deva[प्रादोषः]/@deva[प्रादोषिकः]] (@ref[4.3.14]).

Condition @deva[मृगः]: @deva[निशायां व्याहरति उलूकः]—no affix since non-@deva[मृग]. @deva[व्याहरति] = wander or make noise.
advanced:
Affixes apply after @deva[कालवाचिन्] in @deva[व्याहरति मृगः] sense.

Examples: @deva[नैशः], @deva[प्रादोषः]/@deva[प्रादोषिकः]]. Condition: @deva[मृग]; counter-example: @deva[उलूकः]. @deva[व्याहरति] = wander / make noise.

Affixes apply after @deva[कालवाचिन्] (@deva[प्रथमान्त]) in @deva[तत्सोढम्] sense.

@deva[सोढम्] = @deva[अभ्यस्तम्]. Example: @deva[नैशः]/@deva[नैशिकः]] @deva[छात्रः], @deva[प्रादोषिकः]/@deva[प्रादोषः]].

simple:
The affix @deva[यत्] comes in the sense of 'what stays there' after a word denoting a part of the body (@deva[अङ्गावयव]).

This replaces @deva[अण्].

Examples:
• @deva[दन्तेषु भवम्] = @deva[दन्त्यम्] (dental, what occurs at the teeth)
• @deva[कर्ण्यम्] (aural), @deva[ओष्ठ्यम्] (labial)
• @deva[पादे भवम्] = @deva[पद्यम्] (@ref[6.3.53])
• @deva[नासिकायां भवम्] = @deva[नस्यम्] (nasal)
standard:
The affix @deva[यत्] applies after @deva[अङ्गावयव]-denoting words in @deva[भव] sense, debarring @deva[अण्].

Examples: @deva[दन्त्यम्], @deva[कर्ण्यम्], @deva[ओष्ठ्यम्]; @deva[पद्यम्] (@ref[6.3.53]), @deva[नस्यम्].
advanced:
@deva[यत्] applies after @deva[अङ्गावयव] in @deva[भव] sense, superseding @deva[अण्].

Examples: @deva[दन्त्यम्], @deva[कर्ण्यम्], @deva[ओष्ठ्यम्], @deva[पद्यम्] (@ref[6.3.53]), @deva[नस्यम्].

simple:
The affix @deva[अण्] (as well as @deva[ढञ्]) comes after @deva[ग्रीवा] (neck/blood vessels) in the sense of 'what stays there'.

This replaces @deva[यत्] from 4.3.55.

Examples: @deva[ग्रीवासु भवम्] = @deva[ग्रैवेयम्] (with @deva[ढञ्]) or @deva[ग्रैवम्] (with @deva[अण्]).

@deva[ग्रीवा] means blood vessels (hence plural @deva[ग्रीवाभ्यः] in the @[sūtra]).
standard:
The affix @deva[अण्] also applies after @deva[ग्रीवा] (plural, blood vessels) in @deva[भव] sense, debarring @deva[यत्] (@ref[4.3.55]).

Examples: @deva[ग्रैवेयम्] (with @deva[ढञ्]) / @deva[ग्रैवम्] (with @deva[अण्]).
advanced:
@deva[अण्] (plus @deva[ढञ्] via @deva[च]) applies after @deva[ग्रीवा] (plural) in @deva[भव] sense, superseding @deva[यत्] (@ref[4.3.55]).

Examples: @deva[ग्रैवेयम्]/@deva[ग्रैवम्]].

simple:
The affix @deva[ञ्य] (= @deva[य]) comes in the sense of 'who stays there' after @deva[गम्भीर] (deep/profound).

This replaces @deva[अण्].

Example: @deva[गम्भीरे भवम्] = @deva[गाम्भीर्यम्] (gravity, profundity).

Supplementary rules (@deva[वार्त्तिक]):
• After @deva[बहिस्], @deva[देव], @deva[पञ्चजन]: @deva[बाह्यम्], @deva[दैव्यम्], @deva[पाञ्चजन्यम्]. The final of @deva[बहिस्] is elided (cf. @ref[4.1.85]). @deva[दैव्य] can also form by another @[vārtika].
• @deva[ञ्य] after @deva[चतुर्मास] when meaning sacrifice: @deva[चातुर्मास्यानि व्रतानि], @deva[चातुर्मास्यो यज्ञः]. Otherwise @deva[चातुर्मासः].

After an @deva[अव्ययीभाव] compound, the affix @deva[ञ्य] is used in the sense of 'who stays there'.

This replaces @deva[अण्]. However, @deva[ञ्य] only applies to words in the @deva[परिमुखादि] list.

Examples: @deva[परिमुखं भवम्] = @deva[पारिमुख्यम्], @deva[पारिहनव्यम्].

Not so for @deva[औपकुलम्], since @deva[उपकुल] is not in @deva[परिमुखादि].

@deva[परिमुखादि] list: @deva[परिमुख], @deva[परिहनु], @deva[पर्योष्ठ], @deva[पर्युलूख], @deva[औपमूल], @deva[खल], @deva[परिसीर], @deva[अनुसीर], @deva[उपसीर], @deva[उपस्थल], @deva[उपकलाप], @deva[अनुपथ], @deva[अनुखड्ग], @deva[अनुतिल], @deva[अनुशीत], @deva[अनुमाष], @deva[अनुयव], @deva[अनुयूप], @deva[अनुवंश], @deva[अनुगङ्गम्], @deva[प्रतिशाख], @deva[अनुसाय].

After an @deva[अव्ययीभाव] compound with @deva[अन्तर्] as the prior member, the affix @deva[ठञ्] is used in the sense of 'who stays there'.

This replaces @deva[अण्].

Examples: @deva[आन्तर्वेश्मिकम्] (within the house), @deva[आन्तर्गेहिकम्] (within the home).

Supplementary rules (@deva[वार्त्तिक]):
• @deva[ठञ्] after @deva[समान] and compounds beginning with it: @deva[सामानिकम्], @deva[सामानग्रामिकम्], @deva[सामानदेशिकम्].
• @deva[ठञ्] after @deva[अध्यात्मादि]: @deva[आध्यात्मिकम्], @deva[आधिदैविकम्], @deva[आधिभौतिकम्].
• @deva[ठञ्] after @deva[ऊर्ध्वदम], @deva[ऊर्ध्वदेह]: @deva[और्ध्वदमिकः], @deva[और्ध्वदेहिकम्].
• @deva[ठञ्] after compounds with @deva[लोक] as second member: @deva[ऐहलौकिकम्], @deva[पारलौकिकम्].
• @deva[ईय] after @deva[मुखतस्], @deva[पार्श्वतस्]: @deva[मुखतीयम्], @deva[पार्श्वतीयम्].
• @deva[ईय] with @deva[कुक्] augment after @deva[जन], @deva[पर]: @deva[जनकीयम्], @deva[परकीयम्].
• @deva[ईय], @deva[मण्], @deva[मीय], @deva[दिनण्] after @deva[मध्य]: @deva[मध्यीयः], @deva[माध्यमम्], @deva[माध्यमीयम्], @deva[माध्यन्दिनम्].
• @deva[लुक्] after @deva[स्थाम्न]: @deva[अश्वत्थामा].
• @deva[लुक्] after words ending in @deva[अजिन]: @deva[वृकाजिनः], @deva[सिंहाजिनः].

After an @deva[अव्ययीभाव] compound (indeclinable compound) with @deva[परि] or @deva[अनु] preceding @deva[ग्राम] (village), the affix @deva[ठञ्] is used in the sense of 'who stays there'.

This replaces @deva[अण्].

Examples: @deva[पारिग्रामिकः] (one who dwells around the village), @deva[आनुग्रामिकः] (one who dwells from village to village).
standard:
The affix @deva[ठञ्] applies after @deva[परि]/@deva[अनु]] + @deva[ग्राम] @deva[अव्ययीभाव] compounds in @deva[भव] sense, debarring @deva[अण्].

Examples: @deva[पारिग्रामिकः], @deva[आनुग्रामिकः].
advanced:
@deva[ठञ्] applies after @deva[परि]/@deva[अनु]]-@deva[ग्राम] @deva[अव्ययीभाव] in @deva[भव] sense, superseding @deva[अण्].

Examples: @deva[पारिग्रामिकः], @deva[आनुग्रामिकः].

simple:
The affix @deva[छ] comes after @deva[जिह्वामूल] (root of the tongue) and @deva[अङ्गुलि] (finger) in the sense of 'who stays there'.

This replaces @deva[यत्] from 4.3.55.

Examples: @deva[जिह्वामूलीयम्] (related to the tongue root), @deva[अङ्गुलीयम्] (related to the finger, i.e., a ring).
standard:
The affix @deva[छ] applies after @deva[जिह्वामूल] and @deva[अङ्गुलि] in @deva[भव] sense, debarring @deva[यत्] (@ref[4.3.55]).

Examples: @deva[जिह्वामूलीयम्], @deva[अङ्गुलीयम्].
advanced:
@deva[छ] applies after @deva[जिह्वामूल]/@deva[अङ्गुलि]] in @deva[भव] sense, superseding @deva[यत्] (@ref[4.3.55]).

Examples: @deva[जिह्वामूलीयम्], @deva[अङ्गुलीयम्].

simple:
The affix @deva[छ] comes after a word ending in @deva[वर्ग] (class, group) in the sense of 'who occurs there'.

This replaces @deva[अण्].

Examples: @deva[कवर्गीयम्] (belonging to the @deva[क]-class of consonants), @deva[चवर्गीयम्] (belonging to the @deva[च]-class).
standard:
The affix @deva[छ] applies after @deva[वर्गान्त] words in @deva[भव] sense, debarring @deva[अण्].

Examples: @deva[कवर्गीयम्], @deva[चवर्गीयम्].
advanced:
@deva[छ] applies after @deva[वर्गान्त] in @deva[भव] sense, superseding @deva[अण्].

Examples: @deva[कवर्गीयम्], @deva[चवर्गीयम्].

simple:
After a word ending in @deva[वर्ग] (class), when it does not mean a letter or word (@deva[शब्द]), the affixes @deva[यत्] and @deva[ख] optionally apply in the sense of 'who stays there'.

Examples: @deva[वासुदेववर्ग्यः] (with @deva[यत्]), @deva[वासुदेववर्गीणः] (with @deva[ख]), @deva[वासुदेववर्गीयः] (with @deva[छ]). Similarly @deva[युधिष्ठिरवर्ग्यः], @deva[वर्गीणः], or @deva[वर्गीयः].

When @deva[वर्ग] means a class of letters (@deva[शब्द]), only @deva[छ] applies: @deva[कवर्गीय वर्णः] (a letter of the @deva[क]-class).
standard:
The affixes @deva[यत्] and @deva[ख] optionally apply after @deva[वर्गान्त] (non-@deva[शब्द]-sense) in @deva[भव] sense.

Examples: @deva[वासुदेववर्ग्यः] (with @deva[यत्]), @deva[वासुदेववर्गीणः] (with @deva[ख]), @deva[वासुदेववर्गीयः] (with @deva[छ]).

When @deva[शब्द]-sense: @deva[छ] only; e.g., @deva[कवर्गीय वर्णः].
advanced:
@deva[यत्]/@deva[ख]] optionally apply after @deva[वर्गान्त] (@deva[अशब्दे]) in @deva[भव] sense.

Examples: @deva[वासुदेववर्ग्यः], @deva[वासुदेववर्गीणः], @deva[वासुदेववर्गीयः]. @deva[शब्दे]: @deva[छ] alone; @deva[कवर्गीय वर्णः].

simple:
The affix @deva[कन्] comes after @deva[कर्ण] (ear) and @deva[ललाट] (forehead) in the sense of 'what occurs there', when the word denotes an ornament.

This replaces @deva[यत्] from 4.3.55.

Examples: @deva[कर्णिका] (an ear-ring), @deva[ललाटिका] (a forehead ornament).

When not meaning an ornament: @deva[कर्ण्यम्] (relating to the ear), @deva[ललाट्यम्] (relating to the forehead).
standard:
The affix @deva[कन्] applies after @deva[कर्ण]/@deva[ललाट]] in @deva[भव] sense when denoting @deva[आभरण] (ornament), debarring @deva[यत्] (@ref[4.3.55]).

Examples: @deva[कर्णिका] (ear-ring), @deva[ललाटिका] (forehead ornament).

Non-ornament: @deva[कर्ण्यम्], @deva[ललाट्यम्].
advanced:
@deva[कन्] applies after @deva[कर्ण]/@deva[ललाट]] in @deva[भव] sense when @deva[आभरण]-denoting, superseding @deva[यत्] (@ref[4.3.55]).

Examples: @deva[कर्णिका], @deva[ललाटिका]. Non-@deva[आभरण]: @deva[कर्ण्यम्], @deva[ललाट्यम्].

simple:
After the name of a subject requiring commentary (@deva[व्याख्यातव्यनामन्]), an affix comes in the sense of 'its commentary' (@deva[व्याख्यान]) as well as 'what occurs there' (@deva[तत्र भव]).

The name of the topic to be explained must be a book or treatise, not a city or place.

Examples:
• @deva[सुपां व्याख्यानः] = @deva[सौपो ग्रन्थः] (a book explaining case-affixes)
• @deva[तैङम्] (a commentary on verbal affixes)
• @deva[कार्तः] (a commentary on @deva[कृत्] affixes)

@deva[सौप] can also mean @deva[सुप्सु भवम्] (occurring in case-affixes).

The @deva[अनुवृत्ति] of both @deva[भव] and @deva[व्याख्यान] runs through the subsequent @deva[अपवाद] rules.
standard:
After @deva[व्याख्यातव्यनामन्] (name of an explainable work), an affix applies in @deva[व्याख्यान] (commentary) and @deva[तत्र भव] senses. The @deva[च] draws in @deva[भव].

Examples: @deva[सौपो ग्रन्थः] (commentary on @deva[सुप्]); @deva[तैङम्] (on @deva[तिङ्]); @deva[कार्तः] (on @deva[कृत्]). These also mean 'occurring therein'.

Condition: @deva[व्याख्यातव्य] must be a textual work, not a physical object like a city.

@deva[अनुवृत्ति] of @deva[भव]/@deva[व्याख्यान]] extends to subsequent @deva[अपवाद] rules.
advanced:
After @deva[व्याख्यातव्यनामन्], affixes apply in @deva[व्याख्यान] and @deva[तत्र भव] senses (@deva[च] includes @deva[भव]).

Examples: @deva[सौपः], @deva[तैङम्], @deva[कार्तः]—both commentary and occurrence senses.

Condition: @deva[व्याख्यातव्य] = textual work. @deva[भव]/@deva[व्याख्यान]]-@deva[अनुवृत्ति] extends through @deva[अपवाद] @[sūtras].

simple:
The affix @deva[ठञ्] comes in the senses of 'occurring therein' and 'a commentary thereon' after the name of an explainable work relating to a @deva[क्रतु] (Soma sacrifice) or @deva[यज्ञ] (sacrifice in general).

This replaces @deva[अण्].

Examples for @deva[क्रतु]: @deva[अग्निष्टोमिकः], @deva[वाजपेयिकः], @deva[राजसूयिकः].
Examples for @deva[यज्ञ]: @deva[पाकयज्ञिकः], @deva[नावयज्ञिकः], @deva[पाञ्चौदनिकः], @deva[दाशौदनिकः].

This rule applies to words that are not @deva[अन्तोदात्त] (e.g., @deva[वाजपेय], @deva[राजसूय] have middle accent). The separate mention of @deva[यज्ञ] implies non-Soma sacrifices are included, since @deva[क्रतु] technically means only Soma sacrifices.

The plural prevents @deva[स्वरूपविधि] (@ref[1.1.68]) from applying to the word-forms @deva[क्रतु] and @deva[यज्ञ] themselves.

The affix @deva[ठञ्] comes after the name of an explainable work named after a @deva[ऋषि] (sage), in the senses of 'occurring therein' or 'commentary thereon', when it refers to an @deva[अध्याय] (chapter) only.

@deva[ऋषि] here means a sage from the @deva[प्रवर] list. The word formed always denotes a chapter of the work.

Examples: @deva[वसिष्ठस्य व्याख्यान] = @deva[वासिष्ठिको ऽध्यायः] (a chapter of commentary on @[vasiṣṭha]), @deva[वैश्वामित्रिकः] (a chapter on @[viśvāmitra]).

Why @deva[अध्याय] (chapter)? Observe @deva[वासिष्ठिकी ऋक्] (a verse related to @[vasiṣṭha])—the rule doesn't apply to verses.
standard:
The affix @deva[ठञ्] applies after @deva[ऋषि]-named @deva[व्याख्यातव्य] works in @deva[भव]/@deva[व्याख्यान]] senses, restricted to @deva[अध्याय] (chapter).

@deva[ऋषि] = @deva[प्रवर]-listed sages. @deva[व्याख्यातव्यनाम्नः] understood.

Examples: @deva[वासिष्ठिको ऽध्यायः], @deva[वैश्वामित्रिकः].

Condition @deva[अध्याये]: counter—@deva[वासिष्ठिकी ऋक्].
advanced:
@deva[ठञ्] applies after @deva[ऋषि]-named @deva[व्याख्यातव्य] in @deva[भव]/@deva[व्याख्यान]] senses, @deva[अध्याय]-restricted.

@deva[ऋषि] = @deva[प्रवर]. Examples: @deva[वासिष्ठिको ऽध्यायः], @deva[वैश्वामित्रिकः]. Counter: @deva[वासिष्ठिकी ऋक्].

simple:
The affix @deva[ष्ठन्] comes in the senses of 'occurring therein' and 'commentary thereon' after @deva[पौरोडाश] (verses about the sacred cake) and @deva[पुरोडाश] (the sacred cake itself).

Examples: @deva[पौरोडाशिकः] (commentary on @deva[पौरोडाश] mantras), @deva[पुरोडाशिकः] (commentary on @deva[पुरोडाश]).

The feminine forms are @deva[पौरोडाशिकी] and @deva[पुरोडाशिकी] by 4.1.41. The @deva[ष्] in the affix indicates the feminine is formed with @deva[ङीष्].

The commentary here refers to the mantras/verses relating to @deva[पुरोडाश], not to the sacrifice itself.
standard:
The affix @deva[ष्ठन्] applies after @deva[पौरोडाश]/@deva[पुरोडाश]] in @deva[भव]/@deva[व्याख्यान]] senses.

Examples: @deva[पौरोडाशिकः], @deva[पुरोडाशिकः]. Feminine: @deva[पौरोडाशिकी], @deva[पुरोडाशिकी] (@ref[4.1.41]). @deva[ष्] indicates @deva[ङीष्]-feminine.

Context: @deva[व्याख्यान] refers to mantras, not the sacrifice.
advanced:
@deva[ष्ठन्] applies after @deva[पौरोडाश]/@deva[पुरोडाश]] in @deva[भव]/@deva[व्याख्यान]] senses.

Forms: @deva[पौरोडाशिकः]/@deva[पुरोडाशिकः]]; feminine @deva[ङीष्] by @deva[ष्]-indication (@ref[4.1.41]). Commentary = @deva[मन्त्र]-related.

simple:
The affixes @deva[यत्] and @deva[अण्] come in the senses of 'occurring therein' or 'commentary thereon' after @deva[छन्दस्] (Vedic literature).

This replaces @deva[ठक्] from the next rule (@ref[4.3.72]).

Examples: @deva[छन्दस्यः] (with @deva[यत्]) or @deva[छान्दसः] (with @deva[अण्]).
standard:
The affixes @deva[यत्] and @deva[अण्] apply after @deva[छन्दस्] in @deva[भव]/@deva[व्याख्यान]] senses, debarring @deva[ठक्] (@ref[4.3.72]).

Examples: @deva[छन्दस्यः] / @deva[छान्दसः].
advanced:
@deva[यत्]/@deva[अण्]] apply after @deva[छन्दस्] in @deva[भव]/@deva[व्याख्यान]] senses, superseding @deva[ठक्] (@ref[4.3.72]).

Forms: @deva[छन्दस्यः], @deva[छान्दसः].

simple:
The affix @deva[अण्] comes in the senses of 'occurring therein' and 'commentary thereon' after the words @deva[ऋगयन], @deva[पदव्याख्यान], and others in the @deva[ऋगयनादि] list.

This replaces @deva[ठञ्], @deva[ठक्], and @deva[छ].

Examples: @deva[आर्गयनः], @deva[पादव्याख्यानः], @deva[वास्तुविद्यः].

The repetition of @deva[अण्] (though its @deva[अनुवृत्ति] was already present) prevents subsequent @deva[अपवाद] rules from applying to words in this list.

The @deva[ऋगयनादि] list: @deva[ऋगयन], @deva[पदव्याख्यान], @deva[छन्दोमान], @deva[छन्दोभाषा], @deva[छन्दोविचिति], @deva[न्याय], @deva[पुनरुक्त], @deva[निरुक्त], @deva[व्याकरण], @deva[निगम], @deva[वास्तुविद्या], @deva[क्षत्रविद्या], @deva[अङ्गविद्या], @deva[विद्या], @deva[उत्पात], @deva[उत्पाद], @deva[उद्याव], @deva[संवत्सर], @deva[मुहूर्त], @deva[उपनिषद्], @deva[निमित्त], @deva[शिक्षा], @deva[भिक्षा], @deva[छन्दोविछिति], @deva[व्याय].
advanced:
@deva[अण्] applies after @deva[ऋगयनादि] in @deva[भव]/@deva[व्याख्यान]] senses, superseding @deva[ठञ्]/@deva[ठक्]]/@deva[छ]]. @deva[पुनर्वचन] blocks subsequent @deva[अपवाद] (@deva[बाधकबाधनार्थम्]).

Examples: @deva[आर्गयनः], @deva[पादव्याख्यानः], @deva[वास्तुविद्यः]. @deva[ऋगयनादि] lists 25 items.

simple:
An affix from @deva[प्राग्दीव्यतः] (@ref[4.1.83] onward) comes after a word in the ablative case in the sense of 'what has come thence' (@deva[तत आगतम्]).

Examples: @deva[स्रुघ्नादागतः] = @deva[स्रौघ्नः] (what has come from Srughna), @deva[माथुरः], @deva[राष्ट्रियः].
standard:
Affixes from @deva[प्राग्दीव्यतः] (@ref[4.1.83]) apply after @deva[पञ्चम्यन्त] in @deva[ततआगत] sense.

Examples: @deva[स्रुघ्नादागतः] → @deva[स्रौघ्नः]; @deva[माथुरः], @deva[राष्ट्रियः].
advanced:
@deva[प्राग्दीव्यतः]-affixes apply after @deva[पञ्चम्यन्त] in @deva[ततआगत] sense.

Examples: @deva[स्रौघ्नः], @deva[माथुरः], @deva[राष्ट्रियः].

simple:
The affix @deva[ठक्] comes in the sense of 'come thence' after a word denoting a source of revenue (@deva[आयस्थान]).

@deva[आय] means the share appropriated by the lord; @deva[आयस्थान] is the source of such revenue.

This replaces @deva[अण्] and @deva[छ].

Examples: @deva[शौल्कशालिकम्] (revenue from a customs house), @deva[आकरिकम्] (revenue from mines).

The plural in the @[sūtra] prevents @deva[स्वरूपविधि] (@ref[1.1.68]).
advanced:
@deva[ठक्] applies after @deva[आयस्थान] in @deva[ततआगत] sense, superseding @deva[अण्]/@deva[छ]].

Examples: @deva[शौल्कशालिकम्], @deva[आकरिकम्]. Plural blocks @deva[स्वरूपविधि] (@ref[1.1.68]).

simple:
The affix @deva[अण्] comes in the sense of 'thence come' after the words @deva[शुण्डिक] and others in the @deva[शुण्डिकादि] list.

This replaces @deva[ठक्].

Examples: @deva[शौण्डिकम्] (excise revenue), @deva[कार्कणम्].

The repetition of @deva[अण्] (though @deva[अनुवृत्ति] was present) prevents subsequent @deva[अपवाद] rules from overriding these words. For example: @deva[औदपानम्].

The @deva[शुण्डिकादि] list: @deva[शुण्डिक], @deva[कृकण], @deva[स्थण्डिल], @deva[उदपान], @deva[उपल], @deva[तीर्थ], @deva[भूमि], @deva[तृण], @deva[पर्ण].
advanced:
@deva[अण्] applies after @deva[शुण्डिकादि] in @deva[ततआगत] sense, superseding @deva[ठक्]. @deva[पुनर्वचन] serves @deva[बाधकबाधनार्थम्].

Examples: @deva[शौण्डिकम्], @deva[कार्कणम्], @deva[औदपानम्]. @deva[शुण्डिकादि] lists 9 items.

simple:
The affix @deva[वुञ्] comes in the sense of 'thence come' after a word denoting a person connected through learning (@deva[विद्या]) or family origin (@deva[योनि]).

@deva[विद्यासम्बन्ध] = relationship through learning (teacher and pupil). @deva[योनिसम्बन्ध] = relationship through blood (father and son).

This replaces @deva[अण्] and @deva[छ].

Examples for @deva[विद्या]: @deva[उपाध्यायादागतम्] = @deva[औपाध्यायकम्] (knowledge from a teacher), @deva[शैष्यकम्], @deva[आचार्यकम्].

Examples for @deva[योनि]: @deva[मातामहकम्] (wealth from maternal grandfather), @deva[पितामहकम्], @deva[मातुलकम्].
standard:
The affix @deva[वुञ्] applies after @deva[विद्यायोनिसम्बन्ध]-denoting words in @deva[ततआगत] sense, debarring @deva[अण्]/@deva[छ]].

@deva[विद्यासम्बन्ध]: @deva[औपाध्यायकम्], @deva[शैष्यकम्], @deva[आचार्यकम्]. @deva[योनिसम्बन्ध]: @deva[मातामहकम्], @deva[पितामहकम्], @deva[मातुलकम्].
advanced:
@deva[वुञ्] applies after @deva[विद्यायोनिसम्बन्ध]-words in @deva[ततआगत] sense, superseding @deva[अण्]/@deva[छ]].

Examples: @deva[औपाध्यायकम्], @deva[शैष्यकम्]; @deva[मातामहकम्], @deva[पितामहकम्], @deva[मातुलकम्].

simple:
The affix @deva[ठञ्] comes in the sense of 'come thence' after a word ending in short @deva[ऋ] that denotes a person related through learning or blood.

This replaces @deva[वुञ्].

Examples for @deva[विद्या]: @deva[होतुरागतम्] = @deva[हौतृकम्] (from the @[hotṛ] priest), @deva[पौतृकम्].

Examples for @deva[योनि]: @deva[भ्रातृकम्] (from a brother), @deva[स्वसृकम्] (from a sister), @deva[मातृकम्] (from a mother).

The @deva[त्] in @deva[ऋत्] is for pronunciation ease—no word ends in long @deva[ॠ].

When not denoting relationship: @deva[सावित्रम्] (from @[savitṛ], with @deva[अण्]).
advanced:
@deva[ठञ्] applies after @deva[ऋदन्त] @deva[विद्यायोनिसम्बन्ध] in @deva[ततआगत] sense, superseding @deva[वुञ्].

Examples: @deva[हौतृकम्], @deva[पौतृकम्]; @deva[भ्रातृकम्], @deva[स्वसृकम्], @deva[मातृकम्]. Non-relationship: @deva[सावित्रम्].

simple:
The affixes @deva[यत्] and @deva[ठञ्] come in the sense of 'come thence' after @deva[पितृ] (father).

Examples: @deva[पितुरागतम्] = @deva[पैतृकम्] (paternal, with @deva[ठञ्]) or @deva[पित्र्यम्] (with @deva[यत्], after @ref[7.4.27] and @ref[6.4.148]).

Both mean 'inherited from the father' or 'paternal'.
standard:
The affixes @deva[यत्] and @deva[ठञ्] apply after @deva[पितृ] in @deva[ततआगत] sense.

Examples: @deva[पैतृकम्] (with @deva[ठञ्]), @deva[पित्र्यम्] (with @deva[यत्], @ref[7.4.27], @ref[6.4.148]).
advanced:
@deva[यत्]/@deva[ठञ्]] apply after @deva[पितृ] in @deva[ततआगत] sense.

Forms: @deva[पैतृकम्], @deva[पित्र्यम्] (@ref[7.4.27], @ref[6.4.148]).

simple:
After a word ending with a patronymic affix (@deva[गोत्र]), the rule for the affix meaning 'come thence' is the same as the rule for the affix meaning 'his mark' (@deva[अङ्क]).

@deva[गोत्र] here means any descendant-denoting affix, not just the technical @deva[गोत्र] of grammar. The affixes for @deva[अङ्क] are taught in @ref[4.3.126]-127.

Examples: @deva[औपगवानाम् अङ्कः] = @deva[औपगवकः] (the mark of the Aupagavas). The same word also means 'come from the Aupagavas': @deva[औपगवेभ्य आगतम्] = @deva[औपगवकम्].

Similarly @deva[कापटवकम्], @deva[नाडायनकम्], @deva[चारायणकम्] (with @deva[वुञ्] from @ref[4.3.126]).

With @deva[अण्] from @ref[4.3.127]: @deva[बैदः], @deva[गार्गः], @deva[दाक्षः] mean both 'the mark of the Vaidas/@[gārgyas]/@[dākṣis]' and 'what comes from them'.

The word @deva[वत्] indicates complete similarity.
advanced:
After @deva[गोत्रान्त], @deva[ततआगत]-affixes follow @deva[अङ्क]-affixes (@ref[4.3.126]–@ref[4.3.127]) by @deva[अतिदेश].

Examples: @deva[औपगवकः], @deva[कापटवकम्] (@deva[वुञ्]); @deva[बैदः], @deva[गार्गः] (@deva[अण्]). @deva[वत्] = complete similarity.

simple:
The affix @deva[रूप्य] denotes 'what originates from that' when added to a word denoting a cause (@deva[हेतु]) or a person viewed as a cause (@deva[मनुष्य]).

Examples for @deva[हेतु]: @deva[समादागतम्] = @deva[समरूप्यम्] (proceeding from a similar cause) or @deva[समीयम्] (with @deva[छ] from @ref[4.2.138]). Similarly @deva[विषमरूप्यम्] or @deva[विषमीयम्].

Examples for @deva[मनुष्य]: @deva[देवदत्तरूप्यम्] or @deva[दैवदत्तम्] (originating with Devadatta), @deva[यज्ञदत्तरूप्यम्] or @deva[यज्ञदत्तम्].

The plural prevents @deva[स्वरूपविधि] (@ref[1.1.68]). The ablative case can also denote @deva[हेतु] (@ref[2.3.25]).
advanced:
@deva[रूप्य] applies after @deva[हेतुमनुष्य] in @deva[ततआगत] sense.

Examples: @deva[समरूप्यम्], @deva[देवदत्तरूप्यम्]. Plural blocks @deva[स्वरूपविधि] (@ref[1.1.68]). Ablative-@deva[हेतु] per @ref[2.3.25].

simple:
The affix @deva[मयट्] comes in the sense of 'come thence' after a word denoting a cause or a person viewed as a cause.

Examples: @deva[सममयम्], @deva[विषममयम्], @deva[देवदत्तमयम्], @deva[यज्ञदत्तमयम्].

The @deva[ट्] of @deva[मयट्] indicates the feminine is formed with @deva[ङीप्] (@ref[4.1.15]): @deva[सममयी].

This rule is separated from the previous to prevent @deva[यथासंख्य] (@ref[1.3.10]) from applying.
advanced:
@deva[मयट्] applies after @deva[हेतुमनुष्य] in @deva[ततआगत] sense.

Examples: @deva[सममयम्], @deva[देवदत्तमयम्]. @deva[ट्] → @deva[ङीप्] (@ref[4.1.15]). @deva[योगविभाग] blocks @deva[यथासंख्य] (@ref[1.3.10]).

@deva[प्राग्दीव्यतः]-affixes apply after @deva[पञ्चम्यन्त] in @deva[प्रभव] sense.

@deva[प्रभवति] ≠ @deva[उत्पत्ति] (@ref[4.3.25]); = @deva[प्रकाशते]. Examples: @deva[हैमवती], @deva[दारदी].

simple:
The affix @deva[य] comes in the sense of 'what appears there' after @deva[विदूर].

This replaces @deva[अण्].

Example: @deva[विदूरात् प्रभवति] = @deva[वैदूर्यः] (a gem, the cat's-eye/beryl).

The gem is actually found in a mountain called @deva[वालवाय], but @deva[विदूर] is the city where the raw stone is processed. The grammarians call @deva[वालवाय] by the name @deva[विदूर], similar to how merchants call Benares '@deva[जित्वरी]'.
standard:
The affix @deva[य] applies after @deva[विदूर] in @deva[प्रभव] sense, debarring @deva[अण्].

Example: @deva[वैदूर्यः] (gem). Actual source: @deva[वालवाय] mountain; @deva[विदूर] = processing city. Grammarians name @deva[वालवाय] as @deva[विदूर] (cf. merchants: Benares = @deva[जित्वरी]).
advanced:
@deva[य] applies after @deva[विदूर] in @deva[प्रभव] sense, superseding @deva[अण्].

Example: @deva[वैदूर्यः]. @deva[विदूर] = @deva[वालवाय] by @deva[व्यपदेश]. Cf. Benares = @deva[जित्वरी].

simple:
An affix from @deva[प्राग्दीव्यतः] (@ref[4.1.83]) comes after a word in the accusative case in the sense of 'what goes thereto', provided that what goes is a road (@deva[पथ्]) or a messenger (@deva[दूत]).

Examples: @deva[स्रुघ्नं गच्छति] = @deva[स्रौघ्नः] (a road or messenger that goes to Srughna), @deva[माथुरः].

The road may either lead to Srughna or, being in Srughna, be used for going.

Why @deva[पथिदूतयोः]? Observe @deva[स्रुघ्नं गच्छति सार्थः] (a caravan goes to Srughna for its own sake)—no affix here.
standard:
Affixes from @deva[प्राग्दीव्यतः] (@ref[4.1.83]) apply after @deva[द्वितीयान्त] in @deva[तद्गच्छति] sense when @deva[पथ्]/@deva[दूत]].

Examples: @deva[स्रौघ्नः पन्थाः/दूतः] (road/messenger to Srughna), @deva[माथुरः].

Condition: @deva[पथिदूतयोः]; counter: @deva[स्रुघ्नं गच्छति सार्थः].
advanced:
@deva[प्राग्दीव्यतः]-affixes apply after @deva[द्वितीयान्त] in @deva[तद्गच्छति] sense, @deva[पथिदूत]-restricted.

Examples: @deva[स्रौघ्नः], @deva[माथुरः]. Counter: @deva[सार्थः].

simple:
An affix from @deva[प्राग्दीव्यतः] (@ref[4.1.83]) comes after a word in the accusative case in the sense of 'a door that looks towards that' (@deva[तद् अभिनिष्क्रामति द्वारम्]).

Examples: @deva[स्रौघ्नम्] (a gate facing Srughna, e.g., a gate of Kanyakubja), @deva[माथुरम्], @deva[राष्ट्रियम्].

Objection: The @[sūtra] should say @deva[अभिनिष्क्रमणं द्वारम्] since @deva[अभिनिष्क्रामति] makes the gate seem sentient.

Answer: The gate is the instrument (@deva[करण]) of exiting, and an instrument can be used as an agent: @deva[साध्वसिश्छिनत्ति] (the sword cuts well).

Why @deva[द्वारम्]? Observe @deva[स्रुघ्नम् अभिनिष्क्रामति पुरुषः] (a person facing Srughna)—no affix.
standard:
Affixes from @deva[प्राग्दीव्यतः] (@ref[4.1.83]) apply after @deva[द्वितीयान्त] in @deva[तद्अभिनिष्क्रामति द्वारम्] sense (gate facing that direction).

Examples: @deva[स्रौघ्नम् द्वारम्], @deva[माथुरम्], @deva[राष्ट्रियम्].

Gate = @deva[करण] (instrument) used as agent (cf. @deva[साध्वसिश्छिनत्ति]).

Condition: @deva[द्वारम्]; counter: @deva[पुरुषः].
advanced:
@deva[प्राग्दीव्यतः]-affixes apply after @deva[द्वितीयान्त] in @deva[अभिनिष्क्रामति द्वारम्] sense.

Examples: @deva[स्रौघ्नम्], @deva[माथुरम्]. @deva[द्वार] = @deva[करण] as agent. Condition: @deva[द्वारम्].

simple:
An affix comes after a word in the @deva[द्वितीया] (accusative) case in the sense of 'a book made in relation to that subject' (@deva[अधिकृत्य कृते ग्रन्थे]).

@deva[अधिकृत्य] means 'aiming at', 'relating to', 'about'.

Examples: @deva[सुभद्राम् अधिकृत्य कृतो ग्रन्थः] = @deva[सौभद्रः] (a book about @[subhadrā]), @deva[गौरिमित्रः], @deva[यायातः], @deva[किरातार्जुनीयम्].

Why @deva[ग्रन्थे]? Observe @deva[सुभद्राम् अधिकृत्य कृतः प्रासादः] (a palace built in memory of @[subhadrā])—no affix.

Supplementary rule (@deva[वार्त्तिक]): The affix is diversely elided when the book is a story (@deva[आख्यायिका]): @deva[वासवदत्ता] (story about @[vāsavadattā]), @deva[सुमनोत्तरा], @deva[उर्वशी]. Sometimes not: @deva[भैमरथी].
standard:
An affix applies after @deva[द्वितीयान्त] in @deva[अधिकृत्य कृते ग्रन्थे] sense (book about that subject).

Examples: @deva[सौभद्रः], @deva[गौरिमित्रः], @deva[यायातः], @deva[किरातार्जुनीयम्].

Condition: @deva[ग्रन्थे]; counter: @deva[प्रासादः].

@note[type=@[vārtika]]: @deva[लुक्] @deva[बहुलम्] when @deva[आख्यायिका]: @deva[वासवदत्ता], @deva[सुमनोत्तरा], @deva[उर्वशी]. Counter: @deva[भैमरथी].

simple:
The affix @deva[छ] comes in the sense of 'a book made about that subject' after @deva[शिशुक्रन्द], @deva[यमसभ], @deva[द्वन्द्व] compounds, and the words @deva[इन्द्रजननादि].

This replaces @deva[अण्].

Examples:
• @deva[शिशुक्रन्दीयः] (a book about infant-crying)
• @deva[यमसभीयः] (a book about Yama's court)
• @deva[द्वन्द्व] compounds: @deva[अग्निकाश्यपीयः], @deva[श्येनकपोतीयः], @deva[शब्दार्थसंबन्धीयं प्रकरणम्], @deva[वाक्यपदीयम्]
• @deva[इन्द्रजननादि] (@deva[आकृतिगण]): @deva[इन्द्रजननीयम्], @deva[प्रद्युम्नागमनीयम्]

Supplementary rule (@deva[वार्त्तिक]): @deva[द्वन्द्व] compounds like @deva[देवासुर] take @deva[अण्] instead: @deva[दैवासुरम्] (the book of Gods and Demons), @deva[राक्षोसुरम्], @deva[गौणमुख्यम्].

advanced:
Affixes apply after @deva[प्रथमान्त] in @deva[स अस्य निवासः] sense.

Examples: @deva[स्रौघ्नः], @deva[माथुरः], @deva[राष्ट्रियः].

simple:
An affix comes after a word in the @deva[प्रथमा] (nominative) case in the sense of 'this is his mother-land/ancestral home' (@deva[स अस्य अभिजनः]).

The difference between @deva[निवास] and @deva[अभिजन]: @deva[निवास] is where a person currently lives; @deva[अभिजन] is where his ancestors lived—his native country or ancestral abode.

Examples: @deva[स्रौघ्नः] (one whose ancestral home is Srughna), @deva[माथुरः], @deva[राष्ट्रियः].

This rule is separated from the previous so that only @deva[अभिजन] (not @deva[निवास]) continues to the next rule.
standard:
An affix applies after @deva[प्रथमान्त] in @deva[स अस्य अभिजनः] sense (his ancestral homeland).

@deva[निवास] = current residence; @deva[अभिजन] = where ancestors lived, native land.

Examples: @deva[स्रौघ्नः], @deva[माथुरः], @deva[राष्ट्रियः].

Separated from @ref[4.3.89] for @deva[अभिजन]-only @deva[अनुवृत्ति] into @ref[4.3.91].

simple:
The affixes @deva[यत्] and @deva[तसि] come in the sense of 'in the same direction as that' after the word @deva[उरस्].

**Examples:**
- @deva[उरसा एकदिक्] → @deva[उरस्य] (with @deva[यत्])
- @deva[उरसा एकदिक्] → @deva[उरस्तः] (with @deva[तसि])

The @deva[च] in the rule includes @deva[तसि] from the previous rule.
standard:
The affixes @deva[यत्] and @deva[तसि] come after @deva[उरस्] in the @deva[एकदिक्] sense.

**Examples:** @deva[उरस्य], @deva[उरस्तः]

The @deva[च] draws @deva[तसि] from @ref[4.3.113].
advanced:
This @deva[सूत्र] prescribes @deva[यत्] after @deva[उरस्] in the @deva[एकदिक्] sense. The @deva[च] includes @deva[तसि] from @ref[4.3.113].

**Examples:** @deva[उरस्य], @deva[उरस्तः]

simple:
The affix @deva[ठक्] comes after @deva[हल] (plough) and @deva[सीर] (plough) in the sense of 'this belongs to it.'

**Examples:**
- @deva[हलस्य इदम्] → @deva[हालिकम्] 'belonging to the plough'
- @deva[सीरस्य इदम्] → @deva[सैरिकम्]
standard:
The affix @deva[ठक्] comes after @deva[हल] and @deva[सीर] (both meaning plough) in the @deva[तस्येदम्] sense.

**Examples:** @deva[हालिकम्], @deva[सैरिकम्]
advanced:
This @deva[सूत्र] teaches @deva[ठक्] after @deva[हल] and @deva[सीर] in the @deva[तस्येदम्] sense.

**Examples:** @deva[हालिकम्], @deva[सैरिकम्]

simple:
The affix @deva[कन्] comes in the @deva[विकार] (product) sense after @deva[पिष्ट] (flour) when the resulting word is a proper name (@deva[संज्ञा]). This overrides @deva[मयट्].

**Example:** @deva[पिष्टकः] (a kind of cake — the word itself is a name)
standard:
The affix @deva[कन्] comes after @deva[पिष्ट] in the @deva[विकार] sense when the derivative is a @deva[संज्ञा], debarring @deva[मयट्].

**Example:** @deva[पिष्टकः]
advanced:
This @deva[सूत्र] prescribes @deva[कन्] after @deva[पिष्ट] in the @deva[विकार] sense with @deva[संज्ञा] result, as @deva[अपवाद] to @deva[मयट्].

**Example:** @deva[पिष्टकः]

simple:
The affix @deva[वुञ्] comes in the @deva[विकार] (modification) or @deva[अवयव] (part) sense after the word @deva[उष्ट्र] (camel). This overrides @deva[अञ्] from @ref[4.3.154].

**Example:** @deva[उष्ट्रस्य विकारः अवयवो वा] → @deva[औष्ट्रकः] 'product or part of a camel'
standard:
The affix @deva[वुञ्] comes after @deva[उष्ट्र] in the @deva[विकार]/​@deva[अवयव] sense, debarring @deva[अञ्] of @ref[4.3.154].

**Example:** @deva[औष्ट्रकः]
advanced:
This @deva[सूत्र] prescribes @deva[वुञ्] after @deva[उष्ट्र] in the @deva[विकार]/​@deva[अवयव] sense, as @deva[अपवाद] to @deva[अञ्] of @ref[4.3.154].

**Example:** @deva[औष्ट्रकः]

simple:
The affix @deva[वुञ्] comes optionally in the @deva[विकार] (modification) or @deva[अवयव] (part) sense after the words @deva[उमा] (flax) and @deva[ऊर्णा] (wool).

**Examples:**
- @deva[औमम्] or @deva[औमकम्] 'made of flax'
- @deva[और्णम्] or @deva[और्णकम्] 'made of wool'
standard:
The affix @deva[वुञ्] comes optionally after @deva[उमा] and @deva[ऊर्णा] in the @deva[विकार]/​@deva[अवयव] sense.

**Examples:** @deva[औमम्]/​@deva[औमकम्], @deva[और्णम्]/​@deva[और्णकम्]
advanced:
This @deva[सूत्र] makes @deva[वुञ्] optional after @deva[उमा] and @deva[ऊर्णा] in the @deva[विकार]/​@deva[अवयव] sense.

**Examples:** @deva[औमम्]/​@deva[औमकम्], @deva[और्णम्]/​@deva[और्णकम्]

simple:
The affix @deva[ढञ्] comes optionally in the @deva[विकार] (modification) or @deva[अवयव] (part) sense after the feminine word @deva[एणी] (female black deer).

**Example:** @deva[ऐणेयं मांसम्] 'the flesh of a female black deer'

But for the masculine @deva[एण]: @deva[एणस्य मांसम्] → @deva[ऐणम्] 'flesh of a male deer' (formed with @deva[अञ्]).
standard:
The affix @deva[ढञ्] comes optionally after @deva[एणी] (@deva[स्त्री]) in the @deva[विकार]/​@deva[अवयव] sense.

**Example:** @deva[ऐणेयं मांसम्]

For @deva[एण] (@deva[पुं]): @deva[ऐणम्] (with @deva[अञ्]).
advanced:
This @deva[सूत्र] makes @deva[ढञ्] optional after @deva[एणी] (feminine) in the @deva[विकार]/​@deva[अवयव] sense.

**Example:** @deva[ऐणेयं मांसम्]

Masculine @deva[एण]: @deva[ऐणम्] (@deva[अञ्]).

simple:
The affix @deva[यत्] comes in the @deva[विकार] (modification) or @deva[अवयव] (part) sense after the words @deva[गो] (cow) and @deva[पयस्] (milk).

**Examples:**
- @deva[गव्यम्] 'of/from a cow' (by @ref[6.1.79])
- @deva[पयस्यम्] 'of/from milk'
standard:
The affix @deva[यत्] comes after @deva[गो] and @deva[पयस्] in the @deva[विकार]/​@deva[अवयव] sense.

**Examples:** @deva[गव्यम्] (@ref[6.1.79]), @deva[पयस्यम्]
advanced:
This @deva[सूत्र] teaches @deva[यत्] after @deva[गो] and @deva[पयस्] in the @deva[विकार]/​@deva[अवयव] sense.

**Examples:** @deva[गव्यम्] (@ref[6.1.79]), @deva[पयस्यम्]

simple:
The affix @deva[यत्] comes in the @deva[विकार] (modification) or @deva[अवयव] (part) sense after the word @deva[द्रु] (wood/tree). This overrides @deva[अञ्] from @ref[4.3.139].

**Example:** @deva[द्रु] + @deva[यत्] → @deva[द्रव्यम्] 'product of wood' (by @ref[6.1.79])
standard:
The affix @deva[यत्] comes after @deva[द्रु] in the @deva[विकार]/​@deva[अवयव] sense, debarring @deva[अञ्] of @ref[4.3.139].

**Example:** @deva[द्रव्यम्] (@ref[6.1.79])
advanced:
This @deva[सूत्र] prescribes @deva[यत्] after @deva[द्रु] in the @deva[विकार]/​@deva[अवयव] sense, as @deva[अपवाद] to @deva[अञ्] of @ref[4.3.139].

**Example:** @deva[द्रव्यम्] (@ref[6.1.79])

simple:
The affix @deva[वय] comes in the @deva[विकार] (product) sense after @deva[द्रु] when the product is a measure (@deva[परिमाण]). This overrides @deva[यत्].

**Example:** @deva[द्रुवयम्] 'a wooden measure'
standard:
The affix @deva[वय] comes after @deva[द्रु] when @deva[परिमाण] (measure) is meant, debarring @deva[यत्] of @ref[4.3.161].

**Example:** @deva[द्रुवयम्]
advanced:
This @deva[सूत्र] teaches @deva[वय] after @deva[द्रु] specifically for @deva[परिमाण], as @deva[अपवाद] to @deva[यत्] of @ref[4.3.161].

**Example:** @deva[द्रुवयम्]

simple:
The affix @deva[ठक्] comes after a word in the instrumental case in the sense of 'refined or prepared by means of that thing'.

The word @deva[संस्कृतम्] means 'refined' or 'made better in quality'.

Examples:
- @deva[दध्ना संस्कृतम्] 'refined with curd' → @deva[दाधिकम्] 'prepared with curd'
- @deva[शृङ्गवेरेण संस्कृतम्] → @deva[शार्ङ्गवेरिकम्] 'prepared with ginger'
- @deva[मरीचेन संस्कृतम्] → @deva[मारीचिकम्] 'prepared with pepper'

This @[sūtra] is separated from the previous one so that @deva[संस्कृतम्] alone continues to the next @[sūtra].
standard:
After a @deva[तृतीया]-ending word, @deva[ठक्] applies in the sense @deva[संस्कृतम्] (refined/prepared thereby).

Examples:
- @deva[दध्ना संस्कृतम्] → @deva[दाधिकम्] 'prepared with curd'
- @deva[शृङ्गवेरेण संस्कृतम्] → @deva[शार्ङ्गवेरिकम्] 'with ginger'
- @deva[मरीचेन संस्कृतम्] → @deva[मारीचिकम्] 'with pepper'

This @[sūtra] is separated from @ref[4.4.2] to allow only @deva[संस्कृतम्] (not @deva[दीव्यति] etc.) to continue as @deva[अनुवृत्ति] into the next @[sūtra].
advanced:
After @deva[तृतीयान्त], @deva[ठक्] in sense @deva[संस्कृतम्] (refined thereby).

Examples: @deva[दध्ना संस्कृतम्] → @deva[दाधिकम्]; @deva[शार्ङ्गवेरिकम्], @deva[मारीचिकम्].

The separation from @ref[4.4.2] is @deva[उत्तरार्थम्]—only @deva[संस्कृतम्] (not @deva[दीव्यति], @deva[खनति], @deva[जयति]) continues as @deva[अनुवृत्ति] into @ref[4.4.4].

simple:
After the word @deva[कुलत्थ] (a type of legume) and after words having a penultimate @deva[क्], the affix @deva[अण्] is used instead of @deva[ठक्], in the sense of 'refined or prepared therewith'.

Examples:
- @deva[कुलत्थेन संस्कृतम्] → @deva[कौलत्थम्] 'prepared with @deva[कुलत्थ]'
- @deva[तित्तिडीकेन संस्कृतम्] → @deva[तैत्तिडीकम्] (word with penultimate @deva[क])
- @deva[दर्दभकेन संस्कृतम्] → @deva[दार्दभकम्] (word with penultimate @deva[क])
standard:
After @deva[कुलत्थ] and bases with @deva[उपधा]-@deva[क्], @deva[अण्] replaces @deva[ठक्] in the sense @deva[संस्कृतम्].

This is an @deva[अपवाद] (exception) to @ref[4.4.3].

Examples:
- @deva[कुलत्थ] + @deva[अण्] → @deva[कौलत्थम्]
- @deva[तित्तिडीक] (has @deva[उपधा]-@deva[क्]) + @deva[अण्] → @deva[तैत्तिडीकम्]
- @deva[दर्दभक] + @deva[अण्] → @deva[दार्दभकम्]

simple:
The affix @deva[ठक्] comes after a word in the instrumental case in the sense of 'one who crosses water by means of that thing'.

Examples:
- @deva[काण्डप्लवेन तरति] 'crosses with a bundle-raft' → @deva[काण्डप्लविकः]
- @deva[उडुपेन तरति] 'crosses with a raft' → @deva[औडुपिकः] 'a boatman/raftsman'
standard:
After a @deva[तृतीया]-ending word, @deva[ठक्] applies in the sense @deva[तरति] (crosses water thereby).

Examples:
- @deva[काण्डप्लवेन तरति] → @deva[काण्डप्लविकः]
- @deva[उडुपेन तरति] → @deva[औडुपिकः] 'one who crosses on a raft'
advanced:
After @deva[तृतीयान्त], @deva[ठक्] in sense @deva[तरति] (crosses water).

Examples: @deva[काण्डप्लविकः], @deva[औडुपिकः].

The @deva[अनुवृत्ति] of @deva[तृतीयासमर्थात्] continues from @ref[4.4.2].

simple:
The affix @deva[ठञ्] (instead of @deva[ठक्]) comes after the word @deva[नौ] (boat) and after bases having two syllables (@deva[द्व्यच्]), in the sense of 'one who crosses thereby'.

Examples:
- @deva[नावा तरति] → @deva[नाविकः] 'a sailor, boatman'
- @deva[घटेन तरति] → @deva[घटिकः] (two-syllable base)
- @deva[प्लवेन तरति] → @deva[प्लविकः]
- @deva[बाहुभ्यां तरति] → @deva[बाहुकः]

The @deva[ष्] in @deva[ष्ठन्] is not part of the affix but results from @deva[सन्धि] rules. The feminine of @deva[बाहुकः] is @deva[बाहुका].

A mnemonic verse lists the six @[sūtras] in the @deva[ठक्] section where the affix has indicatory @deva[ष्]: @ref[4.4.9], @ref[4.4.10], @ref[4.4.18], @ref[4.4.53], and @ref[4.4.74].
standard:
After @deva[नौ] and @deva[द्व्यच्] (disyllabic) bases, @deva[ठञ्] (not @deva[ठक्]) applies in sense @deva[तरति].

Examples:
- @deva[नावा तरति] → @deva[नाविकः] 'sailor'
- @deva[घटिकः], @deva[प्लविकः], @deva[बाहुकः] (disyllabic bases)

The @deva[ष्] in the @[sūtra]'s @deva[ष्ठन्] is @deva[सन्धि], not part of the affix. Feminine @deva[बाहुकः] → @deva[बाहुका] (no @deva[ङीष्] since @deva[ठञ्] lacks @deva[षित्]).

@deva[कारिका]: @deva[आकर्षात्पर्पादभेर्स्त्रादिभ्यः कुसीदसूत्राच्च । आवसथात्किशरादेः षितः षडेते ठगधिकारे ॥] — Six @[sūtras] in the @deva[ठक्] section have @deva[षित्] affixes: @ref[4.4.9], @ref[4.4.10], @ref[4.4.18], @ref[4.4.53], @ref[4.4.74].

simple:
The affix @deva[ठक्] comes after a word in the instrumental case in the sense of 'one who goes on or lives by means of that thing'.

The word @deva[चरति] means both 'goes on' and 'eats/lives by'.

Examples:
- @deva[दध्ना चरति] 'lives on curd' → @deva[दाधिकः] 'one who subsists on curd'
- @deva[हस्तिना चरति] 'travels by elephant' → @deva[हास्तिकः] 'one who travels on an elephant'
standard:
After a @deva[तृतीया]-ending word, @deva[ठक्] applies in the sense @deva[चरति] (goes on/lives thereby).

@deva[चरति] has two meanings: 'goes/travels' and 'eats/subsists'.

Examples:
- @deva[दध्ना चरति] → @deva[दाधिकः] 'one who lives on curd'
- @deva[हस्तिना चरति] → @deva[हास्तिकः] 'one who travels by elephant'
advanced:
After @deva[तृतीयान्त], @deva[ठक्] in sense @deva[चरति] (goes/lives thereby).

@deva[चरति] = @deva[गमने] (motion) and @deva[भक्षणे] (eating). Examples: @deva[दाधिकः] (subsists on curd), @deva[हास्तिकः] (travels by elephant).

simple:
The affix @deva[ष्ठल्] (instead of @deva[ठक्]) comes after the word @deva[आकर्ष] (touchstone) in the sense of 'one who goes on by means thereof'.

@deva[आकर्ष] is a touchstone used to test gold.

Example: @deva[आकर्षेण चरति] → @deva[आकर्षिकः] (one who lives by testing gold), feminine @deva[आकर्षिकी]

The @deva[ल्] in @deva[ष्ठल्] affects accent. The @deva[ष्] here is part of the affix (unlike in @ref[4.4.7]), so feminines take @deva[ङीष्] → @deva[ई].
standard:
After @deva[आकर्ष] (touchstone), @deva[ष्ठल्] (not @deva[ठक्]) applies in sense @deva[चरति].

Example: @deva[आकर्षेण चरति] → @deva[आकर्षिकः], f. @deva[आकर्षिकी]

The @deva[ल्] is for accent (@ref[6.1.193]). The @deva[ष्] is @deva[इत्]—unlike @ref[4.4.7] where @deva[ष्] is @deva[सन्धि]. Being @deva[षित्], the feminine takes @deva[ङीष्] by @ref[4.1.41].

The @deva[कारिका] lists six @deva[षित्] affixes in the @deva[ठक्] section: @ref[4.4.9], @ref[4.4.10], @ref[4.4.18], @ref[4.4.53], @ref[4.4.74].

simple:
Both affixes @deva[छ] and @deva[ठन्] come after the word @deva[आयुध] (weapon) in the sense of 'one who lives thereby'.

Examples:
- With @deva[छ]: @deva[आयुधेन जीवति] → @deva[आयुधीयः] 'a mercenary soldier'
- With @deva[ठन्]: @deva[आयुधेन जीवति] → @deva[आयुधिकः] 'a mercenary soldier'
standard:
After @deva[आयुध], both @deva[छ] and @deva[ठन्] apply in sense @deva[जीवति].

Examples:
- @deva[छ]: @deva[आयुधीयः] 'mercenary'
- @deva[ठन्]: @deva[आयुधिकः] 'mercenary'
advanced:
@deva[आयुध] takes @deva[छ] or @deva[ठन्] in sense @deva[जीवति].

@deva[छ] = @deva[ईय] (by @ref[7.1.2]). Examples: @deva[आयुधीयः], @deva[आयुधिकः].

simple:
The affix @deva[अण्] (instead of @deva[ठक्]) comes after the word @deva[कुटिलिका] in the sense of 'one who conveys thereby'.

The word @deva[कुटिलिका] means 'crooked motion' or 'an iron forge'.

Examples:
- @deva[कुटिलिकया हरति मृगो व्याधम्] → @deva[कौटिलिको मृगः] 'a deer that lures a hunter into crooked paths'
- @deva[कुटिलिकया हरति अङ्गारान्] → @deva[कौटिलिकः कर्मारः] 'a blacksmith who carries burning coals on a forge'
standard:
After @deva[कुटिलिका], @deva[अण्] (not @deva[ठक्]) applies in sense @deva[हरति].

@deva[कुटिलिका] = crooked motion; also, an iron forge.

Examples:
- @deva[कुटिलिकया हरति मृगो व्याधम्] → @deva[कौटिलिको मृगः] 'a deer that entices the hunter by crooked paths'
- @deva[कुटिलिकया हरति अङ्गारान्] → @deva[कौटिलिकः कर्मारः] 'a blacksmith (who carries coals on a forge)'

simple:
The affix @deva[मप्] always comes after words ending in the affix @deva[क्त्रि] (@ref[3.3.88]), in the sense of 'completed thereby'.

The affix @deva[क्त्रि] is incomplete by itself—it must always take @deva[मप्] to form a complete word. So @deva[मप्] is like an obligatory addition to @deva[क्त्रि].

Examples:
- @deva[पच्] + @deva[क्त्रि] + @deva[मप्] → @deva[पक्त्रिमम्] 'completed by cooking'
- @deva[वप्] + @deva[क्त्रि] + @deva[मप्] → @deva[उप्त्रिमम्] 'completed by sowing' (by @ref[6.1.15])
- @deva[कृ] + @deva[क्त्रि] + @deva[मप्] → @deva[कृत्रिमम्] 'artificial, man-made'

A @deva[वार्त्तिक] adds: after words ending in @deva[भाव]-affixes, @deva[इमप्] is added. Thus @deva[पाकेन निर्वृत्तम्] → @deva[पाकिमम्] (from @deva[पच्] + @deva[घञ्] + @deva[इमप्]). Similarly @deva[त्यागिमम्], @deva[सेकिमम्], @deva[कुट्टिमम्].
standard:
After @deva[क्त्रि]-ending words (@ref[3.3.88]), @deva[मप्] is obligatory (@deva[नित्य]) in sense @deva[निर्वृत्तम्].

@deva[क्त्रि] never occurs alone—@deva[मप्] is an invariable augment.

Examples:
- @deva[पच्] + @deva[क्त्रि] + @deva[मप्] → @deva[पक्त्रिमम्]
- @deva[वप्] + @deva[क्त्रि] + @deva[मप्] → @deva[उप्त्रिमम्] (@ref[6.1.15])
- @deva[कृ] + @deva[क्त्रि] + @deva[मप्] → @deva[कृत्रिमम्] 'artificial'

@note[type=@[vārtika]]: @deva[भावप्रत्ययान्तादिमप्] — After @deva[भाव]-pratyaya-ending words, @deva[इमप्] is added: @deva[पाकिमम्] (@deva[पच्] + @deva[घञ्] @ref[3.3.18] + @deva[इमप्]), @deva[त्यागिमम्], @deva[सेकिमम्], @deva[कुट्टिमम्].

simple:
The affix @deva[अण्] (instead of @deva[ठक्]) comes after the word @deva[मुद्ग] (a type of pulse/lentil) in the sense of 'mixed therewith'.

Examples:
- @deva[मुद्गेन संसृष्टः] → @deva[मौद्गः ओदनः] 'rice mixed with mung beans'
- @deva[मौद्गा यवागूः] 'barley gruel mixed with mung beans'
standard:
After @deva[मुद्ग], @deva[अण्] (not @deva[ठक्]) applies in sense @deva[संसृष्टम्].

Examples: @deva[मौद्गः ओदनः] 'rice with @deva[मुद्ग]-pulse', @deva[मौद्गा यवागूः].
advanced:
@deva[मुद्ग] takes @deva[अण्] (not @deva[ठक्]) in sense @deva[संसृष्टम्].

Examples: @deva[मौद्गः ओदनः], @deva[मौद्गा यवागूः].

simple:
The affix @deva[ठक्] comes after the words @deva[ओजस्] (energy), @deva[सहस्] (strength), and @deva[अम्भस्] (water) when in the instrumental case, in the sense of 'one who exists by means of that'.

Examples:
- @deva[ओजसा वर्तते] 'lives by energy' → @deva[औजसिकः शूरः] 'a hero (one possessed of energy)'
- @deva[सहसा वर्तते] → @deva[साहसिकः] 'a thief (one who acts by force)'
- @deva[अम्भसा वर्तते] → @deva[आम्भसिकः] 'a fish (one who lives in water)'
standard:
After @deva[ओजस्], @deva[सहस्], and @deva[अम्भस्] (in @deva[तृतीया]), @deva[ठक्] applies in sense @deva[वर्तते] (exists thereby).

Examples:
- @deva[ओजसा वर्तते] → @deva[औजसिकः शूरः] 'a hero'
- @deva[सहसा वर्तते] → @deva[साहसिकः] 'a thief'
- @deva[अम्भसा वर्तते] → @deva[आम्भसिकः] 'a fish'
advanced:
After @deva[ओजस्], @deva[सहस्], @deva[अम्भस्] (in @deva[तृतीया]), @deva[ठक्] in sense @deva[वर्तते].

Examples: @deva[औजसिकः शूरः], @deva[साहसिकः] (thief), @deva[आम्भसिकः] (fish).

simple:
The affix @deva[ठक्] comes after the words @deva[ईप], @deva[लोम], and @deva[कूल] when preceded by @deva[अनु] or @deva[प्रति], in the sense of 'one who exists in relation to that'. The word is in the accusative case (second case, @deva[द्वितीया]).

Though @deva[वर्तते] is intransitive, the accusative here functions adverbially, qualifying the manner of existence.

Examples:
- @deva[प्रतीपं वर्तते] 'exists adversely' → @deva[प्रातीपिकः] 'unfavorable' (@deva[प्रति] + @deva[आप] with @deva[आ] → @deva[ई] by @ref[5.4.74], @ref[6.3.97])
- @deva[अन्वीपं वर्तते] → @deva[आन्वीपिकः] 'favorable'
- @deva[प्रतिलोमं वर्तते] → @deva[प्रातिलोमिकः] 'inverse, against the grain'
- @deva[अनुलोमं वर्तते] → @deva[आनुलोमिकः] 'direct, with the grain'
- @deva[प्रतिकूलं वर्तते] → @deva[प्रातिकूलिकः] 'adverse'
- @deva[अनुकूलं वर्तते] → @deva[आनुकूलिकः] 'favorable'
standard:
After @deva[ईप], @deva[लोम], @deva[कूल] (preceded by @deva[अनु]/@deva[प्रति]) in @deva[द्वितीया], @deva[ठक्] applies in sense @deva[वर्तते].

The accusative functions adverbially (not as object of intransitive @deva[वर्तते]).

Examples:
- @deva[प्रतीपं वर्तते] → @deva[प्रातीपिकः] 'unfavorable' (@deva[प्रति] + @deva[आप], @deva[आ] → @deva[ई] by @ref[5.4.74], @ref[6.3.97])
- @deva[अन्वीपं वर्तते] → @deva[आन्वीपिकः] 'favorable'
- @deva[प्रातिलोमिकः] 'inverse', @deva[आनुलोमिकः] 'direct'
- @deva[प्रातिकूलिकः] 'adverse', @deva[आनुकूलिकः] 'favorable'
advanced:
After @deva[अनु]/[@deva[प्रति] + @deva[ईप]/[@deva[लोम]/[@deva[कूल] (in @deva[द्वितीया]), @deva[ठक्] in sense @deva[वर्तते].

The @deva[द्वितीया] is @deva[क्रियाविशेषण] (adverbial), not @deva[कर्म] of intransitive @deva[वर्तते].

Examples: @deva[प्रातीपिकः] (@deva[प्रतिगता आपोऽस्मिन्], @deva[आ] → @deva[ई] by @ref[5.4.74], @ref[6.3.97]), @deva[आन्वीपिकः], @deva[प्रातिलोमिकः], @deva[आनुलोमिकः], @deva[प्रातिकूलिकः], @deva[आनुकूलिकः].

simple:
The affix @deva[ठक्] comes after a word in the accusative case in the sense of 'one who gleans that'.

The word @deva[उञ्छति] refers to picking up grains (@deva[कण]) fallen on the ground—gleaning.

Examples:
- @deva[बदराणि उञ्छति] 'gleans jujubes' → @deva[बादरिकः]
- @deva[श्यामाकान् उञ्छति] → @deva[श्यामाकिकः] 'one who gleans millet'
- @deva[कणान् उञ्छति] → @deva[काणिकः] 'one who gleans grains'
advanced:
After @deva[द्वितीयान्त], @deva[ठक्] in sense @deva[उञ्छति] (gleans).

Examples: @deva[बादरिकः], @deva[श्यामाकिकः], @deva[काणिकः].

simple:
The affix @deva[ठक्] comes after a word in the accusative case in the sense of 'one who aids or protects that'.

Examples:
- @deva[समाजं रक्षति] 'protects/supports an assembly' → @deva[सामाजिकः] 'a spectator' (one who aids an assembly by presence)
