simple: Five specific stems — @deva[गाथिन्] ("singer of hymns"), @deva[विदथिन्] ("one skilled in assemblies"), @deva[केशिन्] ("long-haired"), @deva[गणिन्] ("leader of a group"), and @deva[पणिन्] ("a trader") — retain their @deva[इन्] unchanged even before the Patronymic @deva[अण्] affix. So the Patronymics are @deva[गाथिनः], @deva[वैदथिनः], @deva[कैशिनः], @deva[गाणिनः], and @deva[पाणिनः] (the grammarian @[pāṇini]'s name). This is an exception to the general rule that would alter @deva[इन्] before a Patronymic @deva[अण्].
standard: While the general rule retains @deva[इन्] before non-Patronymic @deva[अण्] only, these five stems — @deva[गाथिन्], @deva[विदथिन्], @deva[केशिन्], @deva[गणिन्], @deva[पणिन्] — are exceptional in that they also retain @deva[इन्] before the Patronymic @deva[अण्] (@deva[गोत्र]-@deva[अण्]). Patronymic @deva[अण्] ordinarily triggers elision or modification of stem-final @deva[इन्], but this rule blocks that for these five. The resulting Patronymic forms: @deva[गाथिनः], @deva[वैदथिनः], @deva[कैशिनः], @deva[गाणिनः], @deva[पाणिनः]. Notably, @deva[पाणिनः] is the Patronymic of @deva[पणिन्] and gives us the name of the grammarian @[pāṇini].
advanced: 1. @deva[गाथिन्] 2. @deva[विदथिन्] 3. @deva[केशिन्] 4. @deva[गणिन्] and 5. @deva[पणिन्] remain unchanged before the Patronymic also. Thus @deva[गाथिनोऽपत्यं] = @deva[गाथिनः], @deva[वैदथिनः], @deva[कैशिनः], @deva[गाणिनः], and @deva[पाणिनः] @deva[॥] This @[sutra] applies to Patronymics.

simple: When the @deva[इन्] ending of a stem is preceded by a conjunct consonant (a cluster of two or more consonants), the @deva[इन्] remains unchanged before the @deva[तद्धित] @deva[अण्] affix. So @deva[शाङ्किन्] (preceded by @deva[ङ्क्]) gives @deva[शाङ्किनः], @deva[यद्रिन्] gives @deva[याद्रिणः], and @deva[वज्जिन्] gives @deva[वाज्जिणः]. The presence of a consonant cluster protects the @deva[इन्] from alteration.
standard: This rule states that when the @deva[इन्]-final @deva[भ] stem has a conjunct consonant (@deva[संयुक्त]) immediately preceding @deva[इन्], the @deva[इन्] is retained intact before @deva[अण्], whether Patronymic or otherwise. The conditioning environment is: @deva[भ] stem + @deva[इन्] preceded by @deva[संयुक्त व्यञ्जन] + @deva[अण्] following. Forms: @deva[शाङ्किनः] (from @deva[शाङ्किन्] with cluster @deva[ङ्क्]); @deva[याद्रिणः] (from @deva[याद्रिन्] with @deva[द्र्]); @deva[वाज्जिणः] (from @deva[वाज्जिन्] with @deva[ज्ज्]). The rule thus creates a phonological blocking condition: consonant-cluster environments shield the @deva[इन्] from the changes that @deva[अण्] would otherwise trigger.
advanced: The syllable @deva[इन्] of a @deva[अङ्ग] (stem) ending in @deva[इन्] remains unchanged before @deva[अण्] when a conjunct consonant precedes it. Thus @deva[शाङ्किनेऽपत्यं] = @deva[शाङ्किनः], @deva[याद्रिणः], @deva[वाज्जिणः] @deva[॥]

simple: When a stem ending in @deva[अन्] is in the @deva[भ] position, its @deva[अन्] remains unchanged before the @deva[तद्धित] @deva[अण्] affix, whether @deva[अण्] is Patronymic or not. So @deva[सामन्] ("hymn") gives @deva[सामानः], @deva[वेमन्] gives @deva[वैमनः], and @deva[सुत्वन्] gives @deva[सौत्वनः]. In all these cases the @deva[अन्] cluster of the base is preserved in the derivative.
standard: For @deva[भ] stems in @deva[अन्], the general expectation before @deva[अण्] would be some modification of the stem-final @deva[अन्]; this rule suspends any such change unconditionally, regardless of whether @deva[अण्] is Patronymic (@deva[गोत्र]) or non-Patronymic. The conditioning environment: @deva[भ] + @deva[अन्]-final stem + @deva[अण्] following. Forms: @deva[सामानः] (from @deva[सामन्]), @deva[वैमनः] (from @deva[वेमन्]), @deva[सौत्वनः] (from @deva[सुत्वन्]). This parallels the @deva[इन्]-preservation rules (preceding @[sūtras]) but applies universally to @deva[अण्] of both types.
advanced: The syllable @deva[अन्] of a @deva[अङ्ग] (stem) ending in @deva[अन्] remains unchanged before @deva[अण्] affix whether Patronymic or otherwise. As @deva[सामानः], @deva[वैमनः], @deva[सौत्वनः], @deva[जैत्वनः], from @deva[सामन्], @deva[वेमन्], @deva[सुत्वन्] and @deva[जित्वन्] @deva[॥]

simple: The stems @deva[आत्मन्] ("self") and @deva[अध्वन्] ("path") keep their final @deva[अन्] unchanged before the @deva[तद्धित] affix @deva[ख]. So @deva[आत्मन्] + @deva[ख] (see @ref[5.1.9]) gives @deva[आत्मनीनः] ("self-possessed") and @deva[अध्वन्] + @deva[ख] (see @ref[5.2.16]) gives @deva[अध्वनीनः] ("pertaining to a path"). Without this rule, the @deva[अन्] might be altered before @deva[ख].
standard: This rule specifically preserves the @deva[अन्] of @deva[आत्मन्] and @deva[अध्वन्] before the @deva[तद्धित] affix @deva[ख], in contrast to the general principle that might elide or alter stem-final @deva[अन्] in @deva[भ]-position. The conditioning environment: stem = @deva[आत्मन्] or @deva[अध्वन्], in @deva[भ] position, followed by @deva[ख]. Results: @deva[आत्मनीनः] (@ref[5.1.9]) and @deva[अध्वनीनः] (@ref[5.2.16]). The specificity of the two stems (rather than a general rule) indicates that other @deva[अन्]-final stems do not necessarily retain @deva[अन्] before @deva[ख], making this an enumerated exception.
advanced: The finals of the @deva[अङ्ग] (stems) @deva[आत्मन्] and @deva[अध्वन्] remain unchanged before the affix @deva[ख]@deva[।] Thus @deva[आत्मने] @deva[हितं] = @deva[आत्मनीनः] @ref[5.1.9], @deva[अध्वानमलङ्गामी] @ref[5.2.16] = @deva[अध्वनीनः] @deva[॥] But @deva[प्रत्यात्मम्] and @deva[प्राध्वम्] @deva[॥] The first is formed by the @[samasanta] affix @deva[टच्] added to the @[avyayibhava] @ref[5.4.108], and the latter by @deva[अच्] @ref[5.4.85].

simple: The stem @deva[उक्षन्] (bull) forms the adjective @deva[औक्ष] when the meaning is not "descendant of an ox." For instance, @deva[औक्षं पटम्] means "a cloth (marked or associated) with a bull." When the meaning is "son/descendant," the form is instead @deva[औक्ष्णः], where the final @deva[अ] of the base is elided.
standard: From the @[an]-final stem @deva[उक्षन्], the @deva[अण्] affix produces @deva[औक्ष] in non-@[apatya] (non-descendant) senses, as in @deva[औक्षं पटम्] ("a bull-cloth"). In the @[apatya] (descendant) sense, the form is @deva[औक्ष्णः] (@deva[उक्ष्णोऽपत्यम्], "son of the bull"), where elision of the final @deva[अ] of @deva[उक्ष्ण्] applies under @ref[6.4.135]. The contrast between @deva[औक्ष] and @deva[औक्ष्णः] thus illustrates how the semantic category (@[apatya] vs. non-@[apatya]) determines which form of the base is preserved before the suffix.
advanced: From @deva[उक्षन्] is formed @deva[औक्ष] when not meaning a descendant. As @deva[औक्षं] @deva[पटम्] but @deva[औक्ष्णः] = @deva[उक्ष्णोऽपत्यम्], the @deva[अ] is elided in the latter example by @ref[6.4.135].

simple: After a reduplicated stem (@deva[अभ्यस्त]), @deva[झ्] becomes @deva[अत्] instead of @deva[अन्त्].
standard: When the verbal stem is @[abhyasta] (reduplicated), @deva[झ्] → @deva[अत्] rather than @deva[अन्त्]. Examples: @deva[दधति] 'they place' (not *@deva[दधन्ति]), @deva[बिभ्रति] 'they bear', @deva[जुह्वति] 'they offer'. This @deva[अत्] is later replaced by @deva[उस्] to give @deva[ददुः], @deva[बिभ्रुः].
advanced: The @[abhyasta] stems include: (1) perfect stems like @deva[चकृ], (2) intensive stems, (3) reduplicated presents like @deva[दधा], @deva[बिभृ], @deva[जुहु]. The shortened ending @deva[अत्] (vs. @deva[अन्त्]) is characteristic of these archaic formations. By @ref[7.1.4], this @deva[अत्] becomes @deva[उस्] in the perfect: @deva[चक्रुः], @deva[ददुः].

simple: In @[ātmanepada], @deva[झ्] becomes @deva[अत्] when the stem doesn't end in @deva[अ].
standard: For @[ātmanepada] endings after a stem not ending in @deva[अ], @deva[झ्] → @deva[अत्]. Examples: @deva[चिन्वते] 'they gather' (√@deva[चि] class 5), @deva[लुनते] 'they cut' (√@deva[लू] class 9). Compare with @deva[अ]-ending stems: @deva[भवन्ते] (where @deva[झ्] → @deva[अन्त्] → @deva[अन्ते]).
advanced: This rule applies to @[ātmanepada] 3pl (@deva[झ] → @deva[अते], @deva[अताम्], etc.) when the @[aṅga] ends in a consonant or non-@deva[अ] vowel. The condition '@deva[अनत्]' means 'not ending in @deva[अत्]' — i.e., not an @deva[अ]-stem. Class 5, 8, 9 verbs and consonant-final roots trigger this. @deva[अ]-stems take the regular @deva[अन्ते] by 7.1.3.

simple: After √@deva[शी] 'to lie', the @deva[अत्] ending gets @deva[र्] added: @deva[शेरते] 'they lie'.
standard: The root @deva[शीङ्] 'to lie, sleep' takes augment @deva[रुट्] (= @deva[र्]) before the @deva[अत्] substitute, giving @deva[रत्]. Thus @deva[शेरते], @deva[शेरताम्], @deva[अशेरत]. The @deva[र्] attaches to the beginning of @deva[अत्] (as @[ṭit] augments are initial).
advanced: The @deva[रुट्] augment is @[ṭit] (initial-attaching) by @ref[1.1.46]. This creates the distinctive @deva[रत्] ending unique to @deva[शीङ्]. The rule distinguishes @deva[शीङ्] from the homophonous @deva[शी] 'to sharpen'. Historical note: this @deva[र्] may preserve an archaic stem form.

simple: In Vedic, the @deva[रुट्] augment applies variably (not restricted to @deva[शी] and @deva[विद्]).
standard: The @deva[छन्दस्] (Vedic literature) shows @deva[रुट्] after other roots too: @deva[अदुह्र] 'they milked' (√@deva[दुह्] + @deva[झ] → @deva[दुह्] + @deva[रत्]). @deva[बहुलम्] 'variously' indicates Vedic forms don't follow the strict @deva[भाषा] rules.
advanced: Vedic preserves archaic patterns where @deva[रुट्] was more productive. Forms like @deva[अदुह्र], @deva[अरिह्र] show this augment with roots that don't take it in classical Sanskrit. The @[bahula] designation marks Vedic-only usage.

simple: After an @deva[अ]-ending stem, the instrumental plural @deva[भिस्] becomes @deva[ऐस्]: @deva[देवैः] 'by the gods'.
standard: The case-ending @deva[भिस्] (instrumental plural) is replaced by @deva[ऐस्] after stems ending in short @deva[अ]. Thus @deva[देव] + @deva[भिस्] → @deva[देव] + @deva[ऐस्] → @deva[देवैः]. This combines with the stem-final @deva[अ] to form the characteristic @deva[ऐः] ending.
advanced: This substitution is specific to @deva[अ]-stems (masculine/neuter @deva[अकारान्त]). The @deva[ऐ] diphthong results from @deva[अ] + @deva[ऐ] → @deva[ऐ] by @ref[6.1.88]. Other stems keep @deva[भिस्]: @deva[अग्निभिः], @deva[नदीभिः]. This is a major declensional marker distinguishing @deva[अ]-stems.

simple: The pronouns @deva[इदम्] and @deva[अदस्] don't take @deva[ऐस्] — they keep @deva[भिस्]: @deva[एभिः], @deva[अमीभिः].
standard: Exception to @ref[7.1.9]: @deva[इदम्] and @deva[अदस्] retain @deva[भिस्] in the instrumental plural. @deva[इदम्] → @deva[एभिः] 'by these', @deva[अदस्] → @deva[अमीभिः] 'by those'. But if they end in @deva[क] (as @deva[इमक], @deva[अमुक]), then @deva[ऐस्] applies: @deva[इमकैः], @deva[अमुकैः].
advanced: The '@deva[अकोः]' exception means 'except when ending in @deva[क]'. The stems @deva[इदम्]//@deva[अदस्] have suppletive instrumental forms. When the @deva[क]-final variants @deva[इमक]//@deva[अमुक] are used (by @ref[7.2.102]), they behave as regular @deva[अ]-stems taking @deva[ऐस्].

simple: After @deva[अ]-stems, the dative @deva[ङे] becomes @deva[य]: @deva[देवाय] 'to/for the god'.
standard: The dative singular ending @deva[ङे] is replaced by @deva[य] after @deva[अ]-ending stems. Combined with lengthening of the stem-final @deva[अ] → @deva[आ] (by @ref[7.3.102]), this gives @deva[देवाय], @deva[रामाय]. The @deva[य] directly replaces @deva[ए].
advanced: The @deva[य] substitute creates the @deva[आय] dative ending characteristic of @deva[अ]-stems. The stem-final lengthening is handled by @ref[7.3.102] (@deva[सुपि च]). For non-@deva[अ] stems, @deva[ङे] → @deva[ए] remains: @deva[अग्नये], @deva[पित्रे].

simple: After pronominal @deva[अ]-stems, dative @deva[ङे] becomes @deva[स्मै]: @deva[तस्मै] 'to that one'.
standard: For @[sarvanāman] (pronouns) ending in @deva[अ], the dative singular takes @deva[स्मै] instead of @deva[य]. Examples: @deva[सर्वस्मै] 'to all', @deva[तस्मै] 'to that', @deva[यस्मै] 'to whom', @deva[कस्मै] 'to whom?'. But @deva[भवते] (from @deva[भवत्], not @deva[अ]-final) keeps @deva[ए].
advanced: This distinguishes pronominal declension from nominal. The @deva[स्मै] ending is unique to @[sarvanāman] @deva[अ]-stems. When pronominal stems take @deva[अश्]-substitute (by @ref[7.2.102]), they no longer end in @deva[अ] and take regular @deva[ए]. The @deva[स्म्] element appears across pronominal oblique cases.

simple: After pronominal @deva[अ]-stems: ablative @deva[ङसि] → @deva[स्मात्], locative @deva[ङि] → @deva[स्मिन्].
standard: Two more pronominal substitutions: (1) @deva[ङसि] (ablative) → @deva[स्मात्]: @deva[तस्मात्] 'from that', @deva[यस्मात्] 'from which'. (2) @deva[ङि] (locative) → @deva[स्मिन्]: @deva[तस्मिन्] 'in that', @deva[यस्मिन्] 'in which'. These @deva[स्म्]-forms mark pronominal declension.
advanced: The @deva[स्मात्]//@deva[स्मिन्] endings continue the pronominal pattern started in 7.1.14. Together, rules @ref[7.1.14]-15 give the characteristic @[sarvanāman] singular oblique endings: dative @deva[स्मै], ablative @deva[स्मात्], locative @deva[स्मिन्]. Non-@deva[अ] pronouns and non-pronominals use the regular endings.

simple: After pronominal @deva[अ]-stems, the nominative plural @deva[जस्] becomes @deva[शी] (= @deva[ई]): @deva[ते] 'those'.
standard: The nominative plural ending @deva[जस्] is replaced by @deva[शी] (pronounced @deva[ई]) after @[sarvanāman] stems ending in @deva[अ]. Thus @deva[सर्व] + @deva[जस्] → @deva[सर्व] + @deva[ई] → @deva[सर्वे] 'all'. Similarly @deva[ते] 'those', @deva[ये] 'who', @deva[के] 'which?'.
advanced: The @deva[श्] in @deva[शी] is @[it] (marker), leaving @deva[ई]. Combined with stem-final @deva[अ] → @deva[ए] (by @[guṇa] sandhi). This creates the distinctive pronominal nominative plural in @deva[ए] vs. nominal @deva[आः]. The @deva[शित्] marking prevents @[guṇa] of a following vowel.

simple: After feminine @deva[आ]-stems, the dual ending @deva[औ] (nominative/accusative) becomes @deva[ई]: @deva[रामे] 'two @[rāmās]'.
standard: The dual nominative/accusative ending @deva[औ] is replaced by @deva[ई] after stems ending in the feminine affix @deva[आप्] (i.e., @deva[आ]-ending feminines). Thus @deva[रामा] + @deva[औ] → @deva[रामे] 'two @[rāmās]', @deva[सीते] 'two @[sītās]'.
advanced: This applies specifically to @deva[आप्]-derived feminines (by @ref[4.1.4]), not to stems that happen to end in @deva[आ]. The @deva[औङ्] in the @[sūtra] indicates the @deva[औ] ending. Combined with stem-final @deva[आ] + @deva[ई] → @deva[ए] (by @ref[6.1.87] @[guṇa]).

simple: After neuter stems too, dual @deva[औ] becomes @deva[ई]: @deva[कुण्डे] 'two pots', @deva[फले] 'two fruits'.
standard: Neuter stems also take @deva[ई] for dual nominative/accusative @deva[औ]. Thus @deva[कुण्ड] + @deva[औ] → @deva[कुण्ड] + @deva[ई] → @deva[कुण्डे] 'two pots'. This parallels the feminine pattern but applies to all neuters regardless of final vowel.
advanced: The neuter dual in @deva[ई] is universal: @deva[फले] (a-stem), @deva[वारिणी] (i-stem), @deva[मधुनी] (u-stem). The @deva[ई] ending triggers various stem-final changes. This is distinct from the masculine dual in @deva[औ] (@deva[रामौ]).

simple: After @deva[अष्ट] 'eight' (in the form @deva[अष्टा]), the nominative/accusative ending becomes @deva[औ]: @deva[अष्टौ].
standard: The numeral @deva[अष्टन्] takes the form @deva[अष्टा] before case endings (by @ref[7.2.84]). After this @deva[अष्टा], the nominative/accusative plural ending is replaced by @deva[औ]. Result: @deva[अष्टौ] 'eight' (used for all three genders).
advanced: The @deva[औश्] substitute (with @[it] @deva[श्]) gives @deva[औ]. This is unique to @deva[अष्टन्] — other numerals follow different patterns. The form @deva[अष्टौ] is invariable for nominative and accusative across all genders, unlike @deva[पञ्च] etc.

simple: After neuter @deva[अ]-stems, both @deva[सु] and @deva[अम्] become @deva[अम्]: @deva[कुण्डम्] 'pot', @deva[वनम्] 'forest'.
standard: For neuter stems ending in @deva[अ], the nominative singular @deva[सु] and accusative @deva[अम्] are both replaced by @deva[अम्]. Thus @deva[कुण्ड] + @deva[सु]//@deva[अम्] → @deva[कुण्डम्], @deva[वन] → @deva[वनम्]. This gives the characteristic @deva[अम्] ending.
advanced: This contrasts with masculine @deva[अ]-stems where @deva[सु] → @deva[स्] (nominative @deva[रामस्] → @deva[रामः]) and @deva[अम्] → @deva[अम्] (accusative @deva[रामम्]). The neuter uses @deva[अम्] for both cases, merging nominative and accusative as in other neuters.

simple: After @deva[युष्मद्] 'you' and @deva[अस्मद्] 'I/we', genitive @deva[ङस्] becomes @deva[अश्]: @deva[तव] 'your', @deva[मम] 'my'.
standard: The genitive singular ending @deva[ङस्] (= @deva[अस्]) is replaced by @deva[अश्] after the pronoun stems @deva[युष्मद्] and @deva[अस्मद्]. The @deva[श्] is @[it]. Combined with stem changes, this gives @deva[तव] 'your' and @deva[मम] 'my'.
advanced: The @deva[अश्] substitution (being @[śit]) replaces the whole ending. Stem @deva[युष्मद्] → @deva[त्व] (by @ref[7.2.97]), + @deva[अ] → @deva[तव]. Stem @deva[अस्मद्] → @deva[म] (by @ref[7.2.92]), + @deva[अ] → @deva[मम]. These are the basic possessive forms.

simple: For @deva[ऊर्णु] 'to cover', @[vṛddhi] is optional before @deva[इट्]-augmented parasmaipada @deva[स्]-aorist. @deva[प्रौर्णावीत्] or @deva[प्रौर्णवीत्].
standard: The root @deva[ऊर्णु] has optional @[vṛddhi] before @deva[सेट्] parasmaipada @deva[सिच्]: @deva[प्रौर्णावीत्] (with @[vṛddhi]) or @deva[प्रौर्णवीत्] (without). This option applies when @deva[सिच्] is not @deva[ङित्]; when @deva[ङित्] (by optional @ref[1.2.6]), @deva[उवङ्] substitution gives @deva[प्रौर्णुवीत्] (@ref[6.4.77]).
advanced: Three possibilities for @deva[ऊर्णु] + @deva[लुङ्]: (1) @deva[सिच्] not @deva[ङित्], with @[vṛddhi]: @deva[प्रौर्णावीत्]; (2) @deva[सिच्] not @deva[ङित्], without @[vṛddhi]: @deva[प्रौर्णवीत्]; (3) @deva[सिच्] @deva[ङित्] (@ref[1.2.6] option): @deva[प्रौर्णुवीत्] (@deva[उवङ्]-substitute by @ref[6.4.77], no @[guṇa]/@[vṛddhi]). The initial @deva[औ] is from @deva[आ]-prefix + @deva[ऊर्णु] (@deva[आ] + @deva[ऊ] → @deva[औ] by @[vṛddhi] sandhi).

simple: After @deva[आस्], @deva[ई] replaces the @deva[आ] of @deva[आन]. @deva[आस्] + @deva[आन] → @deva[आसीनः] 'seated'.
standard: For @deva[आस्] 'to sit', the @deva[आ] of @deva[आन] becomes @deva[ई]: @deva[आस्] + @deva[आन] → @deva[आसीन] (@deva[आसीनो] @deva[यजते]). By @ref[1.1.54], the first letter of the following element after ablative @deva[आसः] (i.e., @deva[आ] of @deva[आन]) is replaced.
advanced: The @deva[आने] (@ref[7.2.82]) should be read as @deva[षष्ठी] (genitive) here, not @deva[सप्तमी] (locative). The @ref[1.1.54] reference: when a substitute is ordained 'after X' (@deva[पञ्चमी] (ablative)), it replaces the first sound of the following. Thus @deva[आ] of @deva[आन] → @deva[ई] after @deva[आस्], giving the unique form @deva[आसीन].

simple: In genitive singular, @deva[तव] replaces @deva[युष्म्] and @deva[मम] replaces @deva[अस्म्]. @deva[तव], @deva[मम].
standard: For @deva[षष्ठी]-@deva[एकवचन] (genitive singular): @deva[युष्म्] → @deva[तव], @deva[अस्म्] → @deva[मम]. The @deva[द्] is elided by @ref[7.2.90], and the ending simplifies: @deva[तव] + @deva[द्] + @deva[अस्] → @deva[तव] + @deva[अ] (by @ref[7.1.27]) → @deva[तव] (@ref[6.1.97]). Compounds: @deva[परमतव], @deva[परममम].
advanced: Derivation: @deva[युष्मद्] + @deva[ङस्] → @deva[तव] + @deva[द्] + @deva[ङस्] → @deva[तव] + @deva[द्] + @deva[अ] (@ref[7.1.27]) → @deva[तव] + @deva[अ] (@ref[7.2.90] @deva[द्]-lopa) → @deva[तव] (@ref[6.1.97] coalescence). The genitive singular forms are distinctive, lacking the -@deva[म्] of other singular cases.

simple: Before case-endings, @deva[क] replaces @deva[किम्]. @deva[कः], @deva[कौ], @deva[के].
standard: For @deva[किम्], @deva[क] replaces the whole before @deva[विभक्ति]: @deva[कः], @deva[कौ], @deva[के]. The substitute is @deva[क] (not just @deva[अ] for @deva[म्]) to handle @deva[अकच्]-augmented forms properly. If only @deva[अ] replaced @deva[म्], forms would be incorrect.
advanced: Why @deva[क] and not @deva[अ]? If @deva[किम्] + @deva[अ] (for @deva[म्]) → @deva[कि] + @deva[अ] → @deva[क] (by coalescence), the @deva[अकच्]-forms would fail. With @deva[क] as whole substitute: @deva[किम्] → @deva[क] + @deva[विभक्ति]. The @deva[अकच्] augment (@deva[किमक]) would then give @deva[किमक] + @deva[अ] = *@deva[कयक] (wrong). So @deva[क] wholesale substitution is needed.

simple: Before the ending @deva[अत्], @deva[कु] replaces @deva[किम्]. @deva[क्व] @deva[गमिष्यसि] 'where will you go?'
standard: For @deva[किम्] before @deva[अत्] (locative), @deva[कु] replaces: @deva[कु] + @deva[अत्] → @deva[क्व] (by @deva[उ] + @deva[अ] → @deva[व]). If @ref[7.2.104]'s @deva[कु] were used, it would cause @[guṇa] (*@deva[को] + @deva[अ]). This separate @deva[कु]-substitute avoids @[guṇa].
advanced: Why separate @[sūtra]? The @deva[कु] of @ref[7.2.104] before @deva[अ] would trigger @[guṇa]: @deva[कु] + @deva[अ] → @deva[को] (@ref[6.1.87]). But @deva[क्व] shows no @[guṇa]. Hence this specific @deva[कु] before @deva[अत्]. The form @deva[क्व] (not *@deva[को]) requires this separate rule. @deva[अकच्]-augmented @deva[किम्] also takes this: @deva[किमक] → @deva[कुक] before @deva[अत्].

simple: Before case-endings, @deva[म्] replaces the @deva[द्] of @deva[इदम्]. @deva[इमौ], @deva[इमे], @deva[इमम्], @deva[इमान्].
standard: For @deva[इदम्] before @deva[विभक्ति]: @deva[द्] → @deva[म्]: @deva[इदम्] + @deva[अम्] → @deva[इद] + @deva[अ] (@ref[7.2.102]) + @deva[अम्] → @deva[इम] + @deva[अ] (@ref[7.2.109]) + @deva[अम्] → @deva[इमम्] (@ref[6.1.97], @ref[6.1.107]). Similarly: @deva[इमौ], @deva[इमे], @deva[इमान्].
advanced: Derivation: @deva[इदम्] + @deva[औ] → @deva[इद] + @deva[अ] + @deva[औ] (@ref[7.2.102]) → @deva[इम] + @deva[अ] + @deva[औ] (@deva[द्]→@deva[म्], @ref[7.2.109]) → @deva[इमौ] (sandhi). This @deva[द्]→@deva[म्] creates the characteristic @deva[इम]- stem seen in most cases of @deva[इदम्].

simple: Before consonant-initial case-endings, the @deva[इद्] of @deva[इदम्] is elided. @deva[आभ्याम्], @deva[एभिः], @deva[एषाम्], @deva[एषु].
standard: For @deva[इदम्] before @deva[हलादि] @deva[विभक्ति], @deva[इद्]-@deva[लोप]: @deva[इदम्] → @deva[आभ्याम्], @deva[एभिः], @deva[एभ्यः], @deva[एषाम्], @deva[एषु]. The @deva[म्] → @deva[अ] (@ref[7.2.102]), then @deva[अ] + @deva[अ] → @deva[आ] (@ref[6.1.97]). Not just final letter—whole @deva[इद्] is elided.
advanced: The @ref[1.1.52] rule (substitute replaces final only) doesn't apply here, per maxim @deva[मानर्थके] @deva[अलोऽन्त्यविधिः] 'when meaningless, not just final'. Eliding only @deva[द्] would leave @deva[इ], serving no purpose. So entire @deva[इद्] drops: @deva[इदम्] + @deva[भ्याम्] → @deva[आभ्याम्].

simple: The @deva[ठ] of taddhita affixes is replaced by @deva[इक्]. Examples: @deva[आक्षिकः], @deva[शालाकिकः], @deva[लावणिकः].
standard: @deva[ठ]-@deva[आदि] taddhita → @deva[इक्]-@deva[आदेश]. @deva[ठक्] becomes @deva[इक] (the @deva[ठ]→@deva[इ], @deva[क्] remains). @deva[आक्ष] + @deva[ठक्] → @deva[आक्षिकः] 'relating to dice', @deva[शालाक] + @deva[ठक्] → @deva[शालाकिकः]. The substitution affects the initial consonant of @deva[ठ]-affixes.
advanced: @deva[ठादि] (initial @deva[ठ]) of taddhita affixes receives @deva[इक्] substitution. This transforms @deva[ठक्]→@deva[इक], @deva[ठञ्]→@deva[इक], etc. The substitution @deva[इक्] replaces @deva[ठ], not the whole affix. @deva[आक्षिकः], @deva[लावणिकः] demonstrate: the base + @deva[इक] + remaining affix portions.

simple: The @deva[पू]-class roots (@deva[पूञ्] etc.) shorten their vowel before @deva[शित्] affixes. @deva[पूञ्] → @deva[पुनाति].
standard: @deva[पूआदि] roots + @deva[शित्] → @deva[ह्रस्व]. This subgroup of @[kryādi] class (@deva[पूञ्] to @deva[प्लुङ्]) shortens long vowels before @[vikaraṇa]. @deva[पूञ्] @deva[पवने] → @deva[पुनाति] 'purifies'. The shortening is characteristic of this subdivision.
advanced: @deva[पूआदि] (@deva[क्र्यादि] subdivision from @deva[पूञ्] to @deva[प्लुङ्]) undergo @deva[ह्रस्वन] before @deva[शित्]. Long vowels shorten: @deva[पू]→@deva[पु], @deva[लू]→@deva[लु], etc. @deva[पुनाति], @deva[लुनाति] show the pattern. This group-specific shortening contrasts with lengthening in other root classes (@ref[7.3.74]-76).

simple: In Yajurveda @[kāṭhaka], @deva[देव] and @deva[सुम्न] take @deva[आ] before denominative @deva[क्यच्]. @deva[देवायन्तो], @deva[सुम्नायन्तो].
standard: @[kāṭhaka]: @deva[देव]/@deva[सुम्न] + @deva[क्यच्] → @deva[आ]-@deva[आदेश]. @deva[देवायन्तो] @deva[यजमानाः], @deva[सुम्नायन्तो] @deva[हवामहे]. This Yajurvedic recension uses @deva[आ]-denominatives.
advanced: @deva[यजुष्]-@deva[काठक] specific: @deva[देव]→@deva[देवायति], @deva[सुम्न]→@deva[सुम्नायति] with @deva[आ]-substitution. @deva[देवायन्तो], @deva[सुम्नायन्तो] are @[kāṭhaka] Yajurveda forms. Recension-specific morphology is recorded in the grammar.

simple: In reduplication of @deva[द्युत्] and causative @deva[स्वापि], the semivowel vocalizes. @deva[विद्युतत्], @deva[सुष्वाप].
standard: @deva[द्युत्]/@deva[स्वापि] (@deva[स्वप्]-causative) @deva[अभ्यास]: semivowel → vowel. @deva[द्युत्] → @deva[विद्युतत्] (@deva[य्]→@deva[इ]), @deva[स्वापि] → @deva[सुष्वाप] (@deva[व्]→@deva[उ]). The @deva[य्]/@deva[व्] of reduplicant becomes @deva[इ]/@deva[उ].
advanced: @deva[सम्प्रसारण] for @deva[अभ्यास] of @deva[द्युत्]/@deva[स्वापि]. @deva[विद्युतत्] (@deva[द्युत्]), @deva[विद्योतिषते] (desid.); @deva[सुष्वाप], @deva[सुष्वापयिषति] (@deva[स्वप्]-causative). The semivowel-vocalization creates these forms.

simple: For @deva[स्रु], @deva[श्रु], @deva[द्रु], @deva[प्रु], @deva[प्लु], and @deva[च्यु] in desiderative, reduplicant @deva[उ] optionally becomes @deva[इ] when semivowel is followed by @deva[ऋ]. @deva[सुस्रूषते] or @deva[सिस्रूषते].
standard: Listed roots + @deva[सन्]: optional @deva[उ]→@deva[इ] for @deva[अभ्यास] when semivowel+@deva[ऋ] follows. @deva[स्रु] → @deva[सुस्रूषते] or @deva[सिस्रूषते], @deva[श्रु] → @deva[शुश्रूषते] or @deva[शिश्रूषते]. @deva[विभाषा] allows both.
advanced: @deva[विभाषा] for @deva[स्रु]/@deva[श्रु]/@deva[द्रु]/@deva[प्रु]/@deva[प्लु]/@deva[च्यु] @deva[अभ्यास]-@deva[उ] before semivowel+@deva[ऋ] in @deva[सन्]. @deva[सुस्रूषते]/@deva[सिस्रूषते], @deva[शुश्रूषते]/@deva[शिश्रूषते], @deva[दुद्रूषते]/@deva[दिद्रूषते]. Both forms valid; the @deva[ऋ]-following condition triggers optionality.

simple: The word @deva[भित्त] means 'a fragment, portion', formed irregularly from √@deva[भिद्]. Example: @deva[भित्तम्] 'piece'.
standard: The participle @deva[भित्त] from √@deva[भिद्] 'to split' retains @deva[त्] in the specific meaning of 'fragment, portion'. In other senses, the regular form applies. The term denotes something broken off as a piece.
advanced: The @deva[शकलार्थे] restriction specifies that @deva[भित्त] with retained @deva[त्] means 'fragment' (@deva[शकल]). In general 'split' meanings, @deva[भिन्न] is standard. This semantic @[nipātana] distinguishes @deva[भित्त] 'wall/portion' from @deva[भिन्न] 'broken/split'.

simple: Short @deva[इ]/@deva[उ] before penultimate @deva[र्]/@deva[व्] is also lengthened before consonants. Example: √@deva[ऊर्णु] → @deva[ऊर्णोति].
standard: When @deva[र्] or @deva[व्] is penultimate (@deva[उपोत्तम]) and followed by a consonant, the preceding short @deva[इ]/@deva[उ] lengthens. This extends the @deva[दीर्घ] rule to a slightly different structural context within the root.
advanced: This rule handles cases where the @deva[र्]/@deva[व्] is not final but penultimate, with a following consonant. The structural condition @deva[रेफव्योरुपधायाः] @deva[हलि] ensures lengthening in these complex cluster environments.

simple: When reasserting something after refutation, the final syllable optionally becomes @deva[प्लुत], expressing censure.
standard: In debate (@deva[वाद]), when the victor reasserts a position that was wrongly refuted (@deva[निग्रह]), the sentence-final syllable optionally takes @deva[प्लुत]. This marks the triumphant restatement with implied censure of the opponent.
advanced: The @deva[निगृहीतस्य] @deva[प्रत्याख्याने] context is from @deva[शास्त्रार्थ] (philosophical debate). The @deva[प्लुत] signals the speaker's vindication. The optional nature allows stylistic choice in expressing the rebuttal's force.

simple: In a repeated vocative expressing threat, the final syllable becomes @deva[प्लुत]. Example: @deva[देवदत्त] @deva[देवदत्ता३].
standard: When a vocative is repeated (@deva[आम्रेडित]) to express threat (per @ref[8.1.8]), the final syllable of the second occurrence takes @deva[प्लुत]. Thus @deva[देवदत्त] @deva[देवदत्ता३] conveys threatening address. The prolongation intensifies the menace.
advanced: The @deva[तर्जने] @deva[आम्रेडितस्य] condition links to @ref[8.1.8] (vocative repetition for threat). The @deva[प्लुत] falls on the repeated vocative's end, marking the threatening prosody. This creates a distinctive warning intonation.

simple: When diphthongs @deva[ए]/@deva[ओ] become @deva[प्लुत], their second element (@deva[इ]/@deva[उ]) is prolonged. Example: @deva[ए] → @deva[अइ३], @deva[ओ] → @deva[अउ३].
standard: For the diphthongs @deva[ए], @deva[ऐ], @deva[ओ], @deva[औ], the @deva[प्लुत] applies to the final component (@deva[इ] or @deva[उ]). Thus @deva[ए] becomes @deva[अइ३], @deva[ओ] becomes @deva[अउ३]. The diphthong is analyzed as @deva[अ]+@deva[इ] or @deva[अ]+@deva[उ], and the second element takes the prolongation.
advanced: The @deva[एदोतः] @deva[अन्त्यस्य] condition specifies that in diphthong @deva[प्लुत], the @deva[उत्तरस्य] (latter) element—the @deva[इ] or @deva[उ] component—receives the trimoraic extension. This phonetic analysis underlies Vedic recitation practice.

simple: The s of the root skambh always changes to @[ṣ] after the prefix vi. This is an obligatory change with no optionality, unlike some neighboring rules.
standard: Unlike the optional rules for @deva[स्यन्द्] and @deva[स्कन्द्], the @deva[स् → ष्] change for @deva[स्कम्भ्] after @deva[वि] is obligatory (@ref[8.3.77]). Forms like @deva[विष्कभ्नाति] and @deva[विष्कम्भितुम्] always have @deva[ष्] — no alternative with @deva[स्] is allowed.
advanced: @deva[ष्] is always the substitute of the @deva[स्] of @deva[स्कम्भ] after the @deva[उपसर्ग] (Preposition) @deva[वि]@deva[।] Thus @deva[विष्कभ्नाति], @deva[विष्कम्भिता], @deva[विष्कम्भितुम्], @deva[विष्कम्भितव्यम्] @deva[॥]

simple: The s of the word @[svasṛ] (sister) optionally changes to @[ṣ] when it appears in a compound after @[mātṛ] (mother) or @[pitṛ] (father). Both the retroflexed and non-retroflexed compound forms are valid.
standard: After @deva[मातृ] or @deva[पितृ] in a compound, @deva[स्] of @deva[स्वसृ] optionally becomes @deva[ष्] (@ref[8.3.84]). Valid forms: @deva[मातृष्वसा] or @deva[मातृस्वसा] (mother's sister, i.e., maternal aunt); @deva[पितृष्वसा] or @deva[पितृस्वसा] (father's sister, i.e., paternal aunt). The option (@deva[वा]) reflects dialectal variation in @[pāṇinian] usage.
advanced: @deva[ष्] is optionally substituted for the @deva[स्] of @deva[स्वसृ] after @deva[मातृ] and @deva[पितृ] in a compound. As @deva[मातृष्वसा], @deva[पितृष्वसा] @deva[॥] See @ref[6.3.24].

simple: The word for 'ritually pure' that begins with the prefix prati has an irregular retroflex form, but only when it refers to a grammatical rule (sutra). In all other uses the regular form without the retroflex is correct.
standard: The word @deva[प्रतिष्णात] is an irregular @deva[निपात] (listed form) formed when it refers to a @deva[सूत्र] in the sense of 'pure' or 'refined.' Thus @deva[प्रतिष्णातः सूत्रम्] 'a pure @[sūtra].' Outside this specific meaning, the expected form @deva[प्रतिस्नातम्] is used and no @deva[ष्त्व] applies. This is an exception to the general pattern of @ref[8.3.65].
advanced: The word @deva[प्रतिष्णात] is irregularly formed when meaning '@[sūtra]'. Thus @deva[प्रतिष्णातः] @deva[सूत्रम्] i. e. @deva[शुद्धम्] 'pure'. When it has not this meaning we have @deva[प्रतिस्नातं] = 'bathed'

simple: Two specific words meaning 'firmly fixed' or 'stiffened' with the prefixes prati and nis are listed as exceptional — they do not undergo the retroflex change even though the phonological conditions are met.
standard: The words @deva[प्रतिस्तब्ध] and @deva[निस्तब्ध] are @deva[अपवाद]s (exceptions) where @deva[ष्त्व] is not applied, despite the phonological environment qualifying under @ref[8.3.59]. The @deva[स्] of @deva[स्तब्ध] remains unchanged. These are listed irregularly, paralleling other @deva[निपात] exceptions in this section of @[adhyāya] 8.
advanced: The words @deva[प्रतिस्तब्ध] and @deva[निस्तब्ध] are anomalous. In these words the @deva[स] is not changed to @deva[ष], though so required by @ref[8.3.67].

simple: A @deva[नकार] occurring at end of a @deva[पद] does not get @deva[णत्वम्] @deva[।]
standard: @deva[पद]-final @deva[न्] not → @deva[ण्]. @deva[वृक्षान्], @deva[अरीन्], @deva[गिरीन्].
advanced: Of a @deva[न्] final in a @deva[पद] , @deva[ण्] is not the substitute. Thus @deva[वृक्षान्], @deva[प्लक्षान्], @deva[अरीन्], @deva[गिरीन्] @deva[॥]

simple: According to @deva[शाकल्य], a @deva[यर्] letter is never duplicated.
standard: @[śākalya]: No doubling anywhere. @deva[अर्कः], @deva[ब्रह्मा] (no doubling).
advanced: According to the opinion of @deva[शाकल्य] , there is reduplication no where. As @deva[अर्कः], @deva[मर्कः], @deva[ब्रह्मा], @deva[अपह्नुते] @deva[॥]
